Review Text: Best Professor I've Ever Had. He explains complex concepts with such clarity and depth that everything feels understandable. What truly sets him apart is how deeply he cares about his students. His kindness and compassion create an incredibly supportive learning environment. On top of that, he's hilarious, making class not only informative but fun!
Probably one of the best math professors at wash

Schaefer is a very skilled educator and he is very qualified to teach the sensitive 310W.  Schaefer's 310 included a good coverage of foundational topics. The class is very difficult, that's just the nature of it. Schaefer doesn't seem to hold punches but still gives exceptional lectures and is very accessible and helpful outside of class.
Schaefer remains unparalleled for Calculus lectures. Precup was also very good.
Professor Schaefer is an amazing Professor. He is very organized and gives practice tests and quizzes to help people succeed.

Schaefer is a really good calc professor. If you can, take calc 2 +3 with him, you will do a lot better that way. I will say, it would be nice if there was more partial credit on exams, but that's a math department problem, not a schaefer problem.

Take Schaefer if you can, and if you can't, just go to his lectures or watch them online. His lectures are very engaging and he teaches calculus in the easiest way to understand as possible. He's also a very fun and sweet guy.

Schaefer's so kind and sweet. Makes an effort to connect with the class and learn names. On top of that, great lecturer and is excited to answer questions. In office hours, he'll explore material deeply with you. Not enough partial credit on MC exams, but other than that, he's one of my fav professors and makes me want to pursue math more. Calc 2+3
One of the best lecturers I've ever had.
Live laugh love Schaeffer

Professor Schaefer is the goat of calculus at WashU. Clear and concise lectures, easygoing and humorous personality, and is always happy to help. You can feel that you're learn so much after every single lecture.
The best math professor at WashU.
He is an all-time great in the WashU math department. This is unfortunately the last class I will get to take with him but he will forever be one of my favorite teachers here at WashU.
Who doesn't love Schaefer? PS solve the practice exams since they are super similar to actual exams!!!

Schaefer is the actual GOAT of the math department. 4351 is my favorite math class I've taken here, and it's all due to Karl. If he's teaching a class you want to take, then take it. He is a generational talent in the math department.

Schaefer is an incredibly skilled educator. He's a funny and caring guy, and he's great at math. He builds an intuitive understanding that I feel respects pure mathematics, and also acknowledges the relevant applications (especially relevant in Calc II imo). I think you should do everything in your power to take calc with Schaefer.

Schaefer is one of the best teachers I've ever had. He made sure to be available outside of class and did a great job of supporting my class' varying levels of learning. He's responsive, funny, a great lecturer, and his cats are the cutest.

BEST LECTURER. Believe the reviews. He's kind, interesting, and a teacher who truly cares about you understanding the material and how comfortable you feel with it. He is always willing to help if you ask!!!! I wish I went to more of his office hours.
good professor and clear lectures. make sure you pay attention because they'll try to trick you on tests. would definitely reccomend.

While Calc 2 is generally a tough class, Schaefer made lectures super enjoyable, and his writing was always clear and easy to follow. As someone who didn't take any AP-level calc is hs, the class content was pretty difficult to get the hang of, but as long as you go to lectures (or watch them after class), you should be fine.
Absolutely worst course ever taken. You have abosulte no clue what to do in the lab. Worst grading system ever seen, just meant to make you fail.

Professor Lutes is extremely over-hated. She is very nice and helpful, and the grading overall is very fair. The main complaint is that her lectures are not helpful, but that is because they are meant as supplements to the video lectures - not as the main teaching component. If you put in the work to prepare they are very useful for the reports.

I honestly believe that she is overhated and most of the negative comments come from students who didn't bother to attend her lectures. I found that her lectures were very helpful with the pre-lab and content, and she was a chill, knowledgeable professor who put in effort to support her students. Somewhat cluttered and disorganized though.
WashU is blessed to have Dr. Lutes! I never had the opportunity to have chem lab before college, and I am glad to have had the experiences I had in 151 & 152.
Literally dont go to lecture its pointless. Matt Autry will be your savior

weird that they mandate this course, doesnt really coincide with chem 111, can't say anything about chem 105. content itself was never really that hard, but so much of it and easy to get behind so make sure you understand each lab as you do it so you don't have to learn it all before the exam (like I did)

This class was very confusing and I never knew exactly what was going on with the labs, but it's easy to do well if you work with others and keep on top of labs. Most people did not attend lecture, but I found it helpful in finishing the prelab (answers were often discussed in class). She seems nice/caring, although she gets sidetracked easily.
Dr. Lutes just doesn't care if we learn. Grading is all over the place. Exams are ridiculous, but she gives extra credit. Can't Mabbs or Daschback teach the lab? That would be incredible.
Shes is not a good teacher.

Dr. Lutes is engaged, caring, and human. She is explicit about grading criteria and expectations. She expects a fair amount of intrinsic motivation, maturity, and autonomy from students. She is there to help, and does so excellently, but you have to bring something to the table as well. I highly recommend and believe she is rated unfairly here.

She is super nice and cares about her students. Shes also hilarious, and runs a DJing gig outside of school. Although chemistry is hard, she, and the entire chem 151 team, is there with endless amounts of support and help for you to thrive.
If you need help with this course, go to Matt Autry. He is incredible at explaining difficult concepts.

Not sure why she has so many low ratings, but she's one of the best professors I've ever had. She has great prerecorded lectures for students to watch and come to lecture with understanding and questions, it's much more time-effective and allows for collaboration and critical thinking during class. Slightly unprofessional but fun and knowledgeable.

I know Lutes' lecture is hard to follow because she speaks very slowly and sometimes gets distracted but she is really a good person and cares about her students. Lab reports could be hard to do on your own so the key to this class is to go to office hours. I went to her office hours every single week and got good grades on all of my lab reports.

While professor lutes doesn't give great lectures all of the material you need to know is in the lecture videos/lab manual. She was very understanding of personal issues and very generous with rounding up grades. Lab reports are kind of annoying but there is a lot of resources offered. You can definitely do well in this class if you want to.

I rated her poorly last semester, but after having gone to her office hours, I can say that she is one of the nicest professors. She cares a lot about her students being able to understand the assignments. It is just up to the students to seek it.

Lutes is sweet and cares about the department. She does not prepare well for lab lectures but goes off of student questions and still does a good job of explaining. You can also go to her with questions after class. Just listen to the safety rules.
She does not seem to know what she is talking about and does not prepare for her lectures well. skip the lecture for lab. the labs are easy though and the whole class is if you've taken AP Chem
Contrary to popular opinion, I found professor Lutes to be great professor.

This class is required, but you won't enjoy it at all. It's incredibly disorganized and lab reports are impossible to do without attending office hours. Lab lectures are hit or miss -- either somewhat helpful or a complete waste of time. Matt Autry is my savior. He is the only reason I was able to do well in this class.

I really like professor Johnson. He cares so much about the material and his students. The first exam might've been a bit too hard but the final was very fair and we had 2 take-home exams. Psets could get a bit lengthy, but the exams were always easier than the Psets. Would recommend if you love math!

Lectures are boring and go a bit too slowly. He organizes the class very easily so that all the hardest questions will be seen in class or on WebWork (homework), and the exams are honestly far easier than all of those. If you can do the practice exams, you should be able to do rather well on the actual exams.

Professor Johnson was a great professor to take number theory with. He is very knowledgeable about the subject, and he did a good job explaining the material during lectures. I felt the exams were fair, and we were given two take-home exams to help de-weight the in-person exams. He also offered lots of extra credit opportunities on PSets and exams.

Johnson is the best professor I have had in the Math department. His lectures are great and he cares about the material. He also cares about his students and goes out of his way to help students. Highly recommend taking this course with him.

Awful. The lectures are boring (not that most people go), the teaching style is mundane, and the exam will try to trick you. Dr. Johnson also makes no effort to assist struggling students. Try taking this class in a fall with another professor if you can.
He was great.
Bro didn't release exam statistics because of how horribly it went. The only thing that can save me now is Jesus.
Even so much as thinking about this class genuinely makes me want to cry

I want to like this class so bad because Prof Johnson is nice, the content is interesting, and people in the class seem helpful. It is just way too difficult and feels like a full time job with overtime. So I have come to despise it which definitely makes me feel a little sad as a math major.

I can't take this anymore. I am spending so much time on this class yet doing the worst in it. I feel like I've lost my sense of pride in my work and all sense of feeling about how I perform. Someone said the class feels inaccessible and I've never agreed more. I wish i had advice to not take this class before i jumped in head first.

What went from being my favorite class at the beginning of the semester is now my most dreaded, scary, difficult, impossible class. He's not understanding of our time or responsibilities outside of this class and expects us to spend hours on HW while giving us the most impossible exams. Steer clear of this class if you want to keep your sanity.

Came to rate my professor cuz I thought I was the only one feeling terrible about this number theory class. But looks like theres a ton of others that feel this way as well. I guess it speaks for itself, just don't take MATH4351 with Silas Johnson.

This class is so disappointing. As a math major, I was so excited about Number theory and cryptography. but this class has been more annoying than fun and has destroyed my self esteem. Prof. Johnson is a good lecturer but makes the class so inaccessible almost. The problem sets take at least 10 hours and the exam was graded SO harshly.
I genuinely do not think it is possible to get an A in this class. The HW assignments are graded so harshly, and the exam averages are incredibly low.
Too hard

Prof. Johnson is a good lecturer and a terrible grader. He wanted us to learn how to write rigorous proofs so he took off major points on the weekly problem sets for the most trivial errors. Not to mention the problem sets probably took around 5 hours on average to complete. I wanted to like him, but this class was more annoying than fun.

10/10 professor- easily the best for Calc 1. Tests aren't too hard- definitely use the Study hub section that he posts on canvas to study because he puts a lot of extra practice there. Textbook can be found for free online and is only used for optional extra practice. Homework is much harder than exams, but you'll get that with every math prof.
Excellent prof, teaches really well but includes a lot of extra information that would not be tested.

Professor Johnson is a nice guy. Lectures are helpful but not mandatory (he may get off track during lectures by trying to prove theorems but it's okay). Tests weren't too hard. There were also many opportunities given to receive extra credit. If you pay attention and study a bit, then you'll be fine. Textbook isn't really necessary.
Johnson is really good at explaining things and the test  are straight forward.

You do not realize how good Loombach is until you take other courses. The team is incredible and passionate. They just know how to teach. They have remained my biggest mentors at WU. I will miss them.

Professor Daschbach is the best at what she does. I have never met someone so organized and capable of explaining concepts. Anyone will tell you she is the glue of Chem 111-112 and the person who makes Gen Chem the best intro sequence at Washu by far.
Best professor I've ever had!! Every lecture was so clear and well thought out. Class material is difficult... but if you attend lecture and work hard, you will get the grade you want!
The goat
She is amazing

LOVE Daschbach's teaching. I prefer her more than Loomis because she gives better foundational explanations imo. Really well structured class but NEED to study for tests (they are very hard). Engaging lectures that use PollEverywhere to track attendance. Hard class, but great experience with a lot of help options.

Prof Daschbach is an incredible lecturer! You can tell that the presented material is thoughtfully curated and she truly loves chemistry. The exams are hard and the time limits make things stressful, but if you're willing to put in the time to study you'll get the grade you want. Although the course is tough, I feel like I've grown so much from it!

I love professor Dashcbach so much, if you have any interest in chemistry or science you must take a class with her. She cares about her students and wants them to learn the material, and even though the class is very tough, her positive attitude gets you through it.

Loomis + Daschbach = Best class ever!! They will push you, but you will learn. D is great w/ notes and details. L is great w/ explaining concepts and real life connections. If they ever teach together again, you must take this class.
An amazing professor that helps students understand challenging concepts. The class is structured for students to be successful.
Her Chem 111 class is no joke as far as difficulty goes, but she really does try to make it as simple and understandable as possible. Very helpful outside of class too. 100% would take her again!

While the tests are the hardest thing I have ever done, Professor Daschbach is incredible. She is very engaging, funny, and makes the material as understandable as possible. She turned a difficult subject into a puzzle to be solved and inspired me and my friends to work hard. The content is hard, but studying and going to office hours/PLTL helps.
very solid teacher, clear lecturing, straight forward and stays on topic.
Absolute legend!
She clearly loved chemistry so much and made the whole class love it as a result. I was so glad I took this class with her.

One of the nicest and most supportive professors at washu. I love that she loves chemistry and helping students to love chemistry. Lectures were really well organized and given. Chem 111A was a tough class, but both Dashbach and Loomis were really accessible and enthusiastically willing and able to help.

Professor Daschbach is genuinely one of the best professors at WashU. She takes extremely difficult material and makes it accessible to all students. The class is very hard, but she teaches students how to develop the work ethic to succeed in this class and others. She will always meet with students outside of class and is a very caring person!

The class is tough, but the professor is amazing. If you really care about achieving a good grade there are more than enough materials to do so. It is obvious Daschbach really cares about her students.

i love her. so enthusiastic and happy to help students. you can tell she genuinely loves what she teaches and wants students to succeed (as long as they put in the effort!). genchem is difficult, but it's very doable if you use your resources. she's always happy to stay after class/stay on zoom to answer everyone's questions. daschbach i <3 you
Amazing lecturer, her drawings/diagrams are the best by far. Very accessible outside of class, but the class is hard so be ready.
Best of the 3 intro psych professors by a long shot.

Best of the three intro psych professors. Super engaging lectures that keep you entertained and learning. 2 online quizzes during his time teaching, heavily based on the lectures so as long as you go to class you'll be fine.
I loved intro Psych with Sommers. He was so funny and very clearly outlined his expectations.  Sommers is very reasonable and clearly knows what he is doing.
Super engaging lectures. Very awesome respectable guy. Always leaves room for questions during lectures. Easy quizzes.
love him so much. Great passion for the subject

Professor Sommers is one of my favorite professors last semester. He not only explain the concepts very well, but also gives interesting anecdotes (which made the class easy to get through). Quizzes are fairly easy.

I loved Prof. Sommers, I wish he taught the whole semester instead of just the first third. Some people thought he was mean but he's just from Brooklyn and has a dry sense of humor. His slides are very bare bones so you do have to actually listen to take notes.

Sommers was my fav prof out of the three that taught. Actually enjoyed going to his lectures, he obviously knew what he was talking about and talked at a speed that allowed me to take good notes. Since they didn't release the recorded lectures until 48 before each quiz, I went to every lecture but I'm sure you don't have to. Light workload too!

Sommers is truly great at lectures, making them compelling and funny. He sounds like he knows what he's talking about, and his lectures match up well with quiz questions. He also will occasionally go on tangents that are time-wasting and entertaining. Overall, he seems like a great dude professor.

Professor Sommers for Intro to Psych was amazing, and I would highly recommend taking him when he is available for the Fall Semester again. His lectures were very straight to the point, and the way he wrote the questions on his quizzes were fair, as long as you took good notes and are engaged. I thought he answered a lot of question really good too

Really fantastic lecturer. Presented information clearly, at a good pace, and in an engaging manner. Answered students' questions in class. Quizzes for the class were challenging enough to make sure one learns the important content from the class but not overly difficult. Also, try not to procrastinate on the research credit.
Hes funny and made me want to come to class. he gots off topic easily which can be annoying but otherwise good way to start freshman year

Top notch dude. Hilarious and engaging lectures. Some other people here say the quizzes were poorly worded but I thought they were fine. 10/10 would take again. Be aware that if you're taking him for Intro to Psych then you may only have him teaching for a third of the semester.

Professor Sommers is very engaging and gives great lectures. As a few others have said on here, the quiz questions can be very confusing, but if you read carefully and know the content well the quizzes should not be a huge issue.

He's a charming lecturer who is very engaging. Good at explaining topics and cracks jokes during lecture to lighten up the mood. Quizzes varied. Oftentimes, the questions were very poorly written and extremely confusing. Good lecturer, poor design of quiz questions.
Is a nice guy and his lectures are decent. Quizzes are pretty challenging though and are worded strangely.

Very amazing professor. Contrary to another professor who taught Intro to Psych this semester, his lectures actually made sense and his quiz questions were very fair. Could get a little monotone at times but still very engaging. Definitely make the most out of your time with him before you get to the next professor.

(For Intro to psych) Sommers was solid. His lectures are clear and he teaches well. No homework/tests, only quizzes online every other week. My only complaint is that his quiz questions were sometimes poorly written/vague, but I'd recommend for Psych 100

Sommers section of the intro to psych class was really well done. His lectures were engaging and funny and he was always willing to talk to students after. The quizzes are a bit wordy and can be confusing, but if you study and go to lecture you'll be fine.
Engaging lecturer, loved his section of Intro to Psych!

Having just graduated I'm posting reviews of the best courses I took here. This was definitely one, even though it was covid year and all remote. I learned more, and talked about it more, than any other course. Lectures were fascinating and powerpoints helpful. Readings were a bit heavy worth it (with a few exceptions). Loved his book. Thanks!

This is one of the worst classes. Glenn makes us remember specific details from his long lectures to be tested on. He strays away from helping us actually understand bigger concepts. If you're not on top of your work, you will never get a good grade in this class. He didn't foster any sort of live interaction with himself and the students.

The lectures were all remote and recorded and they were mostly really good. Some were pretty opinionated but he backed the opinions up. He covered some remarkable and surprising material. The grading was not too hard.

Super interesting class, but it is very important to stay on top of the course work since it does get a little overwhelming at points. Very knowledgable professor with a serious passion for what he studies and teaches.

Class covers some interesting/ problematic points in history but the lvl of detail that we have to memorize is absurdly unnecessary. Exams test us on whether we are able to spit back specific facts mentioned in lengthy readings/ lectures rather than assessing how well we understand concepts. I do not feel like I'm gaining anything from this course.

If you're the kind of person who's really into corn this is the class/prof for you. There's a LOT of reading and you WILL be tested on it, so doing that and attending lectures are basically the only requirements. But don't get scared of the fact that's it's basically an agriculture class, you'll learn some interesting/scary things about the world.

Interesting content, but not well-delivered-- not a very understanding or flexible professor. Actually ended up curving down one of our tests when the average was a 91 (which I thought was unfair, especially considering the test was open-book and we had access to all the material).

Professor Stone is incredibly disorganized. His slides are confusing and his lectures are really hard to follow, and he doesn't do a good job of connecting all of the material together. The class is interesting in some ways but the lectures do drag on frequently.

The exams may cover personal opinions of his and it isn't clear what is important or not in lecture. Often the readings are emphasized over everything else. He has his own website with factoids you must know verbatim, no extra credit, not very friendly, cusses every other sentence (it's distracting), and digresses into story time more than teaches.

This is a very interesting class and with engaging lectures. I think I truly learned a lot from this class but I was turned off by Stone's attitude. He was quite arrogant and showed little concern for his students. Be prepared to do a lot of readings because you need to know them for the tests! But the class wasn't too hard and is worth taking.

Professor Stone is not the most likable nor approachable professor, but he is very clear in teaching the material and makes the tests simple and based on lectures.  Very interesting class, for majors and non-majors in anthro.

Glen Stone's food seminar was one of the best educational experiences I had at Wash U, even if the reading load was a bit excessive.  But the books were great, the discussions were engaging, and I still think about the seminar regularly.  He is a real teacher, and also went out of his way to help me after I graduated.

I took Brave New Crops with him, and the class was somewhat interesting; some lectures seemed to drag on. Stone can be kind of ignorant of students and not the most friendly or approachable. The quizzes/test are not very hard if you study.
the worst professor i have had at washu in a non-science course.  really acts as if he could care less about any of his students.

Professor Stone is one of the best professors I've had here at WashU.  I would highly recommend to those WUSTL students remotely interested in agriculture and our current industrial agricultural system.
Unusual class but fascinating.  Stone's lectures are brilliant. I'm taking another class of his next year and looking forward to it

The topic is very interesting, and he is an engaging and dynamic lecturer. The tests aren't hard, but you won't know what you got until the end because he waits to curve the tests. Also, he isn't helpful in office hours.

Had very little interest in anthropology, not to mention any real knowledge of genetically modified crops and organizations of societies, but Professor Stone really turned me on to such topics and I took a second class from him. Just a great professor, and love when he introduced his own research and field experiences into class.
Loved this class.

Culture & Environment is one of the best courses here.  Stone gives a perspective on environmental issues that was different from what I had always heard, but always clear and well supported and interesting.  Plus now I know how many pigs Ireland exported in 1846, which I had wondered about.

DO NOT TAKE HUMAN GENETICS. Duncan does not show up to his OH bc he simply FORGETS about it. He has not responded to most of my emails and does not have clear grading criteria. Average is a 60 for the class although tests have curves. The TAs are not helpful and the graders takes FOREVER to grade. Also he missed the last day to submit final grades

Biol324 had a ton of guest lectures, but Duncan was pretty good about taking the information that they gave us and translating it into an exam that wasn't impossible. All slides get posted online so going to class isn't required. He seemed a little scatter-brained at times but is a good professor overall

Especially after taking Bio 2960 at WashU, having Duncan as a bio professor was so refreshing. He was MUCH clearer than any of the other bio professors I had and he wrote on the board rather than relying on powerpoint slides, which made taking notes much easier. Tests are straightforward, easy to know what to expect.
This class was ridiculous but he made it much better.

Awesome dude, he is really into the whole drosophila thing. He may not care too much about you as an individual but he clearly loves what he is teaching and tries really hard to communicate the information thoroughly and clearly. Exams are hard but what do you expect from an premed intro class at at washu?
lectures are scattered, doesn't explain key concepts, struggled to find help, tests are ridiculous, worst class i've taken at WUSTL.
Made introductory bio way too hard.

Duncan is kind of all over the place with notes; it can be very unclear what he is talking about in class at times, even if you've done the reading in the textbook. The textbook isn't very helpful most of the time; it doesn't go nearly as in depth as class. I went through this class by basically teaching myself and going to TA hours a lot.
He's fantastic! Though, at times, not the most exciting lecturer, he teaches interesting material and is great at answering questions. I definitely recommend taking his class!

As unhelpful as a professor gets. I took the class this Summer, I emailed him to set up a meeting time because I didn't understand the material, and he responded with a class-wide email to direct all questions to the TA (who was fabulous, but she couldn't help me as much as he could have.)I highly dislike Dr. Duncan, Larson is better in every way.

Duncan can be dry and his notes sort of scattered. That being said, if you really understand his notes and the problem sets, the exams are doable, though there is always that hard question no one gets. He's not the most riveting, but once I took the time to actually understand his lectures by going over the notes, I found his exams much easier.

Duncan makes the pretty dry material seem slightly interesting. But watch out; he writes frustratingly difficult exams and just doing problem sets won't save you. Another weed-out similar to 2960 and many premeds will cry.

Restored my interest in Bio. It was a pleasure to go to lecture every day. He is a lot tougher than Larson, which is why some reviewers say they like Larson better, but he was way more interesting. Tests were conceptual. Don't bother memorizing old exams because he'll write new curveballs. Make sure to UNDERSTAND the problem sets.

Very interesting lecturer. Sometimes dry and slow but cares a lot for the class. Lectures go through basics and problems go through mediums. Tests are where you have to really think using what you already learned and worked on. Don't read the book unless you don't understand basic concepts... Work the problem sets over 2x w/o looking at answers.
Even though the class wasn't easy, Duncan taught the material very well
Very dry lecturer. Runs through material without really explaining. Writes difficult tests that try to trip you up.

He sounds exactly like Milton from Office Space.  That being said, at first I found him to be a pretty boring prof.  However, as the class progressed his lectures got more interesting.  He really cared about the class, and helped students understand the issues of genetics that affect all of us.

Very good at explaining topics- I think some students overlook that due to the difficulty of genetics itself.  Is surprisingly funny at random times.  Exams are difficult, but you don't need to read the book unless you don't understand lectures.
Very interesting material, tests are difficult.
Very****ty lectures. Repulsive voice.

Great professor. I took classical to renaissance literature. One of my favorite classes (I studied engineering). The texts chosen were interesting and the paper topics made us think about the stories differently. She asks probing questions to facilitate class discussion.

Jami is one of the best professors at WashU. She is hilarious, caring, and gives great feedback on everything. Participation is important but the class is discussion based, though its very casual. If you don't read you won't get much from the class. Jami is phenomenal and the highlight of my semester.

Jami is my favorite professor at this school. She is clear in her grading, her lectures are easy to follow and take notes on, and she is always open to student feedback or questions. Gender Violence can be intense and she does a fab job breaking it down and using humor to lightened intense situations. Take this class- changed my life.

Loved this class! Lots of reading, but tons of opportunities for extra credit. The pop reading quizzes are easy if you did the reading. Super interesting topics and conversations, and Ake is truly a fair grader if you put in the effort. Wonderful woman overall and wonderful class! Take it!

She is a really great professor that a lot of people respect, and a wonderful teacher. Her advice and commentary on my essays really helped for other classes, and she was very clear about her grading. The class is a lot of reading, but it's divided into completely doable proportions. I really recommend the class, and I love Professor Ake.

I want to be Jami Ake when I grow up. She is a wonderful human being and an amazing professor. If you have the opportunity to take one of her classes, do it. Gender Violence definitely changed my life. Jami's lectures are engaging and relevant, plus she is hilarious, upbeat, and cares so deeply about every single student.

Jami is so amazing! She made me laugh every class period with her sarcasm and her witty sense of humor. She is a great teacher, grades fairly and cares about all her students. Definitely worth all the reading.

Dr. Ake is the best professor I have had at WUSTL so far. She is fair and cares deeply. She gets to know all of her students on a personal level. She encourages students to disagree with her and promotes amazing discussions.

I've now taken two classes with Jami, and she really didn't disappoint.  She is by far the coolest person I know, and serves not only as a role model, but also as a true mentor to those who seek her out.  She's a phenomenal lecturer and overall professor and I always look forward to class or just chatting with her!

Jami awake might be the coolest person to ever walk the planet. She's an amazing instructor, underrated lay a genius, and is so passionate not only about what she does, but also about her students and making sure they succeed. Her advice and encouragement is how I discovered my passion and became a WGSS major. She's incredible.

I was told by older students that I had to take a class with her before I graduated -- and it did not disappoint. She makes you think critically about any other class you've taken/ things you think you know and is great at facilitating discussion. She genuinely cares about her students and it shows.

I truly love her. This is the only class I take that I look forward to. Jami, never ever stop teaching because you are one of the most inspirational people I have ever had the chance to learn from. If you have a chance to take a class with her, do it!!

Jami is actually one of the coolest people I've ever met.  She's hilarious, super down to earth, and always there to support her students.  She's actually a genius, and I'm simply amazed by all she does.
Favorite teacher this semester. She is inspirational and funny. Take a class with her!

Ake was the best professor I have had yet at WashU. She made Shakespeare so incredibly fun (which honestly is hard).  She encourages your opinions and discussion, but led most lectures. Always down to laugh, always happy, really helpful and understanding. Wants all her students to succeed which is amazing

Professor Ake is an incredible professor! I've never been interested in english, but I found myself fully interested and focused in all of our discussions! I would take a class just to have her as my professor again.

Jami Ake is basically my idol. She's a funny lecturer, extremely helpful, and generally personable and wonderful. Some people find that she's a tough grader, but I never thought her grading was anything but reasonable. Anyone and everyone should take Violence Against Women if they can-- you'll look forward to going to class.

Best professor I have had yet. I would highly recommend that anyone take Violence against Women with her. Very flexible about attendance, grading, and deadlines as long as you are putting in an effort.

A nice balance between lecture and discussion, I learned a lot, and the weekly quizzes weren't that bad. She's one of the few people who is simultaneously brilliant and good at teaching classes, and she actually cares about her students beyond the classroom. If you get the chance to take a class with her, DO!
Dear Jami Ake: I will hang desperately from your every word. Never ever, never stop teaching.
Chem 111 is a total disaster, but Mabbs is awesome. He gives very clear lectures and is very personable.
I LOVE HIM
difficult class but mabbs is awesome

Best science class I've taken in a while. Mabbs teaches lectures like a continuous story and class is entertaining and easy to follow. Assessments are difficult, but the curve makes it not too hard to do well.

Mabbs, mabbs, mabbs. What a mabbulous little British man! He cares so much about his students. He's very approachable as well, and his accent legitimately makes lecture less boring. Even though Chem 111 is definitely challenging, the instructors do a great job of providing lots of extra resources to help you succeed.

It's clear that Professor mabbs is very passionate about chemistry, and that he tries his best to help students understand course content. However, his expertise sometimes does the opposite because he just uses big words to explain very simple concepts, which can leave some people confused at times.

Mabbs was a good professor. Since 111 is such a hard class, I recommend going to Daschbach for office hours because she is an even better teacher in my opinion. Still, Mabbs is very passionate and quite organized as a lecturer. Be ready to put in the work for this class, but it isn't as impossible at it seems.

lots of tests but mabbs is a good professor that is passionate in what he teaches. he goes fast and there's a lot of tests so be prepared to essentially study the whole semester past October. would recommend if u can put the time in

Mabbs was a good lecturer who left me feeling prepared for the exams. He was also accessible to students outside of class and enjoyed getting to know people who wanted to know more about chemistry. Tests were extremely challenging and constitute most of your grade, so make sure you're prepared. The class incorporates a lot of physics too.

I learned so much, and even though I got a C, I didn't fully utilize the resources that were provided until the end of the semester. he is a really good lecturer, and it is really easy to take notes and to learn. I do suggest going to office hours with prof. daschbach, she is the GOAT!

Mabbs is a great professor. Chem 111 is designed to be a very different course but, with few exceptions, Prof. Mabbs did a great job explaining concepts at a reasonable pace and working through examples. HW is very light but studying is absolutely necessary to get a good grade.

Dr. Mabbs was a caring teacher with very clear grading criteria. Use the calendar to stay on top of things. He was confusing sometimes, and do not drift off or you will be lost. No fear, go back and look at the video. Go to help sessions. I like Dr. Dashback's.
Amazing lecturer, makes class very interesting but you have to pay attention constantly to do well in the class. If you like physics and need to take chem take 111.

Mabbs includes weekly chemistry in the real world segments which combine the topics learned with applications. It is really engaging and unique to this teacher. He sometimes goes through topics a bit quickly and his examples aren't very reflective of quizzes and exams. His handwriting on the board isn't very good and it can be hard to take notes

Professor Mabb's lectures are very clear and easy to follow. The tests are difficult, however there are lots of resources to prepare you for them. Overall, he's a very dedicated and nice professor and truly cares about the topics he teaches.

Professor Mabbs is such an amazing lecturer. He is able to make the topics easy to understand and interesting. He really cares and wants his students to succeed, and he is very accessible outside of class during his office hours.

Nicest and most relatable guy I've ever met, and he is always there with a smile. He'll also help you after class or during DAILY office hours. Theres so many resources to help you succeed, even if the material is hard. Daschbach on the contrary, is not a nice person, but she is very intelligent and helpful.

Professor Mabbs is the sweetest, most passionate, little British man ever. He makes the most difficult topics easy to understand, and his passion for chemistry is contagious. I came into his class thinking chemistry was hell on earth, and now I want to major in chemistry. There are so many resources, and his brilliance is very apparent.
He is an expert in teaching, providing many great insights into the principle of general chemistry.
Very good lecturer, his lectures tell a story about chemistry. He is kind and very interested in helping us succeed.

I took organic 228 for chemistry majors at Texas A&M with Dr. Wooley. She is a great professor and really cares about her students' success. Going to her office hours frequently was hard because she was always changing the days and times, but she was always very helpful. Her exams are extremely difficult, but she curves very well. TAKE HER CLASS!

While the her teaching style is more than demanding, I think it's refreshing that she doesn't treat her students like imbeciles. She believes that all of her students are up for a challenge. That being said, be prepared to eat, sleep, and breather organic chemistry. If you like it (I DID), than you're in luck. If not, take another professor.

Dr. Wooley is one of the most amazing professors/chemists I have ever had the pleasure to to take a course from.  She is brilliant and truly personable.  And even though I did not do well in Chem251 (due to my own lack of intelligence, no doubt), I did enjoy the class tremendously.  Even the horrific chem lab.
No Comments

For 251, she was a good lecturer, although her exams were harder than they needed to be - sometimes to the point of unfairness.  For 257, she made it clear that teaching the class was not one of her priorities, and it showed in the quality of her teaching.  Overall, a brilliant scientist, but an arrogant and condescending professor.
Like people have said, kiss her butt and you get a good grade.
this woman is crazy, and the class was WAY too hard, expecially for only 2 credits.
Like many have said, sometimes pushing your nose in the crap and kissing a lot of butt is required.  Way too much effort for the credit/knowledge/grade involved.
I can honestlly say that this woman's classes have been the most miserable experiences in my life.  Too much work, too much stress for the grade and/or credit received.

She was pretty disorganized and would come in all tousled, but that can be forgiven due to her passion for teaching and concern for the students.  The class wasn't too hard and you could do well with only a good set of class notes. PLUS she's a major hottie -ay ay ay!

If you act like alot of people and kiss her butt and act like u get aroused over polymerizations, she will give u 10 pts. back on a test even if u didnt deserve it. most unprofessional professor ever, always absent, should never teach again. stick to research lady and let eric wise teach instead.

This woman is amazing. She is extremely passionate about teaching and about her research. Very hard to find a prof like that at WU in sciences. She's a great teacher and she's very pleasant to talk to. easy to understand, great pronunciation, great looks (which doesn't hurt) + tests had no surprises
The class is very challenging, but the professor is excellent. She is passionate about the material and really cares about the students- be sure to go to her office hours about exams.

I'm sorry that we have to go to a school with people as ridiculously snotty as some of the previous reviewers.  Anyone who rates this woman a "5" on easiness is obviously full of themselves.  If you did well in the course, it's because you studied your butt off just like everybody else.
Absent once about every two weeks, loves suck ups
very hard class, if you can, take it pass fail - and go to her office hours!!

Good class and very interesting, has raised my interest level in chemistry.  Also happy it was a challenge, obviously all of the people complaining did not do very well - tough.  An excellent example of Darwinian Theory getting rid of all the losers.
hard class, but helpful

fantastic! energetic and engaging, priviledged to learn from such a world class scientist. 251 was fantastic...Wooley was great, TAs were surprisingly helpful and tests, though tough, had no surprises.
Obviously a lot of whining spoilt mommy's boys and girls, I thought it was tough but a challenge. Grow up or go back to high school
hes lowkey kinda mean but a pretty good prof. go to lecture bc he goes over stuff that isn't in the zybook but is on hw and the tests and you can get extra credit.
Brian is the best. No slander will be tolerated.

Super awkward guy but really sweet and good at teaching. Lectures can get kind of boring but they aren't required to go. If you do the Zybooks reading and practice problems, you'll do find in the course.
Kind of an awkward guy but seems to genuinely care. Also active on WashU Reddit which is kind of funny. Exams are like the Zybooks and if you put in the effort you will definitely get an A.
Easy class, his lectures were boring but at least useful

Clearly the better Garnett. Good at teaching logic and discrete math. If you read the textbook and go to class you will do well. Exams are fair and assignments are graded forgivingly. Great class, highly recommend

I don't particularly understand where the negative reviews come from. CSE 240 was a well-taught class and I learned a lot. Lectures were helpful but not mandatory, and the textbook made things quite clear. Exams are fair and as long as you do the homework you'll do well.

Lectures are boring/redundant since the professor just writes/talks through what's covered in the textbook, except the few times there are long examples not found in the book; those will usually be on the test. However, it's still a very straightforward class and the prof is very responsive/helpful on Piazza. Not super enjoyable, but does the job.

To be honest, no disrespect but I didn't go to almost all of the lectures because the Zybooks covered all of the content and everything from the class can be learned in the Zybooks. There is a lot of hw from the Zybooks. However, while the lectures can be boring he seems to be a helpful and caring guy, at least from the few lectures I went to.

Professor Garnett is one of the first professors at WashU that I felt really cared. He actually responds on Piazza all the time and clearly cares about getting to know his students and having them succeed. It also is an interesting course and both his lectures and the textbook help solidify the concepts so you feel well-prepared for the exams.

Some people say his lecture was boring, which might be true, but I found it ok. If you attend classes and be present, you will get decent grade. I attended to all the classes and did not study much outside of class.

At the end of the day the exams look fairly similar to the homework and the content is mangeable. His lectures are boring/not helpful and his notes are very messy, but by closely reading the textbook you are 100% set for the exams.

The worst lecturer I've ever had. Answers in with attitude, barely looks up, and not rly accessible outside of lecture. Lectures suck but he gives random quizzes that add points to exam grades. Exams are on homework stuff but you won't get your homework graded before the exam. Take it with a friend and live & breathe Zybooks to survive this class.
Scrabble champion. Looks straight out of the 80s. Walking CS prof stereotype. I concur with the review below this one wholeheartedly.

His teaching style (partially prewritten iPad notes) is sometimes hard to follow, unorganized, and can be too fast. Got lost easily due to these reasons. He isn't that good at answering or taking questions (awkward, doesn't look up often & can be sassy). The exams are fair/similar to homework problems. Would recommend utilizing the textbook
This class is fairly easy and his lectures are pretty good, but you certainly do not have to go to lectures to do well in this class. His exams are very much like homework problems.

Let's be real: you are not going to go to lecture. Zybooks will be your lecture, your professor, and your god. You will put in just enough effort, get a good grade, and move on. This is the main attitude for 240. Garnett is a nice guy and is a good enough teacher, but low energy. The weekly homework is often a slump, but good for learning.

prof Garnett's lectures were boring at times, but he goes at a slow enough pace for you to get all of the notes down. He gives easy, low-stakes quizzes during class for extra credit. Exams are okay if you study, and the hw he assigns prepares you well. Overall a decent professor for 240.

Been said already. Lectures a bit boring and unorganized, his notes aren't super readable at all times. However, exams are EXTREMELY fair, homework policy adds an extra 100/0 to your HW grade. Lectures give minor extra credit from "quizzes" but not super worth it. Can pay minor attention and still get all the concepts.
Best lecturer on campus. 100% better than roman. Smartest chess player ever, and 240 is good class ok ok

He's a good lecturer, offers guided notes, hw has unlimited attempts, and theres plenty of TA OH. Quizzes & midterms resemble past ones (he provides many past examples). The final is tough though, so stay focused and dedicate some time to studying. Overall the class is fair and expectations are pretty clear, you more or less get what you put in.

Snir is a really good lecturer and professor, but he is not a good person. He is very condescending when answering questions and makes it uncomfortable for students to ask. That said, he teaches content really well (just not a great person).

Snir is a decent lecturer and fair grader, he makes sure all grading criteria are known well ahead of time for assessments... he is pretty boring but mainly because of the material.
This class is hard not because of the statistical material but because you must do the procedure correctly, label everything, put stats in context to get full credit.

Dr. Snir is such a caring professor. I love his lectures; he does the problems with you in class on the board. I really like how his tests, and classes are set up. His homework is a little hard. Make sure to have a study group for the homework or get a tutor. Overall, it was an enjoyable class.

Dr. Snir is a kind professor. His lectures always use real world application and the project is quite fun. It is an easy course due to the fact that every test and quiz comes with numerous practice exams. If you can commit the time to do these, you can easily obtain a high A in the course. Regrades during my semester boosted grades too.

He is easily the least compassionate person who is so absolved in themself that it is amazing that the school allows this man to keep teaching this class aside to stroke his own ego. This man is a testament to the tenure system and its flaws, It is the WORST and LEAST INTERESTING CLASS IN BUSINESS SCHOOL BY FAR.

I found this class to be a bit challenging- the homework takes a bit of time and the tests included processes we don't cover in class. Class is focused on the Excel processes; meanwhile, the tests are handwritten problems- just practice the past exams to get used to it. He is super nice and cares about his students. Great guy.

Great guy and pretty good professor. Interacting with him is often very easy as he's quite nice and rather enjoyable to talk to, but the lectures can definitely get boring quickly. You do have a term paper, a poster (based off of the term paper), and a final exam all due/held within 1-2 weeks of each other, so this class's workload is pretty high.

Professor Snir is a great professor and he cares about his students a lot. He holds plenty of office hours and are more than willing to answer students' questions. He instilled a new policy where you can earn up to 50% of the points missed on exams and quizzes.

Snir is a great professor; his lectures are straightforward and organized and he sets clear expectations for the class at the beginning of the semester. DAT121 can be a bit boring in terms of content at times, but Snir always incorporates examples that makes things easy to understand. Definitely cares about his students success!

Professor Snir is great. While the lectures can be boring (its Managerial Stats what can he do) he is really straight forward about what we need to know and is eager to help if we had any questions. The grade is focused mostly in the Final and the Midterm, but you get 6 cheat sheets, so like just study and put in the time.

Probably the least helpful professor I've had at WashU. Always tried to get help from him outside of class but he never was willing. His lectures are dry and uninteresting. I know it's statistics, but you can at least try to gather student attention.

Snir is as straight-forward as it gets. DO the homework, show up to class, take some notes ON HIS PRINTED OUT NOTES THAT HE GIVES YOU LOL and you'll be just fine.

The pop quizzes are really easy and more for attendence. They're also open book and literally have direct answer choices usually based on the notes that, again, HE prints and gives.

ENOUGH with the Snir slander. Snir is a great professor!! He prints out all the notes for students in advance, and makes complex topics easy to understand. The course is very well organized. Classes went at a good pace and I always felt engaged. He does a really good job explaining the core concepts, and is super helpful outside of class!!

This class is fine if you do the homework and show up to class. Just know this man is an absolute snoozefest and his tests are way too hard. He curves the class and he is genuinely pretty understanding, just super boring. But, he was the only DAT121 professor so I guess if you don't have a choice, you will get through it.

You get in what you put out in Snir's class. Lectures are a bit slow, but if you sit through them, take notes, and put in the effort you'll do well. Unfortunately, I did none of those things. Great teacher all around though.

Snir is a nice person and makes the content easy to follow. Yes, they may not be the most exciting lectures in the world, but we're learning stats -- give him a break. If you follow the lectures or literally just read the lecture notes, you should generally be fine for the quizzes and exams.

Eli Snir is prob the best professor I've had at WashU. he rewards good grades for those who have paid attention and come prepared to class no matter the exam grade that they get. Plus his exams are great because they are well structured and never deviate from the structure and difficulty of past exams. Awesome prof and even better person.
The final exam was just bizarre and unnecessarily obscure and hard. The grading in this class is just so inconsistent with no leniency. The professor was also just unhelpful most of the time.

This man has no business teaching undergraduates. I feel like a lot of topics he just covers as if we already learned them--it feels like he doesn't understand that him knowing the concepts doesn't mean everyone knows them. Lectures are pretty bland and often difficult to follow. He's a nice guy, but please stay away if you can.

Chem 358 is a super fun class, so if you're interested in organic synthesis, take it. The class is essentially run by the TAs, but Dr. Birman is a core resource when working on your project. As long as your science is good and you do your best, you'll be just fine grade-wise. (Also, don't worry if you don't finish your project...most people don't.)

This is a difficult advanced lab class that is meant as a preparation for graduate level work. Don't take just to satisfy advanced lab or writing intensive requirement especially if you are a premed. Overall Birman is a genius who can struggle sometimes to simplify things down to an undergraduate level, as English is not his first language.

The lab was so disorganized. At the beginning of the semester, you would have no idea what you were doing in lab until you got to the class that day, and then you'd receive barely any instruction and were expected to perform the lab perfectly. By the second half of the semester, Dr. Birman would disappear from the class after five minutes.
A must take class for every WashU student! This class will make your college life complete:D LOL

Vlad Birman is an awful teacher. His lectures are hard to follow and his exams are even harder. Study the textbook like it is your bible, make sure you look at his lectures to see if there is stuff not included in the textbook, but besides that only use the textbook. Grading is pretty lax though, very generous curve

Vlad is the worst professor I've ever had at WashU. His grading is inconsistent, his tests are absurdly difficult and downright unfair. I got a 36 out of 100 on a test once and that was an A. Avoid taking a class with him at all costs

Birman himself has a good sense of humor. He sometimes makes leaps in lecture that are difficult to follow but make sense after further explanation. To succeed in the class, make sure you really have all the material in the textbook down. Once you do, work on as many practice problems as possible. The final grade is curved pretty generously.

He clearly wants (and attempts) to help students but falls short. Lectures follow the Wade text closely so it's best to go through the book too. The only way to do well however is with practice tests and problem sets because lectures alone will NOT prepare you for his exam questions. His lack of partial credit makes the class very hard.

As a chemistry major I had passion and interest in orgo until Birman. His lecture ranks the worst I have ever had. He once posted practice problem answer keys on the day of the exam, literally less than 6 hours before the actual exam started. The forth exam had an average of 27 out of 100. Think twice if you want to take his class.

It may seem like 262 is more memorization, but there is actually application written in the test. Problem solving through piecing together syntheses is one example. It's best to read and take notes on the textbook, and use the lecture notes as a supplement with extra syntheses. Was not too bad, if you are willing to read.

Birman seems to have good intentions, and is kind. However, he could improve his organization, clarity, and insight into what organic chemistry is like for a student. Practice work was given very sporadically, which really hurts a student's chance to practice the confusing class material on their own.

Prof. Birman seems to be a smart guy who loves chemistry, but he is unable to give a lecture in a way that can be understood by students. He's also actively rude. Do absolutely everything you can to avoid taking his class!
i managed to pull an A off in this class, but dont let anyone tell you differently, this guy is hard as hell.

He might know chemistry, but he cannot teach.  Who is he talking to?  Not the students.  (I bet he has friends in his head that he is talking to.)  Just memorize everything he says in class, and then pray.  You might pass.

The only B I have received in my entire life. He spends the entire class staring at the blackboard. And if anyone asks him a question, he seems incapable of understanding/answering it. Horrible class. I don't understand how anyone can rate him as a good teacher. Brilliant chemist. Exams basically all synthesis.

This is crazy...Birman is terrible.  He is telling us to just memorize and you will do well.  Last year we were taught to learn and apply material and that is all gone.  Organic has to be better than this.

The difficulty of this class should not undermine his competence as a teacher; Granted the time commitment is very significant, but he is a very considerate person who will go to lengths to make sure you understand the concepts

He is terrible.  I am not sure why, it is not just his poor speaking ability, but he just cannot convey a coherent message.  I don't think it is nerves, because he is the same way in office hours.  He means well, but he should not be teaching.
bad. please avoid.

A hard class to teach but definitely loves his students. His notes are exquisite, he doesnt even need to bring a piece of paper to the lecture it's all in his head. Just pay attention and go to his office hours. He is VERY NICE. Just tell him you want to do well and he wil lnot let you go wrong.  Goodluck in the class and be positive.
She has high expectations on students and takes points off easily from smallest mistakes. But when it comes to her own work and powerpoint she seems less strict with herself.

Johanek uses her personal experience to enrich the class. I appreciated all of the feedback we were given on our projects, and she really helped me enjoy Marketing even more. She appreciates hard work and participation, and if you try your best, she will give you a good grade in the class. I highly recommend taking a class with her!
She uses lots of real world examples. If you read textbook and follow her study guide she gives, you can do well and learn a lot.

Prof Johanek has a great heart and a lot of knowledge about current industries. However, her lectures can be all over the place and a tad boring. I learned the most through the group project, rather than her lectures. That being said, she truly cares about her students and will go out of her way to help them with whatever they need.
Johanek's industry experience is vast! - very up-to-date on technology and analytics

Really enjoyed Prof J - Her lectures were very current and she tied concepts to actual applications which was great! Very fair -never took any sides but presented pros/cons to different types of thinking - learned a lot!

Johanek is an excellent instructor who brings alot of her own experience into the classroom. Very intelligent and current in her field - Engaging instructor - keeps you on your toes - Great sense of humor - Always there if you need her - Highly recommend her -
I love her experience in the real industry! Makes the class very interesting when you can see how the concepts are practiced. Classy person!

This is my 2nd class with Prof. J and I understand the grievances below. However, this year J has turned it all around. She listened to what we said, improved her weaknesses, and focused on her strengths: her real-life knowledge and caring personality. She doesn't have pop quizzes anymore but tests are hard. In short, Prof. J wants you to succeed.

Not a great lecturer, asks redundant questions and can't answer student questions. Tests on textbook material, not much from lecture. Takes off points very easily on homework and also participation in class.

WORST teacher I had by far!  Pop quizzes in class, sort answers, 2 -3 questions. Plus online quizzes each weeks, there are 2 presentations and a midterm. She thinks that we don't have life and no other things to do better but study for her class. btw, her classes are BORING she teaches  from the powerpoints, and the PPT she provided are worthless

The course was boring and useless. Her lecture was all about explaining obvious common sense. The hardest thing was syllabus. She assigned works in all kinds of different forms, which had at least 20 dues over semester. She was literally picky on grading works like paper, making such basic and easy course unnecessarily complicated and meaningless.
She seems just doesn't really care whether you fail or pass her class.

There is very little the professor says that is not covered by the book. As a lecturer, she says a lot of words which provide surprisingly little information. Depends a lot on step-by-step formulas. That said, she is very nice, does her best to be helpful, and in the end, actually does know what she's doing through her industry experience.

Deceptively unintelligent. Very nice and good public speaker, but doing well requires learning the language of her incomprehensible questions, not an understanding of anything having to do with marketing. One of the more frustrating professors I have had.

Very enthusiastic & personable. Assignments are not extremely difficult, but they're tedious and all over the place - hard to keep track of grades as a result. Don't retain significant anything from class, which sometimes makes homework and reports difficult. Just memorize textbook vocab for exams.
Johanek is fun, easy, and overall a good marketing professor... come prepared, do the readings because she does have pop quizzes. the finals/midterms are usually recycled.
Pretty good, really enjoys and is knowledgeable about her topic.  Overall worth taking international with, there is a big group project that is pretty easy to do well on.

Easy class...mostly common sense but she talks and rambles a lot about her work and random companies during lectures. very repetitive, asks the class a fill in the blank question all the time(though no one dares to say a thing). Just read the slides and book and get an easy A.
The class is ridiculously easy, just cram for finals.  Unfortunately class is mandatory and you learn almost nothing.  Very boring.
wdr, what a guy.
N/A
Do exactly what he says, otherwise you are likely to receive 0.
Brother RUN AWAY!!
Professor Richard is awesome!

Do not take this class, I can't imagine a worse person teaching this class. William is more concerned with dating his brother's wife than he is actually teaching this course. The only positive thing I can say is that he's certainly not getting younger so, unless the second coming of Christ himself happens, he'll be down under where he belongs.

Only advantage is his enthusiasm. Grading method is absolutely ridiculous. He doesn't care about the steps and thought process for a question, only look at finial answer and give full or no credit. Student who made a small error in steps receives zero, but a student who did all steps wrong and mysteriously get correct result receives full credit.

This man is the worst professor I have ever had (I'm a senior). He is highly unreasonable, rude, and unpleasant. He has unrealistic expectations and lies to students in class regarding the exam content.

Very unreasonable man. Loves to torment students and has zero awareness that students have other commitments and things to do in their lives because he expects 10 hours a week dedicated to the class. This is an introductory class and he wants you to be perfect on the material despite half the class not needing this information EVER in their lives.

The course is great with excellent material. However, unless you can make everything perfect, you are likely to receive a zero on a question or assignment. Be prepared to learn solely from homework. The exams may make you doubt your life but weighted heavy on overall grade. The average grade is usually C or D. Don't expect getting a B easily.

Test average was a failing grade he joked about wanting to drop dead at the same age the avg score was (60s). Max score for the second exam was 100, min score was -100 could get minus full points on the whole test with no partial credit. Expects 10 hours per week of work for just 3 credits. Very niche subject not applicable to most jobs. Avoid!!!!!
Funny guy and the lecture material is very interesting and important in my opinion. However, he is a very harsh grader, don't make mistakes.
Degrading, would have been fired if he didn't have tenure. If you've seen the movie the incredibles and u remember mr. incredible's boss, this man is that man.

This man is the worst human being on the planet. I would like to create a time machine and talk his mother out of having a child to save her from giving birth to the hell-spawn that is William Richard. She'd thank me because I would save her from a lifetime of having an absolute abomination and disappointment of a son. The world would rejoice.

Atrocious professor. He constantly mocks students for asking questions. His ego is through the roof and he is never wrong. He loves to intimidate his students, very unapproachable, talks to you like you're a first grader, and is not understanding. If you even suggest he do something different, he will probably threaten to talk to the dean about it.
Worst Professor ever! I have never thought about encountering a professor that bad. He nearly knows nothing about modern technologies.

I was curious how a college professor can be that BAD as described in the comments here until I have been in his class... He literally mocks students' clarifying questions during lectures and goes over important concepts in 2 seconds. His responses to my questions always ruin my day... AVOID AT ALL COSTS, PLEASE. Why is the lowest score 1, not 0?
Repeat the same thing in class as the homework video he gives.

The worst professor in CSE department ever. Totally disorganized lecture. Sometimes answering a phone call from his daughter in class. Talk about an important concept for 5 seconds and then move on. The exam given is hilarious, like a question about what we talked about in class. Avoid this prefessor at all cost.

Utterly useless lectures which 50% "umms, ahh" and 50% going through a complex concept in 3 seconds, expecting everyone to get it. Excuses his terrible teaching by posting a Einstein quote about only being able to "organize" information. No partial credit on tests. He is also directly vindictive/rude to students and very passive aggressive.
Rants during lectures. Moves too quickly. Assigns additional lectures to watch before exams since he doesn't plan well enough to get through the necessary material before an exam.

Lectures are relatively enjoyable, but they can also be found online from when it was still online class. Professor Strait is a very knowledgeable and qualified lecturer, and can make lectures funny and interesting. Graded mostly by just 3 tests. The final unit is the most difficult and is weighted the most, but little time is spent on it.

Strait is a true gem. He's a sweetheart and clearly loves his job. Always comes into class with a smile on his face ready to blast his favorite delta blues songs at deafening volumes. Lectures are straightforward and he does post the slides on canvas which is helpful.

Dr Strait is incredible; his class was by far my favourite this year and potentially of my time at WashU. His lectures were engaging and his ability to work humour and sincerity into his lectures made coming to lecture the highlight of my day. The only downside was that we were graded mainly on three exams, two of which were difficult to study for.
He's aight
I absolutely love Dr. Strait. He is really funny and interesting. The class is fascinating.

Dr. Strait is a great lecturer who clearly cares about his students. He tries to keep his lectures entertaining by infusing them with humor and is very inspiring to listen to. He knows exactly what to say and when to say it to make a big impact on his students; his lectures on climate change were very moving. Take a class with him if you can.

An absolute gem!! One of the best professors at WashU. He plays music at the beginning of every class and tells stories and cracks jokes throughout his lectures to keep people engaged. Professor Strait is a really entertaining and sweet professor who cares about his students and the topics he teaches a lot.
its literally impossible to hate this man

I thought Anatomy was quite difficult this semester, as exams were moved back in person and closed note (exams are 90% of your grade). Prof. Strait is an ANGEL though, as he made our final online, open-note, and unlimited time at the last minute (literally in the exam room before the final). He's a great prof, just be prepared to memorize a TON.

Dr. Strait is an incredibly thoughtful, engaging professor whose love for what he teaches is contagious. Human ev is SO interesting (made me want to switch majors). He says it's not memorization heavy but sometimes you just have to sit down and grind flashcards. It becomes a lot easier if you find trends and understand the logic behind them.
Very funny and engaging lectures. Only had 3 labs and 3 tests that made up the grade. If you memorize the lectures, the tests are easy (also didn't need the textbook but he said to get it).

Human evolution was the only biology/anthropology class I've taken at WashU. Even in my senior year, Dr. Strait had me interested in the material and the class. He is a phenomenal person, accomplished but humble and kind. He is an excellent, patient professor, and goes out of his way to try and make class interesting and fun. 10/10

Great professor and a super loveable guy that makes anthropology very interesting with his use of videos but he can be a bit fast when explaining things and powerpoints are more so visual than summarizers of content. Grades in this class consist of 3 chunkey tests and 3 labs, if you suck at tests (me) make sure you study and take really good notes.

Professor Strait's lectures on Human Evolution are entertaining. He is occasionally humorous and tells personal stories. He conveys passion for the material.

There is no homework in the class; there are a few multiple choice exams.

I love Dr.Strait, he is a fantastic professor and he clearly is very passionate about both teaching and the subject he teaches. One of my favorite profs at WashU and I certainly won't forget him and my experience in his class for a long time.
was unclear on directions to print skeleton diagram. had to manually draw the diagram.

Dr. Strait is the best professor I've had at WashU so far! Not only are his lectures interesting and funny, but he truly cares about his students and it shows. He is an incredible man and I am so sad that I'm done taking HumEv. If you show up to class and take good notes, you're golden.

One of the most engaging lectures I've ever had! I also loved how he plays music before every lecture to help set the mood, and also just for the sake of it. He genuinely wants his students to succeed and even said at the beginning of the semester that he doesn't care if all of his students get A's.

Dr. Strait is a funny professor who gives solid lectures and is generally accommodating. While Covid is still a problem, this class is a guaranteed A. Open note, take-home exams are only grades, besides extra credit.
Fantastic professor; ask questions of him. Very big on 1-1 student access and accommodating

There is a lot of material covered in the Pre-Modern class and two cumulative exams on all of it. Since the classes were lecture-heavy, it was insanely difficult to prepare and still graded harshly. I couldn't engage much with the material bc classes would get boring, and Parsons could be condescending and disorganized. Overall, I was unimpressed.

The only reason I wouldn't take his class again is because I hate reading. Overall, he's a really good professor and will answer all of your questions! If you're interested in African history you should definitely look into taking one of his classes.
Definitely reading and lecture heavy, but he has a real talent for making lectures interesting. Learned a lot and would definitely recommend him to others.

Great guy and knowledgeable Professor in his field. Lectures can be very boring in his upper level classes and it is a ton of reading. Very difficult paper assignments in this particular course. He cares a lot about students and is very friendly. Easy to get a B, but the course involves a lot of work.
Parsons is the greatest man to walk the face of this planet. Take as many classes as you can

Good professor; a lot of people take the class pass-fail, so often people don't pay attention. The tests and assignments are relatively easy, but he forces one topic onto you for the final paper which can make it extremely boring and unfun to do. He's a big proponent of gender equality and stuff, so he'll make you focus on it for the final project.

Parsons can be a little condescending, but he knew the subject well and always had interesting angles and topics. Never opened the textbook, did the readings but not necessary. Tests were easy memorization since the info was all online, the essays required a bit more work. Overall, engaging class and a little work but not too bad.

Knowledgeable, funny, sarcastic. Lead the whole class to engage and find the answer instead of just giving them to the students by plain lecture. Really smart guy, you can't fool him. But very nice if you make an effort to show you care. Before I knew nothing about African history but I am taking another class with him next semester.

Took his African History 1800-present seminar. He seriously knows his stuff. Makes class very engaging, pushes you to think harder, doesn't let you get away with lazy responses. Grading expectations are very reasonable. You will learn a lot from him.

He can definitely get moody. He definitely knows his stuff, but sometimes we learn random things that don't seem really important at all. Tests aren't too bad, but the memorization of people and events can get very tedious.

Know that if you take Intro to World History, you will study WW2 and only WW2. The impossible amount of readings is because they're totally unnecessary. It seems intimidating and he talks fast but the tests are really easy. He's a good teacher once you realize you don't need to know details. Make sure you show up to class and DON'T fall asleep.
Very good teaching, keeps my attention throughout class.  As others have mentioned he can be a little moody and is often very opinionated.

Dr. Parsons knows African history well, and he gives great lectures. However, he can be moody and rude--for example, he frequently cuts people off and will shut you down if you say something that he does not agree with. On the plus side, he does grade pretty easily, and he gives you everything you need for the exams beforehand.

best professor i have taken here. Not including his intro to world history, he almost never lectures, instead creating discussion among students. decent amount of reading, but usually very enjoyable. very helpful and approachable. Renowned among African history professors. I've taken one of his courses every semester in which one was offered.
A really hard class with a lot of reading but it is so worth the skill sets you develop, even more so than the understanding you gain of the issues presented.
Lots of history, but a great lecturer. He'll put you on the spot if you haven't done the reading. The group projects are hard but worthwhile.

Excellent history professor. Highly recommended. I did fine w/o doing all the readings and just comig to class. He is very good at this class and made me want to take basically anything else he might decide to teach.

A great lecturer (if you don't mind history).  The reading is ridiculously heavy, though.  I took him pass/fail, skimmed the readings, attended the fairly interesting lectures and had no problem.  In fact, I would have no problem taking him again.

great professor, interesting material, but has a group project that is absolutely deadly.  Make sure you go talk to him if you have any problems with your group or you can get screwed by the group grade.
