No deadlines on papers, which sounds good but hurts later on. Very knowledgable about all material, but does not clearly explain what should be done on papers. Only read first 2 pages of my drafts.
Great professor - knows his stuff!
Incredibly easygoing, concerned about students, always willing to answer questions. Hands down one of the best professors at WU.

I thought he was very cool. he was interested in the subject, very knowledgable and would surprise me with the ability to field any question asked no matter how detailed or obscure. If you pay attention, you will do well.
Long lectures get boring, but he really loves the subject and you can tell.  Go see him before you turn in a paper and he'll like you, then you're all set.
Very rude to me.  Seemed unfairly biased against me, although I could not figure out why.  If you are on his good side, that is great, but I hated this class.
Here's a teacher you can tell really enjoys the subject he's teaching.  Always willing to answer questions even if it gets the class off track.
Excellent prof.  Very knowledgeable and helpful and approachable; good guy to talk with about a lot of things, really.  Highly recommended.
kind of bizarre but very nice; his 1.5-hour lectures can get boring
No Comments
the nicest professor I've ever had
very knowledgeable, good lecturer, very good with student interaction

I was in Dr. Green's lab for multiple semesters. He's an extremely interesting man--and hilarious. Assigns some readings but can be skimmed. He's been at WashU for 40 years and still has the computers he ran experiments on in the 80s. Very supportive of students engaging in cutting-edge research and will approve & fund studies that interest you.
Professor Green is an amazing professor. His lectures are really interesting and you can tell he is passionate about what he does. Definitely do the readings in this class! Exams are entirely written

Dr. Green is an amazing professor. 100% recommend to take his class to fulfill this requirement. Exams are entirely reading and lecture based, so you will not want to miss class but it is not mandatory. If you study hard and learn the studies, you will do just fine as he clearly outlines what you need to know.
Decent guy and decent professor, but could have been better.

Prof Green is a great lecturer and I really enjoyed going to class. However, psych of learning is very different from most other lecture-style psych courses. Be prepared to read... you can't do well on exams without doing the readings. Also, the exams are entirely essay based. Great professor and you will learn a lot, but definitely not an easy A.
His classes are extremely difficult. Take the class with another professor.

Probably the best lecturer I've had. He manages to make his lectures both full of content that he explains well and entertaining. He's hilarious, and I actually looked forward to class and learned tons. It's true that this is a harder class than most, and the exams are entirely essay based. But success is still possible if you're willing to work.
Dr. Green puts up an intimidating front but he's a total softie and he cares about every one of his students. Probably the best lectures I've ever seen.

Professor Green may come off as harsh or intimidating, but he is such a caring and understanding person. He just wants his students to do well and to make learning interesting and fun. His class was fabulous, and I really only took it to get rid of my NS cluster. It is a bit of a challenge but as others have said, completely worth it.
A very genuine and caring guy. Tries to keep material interesting and gives lots of examples. Sometimes doesn't allow for discussion, though.

Dr. Green is awesome. He's always willing to talk to you/help you with anything.  Holds office hours, but I was also able to just drop by his office before exams and such to ask questions.  By far the best class I have taken at WashU.  Be ready to work hard if you want a good grade -- but it's worth it.  Definitely recommended.

Probably the only professor worth going to class at 8:30 for, Dr. Green is hilarious. The material is a challenge, and the workload is heavy, but it's really interesting and unlike any other psychology course.
This class kicked ass. Not an easy A by any means, but you'll definately learn something and Prof. Green is very funny and interesting. Highly recommended.
No Comments
No Comments
Really enthusiastic and energetic, clear lecture, stays after class to answer questions.  Quirky humor.

He assigns a lot of work and it's challenging and mostly massive scale with charcoal. You end up creating a lot of ugly work, but it's fun, and he's helpful and fun. I loved my TA (Meghan). He seems to lighten up towards the end of the semester, so it's fun and I learned a lot.

all the critique of jamie is warranted- he assigns a LOT of work, and definitely likes to hear himself talk. that being said, i liked him as a professor, and learned a lot in his class. assignments were interesting, though fast-paced. has some great technical feedback, but don't be afraid to push back against things that compromise what you want.
Tons of outside work, but you'll learn a lot, and he's a very genuine person

Jamie assigns a lot of work but wants you to succeed. He is very understanding of people having different interests within the art world. Though some of his assignments are annoying and maybe not what students are interested in, he makes it up by having the final project be very open ended, allowing students to be creative.

Jamie's assignments were ridiculously hard. His assignments were bizarre and forced us to create work that reflected poor taste. I hated his class- it made me question my artistic ability and interest in art in general.

Remarkably pompous, unrealistic workloads and expectations, never TAUGHT us anything, wasted time constantly in crits by talking about himself, and didn't bother to learn everyone's names (there were what? fourteen of us?).  In general, I hated every moment of being Jamie's student (if you could even call me that considering he taught me nothing.)
By far the best core drawing teacher. Encouraging grader and very nice man overall. Yes, workload is high, but completely worth it.
jamie's class is hard but i liked him. he's so enthusiastic and loves art and all the sketchbook assignments pay off. if you don't want to put effort into core drawing don't take his class.
Wonderful teacher- he seems very demanding, but in the end it pays off.

Adams is overrated in my opinion.  Slightly pompous.  A bit too interested in chatting and wasting time.  Advice is sometimes a bit vague and not practical.  Nice guy, but irritated me.  Wouldn't take another class of his.

Jaime has decent advice in crits, however he delays a lot of class time talking about himself. Its hard to get past his ego and at times he can come across as **** (though I don't think this is his intent) I didn't learn as much as I had hoped and I thought the final project was ridiculous.
Demands a lot of you; be prepared to work very hard outside of class. At times has a skewed view of how much a student can actually do.
Tough professor, but very helpful.  You'll improve a lot.  Lots of outside assignments (much more homework than other drawing classes).
No Comments
No Comments

Jamie is an amazing teacher. I made incredible improvements during the semester. He assigns a LOT of work, though and holds very high standards. Compared to the rest of the drawing teachers, it seems his students make the most progress.

Maybe one of the worst professors Ive had. The longest, most difficult and ridiculous homework assignments assigned weekly, a terrible textbook with typos, a brutal grader, and an overall confusing instructor. Always made concepts seem more confusing than they were, and hes not understand of student needs. Would not recommend personally.

Lectures were confusing, and content was extremely difficult. He seems to prefer putting students in their place over helping them learn. He is not really willing to help students outside of class, if you go to office hours you'll just be told to figure it out yourself. CHOOSE ANOTHER PROF IF YOU CAN.

Just whatever you do dont take a class that he teaches. He doesnt respect that students are not as knowledgeable as him and doesnt know how to explain concepts in a way that doesnt go right over students heads. Office hours are not that useful, spent about 20 hours per week on HOMEWORK. Dont do it to yourself.

Bongers was a fantastic professor and easily the best math professor I have had at WashU. He is great for people who actually want to understand math at a conceptual level in addition to learning computations. His class was difficult but if you went to class (his lectures are really helpful and well explained) and pay attention, you can do well.

Bongers gives really long homework assignments. His lectures are fine. His office hours are ok, depends on the day. Some days he will help; other days he wants you to figure it out yourself. Take home exams are very difficult. Expect to spend a lot of time on his class.

Decent professor. A little awkward at times but is clearly knowledgable about the topic and does a decent job explaining considering how complex some of the topics were. He is patient with students and tests are not easy but still fair.

Don't go to lecture, just watch professor Leonard on Youtube. It honestly really makes me sad to say this, Wash U is an amazing school but this was the least clear, most frustrating course I've taken here. Professor Bongers is a brilliant mathematician, but sadly, not suited to be a teacher.

calc 3 has become much harder at Wash U especially the exams - if you have the choice to take calc 2 instead, do so; bongers is sweet but goes too fast, exams are hard, sub section is a joke; if you take this course, watch professor leonard videos and don't go to class.

Dr. Bongers, in my opinion, does not explain complex math concepts with a great deal of clarity. Definitely more focused on pushing a lot of material than actually having you understand. I, trying to be as constructive as possible, recommend not taking his class because it is extremely difficult and not taught in a way conduce to understanding.
Not a good professor. Didn't even give a drop for discussion section.

Hands down the best math professor I've had! His lectures are very concise, clear, and informative, combining helpful dosages of big-picture explanations and specific examples. Add on his light humor, genuine care for students, and accessibility outside of class, and he's the perfect math teacher. Kinda wish I could take more math classes now...

Great instructor who really knows his stuff and is able to effectively convey and teach mathematics. He really took student feedback into consideration, to the point where we were able to choose the specific topics he would cover during review sessions before exams. I recommend taking a class with him.
The best math professor I have had at WashU. Great dude, great lectures, and easy to learn from. 10/10 would recommend.
Take his class and you will fall in love with maths

Amazing professor! Does such a good job of really explaining material clearly and thoroughly in class both with general and specific examples. Everything on exams has been covered in depth in class and homework problems prepare you for difficulty and grading. Incredibly helpful at office hours for homework or just a chat. Would highly recommend!

This man explains differential equations in a way that makes it make sense. He is clear, concise, provides a lot of example problems, and has a sense of humor. Honestly, he's everything I'd hope for in a math professor. Take his class if you can - math won't be a struggle if you do.

Palmer's class was a really good experience in my opinion. Not too difficult and overall reasonable exams. The one thing to note is that the take-homes can take a long time, but they are not too hard to succeed on. If you pay attention in class and ask questions, you will get a good grade in the class.

Lectures were kind of boring and the take-home assignments were all pretty hard, but Palmer was open to answering questions and I thought the TAs were nice. If you read the textbook and understand quiz questions, you'll likely do well in the course.

Solid professor with lots of accounting experience. Grading is fair, and if you are willing to put the work in, you can get at least an A- in my opinion. Exams aren't difficult but again, you need to study and take enough time to complete the take-home portion. He is understanding a nice guy to talk to also, I think other reviews are too harsh.

Great professor and great guy. Lectures are slightly monotone but this is accounting, what do you expect? Always accessible out of class and responds to emails. Clearly wants his students to succeed. Tests are easier than the review that he gives. Grading depends on the TA you get. Projects are tough and one TA may give you an A while the other a B
Honestly just a really nice guy and fair grader. Accounting is inherently a mundane subject but his classes are definitely more interesting than I expected. Great Professor!
Great Teacher, super caring but the course material is boring and requires a good amount of reading. If you are ready to put in the effort you should be able to get an A

I honestly learned more by looking at the slides from the textbook then from his lecture. The grading system is weird and makes it hard to tell where you are in the class. Also, assignments are graded very harshly so be careful for flow through errors. He is a fine teacher but I am not a big fan of the way the class is tought.

The course is heavily reliant on the textbook, and his teaching is okay, but a little too problem-focused in my opinion. He also gives two-part test. One part is a written take-home test you work on with a group, while the other part is a multiple-choice in-class test. Both are not too difficult, but again, heavily textbook-reliant.
Nice guy but not a good professor. Lack of clarity leads to self-teaching through textbook

Accounting with prof. Palmer has been a good experience overall. Class is definitely a lot more rigorous than other sections, but it is very manageable. He is charismatic and passionate about the subject, and although he often goes on tangents, I always felt that I had a handle on the material, even when I did not do the lengthy textbook readings.

He uses the textbook to teach and spends class trying to pick complicated problems because he expects you to have a basic foundation for accounting already. In reality, he is supposed to break down the information to a practical level so students can learn from him.
I personally really liked Prof Palmer, if you do the chapter readings before class and make an effort the class if definitely doable!

Supposed to be an introductory Accounting class but waste of the hardest class I have taken at WashU. He is a terrible lecturer and he makes everything so boring and hard to understand. Also gives an insane amount of hard quizzes and take home test and finals. The amount of stress this class caused me is beyond measurable. Don't take !!!
Enjoyed the class. He liked to give advice in real world accounting which I enjoyed. Learned most of the real content in the homeworks online. Only a few paid attention in class.

Palmer's lectures don't help me learn. Last class, he passed out a packet of problems and didn't even go over a single one, but instead lectured about accounting-related stories and was unclear about the points he was making. Class is mandatory and his grading criteria seem clear on paper, but there have been many regrade requests, etc so far.

Palmer doesn't teach during his lectures. Rather, he either does unnecessary practice problems or tells stories about his life. Because of this, students are forced to self-teach from the textbook. Homework takes multiple hours per week, there are bi-weekly quizzes, and exams are incredibly long. Take another professor for this class if you can.

Constance's studio was the most inspiring studio I have taken at WashU. I left the studio with a comprehensive project I was proud of and a newfound interest in what I was investigating. I credit her commitment and encouragement; Constance is always available for honest and constructive feedback. It is evident she wants to see her students succeed!

Constance is a very dedicated professor. She will make sure a lot of work is produced but you will have an amazing portfolio project by the end. She pays a lot of attention to details, is very interested in film as a medium and the way it relates to architecture, and is very innovative.

Constance is perceptive to each student's interests and often roots her feedback to greater design discourses. While she may ask for more than "average", her student's work tends to be highly articulate, thorough, and impassioned. IMO, Constance is a rare researcher and educator open to mentoring students interested in exploring Ideas and growing.

Constance is one of my favorite professors at school, renowned for her innovative ideas and ability to offer each student personalized critiques and directions for their projects. She is incredibly responsible and goes out of her way to support her students. In one of her courses, she even recorded all the readings aloud, purely for our convenience

Constance is my favorite professor during school. Everyone is lucky to have her. She is so inspiring and encouraging and always brings joy and energy to the class. Her passion for teaching and architecture is priceless. I miss her class every day, it's not easy but memorable. She helped me grow so much and I will always be grateful for it.

Constance is awesome. The studio was balanced in terms of theory, design, and fabrication, and the lectures were great learning experiences that is uncommon for studio courses.

Workload is heavy, but you'll grow a lot in the end. Follow through and great projects will come out of this studio!

Constance is a truly remarkable professor. She always gives engaging lectures and thoughtful desk critiques. She made a significant impact on my learning experiences with architecture. She's always very on point with her criticism and shows love to her students. You can't help getting attracted by her charisma!

Constance was a very dedicated instructor, very inspirational and encouraging. She is also very smart and quick to understand how students think and tries her best to help with students' projects. I would take her studio again in a heartbeat.
N/A

Constance is very knowledgeable and inspirational. She gives great precedent references that facilitate the design process. Her studio is indeed work-intensive, but I got so much out of it and the project really enhanced my portfolio. Come to her studio prepared! You will always get more and better feedback when you have materials to show.

In my experience with Constance as a professor, she was always on time and ready to make progress during class time. She is often available and follows through with what she says she will do. If you do take a class with her be ready to produce superior architecture work!
Too much work
Frustrating

Being in Constance's studio was difficult because it felt like both the students and the other professors were often in the dark as to what we were doing and why we were doing it. It was also very theoretical and focused on abstract representation, which was frustrating as someone more technically inclined.
Constance is great.

Constance is one of the best professors in WashU undergrad architecture. She thinks beyond the traditional limit of plans and sections. She can constantly refers to canonical works in canonical ways, thus providing useful and insightful ideas.

Dr. Hu is an amazing professor who really cares about her students. She makes everyone feel welcomed and greatly improved my writing. She often extends deadlines and makes expectations clear. I went in knowing I was not the best writer, however she built my confidence and made college writing a good experience even when  I dreaded taking the class.

Rita is kind to her students and flexible with extending paper deadlines. But, if you don't write the way she wants or argue something she agrees with, she will grade you down. She also rarely gives full credit for assignments. If you don't already know how to write, don't take her class because I think it's harder than real English major courses.

Not a very harsh grader in my opinion. Class is pretty boring--half the class is usually spent talking about deadlines, the other half either talking about a reading no one did or a lesson she borrowed from someone else. Boring is worth it for a (relatively) easy A though. Sometimes deadlines are a little short, but she's flexible with them.

She is a great professor who is good at inspiring students when they are hesitated about their essays, and always willing to answer our questions after class, though sometimes giving scores and feedback a bit late.

Professor Hu is very kind and cares about her students. She will give an extension if needed and I always enjoyed the class discussions. My one criticism would be that she didn't grade or provide feedback to a major essays till after we finished the class.

Professor Hu is literally the worst professor ever. She is ALWAYS unprepared. she does not give clear instructions and on top of that reuses essay prompts from pasts essays and so nothing makes sense. she sets insensible due dates. overall horrible experience, do not take any course with her.

Quite literally the worst professor I've ever had. Blatantly unprofessional and unqualified, yet she works in the law school? Showed up to class 5-12 minutes late everyday, always unprepared. Only offered 2 office hour morning sessions during other classtimes. Didn't provide any useful or clear feedback, "???"s were common. Constantly made excuses.

This professor, Rita Hu, is genuinely HORRENDOUS. I wouldn't wish taking this professor upon my worst enemy. The essay instructions are incredibly vague which allows her to take off points for absolute bogus reasons when grading. Avoid this unqualified professor at all costs.

Very harsh on content of paper, extremely liberal in viewpoint and grades clearly based on that. the identity part of the course only means she talks about race and gender almost exclusively, and almost never mentions religion or age or any other part of a persons identity.

I thought WashU's stupid college writing program was meant to help you *become* a good writer. Guess not in this class, though. She basically gives hardly intelligible, vague, and complex assignments, doesn't explain or teach us anything, and then gives everyone a B. You are expected to be a perfect writer on day 1. Avoid her at all costs, please.

She is an extremely hard grader, you will never receive 100 on any assignment. She is extremely condescending when giving feedback and honestly does not help much. Her office hours fill up fast so getting help outside of the class is tough. This class lowered my confidence in writing and I would not take another class with her.

She gives us these meta and difficult prompts but does not explain or help in any regard, and then on top of that grades really harshly. The class discussions are her talking for an hour and her being super unclear about what a good thesis is. Dont take her college writing class.

Her grading is harsh even though she never explains the prompt clearly. Never really teaches how to write but expects us to be writing experts at an entry, basic mandatory college writing class. The average is low, and without a curve, this will be one of the harshest, toughest college writing classes on the entire CWP roster. DO NOT TAKE HER.

This class is exhaustive. Definitely one of the most difficult college writing classes. Skip her if you have her for CWP because I am drowning in constant essays (smth that other classes don't do) long readings. It's really difficult pushing out long essays weekly without much feedback and clear directions. Interesting reads but no time to enjoy.

I'm only halfway through the semester with this horrible professor but I want to scream everytime I'm in class. She sets us up to fail--never explains the assignments clearly and then complains that all of us are terrible writers and gives all Bs. Literally makes us hate on other students' assignments, which is extremely awkward. Don't take her!!!

The class average is made up of three grades, two of which are tests but he's an easy grader. He explains everything clearly. He could give more direction for the research paper, but he's accessible outside of class for questions. He understands that we're busy students and tries to accommodate varied schedules. Great teacher, and really nice!

The only two assignments are two research-heavy papers worth 50% each. He has very vague grading criteria, and his lectures often have little to do with each other and can be quite dry. Despite this, Kim is a very nice guy and is always wanting students to come to him for help outside of class. Going the extra mile on the papers will be worth it.
SP2021 BEWARE: Harder than before, semi open-ended exams, graded harshly. Do expect to learn a lot, DO NOT EXPECT FOR GOOD GRADES!!
Very smart guy but ambiguous. Exams are meh, research project is not explained clearly, but I recommend starting early (beginning of semester) and finding a group to work with.

Graded on only 2 exams and a research project. First exam was straightforward and easy; the second was viciously hard. He's very ambiguous about everything, from grading procedure to how the research project is done. Very smart, and his lectures are quite interesting, but he often loses his place and the lecture ends up trailing off. Odd professor.

You're graded on two tests and a research project. The tests are free response and based on his lectures, which may seem a bit non sequitur at times, but as long as you go over the slides and can weave together an explanation and justify it, even it it's contrary to what was discussed, he'll give you credit. Project is leniently graded. As and Bs.
Very tough class. The math isn't too bad, but his explanations are terse and often unhelpful. The exams are abusively difficult. Would not recommend.

He is not clear about the requirement. No sample test posted before midterm. He just read out (not loud) but really fast two questions of past paper. What is the point of doing this? Can not learn much from his class. Can not find any logic of his lecture. American economic history is really an interesting topic. But he teaches it in a terrible way

His class was terrible for me. I regularly attended and was very involved in the homework. His "review" sessions are lies: nothing reviewed or that he says you need to know is on the exam, instead everything else not covered or mentioned once is. Expects you to "intuition" out mathematical equations. STAY AWAY

He is a really nice guy who really cares about the students who attend class, but the class is not easy it is very quantitative, and the test are very hard, I would say about 25% of the people ended up dropping the class, but if you survive him your micro understanding will greatly be improved.

A good guy and a pretty good professor. Lectures are organized and is very systematic in his approach, but there is so much information to cover that he often glosses over things. Still, I never usually had trouble following him in class. Homeworks were somewhat tedious and the rest of the grade was based on only 2 very hard exams.

Soks' 401 is the most demoralizing thing to happen to me as an econ major. His teaching method is to put up an equaion and the solution, while ignroing how to solve the equation mathematically in addition to completely glossing over the economic concepts. This class did nothing for my economic understanding and he is a terrible professor. Stay away
no strong feelings about him.  tough class, average teacher.  wasn't impossible to follow, but didn't make it interesting either.
kim is a cool guy, pretty solid teacher but the tests were really hard. got a B and put medium effort into it.
The class was interesting, but the grade was based only on two finals and one large research paper.  Grade received was equal to the effort put into the class.

Was disappointed because the subject seemed very interesting, but you miss out on going in depth on any perspective because you cover so many. Also, many of her attempts at jokes fell short and felt condescending, and she was more focused on emphasizing her own point rather than listening to what students were saying.

Quite literally the worst class I have ever taken at this school. At no point will you have any idea what grades are gonna be. She just stands at the front of the room and talks for an hour and a half nonstop. Knows her stuff, but makes you wish you went to Fontbonne instead. Took this class purely to fill a graduation req and really wish I hadn't.
Disrespectful to students and very cold
Not the most exciting lecturer but a nice person, funny, and gives SUPER easy tests that are very straightforward. Very knowledgable in the information she talks about.
Smart and ok lecturer, but arrogant and inflexible.
Great teacher, but her people skills do need work. Not the friendliest person.

While she is a very interesting and competent professor, she has some of the worst people skills and is impossible to contact and get feedback from. I have been attempting to meet with her for the past 5 about our year end paper which is due in 4 days. I have yet to receive an email from her.
Super smart. Really helpful if you go to her. Makes everything interesting. Always comes prepared.

Good sense of humor. She really likes ancient Mesopotamia and Egypt, so she threw it into the course. Assigns readings that are way too long, but luckily you don't have to do all of it. I think she secretly knows you won't. Assigns fun paper topics. Likes to do the robot walk while lecturing.

Brilliant, and an excellent teacher.  Will spend time outside of class and office hours to help students if necessary.  May seem a bit cold and dull at first, but it's just her academic demeanor - she's truly a great professor and person, and a wellspring of knowledge - if you take the time to pay attention and do the work!
She is very arrogant, her ideas are not representative of the religious Jewish community, and she will try and sway you into her cult.  Do not take her classes and do not fall for her devious ways!!!
Awesome!

clearly very intelligent, but class was not what it was advertised to be. She was not that friendly to me personally outside of class, but others say she is so i dont know. i would not recommend scriptures class though.
Likes to hear herself speak and it gets old quickly. Very knowledgeable but its hard to pay attention b/c the class is boring. I wouldn't take her again, but some ppl like her
good teacher. can be monotone. will go to any length to help her students. puts priority on learning, not on grading. easy grader if you put forth effort (and sometimes if you don't).

Her english sucks and lectures consist of her reading off the slides she already posts online, so attending lecture is pointless. Regular typos on the exams. Helpful outside of class and on Piazza though, and you can find all the homework and quiz answers online The class isn't that bad but you'll have to teach yourself all the material.
No idea what is she talking about.

There were significant typos on exams and the final, not the little kind of typos where you know what they were going for, but serious enough that a large portion of the final was figuring out what it even meant.
She's basically reading the slides, but her English is so poor that nobody understands what she's talking about.

Ruth Miller is the definition of a horrible professor. She clearly doesn't care about the class or her students. Her illogical, incomprehensible teaching style is almost hilariously ironic for a class on logic. The TA's for this class are also horribly unknowledgeable and inaccessible.

Miller is one of the worst professors I've had. Her lectures aren't organized, she doesn't explain topics well and she's not very helpful in answering questions. For a logic class, everything she does is in the least logical, efficient way possible, like giving us a seating assignment 5 minutes before the exam. If this class is required, good luck.

Terrible lecture and grading. The quiz and exam questions are not clear and confusing. And those questions are not really discussed in the class at all. She asked TA to do everything. She's not nice and impatient. Never want to take her class again.

She's a boring lecturer but is great at explaining things one on one. Fortunately, you don't need to attend the lectures. Everything you need to learn will be in her ppt slides and taught to you through the assignments.
Absolutely no need to go to lectures, because you'll end up trying to teach yourself everything from the book and online sources. Smart professor, awful, awful lecturer
Not a particularly interesting lecturer but a fine professor and 332 is a decent amount of work but not particularly hard and can all be found online. No need to attend lecture
Definitely not a good lecturer. Her class is extremely boring.
Extremely heavy work load and very unclear instruction. However, don't expect to learn much from your work. You'd be better off reading a book about database than taking her class.
You will regret it if you take her class.
She is really unclear and her lectures or slides are confusing.

The lecture slides are confusing to understand, and the lecture itself doesn't help either. The exams are not reasonable; things shown up on the exams are not explicit in the lecture slides. long homework. Bad news: you have to take it as a comp sci major.
Good, great, amazing--all good ways to describe Lemayian and her class. Beware, ACCT2610 with her is probably tougher than 2610 with other profs, but it's worth it.

Basically reiterating what previous comments say about Dr. Lemayian, she's extremely invested in her students, very accessible outside of class, and an overall great teacher. She's adapted quite well to the recent virus and transition to online learning as well. The class still requires a good amount of effort to get the grade you want.

Exceptionally caring, revision of topics helps a lot. She is pleasant and will give her time. She is very knowledgeable and speaks clearly. I always look forward to the class, BEST PROF EVER AND EAY TO RELATE TO!!!

Prof. Lemayian always comes to class ready to teach. She really cares about her students and wants to see them do well. Her exams are pretty tough and lengthy, so studying days in advance is a must. Her powerpoints are very thorough and helpful, but her board notes can be distracting/disorganized. Overall a great prof but get ready to work hard.

This professor is just so good and different. She inspires us to succeed. All material covered, she posts online which makes it easy to revise. She cares about our success and not only for the class but for life in general. She is a happy person, which makes It easy for anyone to approach, she is down to earth. Smart and extremely knowledgeable
An Amazing Prof. She explains the materials very clearly. There is so much positive energy around this Prof. I always look forward to her class. Best Prof.

Rushes through lectures, can hardly get through all of the content in time, often missing a bunch of slides at the very end. Tests on all content, even stuff that she might have missed in lecture. Tests are very very tough; expect to study both lectures and textbooks to understand it all.
Best there is and ever will be
Very helpful and wishes her students to excel, will go an extra mile, the lectures are clear and enjoyable. The best professor ever

Lemayian is friendly and funny and helpful, and presents the material quite clearly. The best professor I've ever had. So genuine and helpful. I wish all of my professors were like her.

Lemayian has a deep understanding of the material and gives crystal clear lectures. She is always willing to help her students and leads useful review session prior to tests. She also makes the effort to integrate current events into the material which is very helpful!
Goes an extra mile to assist
Amazing professor. She is so helpful and her lectures are very clear. The tests are difficult but she makes it soo worth it!
I enjoy the class, the prof is friendly and helpful

ONE OF THE BEST PROFESSORS AND CLASSES EVER!!!! Roots of Ferguson quite literally changed my world view and life. His lectures are captivating and I found myself constantly surprised class was over even though he has a technology ban and doesn't use slides very often. The exams are difficult, but if you go to class, you'll do great!
So amazing one of the greatest minds of our generation. engaging lecturer and I learned so much. he backs up his information with facts

LOVED this course. The lectures were engaging and I learned so much valuable information. Although the readings were heavy, my interest in the class made it easier. I would recommend this class to absolutely everyone. Rosenfeld is approachable, knowledgeable, and humorous when appropriate during class. Take lots of notes during lecture.

I took roots of ferguson first sem freshman year and I loved the class. Rosenfeld (Jake) is very knowledgeable and engaging, TAKE GOOD LECTURE NOTES but he will always repeat something if you ask.  I think everyone considering roots of ferguson should take it I enjoyed the class and learned a lot.

I learned the most maybe out of any class at WashU from this. This should be a mandatory class for the whole country, and we're lucky to have it here. Rosenfeld is hilarious and keeps it serious yet light. Rare and hard to do for this topic. Just get the gist of the readings and understand the concepts and you'll be fine for the tests!
Prof. Rosenfeld is the best lecturer I have ever had. He's incredibly engaging and really knowledgable. TAKE HIS CLASS

Getting Paid was my second class with Jake. This is the stuff he's an expert on and it was really interesting. Learned a lot and enjoyed the class. He doesn't use many slides, just lectures from notes which is kind of annoying, but he'll repeat things over and over so everyone can get it. Really nice guy and cares about students. Highly recommend.

It seemed like prof rosenfeld didn't care about this class outside of class. When he finished lectures and students still had questions he would tell students to ask the TA and then walk out. He also never was present when exams were being handed back. LECTURES NOT RECORDED, and he doesn't help when you need to fill in notes from lecture.

Roots of Ferguson is one of the most interesting classes I've ever taken. The material is so fascinating and you learn a lot. Overall, a great class and it really challenges your thinking.

Prof. Rosenfeld is really great and I really enjoyed The Roots of Ferguson. I got a lot out of the class and Prof. Rosenfeld is really interesting and knows his stuff. My only complaint was that he doesn't lecture with anything visual, just talks for an hour and a half straight which makes notetaking a challenge.

Jake is my favorite prof at WashU so far. He is lecture heavy but somehow they aren't long or boring. I have learned SO much from him and he is so knowledgeable. If you have the chance take his class!! also hes not hard on the eyes...

This class was incredibly interesting due to the fact that Rosenfeld is an incredible lecturer. It is easy to stay engaged during class (which I advise you attend). Since the class is bigger now, he relies on 3 exams for grades and they are pretty tricky and somewhat difficult to study for but all in all would take again.

He doesn't really do presentations so you need to take good notes.  He is really interested in what hes teaching and is very engaging.  However, he doesn't really give firm directions so its always a little scary to send things in.  Also yes he's not hard on the eyes

Professor Rosenfeld is both dynamic and extremely knowledgeable.  Tricky prompt for essays and only one test, but the readings are very manageable.  Encouraged a positive classroom environment and easily clarifies controversial topics.  i think most people in the class really appreciated the class and respected him.  also he's super hot

The only grades given in my class were two papers and a final exam. He gave some opportunities for extra credit. He is a very dynamic and enthusiastic lecturer and he really cares about his subject matter. Very willing to help. I enjoyed this class and I would take another class with him.
One of the best lecturers I've had the pleasure of listening to. Incredibly enthusiastic and engaging.

Readings, though helpful, are not necessary to survive the class conversations. Good discussions take place and he has good questions to spur them on. Not the most organized professor (very confusing syllabus), though he does grade papers in a timely manner.

Combines brilliant insight with an easy-going and anecdotal teaching style. He often forces students to ask good questions. He doesn't quiz on the specifics of what you read, (and you read a lot) but focuses on the central ideas and themes, which I like.  Also, his voice is impossible to fall asleep to.

Very nice, wise, and relaxed professor. There are a large amount of essays and the readings are long, but he grades very easily if you put in the effort. You will learn political thought very well. Participation in class is expected and can be very interesting. Great professor and a person to look up to.
Such a nice man. Easy grader and usually decent discussions, sometimes very boring though. He likes getting off topic and making jokes. Gets you thinking.
very nice guy and easy grader, but dullest class i've ever taken. bring coffee to class if you want to make it to the end.
Very funny, very nice man. Invokes a lot of class discussion - teaches you the basics, then lets you go. Fun course, relaxing, easy grade.
Dr. Clancy is the man!  Highly recommended if you have a chance to take a class with him.  And, he's also a pretty lenient grader of papers.

Clancy is a great guy in small class discussions, a little bit boring, but seems more interested in having students learn and absorb the material and think in new ways than in arbitrary, by-the-rubric grading. Highly recommended.
a great guy, funny, easy-going
very funny old guy. really makes you think and analyze stuff. easy to bs easy grader
Genius.
Hilarious. Great guy!

He's a great prof in T&T. A real renaissance man.  After taking his class, I felt like my thought process was elevated. But I would hesistate to recommend him for other classes, unless you're really interested in the subject.
Great, great, GREAT professor. Really makes you think about what you're saying and doing and why you're doing so. Strongly recommend.

This professor truly cares about giving meaningful feedback to her students about their writing, and is always making sure that they understand the course material and feel they are writing about something meaningful to them! The course material was also interesting, current, diverse, and helped facilitate a greater understanding of latin culture.

I took Profesora Anghel's class and it was my favorite Spanish class I've taken. She created a collaborative class environment and supported student participation. She had a deep understanding and passion for topics relating to gender equality and cultural studies and taught us how to properly convey our thoughts in essays. Very kind and helpful.

At first I did not like that she lets students speak english in class. I felt awkward when I would decide to try speaking spanish to practice but she slowly learned her teaching style (it was her first semester teaching) and became much more strict (in a motivating way). Loved the content and discussions, she makes participating easy.

Great professor! Class usually consists of small group discussions about whatever the homework material is. Homework is definitely mandatory and a little bit heavy but it's always things like watching a movie or reading a book. Would 100% take again. Prof. Anghel was amazing and learned a lot from this class!

Office hours with Anghel were always constructive. Discussion-based class format; she values analysis and understanding over rote learning of content. Detects specific weaknesses in class and consciously makes an effort to strengthen them. I suspect she would teach a class with less predefined of a schedule than 303 even better. Great overall.
test dont approve

The sweetest professor, always accommodating to students. She really takes the time and effort to help you out and make sure you're getting the most out of her class. Regardless of your comfort level with Spanish, if you have the opportunity to take a class with Anghel, I highly recommend it.

I took Spanish 102 with Professor Anghel. She is always available and willing to help students. I love that she put an emphasis on lots of interaction between classmates, as that really is the best way to learn and improve your Spanish.
Shirley was a great assistant professor in the class I took. She offered helpful feedback on homework and was always friendly.

Shirley was the TA for a major seminar I was in and she was always so kind and helpful! I had several great discussions with her when we were paired up to talk during class. Her comments and corrections on homework were thoughtful and helpful. When she led the class she encouraged participation but didn't pressure students.

Professor Anghel taught my first Spanish class at WashU and was helpful to me as a beginner, including time outside of class. She's very nice and teaches as well as anyone I've had in the Spanish department here. I definitely felt comfortable participating in her class, which is important in any language class.

Although Shirley was the TA for this class, she really helped in making it engaging and easy to follow as it was a new course at WashU. Shirley was great in giving feedback that allowed for improvement, and never shut down a student. Instead, she worked to help us understand our mistakes so we could correct them in the future.

The poor rating on here does not accurately reflect Anghel's class at all but of 202 in general. Mine was a 7 person class... of course you can't skip class! Anghel creates a fun learning environment without the pressure to feel like you should be at a level you are not. I felt comfortable speaking up because she corrects you in a supportive way.

1-Has lost my grade- twice 2-Required a meeting outside of class so I set up a meeting with her around lunch time. Horrible lecturer even though the class is lecture-based. He also makes typos on every HW and test, even the final! Very carless, both about his work and about the students. He does occasionally go on interesting tangents but those don't make up for how badly he teaches. Exams are most of your grade btw, so be ready for that. Would not take again.

Professor Ahn is a funny guy, as he would constantly share his passion for gambling, but he isn't really good at lecturing. Speeds through lectures, definitely knows his stuff but does NOT know how to teach. Practice tests before midterms were a breeze compared to the real thing. Doesn't care about the students. He loves to tell us that "grades don't matter" but also makes it hard to do well in his class. You really have to teach yourself bc lectures are SO unclear
One of the best professor's I've ever had, solely due to his lectures. They are so entertaining and awesome. Class isn't too much work and the tests are straight forward.

Interesting guy, talks about random things in lectures. Practice exams and answer keys FILLED with errors that really inhibit learning. Not a horrible class if you do all the practice, but plan on reaching out to TA's a lot to get clarification on incorrect solutions.
lectures are usually pretty interesting.

Professor, he's a caring dude, but the lectures he makes is not good. For his tests, do the previous practice test to ensure your success. Also go to office hours for the problem sets, it may or may not be helpful. During class he does make interesting points not related to the lectures.

Lectures are okay but he can ramble and the class loses focus/understanding. Material can be manageable but he makes exams a bit tougher and will admit this. Good lecturer

Professor Ahn is an absolutely outstanding person and teacher. He gives extensions as needed and knows that we are trying our best. He is definitely a top WashU professor.

Professor Ahn is a really amazing person. He truly cares about his students succeeding and was very accommodating when it came to helping students outside of class. He helped to make the difficult content as accessible as possible.

Professor Ahn is a friendly and approachable professor. He truly cares about his students. He understood that being in a once-in-a-century pandemic with little to no breaks could take a toll on students' mental health. Thus, he adjusted accordingly by being extremely accessible outside of class and giving students ample opportunities to succeed.

David is a fantastic person. Very funny and approachable. His lecture notes are usually really helpful. His practice tests are pretty accurate to the exam. Sometimes lectures are a little hard to follow. Really cares for his students. Highly recommend taking a class with David.

Don't pay attention to the positive scores. She is known to delete negative ratings of her on rate my professor. Her score should be much much lower. She yelled at me in class and was very unprofessional.

She should've never been allowed to teach or be around students. She has a temper and will yell at students. Dr. Kleutghen has high expectations for 100-level class. Hard class, but I learnt so much about East Asian arts. Most importantly, I learned about work ethics.
Very challenging class but thought-provoking, a must take at washu
Very challenging class, require a lot of work, but very thought-provoking
N/A

Professor Kleutghen is amazing! She gives good lectures and constructive feedback to every test / assignments, cares about students a lot. Lots of reading to do but the class was very fun. Participation is encouraged but not graded, good for introverts like me.

Has really high, strict standards. I can tell she puts in a lot of work into her lectures and course set up, but she then expects the same level of work ethic from students with homework, discussion, etc. If you're really interested in the topic, and learn best via speed note taking every lecture, you'll do great. Otherwise, you'll struggle.

Class is intense... Everything is graded and the TA's patrol the lecture hall to make sure you don't do anything against the rules of the class... not a very enjoyable experience.... Professor Kleutghen is very smart and genuinely cares about her students, but she is a tough cookie

Kleutghen is great. She's an expert in her field, and really passionate about helping her students. Most of the people who complain are ones who want this to be an easy class. Took this course for sheer interest, Professor Kleugthen is probably one of the best professors I have had at Wash U. This is the most micromanaged lecture class I have been in. She is knowledgeable on the subject but lecture is not engaging and she does has little compassion for her students.
Professor Kleutghen is an inspiration. She is amazingly put-together and eloquent, and she is passionate about what she teaches. She is tough but fair, and expects a lot from you.
Tough but fair. Gave helpful (if slightly acid) feedback on both writing style and the arguments in papers. Good storyteller, which is really helpful in a history class on a foreign culture.
Now over ten years out of my Japanese history class taught by Dr. Tsunoda, I look back at her as a dynamic lecturer and one of my greatest mentors.
Great teacher and advisor - approachable, honest, smart, tough, sincere, serious about her work and therefore yours

Unpleasant, unhelpful, not approachable

This course is simply amazing. reaqdings are great and not too long, assignements and exams fair. She's approachable especially if you don't try to just suck up and try instead to talk about serious issues in her field. Great prof. all around

Great professor. Very interesting and prepared, prrovides helpful comments on both papers and in class. She is a very brilliant thinker. To ddo bad in her class you gotta be stupid and not study. Excellent professor, though some may find her informal style off-putting. She knows her stuff.

Tsunoda is an extremely helpful and clear professor.  The only way to do poorly in her class is to blow it off (i.e.: write a crap paper the night before)

she said a paper "went to hell in a teacup" how helpful is that????

Kessler is amazing and hilarious. The class is very fun and I learned a lot in it. There is little class to class homework, but you should prepare for pop quizzes...
Kessler is great. He's quirky and keeps the class entertaining. He is also a nice guy and good teacher. This class was easily one of my favorites.

Great guy- Kessler is easily one of the best lecturers on campus.  He's clear and entertaining, but sometimes lets a few students take over the class with silly questions and comments.  Tests and quizzes were quite fair given you spend enough time reviewing the material leading up to the exam.
Often goes off topic and devotes entire class periods to taking questions and then just gives ambiguous answers
I learned so much! He knows what he's doing and cares about his students. Well prepared for class and he's always there to help.

Intro to Linguistics: Kessler is very knowledgeable, passionate, and funny in a sometimes weird way. I learned a lot and was very interested, despite the fact that he was often critical and gave negative feedback for student efforts.
very talented, very passionate, very nice

Professor Kessler is a nice guy. His classes make me laugh and the material is interesting. I really enjoyed this class, and this professor.  He's very quiet, but has a subtle sense of humor.  His lectures are very enjoyable to those who acutally listen to him.  He tends to direct his teaching at those few people who actually are paying attention.  Easy exams.
terrible teacher, makes an easy class entirely too hard
A nice guy but a terrible, disorganized, subtlely pompous professor.  I am never taking another linguistics class again.  If you take it Pass/Fail, you can pass with a D-, which is good.
He's a great guy and easy to follow!
Take Intro with another professor.  This guy is weird.  He's nice but is not a good TEACHER.

I took Repps in 2008 as one of my first courses toward my post-Associates Bachelor's degree. IA 5622 with Professor Hudson was an engaging and informative dialogue style course on the Arab Spring and Arab Winter. What I invested in learning about the region has been instrumental in developing my foreign relations outreach and policy formation.

Attendance is crucial. If your one of those who like to skip every other class or go as you please then you will fail his course. It's discussion based which means YOU NEED TO BE THERE! Quizzes are simplistic and papers are general and simplistic as well. He's leans towards the liberal side of things but does offer opposing argument.
Very serious about not being late to class. Very easy class with weekly quizzes as long as you show up and do the required papers and debates you can get an A.

I wish I'd read these reviews before enrolling in any of Hudson's classes. I would have known what to expect from him. Yes, there are some know-nothing students who appreciate the fact that he teaches absolutely nothing, but this outrageously unfair to the rest of us who pay good money to "learn" something from the IA program. Do avoid this creep.

I took Repps class against the advise of all the negative reports on this site & I'm so glad I did!! It turns out, lots of students enjoy his classes & go back to take more of his classes (including myself). His style of teaching is discussion-based rather than lectures. I found him to be quite helpful both in & outside of the course work...

How is that this ignoramus continues giving classes at Wash U? He is the most bias,mean and biggest bully I've ever met.  Why is that nobody complaint with the school about him, is beyond comprenhension. His knowledge and experience about the subjects he teaches is abismal. He's a nice guy. If he likes you, the class will be fun, but he is almost hurtful if he doesn't. Too strict on attendance and doesn't trust students. He can give opportunities to those he does like. Not a good addition to WashU. I wouldn't take him, just on the off chance he doesn't like you.

This was the absolute worst class I ever took at WashU.  I thought I would learn something about NGOs. What I got instead was a series of unstructured and discussions that were so disorganized, we barely referenced any of the numerous and expensive books he made us buy. For a guy who doesn't seem to know much about teaching, but still offers courses on topics he has no qualifications for, Repps has quite a racket going at UCollege. If you play along & pretend he's an awesome teacher you'll get an A; if you challenge his limited knowledge, your grade will suffer. Also, the "textbooks" this ignoramus uses are a joke

Avoid this professor at all cost. He likes to discriminate and is abusive. He grades very unfairly. He will not take into consideration other course work you have done. He should not be teaching. Does not prepare his classes. A disgrace for Wash U

This course was Israeli-Arab Conflict. This course was mediocre. He teaches history and not really current affairs or international relations. He is not updated I doubt I will take another course with him. He likes to bully smart students. I think he has a complex of inferiority. The quality of the course is really bad
I wouldn't take another course at all with him if you what I mean
Shouldn't be teaching. Almost never prepared. Very easily discriminated against women and anyone with a conflicting opinion.
Stiritz is a force of nature, never have I had a professor more dedicated to making a difference in her student's lives.
She's very kind and funny, but her guidelines are unclear and forever changing. If you voice an opinion in class that differs from hers, she will blatantly ignore you.

She's a nice woman, but her teaching style is based so much on opinion and her own personal view points that one doesn't feel like there is any organized learning that goes beyond a 1970 consciousness raising session. Her class is also very biased towards heterosexual relationships & white middle class youth.

The moral of the story is it's a good thing she does sexuality now and not texts. Her sexuality classes are incredible, the best you will ever take at Wash U, they will change your life. You will learn so much about the world and even more about yourself, if you can get in, do it.
This class was amazing. It teaches you to be a confident woman. It was a great class and I reccomend it to everyone.
LOVE LOVE LOVE her!!!!!!!!!!!!  She's just an awesome woman and her sexuality class is a must for all Wash U students
Boring. Class sucked.

What other teacher shows**** in her class? Only Stiritz. She is a crusader for women's rights--a little too extreme at times, but you have to take what she has with a grain of salt. Oh, and she has a FABULOUS home in Ladue to which she invites her classes. Tough grader--not an easy A, but worth it.

i am a ws major and i think stiritz is overrated in the dept.  she is very nice but her class is unstructured, her reading expectations are unrealisitic and i didn't learn anything significant.  not a hard grader tho, if thats what u care about.
A lot of work... but you'll enjoy her class and her hilarious (sometimes random) sexual comments
She is awesome
So great! Love her!!
No Comments
She is the cutest!!  So nice and willing to help out with anything!!  Very attentive to students.
Good professor, the course is tough though
Great professor. Clear lectures and material that has a straightforward style and occasional dry humor. Everyone dreads mec290, but I felt like Gray is the exception.

Great professor! I was really scared of Game Theory because it is a hard course, but he taught it so well, everything made sense. Tests were really fair, lectures were engaging, and office hours were always open and helpful. I would definitely take this class again!

Andy Gray is straight up the best professor I've had in Olin so far. Unbelievable lecturer, nicest guy, and he cares so much. He's like that guy who is really cute but doesn't even know it--but with teaching. He has such a gift for teaching, but also the mentality of someone who is trying to improve themselves every day. It's so inspiring.

Professor Gray's class is designed for you to learn. The lectures are clear, concise, and well planned and you can tell that he cares about and understands on a high level the material being taught. Semi-weekly problem sets helped ensure I was understanding and engaging with the material so I didn't have to study as much when the exams arrived.

Translates material very well, very reasonable, teaches and lectures well. Is always available to help and seems to have great expertise in the material. Would recommend. An upper division class, so obviously expect some more work (weekly Problem Sets) but very manageable overall if you put in the effort.

For all the horror stories of Mec 290, a very straightforward and good lecturer. Show up, take detailed notes, and ask questions when you get confused, and you will get AT LEAST a B. Highly recommend.

Professor Gray gives clear, concise lectures that make micro concepts easy to understand. The content itself is not too difficult, and Gray makes sure that the problem sets are similar to tests. Gray seems to be the best MEC 290 teacher.
imo Gray is the best prof for mec 290. In general, it's a difficult course, but he really cares about teaching the material and making sure it gets understood.

Professor Gray is honestly the best professor that you can have for MEC 290. He explains the concepts really well and is willing to help the students. As long as you do the practice materials, you will PASS.

MEC290 is a pretty boring class to take, and the concepts are hard to digest sometimes, but Gray is a great lecturer who helps his students understand the topics well. Exams are open notebook and curved generously, and problem sets are graded for completion unless you really mess them up. MEC290 will suck, but take it with Gray if you can.

Micro is boring but Gray does a pretty good job of explaining the concepts. Sometimes his answers to people's questions can get a little long winded but other than that, lectures are useful to attend in person in case you have questions. Would strongly rec going to his office hours. Problem sets are fine, go to TA office hours if you need help.

Professor Gray is a great person and lecturer who genuinely cares about each of his students and makes himself available to help. Class is pretty lecture heavy, grade is determined mostly by two midterms and a final; there's also 6 problem sets and a case competition that's 10% of the course grade (case competition is completely outside of class).

Micro is really a boring class by nature. 1.5hr lectures feel like 2+hrs every time. Prof Gray is a personable guy who does about as good as a job as I'd hope for a professor in this class. His office hours are great, exams are open note and curved ~87, problem sets are helpful, and he gives a TON of practice exams. Nice guy if you get to know him!
Decent lecturer (econ isn't the most exciting topic to teach and he does not change this but doesn't do a bad job either). Concepts are hard to understand and tests are hard (but curved)
I personally loved his class, I know some people don't like his teaching style though.  expect to work hard and practice beyond class time. Although it's not easy, his class is definitely rewarding.
He will push you to the best of your ability which is exactly what you should do in a dance class.  He genuinely wants his students to improve.  He can seem harsh but is a fantastic professor.
He is very tough and going to push you. He def brings out the best in each dancer.
Pushes you and wants to bring out the best in you.

Cecil is absolutely the man. for an intro class, we did a lot and it was definitely more difficult than i expected, but i kind of liked that. he grades 100% on effort and your growth as a dancer because some people in your class will suck, like me, and others will come with experience.
This class really got my butt into shape.  Hard class if you're not a dance major, but he grades on individual growth.  Not a class to take if you're not looking to work work work.
I loved his horton class!
I loved Cecil's class I would take it every semester if I could!
No Comments

He is amazing. Tough class but he knows what he is doing and he will get to know you and your body. He'll push you to push your limitations and exceed your own expectations. If you're laid back and have a sense of humor you'll really connect with him.
Awesome Teacher.  Really understanding.  Fun class.
Awesome and dynamic teacher.  He'll bring out the star in you!
Cecil is da Bomb!  He'll make you get to know your body.  And man can he move.
Great guy.  Take his class.

Amazing professor, would take every class he offers if I had the chance, genuinely cares about students' welfare and is willing to help with anything, answer any question. Plus he keeps making analogies in a political economy context which somehow mention his girlfriend, which is pretty entertaining.

jensen is the best teacher ever... definitely take his international politics class.. he tells you exactly what will be on every test which makes studying very easy and his lectures are pretty entertaining too!
Nice and makes lectures interesting. He cares about students and genuinely wants them to succeed. He is also fair.

One of the best profs at Wash U. I take classes with him that I'm not even interested in just because he's teaching them. He makes even the most dense and tedious reading seem crystal clear. Class atmosphere is relaxed and he is definitely concerned with how his students react to his teaching. TAKE HIS CLASS! You wont regret it!
Awesome teacher, awesome and easy class. Funny too.

The best professor I have had. Gives great lectures, presents material clearly, his exams are easy, he's young, and relates to the students. Take any and every class that he offers....it's worth it!!!
Guy's just the man
No Comments

Awesome prof.  Hilarious.  Young, which is nice.  Very relaxed atmosphere in class.  The readings are kind of boring, but that's the material's fault, not his.  I'm definitely going to take more classes with him.
Nathan Jensen is the freakin' man.

He's amazing! He managed to make the most mundane topics fasnicating and he's a great lecturer. Who would have thought that world political economy would be interesting? Not me, that's for sure. And it helps that he's gorgeous...  I highly recommend h
He's super-hot.  He's also really nice and manages to make economics interesting.

10 ago, Dr. Fields was the first college professor I'd ever had. I was a first generation college student from nowhere special, nervous and out of my depth. In retrospect, he was the worst professor of the 50-60 I've had between college and grad school. Thanks for nothing, Doc! Hope you're reading this review and getting pissy like the first time.

Don't ask him questions during class, but if you ask him after class or during office hours, he is more than willing to help you. Also his hand writing is a little messy, so get used to it but don't comment on it.

The moment I laid eyes on Harvey was the moment I said I do...I do not want to be in this class. Why you may ask? Is because he wrote in piglatin, despite his 6-pack and sexy ass and dark chocolate complexion. He also taught me to learn how to learn...

He's...well, he's Dr. Fields. He's got a lively personality, and he has a lot of ideals regarding how students need to learn to think. I personally like the pace he sets in lectures. All of that said, I think the ideals he tries to uphold are as hopeful and encouraging as they can be ridiculous.
N/A

I suspect that Dr. Fields is an adequate professor. We are ultimately responsible for our education so take the aforementioned comments with a grain of salt. He has a job. This is more than can be said about WashU students who will graduate soon. The majority of whom can't wipe their asses without parental support from their mommies and daddies.
Not bad, but he did go to Morehouse. What can you expect?
In his eyes, if you're taking this class, he's automatically better than you...at life.
This professor made me cry because he said I was wrong in asking something, but as it turned out, I was right. He is extremely arrogant.
He'll make you feel worth less than the cost of the textbook.

Lectures are interesting, but he talks way too quickly and just writes things on board without teaching them. If you try to ask him a question he will make you feel like crap. He's not a bad teacher, but he's arrogant. If you can handle his arrogance, you might be okay in this course.
Not even Alvin and the Chipmunks talk as quickly as he does...
Tim is very kind. His first test was really hard but he also was helpful and is passionate in the lectures
took summer 2022 for FSAP. relatable examples in lectures, clear expectations, understands student mental health, great TA and review sessions too.
THE BEST PROFESSOR EVER

I had Bono for YA Psych, and I absolutely loved him. He is an amazing lecturer and knows how to keep students engaged. You can tell he's also so passionate about what he's teaching and is always available outside of class to talk more about it. He is also relatively young and went to WashU, so he's a very relatable person. Highly recommend.

Bono is a really great teacher and person. Psych 105 was once a week and a pass/fail course. Sometimes it was awesome, but other weeks it really felt like a waste of time. Tim Bono is a great lecturer for the first three times you hear him. After that, he gets a bit repetitive. I still think his work is amazing and he's really funny.

He's a super nice guy but his classes are rehearsed and read like a semi-inspired TEDTalk. Most of the class is presenting 7 studies that all have the same conclusion so lectures can be boring. Tests are hard and you MUST do the readings. If you are a senior with nothing better to do and want some life tips, that is what this class is good for.
Interesting, easy class. I'd recommend.
He is hands-down the best lecturer at WashU. I enjoy his lectures and it feels like time flies by. His exams are tough, so get ready to study a lot and it might not pay off.
Passionate about what he teaches, so intelligent, inspiring, clear, interesting, adorable, great butt.

Tim Bono is probably the best professor I've had at Wash U.  I had him for a freshman seminar, which was about 160 people, and a lecture class, which was about 300 people.  Although the classes were large, he made sure to get to know each student individually and made class fun and engaging.
Pretty standard psychology lecture class. Talks about pretty interesting stuff, and always backs it up with some legitimate research. He's a fun guy, but I did get bored in his class occasionally.
This guy is the real deal. Incredible lecturer, funny, charismatic, and seemingly knows everyone in a 300 person class. I find myself actually looking forward to class.

I absolutely loved him. Did a summer class with him during FSAP and he was great. Energetic, told us if the book was needed and his powerpoints were always clear and he answered all our questions. He gave great examples and clips to watch. Tests were not too hard, it was always stuff we talked about. Great time with Tim!

Professor Reagh is a very kind professor and seems like he truly cares about all his students. I really enjoyed his lectures, and he also seemed to have much better grad student TA lectures than most of my other classes. He is a very fair professor and the grading is also very forgiving.

Zach seems to be a great person; However, as a professor I felt his lectures were a bit disorganized, unlike other biopsychological classes. Some of the quiz and test questions I felt were trick answers but he also makes sure to throw in some easy questions as well. Overall I would say his grading criteria for papers is fairly clear.

Enjoyed and found him to be a good teacher overall. Personally was not the biggest fan of his humor and sometimes I wish language used to describe the science was more formal. Some test and quiz questions were ambiguous. I found Dr. Reagh to be a nice and caring professor, and I think many people vibe with him, I just did not particularly.

I really like Reagh as a professor: he was really funny and down-to-earth. However, this class is one of the more challenging psych classes in my opinion. There is a lot of material and the tests can get very specific. Regardless, if you have to take it, I would take it with Reagh.

Entertaining lectures and funny guy. Test questions are very particular and specific -- you can know the study guide by heart and do badly on the tests. Exam and testing security is horrendous so you get put in an awful situation where you either cheat like 1/3 of the class is doing, or take the L and get a poor score on the test.

Zach is awesome. His lectures are fantastic and provide everything we need for tests. He outlines all the content concisely and reiterates specific topics. The tests can be challenging, but it's cause the content is difficult. I wish he would incorporate the quizzes after each lesson, like a lecture review quiz, so we could encode content better.

Zach is super friendly and material covered in lecture is interesting. The exams are harder than they need to be because the multiple-choice answer choices are convoluted and written with confusing syntax. Unfortunate because I understand the material well but pick the wrong answers due to misunderstanding sentence meaning.

Zach is so open and engaging with his students in and out of the classroom. He is very accommodating and understanding of unforeseen circumstances. Clearly, he wants his students to do well. Cog Neuro is a basic psych class... lecture-heavy with tests/quizzes, but with him, it is very interesting and fun. Best psych class I've taken at WashU!
This was a very great class, but the lectures were very fast. However, he is a very great professor, and he leads an amazing course.
Lectures can cover lots of material. Expect to spend significant amount of time reviewing notes. But his exams and grading policy are very lenient.
He leads a great class, but it is very lecture-heavy, and taking notes during lectures can sometimes be stressful. Overall, he makes the class very interesting and enjoyable.
Zach is one of the best professors I have had at WashU. He displays care for both the content he presents and his students. Every lecture, I walked away having learned something interesting.

Prof. Reagh is an interesting lecturer, and is very accessible if you have any questions after class. Grades mainly based on four exams (drop lowest one), and online quizzes (drop lowest 1/3). Exams are mostly multiple choice with around 4 short answers, average is high. Overall fun class! Would recommend.
Jacoby is a nice (though somewhat intimidating) guy, but his lectures were incredibly unclear & disorganized. The material was interesting, but not well explained.

This class was interesting, but the later topics definitely were not what I expected from a memory class which I found pretty cool. However the tests are pretty hard and he is not very lenient on grading them.
Good lecturer, very funny guy. Always willing to talk with you if you have questions. Not a life-changing experience, but great (especially if you're not a psych major)

Jacoby is pretty well-known in his field and he certainly knows a lot about memory. His lectures are sometimes very funny, oftentimes yawn-inducing, but the killer is the grading scale. No curve, 93 lowest A 90 lowest A-. Mean of the class is usually a 73, which is a C. Don't take this class with Orgo 261! Exams overlap to the day. Don't do it.

For some reason this class was difficult for me. I think it's because his tests are so short- if you miss something, you're basically already at a B. Gives way too many slides which are sometimes poorly organized. But the topic is interesting. For being near 70 (I'm guessing), he relates to the students well and keeps us entertained.

Lectures mildly boring and confusing...does not answer questions well and tries to be funny. Slide shows go a little fast so print them out ahead of time. Tricky exams. Interesting topic. I don't doubt his brilliance in the field, just his teaching ability.
never followed the slides he had us print, and was difficult to follow.
I enjoyed Jacoby's lectures and found the material both useful and interesting.  Recommended

Larry Jacoby is incredibly accomplished and respected in the field of psychology.  He's very intelligent and really knows what he's talking about.  He uses a lot of examples and demonstrations to illustrate concepts, and he is always willing to answer questions if things aren't clear.  All-around great professor.

Jacoby is extremely knowledgable and has years and years of experience in the field. He's got lots of stories and jokes that are funny if you share his dry wit. The material is difficult, and he doesn't always stick right to the book, but he's very willing to answer questions in lecture.

The material in this class is very interesting.  Though Prof. Jacoby knows how to make the lectures not.  He enjoys describing his college days as if he wants us to be able to better relate to him.  His humor is also a little out there.  If he just stuck to the material, I feel this would be a bit better class.
Interesting class but very difficult if not already a little familiar with the material.  I would recommend taking a biological psych or PNP class before taking this one.  Extremely difficult grader.

Madonna is super knowledgeable about her field and makes the information super interesting. She mainly uses powerpoints to give information, which can sometimes be a little boring (its hard to pay attention for a whole 3 hour class). We did a lot of group projects to help us learn and understand the information she was teaching us. Fav professor!

Easiest WashU class I've ever taken, and it was really interesting too! Zero work required outside of class apart from the take-home midterm and final (both super easy). You do need to attend every day, but if you show up, you get an A. Learned fascinating things about the obstacles facing children worldwide.
Madonna is AMAZING! She is so knowledgable and everyone looks forward to going to her class!
