I really enjoyed taking Education, Childhood, and Society. It was worth waiting on the waitlist for multiple semesters to get in. I learned a lot from Madonna, she is a real expert on the topic. She can spew out facts and statistics for literally every question someone asked in the small course.

Amazing professor.  Her lectures are engaging and informative.  I've learned so much in this class and I will definitely take another one of her classes in the future.  Participation and attendance are mandatory but not a problem as I find myself looking forward to class every day.
She has an amazing background as a teacher and a psychologist, so she has stories and first-hand examples to go with everything she is lecturing on.
For being the first year that she taught this course, Madonna did an amazing job. The class was engaging and hands on and Madonna is extremely knowledgeable about the subject.
American School.
awesome lady! you REALLY want to take a class with her.

This class (ed psych) is so laid back, yet she knows so much about the topic.  It's a perfect balance.  I never want to miss because I don't want to miss her weekly insights, or anything that she says.
She knows what she's talking about
No Comments
The course is difficult, but she is incredibly helpful. She has lots of self-made materials. She is really trying to help you.

Dr. Head-Marsden is an amazing professor! The material is tough but her lectures are very helpful and she is extremely accessible for help when you need it. She is very receptive to class feedback and will adjust the lecture schedule and deadlines to meet the needs of her students. Her supplemental notes and office hours are also amazing!

Professor Head-Marsden systematically taught quantum chemistry from ideal models to the approximation methods in this class. The in-class teaching is fast-paced and interesting. Provided materials are sufficient and the professor offered abundant office hours to help. Some of the questions in the Problem Sets and exams were challenging!

I love Dr. Head-Marsden as a person. Full of energy. She even makes fun of her mistakes. She goes too fast in lecture, and if you are not a math major you will miss the details. I ended up spending more time thinking about the calculus and next to no time on the meaning of it all. I would like to retake this paying attention to the latter.

Pchem 1 was a tough class but totally doable if you put the time in. The exams are math heavy (as you would expect for ~physical~ chemistry) but be sure you have a detailed fundamental understanding of the topics discussed in class because most/many of the points are found in setting up the math. Go into pchem with a fresh mindset: you've got this!
Best professor

Kade is the single best science professor I've had at WashU - and it's not even close. She is the crown jewel of the chemistry department and she deserves a perfect review. I have never had a professor teach chemistry in such an understandable way. She is the definition of a teacher who is not out to get you. Love love love

Dr. Head-Marsden was an excellent lecturer and made the physical chemistry material much more accessible than it would have been otherwise. Although the homework and tests were difficult, she was very accessible to answer questions and provide assistance. It should be mentioned she is a theorist though, so the class is math heavy.

PChem is very hard, but Kade made the class fun! I was honestly dreading this class, but between her knowledgeable and very clear lecture style as well as how open she was to helping students outside of class, I actually liked it. I really like the group work style of office hours, and appreciated how she valued effort over genius. Top tier prof.

Kade is the type of professor that makes a hard class easier to get through. It's a very math-heavy class, but she explains everything very clearly. She is very organized -- posts summary/extra notes almost every week for reference. She's very accessible out of class through office hours and emails and clearly wants you to do well.
goated prof. very interesting to learn math origin of some gen chem info. somewhat difficult content but grading is too fair so ye. very organized and lots of office hours etc and nice.

Chem401 is my favorite class in this semester. It opened my eyes to quantum mechanics and promoted me to view chemistry in a novel perspective that I never thought about. I cannot imagine how Dr. Kade organized the materials for this challenging course into weekly notes that clearly summarized the key concepts for the class. Highly recommended!

Dr. Studlar was a highlight for me at WashU. She is incredibly intelligent and knowledgeable, while also being incredibly kind and caring. Her humble and humorous demeanor often made me forget how well respected she is in film studies. It was an honor to be her student! She cares about her students and you will do well if you pay attention in class

You don't across such passionate teachers often. I went to the class not knowing a lot about film theory and with a reservation about how useful it would be for people who are not into writing and analysis but the course made me to re-consider theoretical knowledge as a resource to better equip and conceptualise visual language and visual art.
This is one of my favorite film classes I have taken at WashU. I thought I would hate theory, but professor Studlar made me love it with all over funny jokes and passion for the subjects.
Would have been great if this was not a requirement!

Taking a film class from Gaylyn Studlar is sort of the equivalent of taking a science class from David Hume. Do not miss out on an extremely rare opportunity as such. Maybe try criticizing the level of rigor maintained and demanded in today's undergraduate and graduate level university courses and academic education overall.

Prof. Studlar is a well-established scholar in the field, and apparently she knows that better than anyone. She would lecture nonstop even though it was a graduate seminar. It is clear that she is not interested in what students have to say on the subject matter. And for grad students, expect to get no comment at all on your 20+ pages final paper.

She is incredibly knowledgeable and very passionate about film, which shows. She's truly a pioneer in the field so it was great learning from her. However, grading criteria is non-existent, lectures are scattered, and it's difficult getting feedback. When paired with how hard she grades it just leaves a bad taste in your mouth.

She's very passionate about the topics, but doesn't make expectations clear. Extremely tough grader. I would not take this class for credit unless it is absolutely necessary. Lectures were poorly structured and she gets off track. Don't let my grade deceive you--the class averages were in the low-70s for most assignments. Take any other professor.

Professor Studlar is pretty scattered in general. Her lectures are chaotic-she poses and laughs at her own jokes for half of the class, but the other half it's clear that she's incredibly knowledgable on the subject. But not all scholars should be professors--in fact, most shouldn't. She's not kind or understanding, and one-on-one meetings show it

You will never meet a professor more passionate about their subject, and it's infectious. Loved the class but she's an absolutely brutal grader who doesn't do a great job of making expectations clear. Meet with her to talk about essays even though she won't help you too much. Basically, you should take her classes but make sure it's pass/fail.

tough class and hard grader, but class was always a pleasure to attend. she is incredibly knowledgeable in the field and has a sardonic sense of humor, but can soften if you make the effort to seek help/clarification.

She's tough, but she's one of the most important scholars in the field today. Funny, whip smart, endlessly interesting, she will rip your work apart but you will not mind. OK, you WILL for a few minutes, but she'll charm and inspire you again within an instant. You owe it to yourself to take the Dr. Studlar ride.

The worst professor I've had in my 4 years. boring lectures, writes all over his own slides yet refuses to post these annotations after class. Constantly contradicts his previous word, adding quizzes or moving assignment deadlines. His teaching style does not promote learning. Unless you have a solid understanding of R already, stay away.

Not a good class for people who do not have a reasonable coding or statistics background (run now). If you need to use R in the future, this class is worth it. Chib is an expert in his field but not the best lecturer.

His lectures are terribly poor for introductory R. The class was running weeks behind schedule because he stopped class for every little software hiccup that students had. Not receptive to feedback about how the class was being run.
extremely boring. can't understand his lecture. drop this class before it is too late
You can at least get an B+ if you just turn in the assignments. He has a great research background and expertise in Bayesian. He is the coolest Indian professor i've ever met. He mad chill.
Amazing expert, terrible professor. Run away before it's too late
Extremely boring. He needs to engage the class more, and be less monotonous, most people are falling a sleep. I would not recommend him.
Amazing researcher obviously one of the tops in his field. Unfortunately he has lost his ability to teach. Poor guy
His graduate courses are amazing! Go Bayes!
No Comments
Extremely knowledgeable. Very interested in his work. Cares about the students. Easy grading.
Boring!!!

Bergstrom is an amazing professor and also a really nice guy! He genuinely cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Very humble, and admits when things are wrong / need changes. There is a lot of textbook reading assigned in this class, but as long as you read them and pay attention in class, you'll do fine. Really enjoyed 100B!

He's the best professor I've ever had here. He's passionate about everything he teaches and you can tell he knows what he's talking about. He makes it really easy to get a good grade. The class is mostly based on quizzes and exams. He lets you drop an exam and on top of that, the final is optional. He also gives you 3 attempts per review quiz.

Hands down best professor I've ever had at WashU. For a 2 hour class twice a week, he kept it engaging and made sure to give breaks. Prof Bergstrom is very caring, accommodating, and truly wants students to learn and succeed. For one of the hardest psych classes, he made it less of a burden which we were all so grateful for.
Sometimes I would get lost in lectures, just because he would deviate from the slides a lot. But, overall, if you are in tune, he is an awesome professor!

Love love loved Bergstrom! He was super flexible and understanding about us having other stuff outside of his class and would always change deadlines to give us more time. Really appreciated him this semester

Was my favorite of the 3 Intro to Psych professors. Tests can be a little tough but you should do very well if you pay attention in the lecture and look at the slides. Nice sense of humor in lectures.

I love this man, and if I could, I would take the rest of my psych classes with him. Amazing professor and genuinely cares not only about his work but also his students. Can be pretty late with grading things, but more than makes up with be super understanding and flexible with you.

Lectures can be a little boring, but he truly cares about the success of his students. Powerpoints are largely based on the textbook and offers optional practice quizzes to test our learning. Lectures are funny and interesting if you pay attention.

Professor Bergstrom is the best professor I have had at WashU. He makes the class incredibly engaging, he gives very detailed instructions, and he is incredibly understand. Even though Experimental Psych is known to be one of the more difficult classes and work heavy classes in the Psych Department, Bergstrom made the class incredibly manageable.

He is just the best! He truly cares about his students in and out of the classroom and only wants to see them succeed. This class can be very content-heavy, but he moves through it well and in an engaging manner. Definitely one of my favorite professors!

I love Professor Bergstrom!! He is so engaging and considerate to his students. He loves to chat with you in or out of class and is a great resource. The few lectures we had were very entertaining and informative. It is clear he wants his study to do well and be healthy. Very awesome would highly recommend him.
Bergstrom was so nice and so funny! Very engaging and really cares about you as a student. He's been my favorite professor at WashU so far! 10/10 recommend.

Dr Bergstrom is awesome! He is a great lecturer and is funny. He gives good feedback on papers and allows you to do corrections. Regarding the research paper, he is instrumental in helping you develop your idea and offers great insight. Take experimental psych with him! Trigger warning though: this class is a lot of work.

Unbearable, unpredictable, and insufferably boring. Montano has been the worst professor I've had at WashU so far. Do NOT take this class. She is incredibly disorganized, passive-aggressive, and unclear on her expectations. She expects everyone to agree with her and does not explain what she looks for on assignments
Her lectures are disorganized and her gradings are unclear.

The reason I chose this class was because it was interesting and seemed fun. Oh man how much I regretted this class. Be prepared to read and how she thinks. If you don't see it how she sees it, she tends to degrade your thinking. A lot of repetitive reading and how boring her lectures are. She also tends to go on long tangents and not grade at all.

Boring class with no intellectual stimulation whatsoever. The readings are repetitive and pleonastic, the seminar days are more like quizzes, and she's rude to students. I found her to be extremely judgemental. Do not, do not, do not take a class with her.
Extremely disorganized, changes dates often and does not update the syllabus. Lectures as if she is reading right off of a piece of paper which is very boring.
Professor Montano is disorganized. It is often very unclear what the homework assignment is, and she often uses last minute emails to change our schedule.

EMPHASIS on Get Ready To Read. Not as bad as the other reviews say. She is not the best teacher, but her research is very interesting. Do the readings, GET READY TO PARTICIPATE IN CLASS, and you'll do fine. I took the class because "Angels, Prostitutes and Chicas Modernas: Women in Latin American History" was an interesting-sounding name.

Montano is inaccessible outside of class. She does not provide clear instructions and provides no feedback. Grades are determined by participation and very few large assignments (which you do not get feedback on, even the midterm). There is a lot of reading required and she gets very specific and angry if you do not remember/reply correctly.

Awful. Provides unclear directions (if any) and odd assignments. Randomly calls on students in class and gets outwardly angry and yells if they do not give an answer she likes. Only provided feedback on one essay this semester and assigned grades with no feedback for the rest of the sem. Inaccessible outside of class and never responds to emails.

She calls on people randomly and asks difficult questions about the readings (and gets annoyed if you don't know the answer). You have to read and take thorough notes on all the readings. Also, sometimes her instructions and lectures are unclear

Absurd amount of reading. 3 textbooks with supplemental articles. Expected us to remember reading assignments from3 weeks ago and gave pop quizzes when we didn't. Participation can make or break your grade. Gave opportunity to rewrite midterm and gave good feedback to improve paper and better write the final, both were out of class.
One of the worst classes I've ever taken. She gave so much homework it was literally impossible to keep up and didn't present the material in an engaging way.

AVOID. Makes class unnecessarily challanging, misleads students as to what will be on the exam, and makes her section way harder then the other teachers. Has to curve exams so much because students are so unprepared and generally perform so poorly... the average on her exams is usually around a 50%.

She is so caring, smart, and passionate. Yes, the class is really hard, but she will do everything to help her students; she cares and is very reasonable. If you go to TA office hours, you will get problem-set answers, but MAKE SURE to go to her office hours and stay for the whole time. She will teach you lots, and that time is very valuable.

Professor Diaz-Campo does have more difficult tests, but she curves each test so that the average is an 85. Her lectures are thorough and easy to understand. Problem sets tend to be longer than necessary, but the office hours provided by the TAs and/or herself make them extremely doable. Show up, study, and you'll be fine.

Her lectures aren't bad and she's a nice person - plus the curve is best in her class - but she does move really fast, gives out pop quizzes, problem sets are long, and tests are pretty hard and may include content not covered in class. I would recommend trying to take MEC290 with a different professor if possible

Professor Diaz-Campo is a diligent and caring professor; she is always open to answering questions. Attendance isn't mandatory, but she will give pop quizzes. If you can't come just email, she is very understanding. MEC will always be a hard course but as long as you come to class and study well you'll do fine, don't be fooled by the salty reviews.
Abnormally difficult and fast-paced class compared to the other professors for this course. Not the greatest lecturer either.

Worst professor in the B school. Lectures are terrible, she makes no sense in class, and the test are nothing like any homework or class or practice test. You can't do good in this class. Don't take her. Take it in the fall just to have another teacher.

I sent an email to her inquiring about my final grade and found out that I had earned an A. I had been striving for an A since my grade was very near the cutoff point. My performance met the challenging standards set by the professor. The professor believed that I had the potential to excel in business. Do not take her class!!!!!

MEC 290 is a rigorous and very fast paced class. Although her exams were ridiculously difficult, Professor Diaz-Campo is very accessible outside of class and is willing to work with you to improve your grade. Make sure you participate in class and attend those office hours to be clear on exam contents. Also take the Problem sets seriously!!

Yes, her exams are fast-paced, but that doesn't mean she doesn't teach. In my opinion, her teaching was not bad. And if you find that you aren't getting much from the lectures, they're recorded for later reference. Her office hours are super helpful, too, and she's communicative via email. Overall a good, kind professor.

If you can keep up with her fast-paced lectures and somehow manage to teach yourself the concepts that are on the exam (that she barely covers), you will do great in this class. If not, you will still do okay. She's a somewhat friendly professor and will be generous about exam content if you attend her office hours. Overall, not a bad professor!

Very boring and average teacher. She knows her stuff well but lectures aren't very engaging. The first test was very hard but second much easier. It's her first year so I'm sure she'll adapt the tests to be more fair. Never calls on people. Just a boring class

Great person. Recently received his Doctorate! Took CAD and Numerical Methods (Matrix algebra) with him. Learned a lot about MATLAB and SolidWorks. Very knowledgeable and incredibly friendly and accessible. Nicest professor, will remember you.

Course content overall was fairly straightforward and pretty interesting at certain points. I felt the pace of the class was good. It can be a bit tricky at first to understand what the professor is explaining but there are examples and homework problems which help solidify concepts.

I learned a lot in this class and I think the course is structured well. Material can be a bit challenging at first but professor is always available to answer questions. You can usually finish the weekly homework in class if you work efficiently. Definitely recommend doing the workbook practice problems, (there are weekly quizzes).

Pretty chill; no lectures and attendance isn't mandatory, but I would recommend going to as many classes as possible. Do your work and read through the workbook and you'll be solid. I easily could've gotten a higher grade if I went to class more.

Woodhams is a great professor who actually understands the need to show students how to do different problems. Lectures are really helpful, and while the material isnt particularly easy, we would be so lost without him. Hes also just a pleasant person to be around. I wish every engineering class was taught by him.
Not really lectures, just has you run through CAD exercises to learn CAD, which I prefer because I learn by doing. Definitely a great intro to CAD and the TAs are very knowledgeable .

He doesn't lecture, he will give you ton of hw and grade really hard on it. He doesn't care about your other class. He always gives you 2HW on the same week as your 2 tests that he gives out. Very unreasonable for 2hr credit course.

Professor Woodhams is a great professor! CAD with him was straightforward and interesting- I struggled a bit on the exams, but he was very available for extra help and I was still able to get an A in the class. He's very approachable and a good lecturer.

Louie Woodhams is too pure for this world <3 Seriously though, super nice guy and CAD with him is great. Pretty easy, but you'll still learn the ropes of SolidWorks - everything a CAD class should be.

Incredibly nice guy. Always so willing to help. He explains things pretty well and gives brief, concise lectures. The homework assignments and quizzes are not too hard, but make sure you keep up and not get lazy. The exams can be a little tricky, but he lets you retake them up to an 80%. He is a great professor, definitely worth taking CAD from him

Not too easy or too hard, just a pretty typical CAD class. I almost gave a 2 for difficulty. He is a very sweet guy that loves to answer peoples questions, and his lectures are short to allow you to work on the homework. The tests are comprehensive but fair. Lots of assignments so best to keep up and get help when you need it!

He is a really great instructor. He is willing to help you with whatever you need at the drop of a hat. If you are ready to put in the work you can easily get an A as he lets you resubmit work that have errors. Some people find his tests tricky, but if you understand the sketch book you will be fine.

Professor Christenson is a nice person, but not a great lecturer. You can skip lecture completely, as long as you read the textbook you'll be fine. Tests have a strict time limit but they're all multiple choice, so as long as you have a general sense of the content his class is a pretty easy A, just don't expect to actually learn anything.
Great professor, if you go to lecture he obviously cares about his students
He does really care about his students, but his lectures can get boring at times. The exams are heavily based on the textbook, so if you read the textbook you should do fine in his class.

He's a great guy, really wants students to do well in class. His lectures mostly cover base material so I heavily recommend reading the assigned chapters in textbook. Just read and study for the exams and you'll do just fine!

Honestly not a super hard class, his lectures just suck. He pretty much reads off the slides and doesn't really say anything insightful or interesting. He's probably the worst professor in the poli sci department at Washu. The class is basically, a repeat of AP Gov. Try to take this class with Reeves if possible.
Not bad if you complete all the assignments and participate in small section. Reading the textbook and taking detailed notes are a must! He is generally a nice professor.

Christenson is one of the worst teachers I've ever had. Coming into college, I was interested in a political science major...I am now mostly turned away from this career as I had such a displeasurable experience in his class. His lectures are difficult to pay attention to and his tests cover lots of material not discussed in class. Just bad.

Can't stress this enough - MAKE SURE TO TURN IN ALL OF THE WEEKLY ASSIGNMENTS AND ATTEND EVERYTHING. They are for completion and worth 40% of your grade. If you do these you only need a 88% test average for an A which isn't bad because the tests are fine. Prof is decent and will help outside of class. Not my favorite class but not a bad one either

He was a pretty average professor. He is knowledgeable and passionate about politics but his lectures can be boring. You don't really need his lectures as the tests are based on the reading but Professor Christenson seems like a nice guy who would be willing to help outside of class. He is new to WashU + this class so he may improve with experience

He's a TERRIBLE professor. You can go/not go to lecture and get the same grade. Tests are automatically graded when you submit (and worth 60% of your final grade), so he doesn't look at them at all. You have zero time per question, AND he has TYPOS and some WRONG answers to the automatically graded test questions. Honestly disappointed with him.
Lots of work, 94+ is an A

Maya is a huge program, and the field of 3D modeling and animating is, too.  If there's anything I've learned from taking on an art degree, it's that there's no substitute for your own motivation and a teacher who's willing to give you personalized time and care.  Jon is that teacher, even though the lectures can be hard to follow.  Amazing guy.

Very nice, knowledgeable professor who's clearly passionate about the subject, but has difficulty staying on topic. Most of class is spent going over irrelevant material. Did learn Maya Autodesk pretty well, but it would've been so much easier to learn by myself.
Jon's class was a drag to get through. He's a nice guy but the course material can be extremely dull if you don't have any previous experience with 3D animation processes.

Jon is very knowledgable of Maya and gave me a great introduction into learning the program. The class is a little dull and drags on but he will always help you out and wants you to understand the program. He also doesn't overanalyze your work and you'll do fine in the class if you put in effort.

greatest strength is his knowledge of maya/digital animation programs. he also is extremely sweet and as long as you are friendly and hard working, he will give you a good grade. his class made the process of animating a little dull, and he goes off on MANY tangents when you ask for help. but he means well. if you really take initiative it pays off
Amazing instructor.  Genuinely cares about his students.  Really understand software and if you take the time to talk to him, he will really help you out and give you hints.
Very Nice professor, knows 3D software very well. Always willing to give personal help. The class is pretty cool if you want to try out 3D animating.

Pretty easy-going guy, lets you do your own stuff in class as long as you finish the work. Can be boring some times and extremely undemanding.  But on the other hand, if you have questions, even if it's unrelated to class, he's ALWAYS happy to help you out, even outside class hours... good professor overall.

incredibly boring professor.  does know his stuff though. he didn't take our class or grades seriously throughout the semester but in the end he came down like a nit-picky hardass.  in my opinion, not friendly or likeable.  for some reason, however, others do seem to like him.
Pretty amicable, considering my class was at 8 AM. Knows his stuff about Computer Graphics programs. Projects were interesting, though we got behind a bit.
Navy is very knowledgeable with computer programs but his assignments are worthless and allow now room for your own creativity. He is a great guy, but sort of a flake. Not a recommended class.

He is accessible outside of class and willing to meet 1-on-1 if you are struggling. His class can require a lot of studying and reviewing outside of class though. He can be harsh with grading but there is also a generous curve, although this does depend on how well he thinks you understand the material. He tends to announce quizzes two days ahead

He is clearly enthusiastic about the subjects he teaches, but somehow is able to turn the simplest concepts into incredibly confusing ideas. My recommendation, first find a good textbook (For Chem 402 Atkins is a good choice), read the relevant section(s) before every lecture, go to lecture, watch the box videos and then do the practice problems.

My husband and I both went to Wash U and still occasionally bring up how awful a Professor Sobotka was. He appears to loathe his students, his lectures are incredibly boring and difficult to follow, and his tests cover nothing that was covered in class or the textbook! People were in tears leaving the first exam.
seems enthusiastic but just unable to teach. Makes even simple concepts seem twisted.

Most discouraging class I've had in my WashU career. I can study for a week straight and still fail his exams and quizzes. It's hard to know what is important from lectures and notes and if you don't have much experience in thermo you will be overwhelmed. Get ready to study, ask questions, and cry.
Had this guy 10 years ago...managed to turn me off to the sciences...

It was a very difficult class, but he was very willing to meet with the students in the computer lab to make sure we were able to understand everything.  If you are willing to spend approx 5 hours a week in the computer lab, this is a great class that aids in the understanding of many concepts taught in PChem.

This man should not be teaching anywhere. He basically does not even make a pretense of attempting to explain chem concepts to undergrads. Compared with Regina Frey (God bless her), they are night and day. Chem is hard, he makes it incomprehensible. If I just wanted formulas, I read book.
No Comments

Great combination of theory, hands on simulations, and in class discussions of problem set solutions. Dr. Sobotka has been teaching this course for many years and it shows with his thorough notes and presentation of difficult topics. Highly recommended!
Professor is very enthusiastic but lectures are often too confusing and skips over fundamentals that are integral to understanding the more complex topics.

Her lectures are interesting. The readings that we did were interesting; I just wished the class was more structured because it seemed like the materials were all over the place. I enjoyed the TA lectures; they offered great perspectives.

Applies also to grad classes.

Pros: not much group work
Cons: lack of lecture and discussion prep, hostile to different critical opinions (grades lower without explanation). Unhelpful feedback to assignments ("interesting"). Will try to gaslight/threaten you if you attempt to privately express concerns about the class or class-adjacent issues.

Because of her background, she unnecessarily focuses on too much political ideology part rather than analyzing novels and stories themselves. Sometimes very biased with extreme points of view. Need to purchase expensive textbooks. Avoid if you really want to know anything about Chinese literature instead of being brainwashed by anti-China opinions.

There is a lot of reading for this class, but it is really interesting and helped me learn a lot. The lectures provide the historical situation of the readings, then a lot of class is spent discussing. Prof. Chen has a really cool background and brings that to class. I learned so much from this course and enjoyed taking it.

Class is pretty straightforward - usually you discuss the readings assigned. Definitely have to buy the assigned books. She lectures occasionally to give historical background of readings. Prof. Chen cares a lot about the class content and wants to share her knowledge, but class can be boring. Great TAs. 3 papers are basically the whole grade.
I don't know where you people are getting that she is easy, but she is not. We have one week to write a 20+ page midterm. No joke. I'd avoid if I were you.

Interesting class but professor is extremely biased. Discussion is usually guided toward her view, shoots down students' comments sometimes, gives a one-sided view of complex history. Be careful with her essay assignments, because she will grade you on much she agrees with your interpretation.
unclear on assginments but casual to talk to

Grading's not hard.  But extreme unclear and vague about projects.  Grade is extremely subjective, because 60% is 2 group presentations and 40% on participation and attendance.  There's no hardly any work besides reading.
N/A
Everyone participates and debates heatedly in her class... She's the BOMB!

Dr. Campbell is an amazing teacher who gives amazing feedback. He is very kind and a good listener. He even had my class over for dinner (great cook). I definitely became a better writer because of this class. My only heads up is to participate in the class discussions, it is super important for both your grade and your writing development.

Without getting too much into details of what happened in our class, it is important to note that reports were filed against Dr. Campbell this semester(FL23). He would take a long time to get our essays back to us. When he would get them back to us, the feedback he would give wasn't helpful. Our averages for each assignment were in the low B range.
Gives an okay amount of feedback, but the number grade doesn't match the feedback he gives. Lectures mostly about history because that's what he's more interested in. Takes forever to return papers.

Yes Dr. Campbell was a tough grader sometimes, but setting up one on one meetings for feedback and ideas on papers was extremely helpful. He also is very experienced in academia with so much knowledge that he is so willing to share if you ask. I definitely became a better writer due to his class.

If you dont want a miserable college writing experience I would avoid him. He can be extremely rude to students at times and was an extremely harsh grader for my class. We usually had our average essays in the C+/B-/B range. Also took months to return essays sometimes. Really disappointed.

The only thing that got me through this course was knowing how to write from my high school English classes. Dr. Campbell's lectures were very meandering and never really seemed to have a point to them. He also picked on individual students and was very condescending at times. Never took points off for me turning papers in late though.

Professor Campbell was a funny professor, but most times the lectures were unhelpful and irrelevant. While he was accessible outside of class, he didn't give great or constructive writing feedback, and often interrupted students. Very flexible with deadlines.
Amazing teacher makes an attempt to relate to students on a genuine level. You will look forward to this class.

Professor Campbell is an interesting teacher. If youre looking for writing skills and tips, you wont find them here beyond a vague cater to your audience repeated every class. His lecture style is engaging and fun, but his grading process is unclear and contradicts itself. He is also messy with due dates and communication - super frustrating.

Prof. Campbell is great!! His lectures are so much fun; you'll actually want to go to class. The readings were really interesting and the papers weren't hard at all. He's a really funny guy and I would definitely take a class from him again. I had him for College Writing: Writing Identity.
Campbell was amazing. Super chill guy and very flexible with due dates. Classes were super entertaining and he was always getting everyone engaged. Had him for college writing writing identity.

Took this class a while ago. However, I now feel it is important to speak out. His class was not intellectually rigorous, but Dr. Martin appeared to support discrimination against white Americans with a focus on white men in particular. Please avoid Dr. Martin and stand against his promotion of deeply racist rhetoric.

This class focused mostly on pre-recorded guest interviews that we watched asynchronously each week. I thought that the workload was manageable and the material was interesting. We didn't have much interaction with Dr. Martin, but he seemed like a caring and passionate professor, and his interviews were almost always interesting to watch.

If you have the chance to take Between Malcolm X and MLK Jr. with Professor Martin and Professor Ali together, don't pass it up! This was one of the best courses I've taken. Both professors are amazing lecturers and having them interact together in class made the class so engaging.
Professor Martin is one of the best teachers I've ever had- he cares so much about his students and is an amazing lecturer. I'm definitely going to take another of his classes

Dr. Martin is one of my favorite professors I've ever had. The material of his class is engaging, he clearly cares about his students, and he inspired me to think in new ways. He establishes rapport with all of his students and gives encouraging feedback, and his personality shines through in his lectures. 10/10 would recommend.

such a fascinating professor! rly admirable dedication to the (often controversial/sensitive) material he teaches; its v clear he cares a lot abt having a real understanding of the past and relating it responsibly to the present. also discusses historical figures as human beings better than most profs i've had.

Martin is an excellent moderator for the class discussions, which can get dicey at times. When studying events and texts from the early 20th century, he finds a way to make them relevant and worth our attention. Would recommend taking any of his classes!

Dr. Martin is the best! He is easily my favorite professor at Wash U. On top of making interesting material even more engaging, he helps every single student feel like their opinions on the subject matter are valuable. If a student ever misses the mark, he helps guide them to understanding something better. No judgements. Take a class with him!
Dr. Martin is an amazing lecturer. He's super funny and approachable too. I plan on taking another class with him if possible.

Professor Martin is amazing. He is so helpful, and does a lot to make his classes interesting. The class I took with him is the FBI and Religion class, and he is a pretty easy grader and an interesting lecturer. I'd recommend taking at least one class with him if you can!!!

Prof Spriggs is very knowledgeable in the area of judicial politics and the Supreme Court. However, his lectures are very hard to follow and often turn into tangents, with little to no slides with information on them. The class is based on a group project and 2 exams. The 2 exams were extremely difficult for a lot of people.

Would not recommend course unless you are VERY interested in the topic - Supreme Court (Justices more so than cases). Rambles in lectures, hard to pay attention to, disorganized/unused Canvas, course grade is determined only by midterm, final, and group paper - grading criteria is unclear. Class grade distribution was:
A+ 1
A 7
A- 17
B+ 12
B 2
Lots and lots of reading, tough grader, you will learn a ton.

Spriggs is an interesting lecturer, but not a great teacher. He isn't accessible and can be obnoxious. With that said, the class wasn't too tough. The exams were pretty reasonable as long as you study the cases beforehand. Not a big fan of Spriggs, but still an interesting class.

I thought he was a pretty pretentious lecturer. Having said that, if you went to the lectures, and wrote down notes for all the court cases & just memorize them word for word (obviously know how to apply them), the tests were easy. He says he wants students to answer his questions, but he mostly answers them on his own

Great class, one of my absolute favorites.  Really interesting material and he doesn't really care if you keep up (as long as you are prepared come test day).  The paper seems overwhelming at first but go meet with him or the TA and you'll be fine.

He is a fun and smart professor, but his TA was a tough grader. Also, the curves were pretty brutal. I would recommend taking a class, such as this one PASS/FAIL. I don't know a single person who received an A with him, and this class will likely drag down your GPA.
VERY Smart professor. Extremely well structured class, no surprises. Expectations are perfectly outlined. Test is straightforward. GREAT CLASS

An excellent, well structured class.  Prof Spriggs was always willing to answer my questions in class and office hours, and was very flexible when I had to reschedule the midterm. Lots of cases and material to read for the paper but there was only one and he gave plenty of time to do it If yo're at all interested in the Supreme Court take it
Very interesting class. Definitely need to do the readings in advance. Tries to promote in-class discussions -- doesn't always work. I defintiely recommend this class to polisci majors.

Lubo was the man. Took him for FIN 3303 at OU and he honestly helped me and the class a lot. Before tests he has a review session and he goes over what all you would need to know prior to the test. Really helpful, if you go to his office hours he will help even more! Recommend him for finance. Definitely have to study pretty well but he helps a lot
Professor is well organized. The class is very demanding. He would give very hard exams, but is also very fair. I learned a lot from this class, more than any other in Eller.
Professor is meticulously organized. He explains really well. The class pace is fast. Overall I would recommend taking M&A with him, but be mindful that it would take a lot of your time.
He is very knowledgeable about mergers & acquisitions. I learn a ton every class, but it is a lot of information!

Lubo is pretty good.  Very organized, and he needs to be with the amount of info he throws at you.  If you're not prepared to work, don't take his class, but if you really want to know a lot about finance, AFM with Lubo (or Todd Milbourn) will do that
Undisputedly the best at WashU! If you are headed for any kind of investment banking career, you are better off taking his class. It is amazing, even now I am still using his lectures.

The man works investment banking hours as a teacher.  Honestly, no teacher who has ever taught at our school has worked harder at the teaching aspect of the job.  He will help you, but will not hold your hand and do things for you.  Get a good group (preferably someone good at excel or someone who had a banking internship) and you should be all set
awesome dude, very nice, very helpful, really cares

One of the best professors at Wash U.  Very knowledgable about corporate finance and has lots of practical experience.  His class is very demanding and time consuming but you learn a lot.  He is ALWAYS available and willing to help you understand.  Awesome teacher, cannot say enough good things about him.

ALWAYS available for help outside of class.  Really wants students to do well in a class that has difficult material.  Problem sets and cases are tough and time-consuming, but anyone that wants to go into any sort of banking will be better off for having taken this class with Lubo.

Warsi Ji was hands down my favorite professor at WashU. Not only was he able to teach me how to read and write hindi in one semester (with no prior knowledge of the script), he was also personally invested in my success at WashU. He was easy to talk to and gave great advice about everything.

Warsi-ji's Hindi class shaped my college experience.  Professor Warsi-ji is brilliant, helpful, and remarkably kind.  We were able to learn Hindi script and vocabulary quickly; beyond his ability to teach the material, he instills a confidence and sense of humor in every student.  I am beyond grateful for my 3 years of Hindi class with Warsi-ji.
Amazing professor - one of the nicest people you will ever meet.

absolutely amazing professor, one of the highlights of my college experience. he is the one professor you always know genuinely cares about all of his students, and he will bend over backwards to make sure that your hindi experience is the best it can be. I've learned an enormous amount from him, and couldn't possibly rate him higher than I do now.
he is awesome.
It's a class where I start sleeping. Teacher need to use new teaching material. Not a very nice class to know about Hindi at all.

Warsiji is an incredible teacher who cares a lot for his students. He has an amazing command of Hindi/Urdu and is always able to make explanations clear and concise. Grading is incredibly lenient, because he really wants everyone to learn and do well.

He is a good teacher and a person. The way he carriers himself makes him a person to be followed. In addition to being a excellent teacher he also inculcates the values of good life and work among his students, which sets him apart from the rest in the same line of interest.
He is an incredible professor. His concepts are pretty clear and above that his explanations are very convincing. My suggestion is TAKE HIS CLASS AT LEAST ONCE THEN ONLY YOU BELIEVE ME.
This teacher spoils our taste of Hindi what we had from the great lakhanji. Lakhan does not and must not have an alternative. Prof Warsi tries to imitate Lakhanji's style but is not able to.

Prof. Portnoy was one of the best professors I've had. He really cares about his students and the focus in class is learning, not grades. His lectures are interesting and applicable to the real world. There's a couple of essays you have to write, but he grades them fairly. There's not a ton of other homework. Overall, a wonderful prof and class.

Rob Portnoy is the BEST, though he needs to re-do the quizzes for this class. They are unbelievably random in terms of what they ask; besides that the class is pretty fair and if you use the TAs, go to office hours, put in a decent amount of time into readings/papers, you'll get what you deserve. Can't say that abt quizzes tho.

I had Professor Portnoy for Human Resources Management and Leadership in Organizations and he is one of the best professors I've had. He makes class super interactive and engaging. It is easy to get an A if you attend class and do the few assignments so I was never concerned about my grades - only about learning, which is honestly rare and a gift
I got an A on the midterm, and an A- on the final. I got a B in the class!?  If you take this class, you're risking facing a deadly grading curve.
Great class. Very nice and caring person. interesting topics. Quite easy as long as you go to class.

Extremely easy. Very boring. Take home midterm and final. Required to keep a "learning journal" where you write about what you did in class everyday from an "HR Perspective". Almost no work. 1/20th of a finance class in terms of work. 1/5th of a marketing class. Really nice guy. Bring a laptop so you can surf the web during class.
he makes you laugh..easy but informative class.
This was the best and one of the easiest classes that I have ever taken.  If you need an upper level course, take this, you won't regret it!
He is one of the best professors ever that inspires you to learn and take interest in class material
Really nice, always allowed re-takes of take-home exams, and gave full credit. Wants people to do really well.

2151* Wonderful, knowledgeable professor. Creates incredibly welcoming atmosphere for class discussions, which are v low pressure. Readings are rarely too lengthy. Essays are straightforward and purposeful. A solid effort will get a good grade. Cares about her students, her field of expertise, and the Eng dept as a whole. Couldn't recommend more.

Would cut off my left hand for Prof Rosenfeld. Literally such a wonderful person, so knowledgeable in her field and makes medieval literature into something so entertaining. Medieval Sexuality is a 10/10 class, so funny and fascinating.

Even if you're not into medieval literature, take any and all classes Professor Rosenfeld offers!  She is incredibly knowledgeable, hilarious, and  totally adorable.  Readings are manageable (if not light) and interesting, class discussions are a joy, and she is a very fair grader if you do the work!

Prof. Rosenfeld is the best.  She understands pedagogical theory, but she's willing to shift her pedagogy as needed.  She clearly loves what she reads and writes about.  She's smart, and she's funny.  She's available and willing to meet in office hours.  She's pretty much the best ever.
Whoever gave the negative review is just bitter. Workload was manageable, maybe even light. Fair grading. Interesting material.
A great professor. I had no interest in medieval literature, but she actually cultivated a very positive class dynamic and knows tons about the subject.
Not interesting at all - unjustly hard. I subsequently took a 400 class and it was much easier to get the same outcome.
Prof Rosenfeld is just awesome in general.  Way nice and super helpful.  Love her!

Professor Rosenfeld is extremely compassionate, articulate and intelligent. She cares a great deal for her students, but she is not easy. The grades that I received on my papers were not as high as I expected them to be. Also, Rosenfeld teaches more text-intensive than the rest of the English department, without much emphasis on overall analysis.

Although this class was quite daunting (read Chaucer, Milton, Donne, Spenser, Shakespeare), Rosenfeld transcended all the Washu "stay away from the "R" english teachers" rumors and showed herself to be a fabulous professor.  She was funny, intelligent and knew how to lead the discussions really well. Hard paper grader though, but allows rewrites

Chris King is chill. You can guarantee about half of the points by completing the assignments, and he's quite flexible with deadlines. Essays are long and annoying at times, but the workload is mostly manageable. He gives harsh comments, but the scoring criteria are pretty clear and grading isn't horrendous (I survived as an international student).

this man is a joke. He comes in here and gives you these expectations that he is not able to deliver on. His grading scale is bogus. It is out of 100 points, so if you lose a point on an assignment, it could mean a whole letter grade. His guest speakers are fine, but during this time he literally falls asleep. class=joke. just dont expect to learn.
very enjoyable class
N/A

This class is a massive waste of time; for half of the semester, King just brings in guest speakers to talk about their careers, rather than actually teaching us writing techniques, which made going to class seem useless. King also gives really harsh oral critiques, so it can be demoralizing to listen to his feedback since it's always negative.
he is so nice and so easygoing. not hard at all to do well. deff take.
Super interesting guy, friendly, and funny. Harsh critique but if you work to address comments it will translate to a good grade.

Mr. King is a good teacher for students who are already pretty good at writing and are able to take criticism. Regarding feedback, he doesn't hold back and tries to make the grading criteria as objective as possible. He is pretty eccentric, which turned off some of my classmates. Overall, I say he's a good teacher but it depends on personality.

Did not teach students how to write. So you were stuck if you didn't have prior experience. He even said he didn't like the analysis paper so simply didn't teach students how to do it. When asking for extra feedback, he didn't respond back. Didn't learn anything from this class, don't recommend.

Mr. King is the best. He has a very unique personality, and it's great. He puts in a lot of effort to make the class great. He grades pretty hard, but luckily there is a rough draft for you to get feedback first. 10/10 recommend

Not only is Prof. Flowe a very kind, forgiving person, but the content he presents is consistently fascinating, the readings are interesting and often pretty short, and the workload is manageable. If the workload becomes unmanageable, Flowe is flexible. His kindness shines through and makes his class environment so comfortable.

DJ Flowe is the goat. Really interesting class, though I wish people engaged more because often times people just wouldn't do the readings and wouldn't speak in class so few people ended up talking. The more you engage the better. Flowe is super sweet and funny, and I really enjoyed his lectures.

This class was exciting and Professor Flowe was great. We took field trips etc. to learn about archival work. The class was really easy, only graded on a few participatory team work projects, and professor flowe is super supportive. Tbh I wish it was harder because I wanted to get a bit more from the topic, but nice to have a easy class.

Professor Flowe's class was very interesting and yet the workload was very chill.  I wish we would've gone a little more in depth with some concepts but nonetheless very enjoyable and a great professor!

I love Professor Flowe. He is such a caring and interesting professor. The course topic was super interesting and provides a lot of real life examples. Professor Flowe creates a relaxed environment and Im always excited to show up for this class. If you're considering this class, you should take it.

One of the best professors & courses I've had! I took "The Politics of Black Criminality and Popular Protest". Given it was remote, Flowe kept me engaged and excited about the material. It was student driven, but he often interjected with insightful comments that added to the content. I'm also pretty sure he gave everyone an A on every assignment.
Heavy reading and moderate writing.

Professor Flowe is amazing and definitely my favorite at WashU. I loved taking his class this semester and I hope to take more classes with him in the future. He is an incredible lecturer and a caring person. He is funny and makes jokes in class to keep the energy up. He is very understanding when students are sick or need to miss class.

DJ Flowe is one of my favorite professors. He is passionate about what he teaches and it really comes across in his lectures. The class is very easy, but we did talk about some pretty heavy topics regarding race/gender. Be prepared to have uncomfortable conversations (definitely a good thing in college). Flowe is very chill about grades/deadlines.
great lectures, deep topics, really cool dude, not very difficult but so worthwhile.
Adcock is a professor to be avoided. I cried about her class many times as she made me feel incredibly stupid no matter how hard I worked.

Very intelligent, but would be much better suited as a researcher than a professor. She is very abrasive in person and aggressive over email, no matter how nice you are to her. She shoots down any topic that explore impact of religion on SA, something I found blatantly offensive at times. I would not recommend taking her classes to anyone.

I LOVE Professor Adcock! She's really nice and helpful. The class isn't easy, but she will stand by you and help you along the way. Tough on papers, but will help. Fair on tests. She will only test on what's been discussed in class. LOVELOVELOVExmillion
Smokin' Hot!

She is very passionate about teaching and making sure students understand what she teaches. She grades harshly on papers but its not that bad. You have to go to class and take notes in order to do the papers. The midterm and final are ok but it require a little studying.
Very open to suggestions but very clear when lecturing on topics of great complexity
No Comments
Just take good notes and you'll be set.  You don't really need to read every week, but it helps.  Base your papers off class notes.
very enthusiastic...go to class, take good notes, and you have an A
Easy teacher. Nice person. Gives good notes in class. Tests are quite easy.
GOAT

Dr. Polites is an amazing professor and such a nice guy. This class was so informative and I have learned information from it that I will want to know for years to come. Take this class, you won't regret it.

I had him for Medprep. The lectures are 2 hours long, which can seem like a doozy, but he is engaging the entire time. He's so funny, and there is a lot of valuable stuff to learn. The "Ooh-Aah" lecture is probably the most engrossed I've ever been in a lecture EVER - highly recommend!

I know the student who provided the only negative comments on this site. I knew her in both in college and medical school. She was told by Dr. Polites to stop coming to class late. Of course, that's all some students need to attack a professor and course that has among the highest approval ratings in the university.
Really resourceful class. Learned a lot, definitely every considering pre-med should take this class.
AMAZING!! Such a helpful class.
DR. GREGORY POLITES IS A GOD AMONG MEN. TAKE MEDPREP
This class is literally "show up and pass" that's how it works. Polites is funny and a good lecturer. If you are considering a career in medicine, take this class.

Greg Polites is an annoying, self righteous person. He told the same stories over and over again and bores you out of being pre-med. As a current WUSM student and a former wustl undergrad, I can say that Greg Polites does NOT offer an accurate portrait of the medical school. Please do not take Med Prep.

Took most of the classes she offered. Expect a lot of reading (or google the cases) but her exam was not any harder than others. She makes it clear in class what is most important. She has 'favorite students' - but if you are polite and take part, she will like you. Grades are clearly not affected by her favoritism though.

one of the toughest classes I ever took at WashU, would I take it again? Probably not but prof sadat was a good teacher who is passionate in her field. However- beware of homework and the written exams
Extremely difficult course, only take this if you are truly passionate about the subject matter- Sadat is not soft but a good teacher.
I found Professor Sadat to be one of the top 2 or 3 professors at Wash. U.  She is outstanding.

Same as it ever was.  In her class 14 years ago, and it appears it is no better.  A miserable, relentlessly condescending professor with no respect for her students and no love of what she tries to teach.  I learned more from my 1st job in 2 weeks than with her in a semester. Take heart current students, it can only get better

More interested in notoriety than teaching. Lecturing at Wulaw lets her sell her text. Getting her name in the paper is her career. In class and in her exams she cannot bring herself to ask a coherent question. A 24 hour handwritten exam? She should make the effort to read 12 point font. Her pricey, rambling textbook has smaller print than that.
No Comments
No Comments

It was very clear that she is only interested in advancing her own career - she definately thinks she's important. Unfortunately, she teaches some great classes, but the only way to get a good grade is if you're male and suck up!!

I think calling Aileen Waters a professor or even a teacher is rude to other professors. Aileen Waters is a grader, she will not teach you anything in the class, moreover expect that you already know it. She comes off as arrogant, and thinks that nobody is better with grammar rules than her. Overall, harsh grader, not that pleasant of a person.

Really nice professor! Approachable and always accessible during office hours. Gives useful feedback on assignments. Not a harsh grader, the lowest grade for me is an A- (I'm not good at writing at all). Readings are kind of long but manageable. There are some small assignments but they are preparatory for essays.

Great professor who genuinely cares about her students and offers lots of valuable feedback. Tough grader, so go to office hours if you are struggling, she will guide you in the right direction. Also, the class is a lot of work and a lot of writing, so be prepared to put in the work. Participate in class, keep up with the work and you'll be fine.
Go to her Office hours. She is extremely helpful.

Prof Waters was a great writing professor. I am someone with very little interest in writing, but she made this class one of my favorites this semester. There is a good amount of reading before each class. She is very available for feedback on assignments and goes out of her way to help students. Text and traditions is a very interesting class.

I think most people don't want to take college writing, but as a professor she is pretty good. She gives you good advice on what to do; the only thing that is a deterrent is that she is not an easy grader. I have not yet gotten an A on my assignments and her essays are required to be 4-5 pages, with the last one being 10. Also lots of busywork.
Professor Waters is kind, understanding, and overall a good teacher. She is a harsh grader, though.

Really loved her in college writing. Definitely heavy on the essays. She really cares about her students though and will always give an extension if you need one. Would definitely take her class again.

Classic English teacher. Assigns lots of reading without realizing how much work she's actually giving, comes off as a bit snarky at times, will probably never give you a full A, and relatively disorganized. However, she gives good feedback on papers and promotes a good environment for in-class discussions. As far as CWP profs go, she's alright.
Excellent professor. His powerpoint slides are legendary. Really a shame he's retiring.

i love him like any thing partyly because contracts is my favorite subject. He is very helpful inside and out of class. He does give more and less then B+ I had the personal experiance with that. He loves to help explaining any Contract issue or qyeuestion you might have and will probably never forget it.

You could ask him about anything to do with contracts at anytime and he would go into a detailed discussion of the point that was interesting and helpful, and he would do it without being showoffy like some profs. He knows the material, he enjoys it and it shows. His class was one of my all time favorite educational experiences!

Mumbles through classes, writes incomprehensible diagrams on whiteboards, rarely sums up points coherently. He also didn't tell us about a work limit on a exam, it then showed up on the directions, and did not dock anyone who went over the limit. It was a big deal at the school that was handled horribly.
Nice old man. Character at times. Never mean. seemed to want us to learn the info. Very helpful after class.

He basically gives everyone a B+.  I recommend not stuyding b/c you'll get a B+ no matter what.  Basically gave no A's or A+'s.  Just A-/B+ and a few Bs.  Very little incentive to work when there's no chance of reward.
No Comments
No Comments
DAMN CONTRACT BASKET!!!!  I still have nightmares about that class.
He was cool
Very easy professor to get an A on. He mumbles a bit but is very funny and entertaining and is a great teacher.

Nice guy but he mumbles and it is nearly impossible to understand what he says. There is no set schedule for the class so planning ahead is near impossible. Going to class is useless. A very frustrating experience.
He is nice except the fact that he covers material too fest.
He is very nice except the fact that he sometimes covers the material too fest
Such a bro, just do his homeworks and prepare for the tests 2 days in advance you'll be fine.
Nice guy, but mumbles to such an extent that I really can't hear a thing he says in class. Self study, fewer than 20 people go to his lectures because they are terrible.
Awesome professor. He's really nice. Super laid back. And you don't have to go to class. And his tests are easy.
His tests are ridiculously easy and only take about 20 minutes to do!!! This is not a time intensive class as long as you do the webwork every week! If you don't have to take 131 take 127!!!!

Heavily focused on proofs and pure mathematical theory, as opposed to applications. If the former is your style of thinking, you'll be fine (with a fair amount of effort), but if you expect an applied math course take it with someone else.
Know what he is talking about however only focuses on derivations and not uses of statistics
Excellent introduction to stochastic processes that review some probability theory. I highly recommend it to people interested in those subjects.

Nice, interesting man. Just not a very good presenter and class was therefore somewhat boring. Nice, loosely structured course though. Allowed for nice exploration of multiple topics. Only 1 midterm and a few hw assignments. 3 pg paper final.
Exams ok and curves nicely.  I learned a lot from the class.

Yes he does things the mathematician way, but he explains the theories very well.  Really cares for his students.  He slows down a lot and cuts out some of the technical stuff when he senses students are having trouble.  Also, really funny when he wants to be.

Alot of theory, with not much comp.  Class is important but does not help as much as it could as he feels necessary to prove all theoretic stuff.  Take from easier prof if possible.  Exams doable but harshly graded.
The class is relatively easy. The HW and exams are not impossible. He teaches a lot of theory and that's the hardest stuff you will see in his class.

In class, his lectures focus on the theory and derivations behind statistics, but all the hw and tests are primarily based on the book which is more straight forward and worth holding onto. There is a lot of material but on the final he allowed 2 full note pages front and back. His tests are fair but require you to really know what you're doing

Most disorganized and confusing class I have ever taken. Didn't seem to prepare lecture materials before class, frequently said contradictory statements concerning the syllabus, and gave incoherent proofs and explanations. Extremely disappointed with the complete lack of structure in the class. Had to self learn the entire course.

Prof Rodsphon is super kind and friendly towards students especially when you need help understanding the material during office hours. Invested in making sure students were learning and understanding not just specific examples but the concepts overall.

Kind professor although awfully disorganized in general. He changed our final grades last minute after having told us that he used the wrong curving system to grade and give us feedback on all of our previous assessments but most people didn't seem to have been affected much. Easy to get a good grade as he gives solutions in class.

Very nice guy. Lectures can be a little disorganized, but if you show up regularly you should be fine. Exams were not all that hard and he is a very generous grader. Overall, great class, would take again.

Rudy is great. He is so smart & passionate about math. He teaches in a way that spreads that passion to his students. He is patient, kind, and understanding. His main goal is to help students learn. But, he expects students to have a full understanding of the concepts, not just a memorization of procedures. He is very generous with grading tho

Professor Rodsphon is a great guy. Very interesting and lighthearted, but lectures seem to be erratic at times. Also doesn't do a lot of student problem-solving, so much of the work is self-studying. Was a little behind material wise but was a very knowledgeable guy.

Rudy is a fantastic person, but a lot of the lectures seemed haphazard and unprepared. I didn't really understand a lot of his explanations and had to look at khan academy, etc. to find out what was really going on (EXPECIALLY with surface integrals)
Prepares us well for exams, gets to know students well and makes the learning environment very comfortable. For multivar, he is good with the diagrams and visualizations for the multiple integrals.
One of the best professors Ive ever had. Amazing at drawing figures, which is important for Calc 3. Over all a really good guy and a good teacher.
Really mean, would not take any of his classes again

Professor Lew demonstrates excellent expertise in the field of Electrical and Systems Engineering. However, the class itself is very challenging with a heavy workload. Be ready to invest a lot of time in homework assignments and case studies, go to office hours frequently, attend PST sessions on weekends, and be prepared to self-learn MATLAB.

Professor Lew is really nice and helpful. He offers excellent lectures and high quality after-class zoom session. However, lectures never teach Matlab but homework is 90% Matlab. There will be 3-4 case studies and will cost you a lot of time. WARM NOTICE: START THEM EARLY. It will be helpful if you can find a great teammate to do with you.

Professor Lew and Professor Ching do a really good job with ESE105. While the class is difficult, it's a great way for ESE first-years to get their feet wet. The case studies and analysis and design exercises are difficult but that does not take away from the quality of Professor Lew's teaching style. Prof. Lew is very accessible outside of class.

You can tell Professor Lew loves teaching this course, as his passion for optical imaging shines through in his lectures. Homework and projects are useful exercises, testing on both the fundamental concepts and the mathematics. There is a presentation to get students to explore research frontiers in the field by analyzing a research paper.
Taken Fall 2020. This class is hard, but Professor Lew was super approachable. He really cared about the material and wanted his students to succeed.

Taken FL 2019. Professor Lew did quite well at introducing certain linear algebra concepts to the class, but the course could offer a deeper MATLAB intro instead of the Mathworks onramp. Problem sets and case studies are TOUGH; some people were discouraged from pursuing ESE after this course. I highly recommend signing up for Problem Solving Teams.

Dr. Lew along with his co-lecturer (Ching) teach material that is FAR too advanced for an incoming first-year student with little to no background in linear algebra. In addition, his test questions are unreasonably difficult with little partial credit being offered. Shame that he is turning off first-years from electrical engineering at WashU.
Introductory class to Electrical Engineering . Labs are interesting but hard. He is a tough grader for exams and labs.
Fantastic teacher, very understanding about troubles university students face. She obviously cares about her students, and had very interesting and fun projects.
Tracy is THE most understanding, nicest, helpful person in the world. She will push back deadlines if need be, and she makes class overall FUN! take this class!
Traci is a WONDERFUL teacher.  She gives a really nice introduction to Visual Communications in general, covering all of the major elements project by project.  Definitely a refreshing class.
Great teacher!  I learned tremendously from her!

What a nice, down to earth person.  Not always prepared to fill a three hour class, and can ramble on sometimes.  All in all though, a great person.  Sometimes you feel like you're not learning anything, but you must have somewhere along the way.  I came out with more skills and ideas than when I went in.  Always lets you out early

Class started off way to slow (we didn't do anything but talk for the first month) but once projects got going the class was pretty fun. Traci's good at encouraging students of all different design levels, but she's not so good at constructive critism. she just tells you she thinks your work is good. it's not very helpful for improving your skills.

Tracy is a wonderful person, but her class is structured haphazardly and I didn't learn much. Cool field trips are a plus and her experience is helpful and interesting, but there is never enough planned to fill a 3 hour studio. You get out early...but dont expect to be better at graphic design.
GREAT teacher! fun class!

I had Professor Henke for Literature and Democracy and it was definitely a very reading-intensive course. Going to office hours helps a lot as he is very patient and gives you a lot of detailed feedback on essays. I highly recommend the course if you like the small seminar vibe rather than having class in a huge lecture hall.

Professor Henke was great! I had him for Literature and Democracy; he was incredibly engaging and very kind to his students. It was a lot of reading -- around 9 or 10 books -- but it was mostly manageable. You write around 4 papers throughout the semester and then a larger final. If you go to class ready to engage he will love you.

Be expected to read 4-6 hours outside of class. Prof. assigns around 9 books to buy, and wants you to have the physical copies. Tocqueville, Whitman, and Shakespear focused. The last units that touch on women and Black people's role in democracy got cropped short. Very nit-picky with papers, expand on what he says during class you should be fine.

Literature and Democracy was deff my favorite class to sit through. So interesting and he is a very good lecturer. However, only take this class if you want to read about 4 hours between each class. The readings are good but there is a TON OF READING. He is a tough grader. But it is manageable.

Henke is okay: I would take him again if I had to. I had him for Literature and Democracy. The main problem I had was communication was varied in speed when it mattered. His critiques on papers are very helpful.
Professor Henke is by far one of the best and most caring professors I've had at WashU. Don't skip out on the opportunity to take a class with him.

Prof. Henke is an extremely knowledgeable professor, and he is adept at interpreting the nuances of literature. He also is very accessible and is always happy to chat after class or whenever honestly. Hes not a tough grader and he is always receptive to debate and commentary.
One of the worst teachers in the PAD. Take your Theater Culture classes with a different professor.

Amazing lecture for State Level Lobbying. After her class I feel that I am actually prepared for lobbying. Her lectures are fulfilling, engaging, and she is one of the few professors that can design curriculum that sticks with students. Contentwise, You will not regret spending your time in this class.

Anna's a great professor, and so much of classes are intellectually stimulating through a combination of lecture/class discussion. I learned a lot from her courses and was enlightened in topics I had little familiarity with. That being said, she's a very tough grader, and a LOT of reading. If you're into that, take her classes.
Awesome professor. Does not sugar coat or make life easier for you. Very realistic

Likes to play devil's advocate to the point where it is no longer useful, but is just intended to get a rise out of people. Made more than one student leave the class crying because her activities made them so uncomfortable.
Really tough class, especially if you aren't familiar with policy. Would tell you now that you're in for a tough class, but it's quite rewarding in the end.

Rewarding class, but definitely a lot of time and energy must be spent in here. Expect to read a lot and be called on for your opinion. The final paper is a huge pain in the butt, but you'll likely get through this class unscathed if you care about the issues discussed.

Great class. No busy work. Very interesting readings. Class challenges views and can be 'triggering,' but that is expected. People say she is a difficult grader, but I think she is one of the few professors at Wash U that does not engage in grade inflation.

Shows passion for the subject matter and incorporates hands-on learning activities. However, I found her bias in the classroom to be distracting and counterproductive to the learning environment. She is not the best model of tolerating diversity in the political and religious sense.  She rolls her eyes and mocks people w/ a different opinion.

Does not teach; provides examples in class and then contradicts them in "grading" your paper.  He has become progressively more illogical and incomprehensible with age, to the point of near-dementia. Class tangents lead nowhere so not even "interesting" anymore.  Pray you are not forced to have him 1L year.

Professor Dorothy is quirky. I'll concede that he doesn't teach you to write during class periods. But do what you're supposed to and bring a full memo to your conferences, and then do to it exactly what he tells you to do. HE WILL MAKE YOU A MUCH BETTER LEGAL WRITER. I owe SO MUCH to this guy.

the 'conference' is him reading your paper, leaving no time for questions.  he writes his notes in marker so you can't read them.  he tells you your paper is good then gives you low grades.  does not do much teaching in general.  nice guy though.

Def one of the most helpful profs. He critiques your entire paper from page to page in front of you. He tells you word for word what he wants. If you dont do it, its on you. Very laidback, which is a great change from other profs. Unprepared sometimes...but that just adds to the "laidback" approach.

Very helpful. He conferences with students about their papers, and he will go through your entire memo, all 10+ pages, and make suggestions and comment on it.  If you don't find him helpful, it's because you don't bring him a completed memo to go through.  He tells you exactly what to do, and if you do it, you'll get a great grade.

Least helpful "Professor" ever. My favorite Dorothy-ism is, in response to a request for guidance on a paper, "You're an adult. You can do what you want." Thanks, ****, but I was asking what the page limit was!
Great guy; great teacher.

1st class:"no one will learn anything in this class, you'll learn when you're a 1st year assoc, a partner throws your work at you, you spend all night redoing it". He'd: take roll+ dismiss us after 8 minutes because he had nothing to say; cancel class for golf; spit tobacco in class; answer pleas for help with:"don't worry, you're doing fine". BAD!
Nice on teaching and grading

Professor Dan knew the material very well and was able to convey concepts learned within the course by using personal experience. He is also open to answering and clarifying questions on issues you may have during amd after each lesson.
Best person and professor ever meet! He has professional knowledge and is also a really nice person. He is very kind and friendly. Love him.
Would definitely recommend him.
Mainly group assignments with only little individual work. Good professor!
Dan has a really good teaching skill and he arrange this course and give the grade very well.
Dan is definitely one of my favorite instructors at WashU, he's easygoing and always welcomed to help. Highly recommend his class!
