The teaching style worked well for me. I struggled a bit during the semester, but the professor helped me understand the material clearly when I needed help. I enjoyed being able to discuss the subject during class as an open discussion. Professor is great and I would definitely take a class taught by him again!
Horrible teacher. Like actually so bad

Cruz is a sweet & humorous person, but she is a horrible orgo lab prof. She jokes about us struggling in orgo and never gives direct answers to simple questions during recitation. I hate to hate her since she's so warm in person, but I can barely understand her cryptic hints during recitation-- & it's not even bc of her accent. She's just confusing
L teacher
Horrible teacher.
Simply incompetent. The most unorganized class I have taken at WashU.
Very kind and caring instructor

Dr. de la Cruz really cares about her students and goes out of her way to be accessible to students. That being said, she is not clear with her grading criteria and is not very organized which was frustrating. The best way to get through the class is to regularly go to office hours to clear up anything that's confusing.

This professor has not been clear all semester and is really condescending. Also her English is horrible. I have attempted to explain to her my situation with regard to the practice sets and she doesn't even know her own assignment schedule--claiming the problem set was due way after it actually was. Big waste of time.

Very well organized prof. He posts slide printouts for all of his lectures online. He is always willing to help during office hours, and really knows what he is talking about. Tests can be challenging if you don't really know the material.
This professor is the best.  I would not trade the experience.  Thumbs up!!
Knows his stuff! Makes class fun with pop culture examples.  It's not hard if you take it during the summer session
tough (but interesting) class, awesome professor!
Great teacher!  very clear, helpful, interesting.  Class is great.
No Comments
Excellent...nice, clear, reasonable. He was my advisor.

Professor Strube is easily my favorite professor that I've had since I've been at WashU! He really cares about what he's teaching, and always makes class interesting. He also provides copies of his overheads, which are very helpful.

Julie is such a sweet professor. I've thoroughly enjoyed class with her this semester. The topics she teaches on are very interesting, I find her to be very engaging, and she really cares about her students. Strongly recommend taking her classes.

Honestly, I really enjoyed Dr. Singer's class. She is very adamant about participation, but she is also rather enthusiastic! Homework was given every night, but not always taken up. There was only a midterm and final in terms of tests, but there were 6 papers over the course of the semester. Our grades were  cushioned, no reason to perform poorly.

She gives a lot of interesting lectures, but I didn't feel like I really learned a whole lot in the course. More culture focus, but the course is advertised as writing-oriented... We did six essays, but they were more like peripheral assignments than core figures in the learning process of the course. Overall, good teacher teaching the wrong stuff
Prof. Singer is very enthusiastic and seems to really care about her students. I've definitely improved my French in her class.
She's great best French prof at this school take as many classes as you can with her
The best French professor I've had so far. Endearing, understanding, helpful, clear, and extremely likeable. You'll learn a lot and never feel overwhelmed.
Excellent teacher.

Julie is really sweet and cute. She likes her job but she has those days and understands if you do too. Her powerpoints are kindof tedious and she teaches material after we've already been assigned and completed homework on the subject but she's not too unrealistic and actually respects her students

It felt like she didn't know what she was doing sometimes. It was her first time teaching this class. She is obsessed with primates because they're her specialty. Studying all the names, dates, and concepts you need to know is the toughest part; it's quite a bit of material. I learned that I don't fare as well with boring lectures and MCQ tests.
Very funny and personable, just pay attention to lectures and you'll do fine.
Incredibly boring. Reads off the slides. Takes forever to speak. Always says "you know". Avoid classes with her at all costs

Her love for primatology is somewhat permeating. Her love for the material kinda makes you want to learn about them yourself. If you treat the class as one large connected story tracing the evolution of humans, it'll be easier.

I really liked this class and Dr. Hores. The content was interesting and I think Hores did a great job showing her passion for it. There is a lot of memorization though, and the slides can be very disorganized, with ideas sort of mismatched together.

While Rose is a great professor there is little motivating you to attend lectures. Lectures are heavily slides based and the slides are posted. There are only 3 MC exams the entire semester . Tons of memorization (species names, dates etc.) some MC questions can be extremely specific. If memorization isn't your jam prepare for a lot of work.

Graded on three exams. Exams are relatively easy, just need to memorize. There is a textbook, but you aren't required to read it. Posts lecture notes so that you can review them after class. Knows and cares about what she's teaching, lecture can get a little dull sometimes though. Gave everyone a 100% on the exam once due to a grading error.

I really enjoyed taking Evolution of the Human Diet with Dr. Hores. She is a great lecturer and seems to be really passionate about what she is teaching. The class seemed a little bit disorganized at times but if deadlines were changed it was always clear so I really did not mind it that much.

Jesse takes some getting used to and at first seems rude but he is a great guy, very funny and knowledgable. You get more out of the class if you engage him or talk to him about your interests/goals in the class than if you just attend passively. Gives great assignments.
Jesse is so helpful if you show that you're interested.  If you don't put anything into it, he's not going to help you.  Fair grader, very understanding and helpful.

Yes, it's true, Jesse doesn't give as much criticism and instruction as other drawing teachers.  But honestly, you will get out of this class whatever you put into it.  And he definitely gives more help to people who he sees are trying harder than others.  So don't be afraid to enjoy his teaching.  Also, he gives so much less homework!

He's not the greatest teacher. His class is actually pretty boring. BUT, he assigns NO WORK compared to other teachers, listens to very cool music (like the Beetles) in class, and is easy to relate to.  I didn't enjoy the class at all, but I wouldn't have wanted to take Drawing with anyone else!

Makes assumptions about people's intentions and uses that against them. Vague comments, sometimes draws on your work without asking, but he wants you to do something specific--god knows what. Pissy, chooses favorites and enemies. Too immature to teach. No credibility No encouragement No improvemt

I have heard that most other drawing teachers give an unreasonable amount of work. I thought the course load for 1st semester Drawing was quite manageable, which is good if you're not an art major. He was less negative in critiques too, which is nice if you take neg. remarks personally.

Jesse is a cool guy but not a good teacher.  He can't explaing things verbally at all.  If you want to get better in his class, it has to be on your own steam.  Don't count on his remarks for improvement.

I graduated about 20 years ago. Professor Zwicker was a profound, positive influence in my life -- one of the 3 best teachers I had at any stage during my formal education, and a generous, brilliant, interesting person outside of it.  It should say enough that 20 years later a former student of his writes this review.

One of the dullest classes I've ever been in.  The paper topics were awful.  Prof Zwicker knows a lot, but it seems like he just wants you to guess what he knows and isn't really interested in hearing anything new.
Definitely the best professor I've had at WashU.  He's the reason I am majoring in English.
Brilliant professor who gives great lectures. Ok, his jokes are sometimes very subtle, and he makes you work, but he is the best of the best.
Wonderful, brilliant teacher.
this class is death. he has a fake british accent and insists upon doing his own dramatic monologue renditions of... milton. so boring, such a random grader, get out while you can
This is my advisor. He has never once made an effort to see me or advise me. He does not respond to my emails and he has made no effort to help me meet my graduation requirements.

She is an incredibly dedicated professor who genuinely cares about her students and provides valuable, constructive feedback. She stands out as one of the top professors I've had the privilege of learning from in the Sam Fox School.

Very approachable in class, but did not give explicit critique feedback for our 2nd project and 3rd (the final) grades. Tight deadlines (e.g. 2 days), heavy assignments (e.g. have ## of iterations). The class structure depended on Becca's feedback. Becca and our TA gave 1-1 or small group feedback but sometimes ran out of time due to ~18 students.

Becca was a great, talented, and knowledgeable professor who made classes enjoyable. My only complaint was how much work was expected of us in the second half of the semester, when the workload increased quite a bit with little time. It was very stressful to manage with other classes, but I would definitely take a class with her again.
SHE IS HANDS DOWN MY FAVORITE PROFESSOR IN SFS. Her feedback is always practical and helpful; I learnt a lot from her class. She's also very caring too.

Becca is amazing! She is so nice and approachable. You will spend a lot of class time working on the projects and she is always walking around to answer questions and give feedback. She cares a lot about process work, so this is very beneficial. She also pays special attention to craft. Overall, I found her grading to be fair, if not on easier.

I love Becca! She gives really constructive feedback, and I've grown so much from her class. She's a pro in type and you'll learn a lot as a designer. She's a little tough in grading and ask a lot from students, but it's because she wants you to develop and grow. She values process more than result, so if you work hard you'll do just fine.
Really good feedback and also really nice

i love bialek!!! i've taken good life and love and reason with her (good life is very philosophical, but those lectures were amazing. legitimately) i am not a reader but she is so very good at breaking down the reading to make sense and integrate it with other materials and ideas. she is so understanding and flexible about extensions. 1000/10

"The good life between religion and politics" is a lot more philosophical than the course description sounds (especially for the first half of the semester). Because of that, it wasn't my favorite class but great professor and not too much work

Professor Bialek does an amazing job discussing very interesting topics. She is very understanding of extenuating circumstances and is such a good person. The readings she chooses are genuinely interesting, and I have nothing but good things to say about her!

Professor Bialek is caring and thoughtful. She is an engaging lecturer and is friendly to the students. Her major assignments take time but she is more than happy to help you and you get chances to improve your grade.

Professor Bialek is insightful, helpful, and super nice and accommodating. I highly recommend taking "The Good Life Between Religion and Politics" with her at some point. She ensures you have multiple chances to get a good grade, and the course content is super interesting and relevant to how we live our lives.

She's amazing and hands-down the best professor I've ever had- and perhaps the most knowledgeable, as well. It is a little intimidating to speak up in class because of it but she is very validating and respects all ideas, even if they're nowhere near as informed or complex as her own. Would absolutely recommend for Thinking about Religion!

Great professor -- very passionate about the subject and incredibly knowledgeable. A conversation with Prof. Bialek will show you how little you actually know about the readings for her class. Very accessible, and gives great, thorough feedback on essays. Would highly recommend Thinking About Religion as an introductory RS course.

Discusses course concepts in relation to real-world events in lectures, which make them engaging. Lectures are sometimes not as focused, though. There are a lot of readings, but that's just the nature of the class.

Either Prof Gabel has done some serious soul-searching over the last few years, or these reviews are way off-base. Politics of the EU is a lot of reading but it's not overwhelming, especially if you keep up throughout the semester. Lectures are super engaging and grading standards are reasonable.

workload was truly insane(~1000pages vs. ~500 for my other classes);lectures are interesting but sometimes hard to follow as he tries to be oratorical;occasionally arrogant;exams-very hard;you've got to be very precise in your answers;yes there were reviews for the exams but still too much reading-this class must be your 1st priority!!!

Politics of the EU. Great class. Yes there's a LOT of reading especially in the beginning, but come on you are in college now (and in the top rated school in the country). Professor is knowledgeable, lectures are interesting. Majority of the grade (70%): midterm and final, professor is very clear about what will be on the exam in the review session

Gabel is on his game when it comes to the EU, but expectations are very high in regard to absorbing 1000's of pages of material for exams.  Hmmm what will he ask on his exams, overarching questions---NOT, in fact, he asks detailed info on less important things that you wouldn't think would be on an exam.  wording on his exams are difficult

Pretty arrogant.  Each test covered ~1000 pages of reading and was the majority of the test.  Gabel talks down to his students.  It isn't "daring" them to challenge him.  He responded to about 50% of my emails and blamed me for something that was his fault.  I'm not taking any more polysci classes bc of this experience.
Dr. Gabel is incredibly smart and dares you to challenge him - he loves it.  You have to be on your game if you do.
Santiago was really invested in our well-being and was very flexible.

Santiago was incredibly caring and understanding of the changes that the pandemic created in my life as a single mother. His decision to change our course from asynchronous to synchronous after the pandemic hit was the best thing that could've happened. I do t junk I would've been able to finish it without that.
Santiago helped me feel prepared when I was lost and challenged when I was comfortable.
Santiagos long long talks.
GREAT!

Santiago was instrumental for my experience as a freshman in Washu. He always made himself available in and outside the classroom and was supportive not only when it came to our course, but generally in regards to college as an experience and a challenge. Even though, this course is grammar heavy, Santiago made it incredibly interesting & relevant.

A really influential person for my college experience. He definitely likes to talk, but, at the same time, is incredibly encouraging to everyone to speak their minds critically and from their one positions. This course motivated me to get a minor in Spanish and seriously consider LAS.
Use CMU's slides and read them but actually teach nothing.
Super hard lab. The last two was insanely hard.

She doesn't care about undergrads at all and is so focused on her own research. Her lecture videos were so boring, and she basically read the slides. Her exam was T/F on super minor points. Really? It's just lazy to give a T/F exam in college. I went to her OH and she was incredibly unhelpful. I regret taking this course with her.

This professor sucks. The lecture is straight from CMU and she just reads off the slides and sometimes stares at them cause she constantly doesn't know what she was talking about. She's extremely unreasonable with accommodations.
The pace and the structure of the course is perfect for a junior year CSE student. Slides are clear and the prof is always accessible outside of class.
Lectures are hard but really helpful. Materials are really good (because it's from CMU). Be prepared to challenge yourself in labs and exams.

Class was insanely hard. She's not great at teaching, she mostly just reads off the slides, which frequently have mistakes in them. 5 lab homeworks, the first 2 aren't bad but the others are pretty hard. Midterm and final were brutal. I don't recommend this class, but if you need it then take it with someone else if you can.

Dr. Wayne is very friendly if you speak with her outside of class, but inside of class she does not seem the most approachable. Course was relatively easy, grading wasn't too tough. However, participation really matters. The class was fine, but dry at times.

I took a great class by this professor (Not 330). Anything that was graded was explained well and the professor gave out detailed feedback. The lectures were extremely interesting and the professor was very accessible outside of class. I loved the readings that were assigned as well. Great class!

Interesting class but not a huge fan of the prof. Very clear bias toward Israel, to the point where she was contradicting stuff my other profs presented as fact. Grades are based on papers graded by the TA, who sat in the corner and did not speak to anyone. Wayne seems to be an academic who enjoys research far more than students.

Overall OK but some problems. Well prepared and clearly meticulous. She is not friendly outside of class nor does she seem particularly fond of any of us. Definitely biased pro-Israel and the lectures about Israel Palestine contained historical assertions and no Palestinian sources. TA grading system is also bizarre and at times arbitrary.

She was a good professor, but I always had this feeling that she didn't like any of us. Then again, this semester was rough on her for a lot of personal reasons. Overall, very knowledgeable about terrorism and counterterrorism, would recommend her to anyone interested in working for an intelligence agency like the CIA. Attendance matters.
Great professor was a little bit less involved later in the semester but that was because she was having a baby.

Great professor who teaches about really interesting subjects. She's super caring and kind, and I felt like I could talk to her about anything. A little disorganized at times, but not in a way that seriously detracts from the quality of the class. Definitely recommend her class if you're interested in learning more about terrorism.

Every psych major should take this course.
An easy A, attendance isn't mandatory, extra credits, easy final project

100% recommend taking Psychopathology with Professor Karcher! Great lecturer, useful slides, and cares about student success. Exams also were easier than class material, I always felt over prepared after exams (in a good way).

Goated professor. Caring, accessible, and wants you to succeed. Going to be completely real here, studied max of 3 hours for each test (12 am - 4 am the day of the test) to focus on short-term memory encoding/storage, and went through 2-3 repeats of the material on the study guide as it corresponded to my notes. Ended up almost acing each test ;)

Professor Karcher is an amazing professor who genuinely cares about her students and wants them to succeed. She is a great lecturer and makes the long class periods seem short with videos, interactive portions, and personal anecdotes. I wish I could take another class with her and I thoroughly recommend this course! need to study for tests tho!

She is super sweet and very knowledgeable. There is a lot of memorization but it is pretty manageable, I find her lectures to be engaging and we cover a lot of interesting topics. Definitely recommend!
Professor Karcher is an excellent lecturer, and Psych 354 has been very interesting. I found that the content has been easy to learn, and she posts exam reviews which are helpful and reliable.

Professor Karcher seems like a nice enough person, but she goes through hundreds of facts, then expects you to memorize every single one of them for a poorly designed multiple choice exam. It has twice the amount of stuff to memorize than an average psych class. Lectures are average, slides are below average. Would take with a different professor.
Really interesting class, and as long as you participate, you will do well. Highly recommend this professor and this class.

The class honestly had super interesting material that covered various topics such as medical racism, animal ethics, abortion and eugenics, intersex care, addiction, nuclear experiments, and more. The class was entirely discussion-based, meaning that you just had to show up to class and participate once or twice. Final projects are super easy.

Interesting list of topics to discuss but the course is defined by discussion. Some of the readings are long but you can get by without all of them. Class discussion dependent on how much others participate too. Can get boring at times but she's nice and open to discussion which was good. Best part was that class ended 10 mins early every time.

I like Ciurria a lot but I didn't find this class as interesting as I was hoping it was. You kind of just sit there and talk sporadically and then get talked to a lot, and along the way you might learn a few interesting things.

The professor includes interesting topics which display a variety of issues in the biomedical field. The course isn't formatted great and doesn't show both sides of some of the issues. Professor is nice but sometimes says controversial things. Class is very dependent on peers and if they are talkative/what they say so participation is important
Super interesting topics and professor facilitates class discussions really well. This class is what got me interested in majoring in philosophy.
Great professor, excellent lectures, amazing personality. Participate in discussions because it adds to the enjoyment of the class. Very simple class structure and easy to follow along.

The whole grade for this theo class was based on two projects and one paper, all due in the last month of the class so the lectures weren't worth much unless they interest you that day. Didn't take attendance.

Grades can be an anomaly. She is very smart and interesting, go to office hours if you need help. I enjoy her world religions class. Learned a lot from it. Attend class, for participation and lectures. However, participation is such a small portion of the grade that you don't need to.
A brilliant and dreamy wondrous soul. Listens, respects, conducts class fairly, loves and understands the topic. Extremely modest, kind, what more can I say?

She is very kind and very open minded.  I agree with the previous comment that she will always try to find something good in your answer.  It makes it easy to partake in the discussion because she never makes you feel stupid about your answer.  She orchestrates interesting discussions.  She is very helpful, as well.
So incredibly nice and leads really interesting discussions. She'll never tell you your opinion is wrong and always tries to find something good about your answer. Really knows her stuff.

I loved the subject matter, and she did fairly well for the intro course.  She's a good professor to talk to outside of class too.  Her tests were a bit challenging.  Overall, I enjoyed it and would recommend her class.

The first three reviews are off. DArcy is the worst. What a waste of a semester. This was the worst course I have taken at Wash U. I learned more in hs. What a letdown. There must be better Profs in Chemistry than this.

DArcy is terrible, awkward, nervous, and not smart. I am not sure he knows what he is saying. He cannot answer questions. I went to one of his help sessions, and he contradicted the text on numerous occasions.
D'Arcy is terrible. He cannot teach, and he often seems to not understand what he is saying or writing on the board. There is no way he can pass his own exams. Avoid! Sign up for Bleeke's section.
D'arcy is so excited to teach chemistry and to form connections with his students. Going to his office hours was really worth it, and I could tell he had a great, great passion for the subject.

D'arcy's enthusiasm for chemistry is contagious. Lecture is very organized with lots of writing on the board (with multicolored chalk!), and if you don't understand a concept in class, he's always willing to explain it in greater detail in office hours.

D'arcy is very passionate about chemistry. He is really interested in applications of topics. He is really nice, and is great to talk to individually. He can really make lecture an interesting place to be.

Funny guy; makes lectures enjoyable. He gets the whole class to participate and feel like a little community. Class is pretty light; the midterm and final are somewhat difficult but only make up 25% of your grade total. Attendance is really important bc lectures are not recorded and you learn everything he needs you to in class.

Show up, pay attention, and you'll do fine. There is a midterm, a final, a project, and weekly homework assignments (which sometimes take a lot of time). Overall a chill class, especially if u have any music background. This part of the sequence is harder since there are completely new chords and concepts but it is def more interesting than 121C.

Great lectures with lots of group examples that prepare you well for exams. Homework takes a couple hours a week but is not too difficult for 121. Couldn't ask for a better intro music theory professor.
Goat prof if u show up to class u r gonna learn and do well.
Great professor if you have never had any music theory training before.
Classical Theory I with Professor Steinbeck has been my favorite class this semester. He is incredibly kind, and runs a consistently fun and reasonable class. Strongly recommend!

Nice person, not my favorite lecturer. Readings done outside of class, and she walks through problems in class. Then we work as a group for quizzes, and the problems were hard. Lectures weren't super exciting and engaging. Required class so you'll have to take it, just beware of the amount of outside the class learning you'll have to do.

Professor Boyd was one of my favorite professors at WashU. Her fluid dynamics course was amazing. A TA once rushed through the grading process of one of my tests and gave me well below what I deserved. I showed professor Boyd my work and she was extremely caring and raised my grade. Overall awesome professor.

Ok teacher. Tests aren't too hard but the in class reading quizzes are hard. She assigns reading for homework so she barely teaches in class and just has us do questions which is okay practice. The readings can range from 20-60 pages of heavy textbook content and the reading guide is useless. Part of ur grade is also determined by your group

Dr. Boyd is one of the best professors I have ever had. She provides amazing lecture materials and reading guides. Class time was spent solving problems in groups which was useful when identifying what I needed help with. Dr. Boyd is so helpful and her expectations are reasonable, so if you put forth an honest effort, you can do well. 10/10

Dr. Boyd is amazing. She is good at explaining tricky concepts and very receptive to individual questions. She provides very thorough course materials, including reading guides and practice problems. The homework assignments are pretty reasonable as well. 10/10 would recommend.

She is mainly reasonable and wants to help you. She is a nice lady. But she is the worst professor, she records her lectures by reading off of her notes and really skips many concepts and steps. Her AI pretty much teaches 90% of material. She will drop you an hour long lectures that you need to take notes on on the same day that it is due.
She's an absolute gem of a professor, definitely among the top three I've had at WashU

Dr. Silbaugh is a great professor. Although the material is very difficult, he does a good job of teaching the basic concepts and the applications of them. The homework is very heavy, but his office hours and PST are really helpful if you're stuck on a question. His office hours for projects were really helpful, and he's overall a very nice guy.

Prof Silbaugh was a great professor. He knows that this class is extremely difficult and made sure to be very accessible and understanding, especially in office hours. Lectures sometimes feel rushed, but he makes sure to clear up any questions outside of class. Homework is very hard, but exams will raise your grade with partial credit&cheat sheet.

Professor silbaugh did a great job teaching this course. He was accessible in office hours and gave generous partial credit on exams, emphasizing understanding. Homework was graded pretty strictly, and would be pretty much impossible to complete without joining a weekend PST. Exams were fair, though challenging, but you can have a cheat sheet.

He's a solid lecturer. Despite attendance not being required, missing a class is kind of detrimental if you don't have someone to take notes and send them your way. Homework is hard, near impossible to do without PST, but fine if you are in it. Tests are infrequent and you have a cheat sheet. Interesting class (fave of sem.) and...he's lowkey hot

Professor Silbaugh is amazing--his lectures are very easy to follow and he does a great job explaining hard to understand concepts. He really cares about his students and wants them to succeed. The class is hard but was still my favorite of the semester.

Really nice guy and a pretty good lecturer too. Sometimes explanations were breezed over too much (esp. with the mathy stuff like mass transfer, "chained" heat transfer analogous to resistors in series, etc.), but via office hours and PST I was able to sort out problems. Really wants to help you!

He is the man of the department. I've had 5 classes with him already and will have the 6th class next semester. He truly cares about students' well-being and knows a lot about ChemE. He also give pretty good grades if you truly pay effort on the homework and assignment. He makes Reactions interesting and Unit Ops less stressful.

Dr. Gardner was difficult, but very good and knowledgeable. He always had time to help explain things and was very encouraging. He was my advisor and an awesome professor and mentor!!! I'm sorry that the other responders didn't get to see that side.
Hands down, worst teacher and class ever taken in the ME dept. Hard to understand, barely any notes to follow. Textbook is useful. He is retired, and hopefully will stay that way.
Kind of unapproachable.  Do your homework and go to class!!
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments

Getty is one of the silliest professors I've ever had which makes going to class sometimes seem like an adventure!  He does a good job of explaining the material and really wants all his students to do well.

Getty is the best of all of the linguistics teachers. He is very thorough and is quite a character too! I really enjoyed this class...although linguistics is a counter intuitive subject, Getty makes is fun! I highly recommend this class.

He tries, really hard. He does make mistakes and it can be confusing and it's really hard work, but I found his class really gratifying. He's obsessed with linguistics! He really likes it! And he wants you to really like it too, so even if he can be difficult, his enthusiasm rubs off. I liked him a lot.

I really enjoyed the class, but Getty could be EXTREMELY obtuse.  He would also get confused and even make mistakes on things, which was difficult to deal with, especially when you've been working on a problem set for 3 hrs.  I think he knows an awful lot but may not be the greatest prof.
No Comments
Intro linguistics is supposed to be one of the most fun and interesting classes to take during your 4 years at Wash U . . . do NOT take it with Getty!

I loved Ron's class.  I had no experience with 3D design before, but he made the process of transferring 2D thoughts into 3D designs easy by giving really good advice.  He encourages independent thinking and is very understanding of the different methods in which people approach projects.  He's amazing, one my favorite professors I've had at Wash U

Ron is one of my favorite teachers here at Washu.  If 3D is new to you, he eases you through the process smoothly, challenges you, yet cuts you some slack when you really need it.  A great guy and one of the nicest teachers here.
Ron is a total teddy bear-nice and compassionate, but he still challenges you to make great work. Taking his class was fun and interesting.
My favorite person ever! Ron Leax can say something to you in passing that changes the entire was you look at art. I feel honored to have experience learning from Ron.
Has interesting things to say but often gets off topic. Is supportive for students.

Ron is an asset to Wash U's art program.  More willing to support and council his students than anyone I've ever encountered.  This man has more knowledge of ANY other teacher I've encountered- art, science, english, etc.  And to top it off, he splits his time between his students and his work perfectly.

Easy A. The assignments are pretty long, but they are graded very leniently. He is very smart and knowledgeable in his subject area, and conversations with him are truly intellectual. There is a final paper and presentation that requires a considerable amount of effort, though. Low stress, learned a lot, and again, easy A.

One of the easiest professors at WashU. The class is lecture heavy and sometimes they can be boring, but grading only consists of ten reading assignments and the final paper. He is very knowledge, easy grader, and gets excited when people participate in class. He is easy to reach outside of class and provides help for the final paper.

Great professor! Cares about his students and the course is a great introduction into the academic field of political science. He's an expert in poli sci, and familiar with professors around the US and they're exciting work in politics. If you're interested in representation and political mechanisms, couldn't recommend more!
by far best teacher have had at this school. hws were thought provoking and interesting

Explained material clearly. Tests were incredibly easy and problem sets were hard. You'll walk away with an A and a solid understanding of basic game theory. Beware though, slow pace at the beginning of the class picks up dramatically by the last month or so.
Fox is passionate, very approachable, and wants his students to succeed.

The material is rather dense but the things you need to do to do well in the class is pretty clear cut. Write down literally everything on the board and rewrite them after class to piece together info and make sense of them. Take the time to do homework step by step and redo them before exams.

Sit in the first two rows, go to class, do homework and study for exams. If you can solve the HW problems moderately quickly, you will be fine on the exam. I suggest collaborating with friends to arrange notes, because it's impossible to collect perfect lecture notes alone.
Messy handwriting on the board but great guy. Lectures are kinda dry, but I don't think that's his fault. Looking to take classes with him again.

Not bad.  Pretty dry.  Likes teaching early morning classes, unfortunately.  Bad handwriting--make sure to sit in the first 2 or 3 rows (not joking).  Writes TONS of notes each lecture; everything makes sense but it's hard to comprehend everything while trying to copy it all down.  Exams are fairly difficult--lots of definitions and derivations.

He is very dry and probably will put you to sleep. He also writes too illegibly and too small to read his notes if you aren't in the first two rows. However you have to pay attention because he tests definitions of terms he writes on the board. Steer clear if you can.
No Comments

He knows his stuff and helps you to achieve your potential. Not the most accommodating on mental health issues, but I'm attributing part of that to the class being strict and part of the commdes major. This class was hell but John helped.
nice guy, has experience and a lot to say, but crits are kind of weak. kind of average overall.

Hendrix is the best for Drawing 2 at WashU. Interesting projects, interesting guy. It's great to have a professor whose work is featured in Illustration Now, etc. Pictures for Communication = best class ever. Almost. Chill guy, tough grader, knows his****. And as has been previously stated, HOT. I almost picked my major so he'd be my advisor.

I come to class just cause he's hot, but he knows what he's talking about. Dresses nicely, funny, talks in a lot of metaphors and it's hard to not take him seriously. Nice guy and gets back to you when you miss class. Really emphasizes the use of a sharpie marker, and millions of sketches. The comic book we read was fun.
Super hot. kinda haughty. good prof, dresses well, thinks the students are idots but that we all have potential

Interesting projects, never predictable. Lots of drawing from life. If you really need training in the accuracy department, or hate pen and ink, or want your projects to look like the work the students in mary borgman's class do, don't take Hendrix. If you've progressed beyond that level, and want to challenge your creativity, Hendrix is the man.
Great professor, great guy. Tries his best to make the class interesting. If you take 318, be ready for spherical cow day.
It's really refreshing when someone genuinely cares about what they're teaching. Kater's lectures were always something I looked forward to! Great guy, rewarding class. Would take it again.

Wonderful lecturer who is fun and relatable. Does a great job in helping majors through what is normally their first advanced course. While quantum is necessarily rigorous, he makes sure to keep the big picture in mind. In the end, he really just wants to share his beautiful quantum world with us, and will likely become your friend in the process.

Kater puts a lot of effort into his lectures,notes & videos. Engaging & it doesnt feels likes hes talking at you. Occasional demos, fun videos &donut holes. Encourages discussion & questions. Friendly & clearly loves what he does. Office hrs a must. V willing to help. Fave class, fave prof @ washu. Im gonna miss his cute lil face sO MUCH

Kater's class was one of the most difficult I have taken, but I loved every second of it. The material is very tough, but Kater makes sure you learn as much as is possible. When you look back on the semester you will be amazed by how much you learned. Make sure you go to his office hours for hw though.

Professor Murch was so awesome I went out of my way to make a ratemyprofessor page for him just to communicate how great he was! Informative, fun lectures, very helpful (when I missed my midterm he let me do a makeup test for it). Take this class!
Not a typical academic professor. Started teaching after working for many years, which brought the "real world" to the class. Much more fun, and less dry than most engineering professors.

The type of engineering professor I would expect at a top 20 university.  Clear, detailed, challenging, and helpful when you need it.  He stayed long after class sometimes to help us make sure we were ready for the homework.  The tests were always very tough, but if you did the work, you would get the grade.  Not a BS course.  I learned much.

Professor Nobs made a tough course understandable.  He was always prepared with a full lecture.  I had to work my butt off, but I learned much more than I expected at the beginning of the course.  I am actually using what I learned in this course.
Very challenging course. Would have been much more difficult without the professOr.

Extremely arrogant, thinks he knows a lot and thinks the class knows nothing. Thinks he is funny. Pretty annoying. There is no way to make the class hard, so tries to make tests as complicated as possible.
friendly, delivers well-organized lectures

Ruth Clark is so amazing. I wish she could be my grandma. She's pretty cut and dry, but also super funny. She constantly cracks subtle jokes in lecture. She's super accessible outside of class, so go to office hours for help or for additional laughs. She has great video lectures and problem sets. Only four 50 question multiple choice exams.

Like everyone else we moved to recorded lectures a couple weeks ago and.... I think if I could put Dr. Clark on 2.0x speed in real life I might. Her normal voice is pretty great though. Funny prof. Lots of material, but her handouts + course objectives cover pretty much everything. 4 exams, 25% each of your total grade. No homework is nice.

Incompetent professor. After 20+ years you would assume that this professor would know how to teach this course but her lectures disproportionately focus on easy material and skim over the most challenging material. Reading the textbook and watching KA videos are necessary to piece together her badly disorganized notes. Stay away from this class.

Probably the best teacher I've ever had. Lectures with interesting material presented well, with jokes interspersed to keep them fun, and definitely the most fair grading system I've taken. Can't recommend enough!

Good sense of humor, entertaining lecturer, but taking notes in class is stressful because she says a lot of information that she does not write down. You may have to rely heavily on the textbook like I did, but that should be enough for an A. The lectures are always confusing, but the exams are straightforward.
Interesting material. Straightforward exams. Well organized.

I have nothing against Wendl as a person but I didn't enjoy his class. Fluid Mechanics is already hard, but we had no way of tracking our grade. The grade was subjective to his belief of what the grade was. He curved grades and the canvas page didn't reflect what our true grades were. The class is too difficult to not be able to track progress.

I aspire to be like Dr. Wendl one day. He truly cares about both his students, and the content of his classes. He has clearly gone above and beyond in creating his Fluid Mechanics and Heat Transfer classes. He is very good at teaching and making students comfortable with new material.
I took Fluid Mechanics with him. Simply, the most difficult class in Engineering made fun, easy and understandable.

Incredible professor who takes teaching seriously. His ability to proof theorems and equations is unparalleled. Homework is difficult, but applicable to engineering. Wendl is also approachable and genuinely interested in his field of expertise. I'd recommend him to anyone.
He is a really good professor, who cares alot about his students and truly wants them to succed.  He is also a good teacher.

Wendl is the most in depth and inteligent professot i have had. His answers to any questions are well thought out & clear. I have not had anyone who could explain anything so clearly. Not only will he teach teh material, he will show you the method of thinking that goes along with it. I have not learned this much in a course-ever. highly recommend.

Jay was a good lecturer and obviously very enthusiastic about the course. If only enthusiasm reflected competency! The syllabus was not followed at all. He was extremely hard to reach via email or phone: it took over a month for him to actually respond to my requests for an exam regrade. The exams were not reflective of lecture materials.

Prof Turner has a super interesting job that he talks about a lot because it has to do with the class. I find it really inspirational because this is the first EECE course that has allowed me to see the potential of what I could do post-graduation. Homework is hard but he's *INCREDIBLY* helpful during office hours and you can email him too.

Professor Turner, as always in 311, gives very interesting lectures and really cares about students. His assignment is a lot but very clear expectations. I just want to give an appreciation for him after I graduate. He hesitates to use technology though. I believe the in person class might be hard.

Dr. Turner is a cool guy at the top of his field, but he was a very difficult professor this semester. He teaches Intro ChemE alongside Dr. Giammar and Turner is widely known as the more difficult professor. He writes notoriously unclear test questions that the entire class struggled to understand.
Class materials are attractive and inspiring. Jay is really a helpful and talented teacher. Though the workload is pretty big.
awesome teacher, he is available to answer questions, class is not boring at all.  take classes with this teacher, you'll like it if you're an engineer

Yasha is very smart but a god-awful lecturer. He goes way too fast and covers only super simple examples in class. Going to lecture does not prepare you at all to do the homework sets. In class, when his style of lecture was criticized, he blamed the students for not reading the textbook. I would avoid him at all costs.

Yasha is definitely a math genius, but that means that he goes way too fast in class and assumes that his students have background knowledge that they don't have. The way he explains things makes no sense: I've never been so confused for so long in a class before. I had to switch professors so that I wouldn't fail out of calc II.

Assigned a ton of homework, the most of any of my classes. He's a math genius but that just makes him awful at explaining concepts in class. Exams were really hard and got more difficult as the year went along. Not much sympathy for his students either.

Not good. Hard to follow in class, and assumed we knew things that we didn't know. Homework was absurdly long. Spent so long trying to complete the homework that my studying for the tests was cut short, and so I did not do as well. Diffeq is hard enough with a good professor - Yasha just made it harder.

Took DiffEq with Yasha this past semester, exams were mainly based on practice exams but the last two were way more difficult than the first two. Gives WAY too much homework (both written and WebWork), homework sometimes took 12-15 hours to complete every week. Office hours very helpful

Blankenship is probably the worst professor in the bio department. This course taught me nothing. It restated everything i learned in the intro bio courses with a far more ridiculous grading system. You would lose points if your answers were right but didnt match up with what the professor had in his key. Absolutely ridiculous.

It was difficult simply because the TA's knocked off as many points as they possibly could. The material wasn't difficult to understand but the TA's were very picky. The professor was extremely nice and wanted to help but was often very busy.

Grading policies aren't transparent in the slightest.  You just kind of get a grade in the end, and hope that you are far enough above the median to get in the A range.  Decent lecturer, just make sure to memorize every detail on every slide.  Sounds like a pain, but you'll get an A if you do it.

Unfortunately, this class is taught much more as a biology course (massive amounts of route memorization,) as opposed to a conceptual course. That being said, if memorization is a strength, this class shouldn't be too difficult. Note: 2/3 of the class get some type of A or B, so grading is fairly lenient.

Teacher does not understand math, and can not teach biochemistry. Needs to learn what normalization means, and needs to learn what is relevant and irrelevant. The guy wastes some much time taking about how cool a picture is and barely talks about the important topics. Anybody could have done a better job than him.

Awful teacher. Forgets his class notes, doesn't explain any of his mathematical steps, and skips all of the basics. He only goes through easy T/F questions in class that are completely irrelevant to the tests. I may as well be learning biology in class and I would still do the exact same on an exam. If you haven't taken physics before, good luck.
Professor Seidel is great
His course is just beyond hard. It is impossible to get a solid A for any of your homework. In addition, the cutoff score for A is "95" Be careful

Professor Seidel is one of the best in the department. While the class is challenging, every result is arrived at carefully and mathematically, and the material is presented clearly. The course is well motivated, and it's central ideas are evident. Also, fascinating subject material.

Competent and organized, his lectures are very clear, structured, and thorough. His high intelligence is evident, as he never misspeaks or pauses; he is like a robot. He does seem to be interested in the subject and his students, but he is very serious and slightly cold and intimidating. Nevertheless, he answers questions willingly when approached.

If you want to maintain your sanity, never take Dr. Haglund. She assigns dozens of short answer questions that each are from the hundreds of pages of reading that you have to do a week and you have maybe an hour to do it. And that is every single week. She teaches absolutely nothing and you have to teach yourself. You have to teach yourself.
basically reads the textbook for an hour and a half

There is nothing wrong with Prof. Haglund except that she is a new professor, and therefore, somewhat inexperienced. If you go to her for extra help she will be more than helpful to you and may even give points back. The lectures are a little long and boring, but if you pay attention you'll do fine on the pop quizzes. Do take her class.

Very very very boring, and lectures are just a waste of time (except for the rare pop quizzes). You're best off doing the readings in advance and coming to class with 15 minutes left every day-- even if you pay attention you still might fail the pop quiz because she asks very specific questions that involve rote memory.

Haglund's International Politics class has probably been the worst class I've taken at Wash U. She literally reads from the slides. That is it. Don't try to skip class though because the slides she uploads have the most random blanks in them that you can only get from class. This was the most boring and frustrating class of the semester.

I loved everything about this class! I suggest you take the class if you like the topic because if you don't then the readings can seem boring. The professor was so nice and honestly cared about what she was teaching and was definitely accessible outside of class and easy to talk to. She was very clear about instructions and expectations. 10/10!

Dr. Baxter is a great professor and who clearly has a passion for philosophy and tries to spread and develop that in her own students. She had a clear grading rubric for each essay and assignment and was always available during office hours to look over rough drafts and any questions. As with anything, put in the work and you'll do well.

I don't really get her. She assigns impossibly large readings. 85% of the final grade is determined by 3 papers, which she grades extremely harshly. Sure some teachers grade harshly, but Prof. Baxter doesn't explain why you get the grade you get. Often if you don't write in support of an opinion held be her, the grade will reflect it. I'd avoid.
1. Do not disagree or expect a bad grade on papers 2. Very boring lectures!!!!!! 3. Repetitive 4. Grading criteria was very vague 5. Not a lot of points make up your grade
Grading, due dates, expectations were incredibly unclear. Class was very boring. She often did not even understand our readings or what was on her own slides. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS!
Very caring for students. The lecture materials are interesting and although there are a lot to memorize, gradings are not tough at all.
Prof. Aravecchia is my favorite professor in the art history department. Highly recommend his classes! If you put in the work, the classes are not difficult.
prof aravecchia is super enthusiastic! there's a lot of material to cover in his classes, but it's all interesting and he's a really engaging lecturer

Prof. Aravecchia is awesome. He is very knowledgeable in both Archaeology and Classics, especially in Egyptian matters. His Reception of Egypt in the Greco-Roman Antiquity class is quite interdisciplinary. The discussion on his class is quite active and inspiring, and he pushes students to think about readings.
Prof. Aravecchia is very friendly and fun. Hes also passionate about teaching!

Have really enjoyed taking his class. Super passionate about Egyptian art and throws a lot of information at you, but you really only need to memorize a few things to do well on exams. Will grade so that his students succeed
Very nice and smart professor!

Really cares what students get out of the course. He has clear objectives about what to teach, and we achieved them by the end of the course. Could improve communication of some definitions and relies on you doing a lot of "recommended" reading which is not optional in order to well. Better than other algorithms courses at conveying deep material.

Baruah was fantastic at explaining complex concepts. Many of the early WashU classes (247 in particular) are very deficient in what they teach. Baruah was great at figuring out exactly what Cytron missed in 247 and explaining it clearly.

He assumes that everyone has seen the material before and therefore doesn't explain many things. I'm not sure how great of a teacher he is...I learned more from doing the homework and watching YouTube than his lectures. Exams are really difficult, but he ended up dropping one of them by making the final optional. Textbook practice problems help.

He is very nice and tries to explain things very thoroughly during lecture. However, he sometimes assumes students already have knowledge of certain topics and breezes over explanations. He goes through examples of proofs during lecture (which are not included in lecture slides), so lecture attendance is important. YouTube is your friend.
He's a nice person, doesnt give feedback on writing, easy class!
Super nice and laid back guy.  Definitely made writing feel approachable regardless of your previous ability.

Professor Austin is a caring teacher, but we received no feedback on our work for the class. It was nice to earn good grades for putting in the effort, but I was hoping for more substantial feedback on my work. We also didn't really discuss readings and often jumped around randomly doing things that weren't in the syllabus.

Professor Austin is really supportive and cares about his students. College writing is not easy, and I earned a pretty low grade at the beginning of this semester. However, Prof. Austin is always there to help you. I just attend the office hour to talk about my essay, and then I did well in the end. Just take college writing with this professor.

Ron is the best. i hadn't had a good writing teacher until i had him for college writing. he offered to help me outside of class and reached out to me multiple times asking how each paper was going. he really cares about his students and he's also not very strict with deadlines. overall, a chill guy with interesting things to say about each reading

Bapi was a complete disaster. Didn't explain the final project to us until the last week of class, then disappeared completely during finals week and never responded to emails, even though we had the project AND a final. Probably one of the worst professors I've had at wash u.

Class was very easy if you had prior programming experience. Gupta taught pertty well, and had a fun personality. He answered questions well. He overslept through class on 4 or so occasions, which was amusing.  Class not mandatory, but a lot of people stopped coming and he locked his (online) code out of annoyance. So it's probably better to come.
No Comments
Class was a good concept, but he was unprepared for most lectures, and much of our time was subsequently wasted literally doing nothing but surfing the net.
This guy is the MAN.  Very clear explanations, very accessable outside of class, very willing to help.  In terms of personality, probably the coolest teacher I've had here.
Really enjoyed this class, the professor is very knowledgeable and passionate about her work. Class is an hour and a half so be ready to sit there for a while.
Not the most engaging teacher, talks in monotone.
Wonderful teacher.  Doesn't waste time.  Learned tons.  Couldn't be more clear in lectures.  Very fair grader.  Expects just the right amount from her students.  Highly recommended.
A real asset to the university, the friendliest and most caring person in the school
No Comments
TAKE THIS CLASS. you WILL get an A. He is the sweetest man to ever have walked WashU's campus
Really great professor.

Prof. Merbaum is wonderful, ESPECIALLY for this course. He is incredibly interested in helping and teaching his students both academic material and lifetime psychological skills, even if it means spending many extra office hours.
No Comments
No Comments
Extremely talented professor.  I wish I could take more classes by him.  Thanks.
Best part of the class when I took it was the TA.  Gus is HOT
Worst teacher in the college
Worst teacher in the department. Period.
No Comments

Young-Shin cares about the students in her class more than most professors I know. It takes time to get used to her unusual accent. Once you do, though, she is very helpful and knowledgeable in the field of environmental nanochemistry. Young-Shin is also awesome in that she replies to your e-mails at 12 or 1 AM.
great class! had to get used to her but she ended up being a great prof

The most incoherent professor I have encountered. I can deal with accent, but it felt more like she doesn't understand English. The TAs usually have better ways to solve a problem. Three absents and you fail, and does not accept late homework even by a minute. Class are extremely boring, sometimes she even forgets how to do a problem in class.

Takes what should be an interesting, important class for chemical engineers and turns it into a boring, tedious, and convoluted experience. She has a nack for making relatively simple concepts difficult to understand.

This is bad. She cannot speak english - it's not just a matter of accent; she actually can't put a coherent sentence together. Can't answer students' questions becuase she misinterprets what they're asking. Class will only make you more confused, but she forces you to come by taking attendance and giving pop quizzes.
Worst professor ever. Avoid her at all cost. She makes my college life 10 times worse. Very condescending. Not willing to help. Wish the school fire her immediately.

I agree that she is condescending and makes whoever that talks to her during office hours extremely uncomfortable. One time I asked her a question in the office and after I finished talking, she said "you tell me" and started playing with her pens as if I weren't there. I wish the school could fire her seriously. Avoid her at all cost.

She doesn't know what she is talking about in class, seriously. You get nothing out of her class unless you read the textbook and teach yourself. I agree with an earlier post that she is condescending, and it makes anyone who goes to her office hours very uncomfortable. In general she is very mean to the students. Avoid her AT ALL COST, no kidding.
I agree with all the comments of the previous poster.

Provides almost no assistance out of class; makes no effort to reach out to students.  When classmates had serious issues, she stopped allowing office appointments and stuck to her insufficient office hours, during which huge lines of panicked students choked the hallway to her office.  She is condescending when help is needed (often).

A good professor overall - he made his powerpoints accessible to students and was very clear in his lectures. However, he often seemed like he was trying to say things to fit the mold for the professor he thought he should be, but couldn't back up what he says, i.e., he says he's always available, but doesn't help when you go and see him.
worst professor Ive had in a while. could not back up his facts or options. Very one sided. Pretty easy tests but answers need to have exactly what he wants or he will take off points

I took Dr. Locker's course and felt that it was the biggest waste of time out of any course I've ever taken at WashU. He is generally clueless about specific details that he teaches and I got the impression that he doesn't care about his students. He teaches students as if they don't like to learn. It's very brute-force/rote memorization based.

uninteresting material that was repeated over and over. never felt like we were going anywhere. too many assignments, a lot of them were busywork. generally felt like the class was a waste of my time, and he didn't impress me as an anthropologist... lots of broad sweeping statements that bugged me.
Terrific professor.
Nice guy who gives good feedback. Absurd amount of work outside class, though.

He is undeniably good at what he does, having shown some of his work in class.  However, His teaching methods are god-awful. I dropped that class after 4 weeks. In a drawing principles class, he had us recreating old paintings and sculpting stuff.  The workload he assigns is huge. The way he taught it always felt like I was doing  something wrong

Makes me hate drawing & want to switch out of art school( & i love art). So much work, that you actually dont even improve,cause you try to just finish the assignments. Spend all my time on his homework & it doesnt even pay off in terms of good grades. Cant focus on work for other classes. Good feedback if asked. Switch out while you can.

His drawing class is the hardest class I have taken. The workload borders on unreasonable, and I spent many nights up past 3AM to finish homework. Assigns work as if his class is the only one you take. He gives feedback if you ask for it and can be helpful. Picky in how he wants everything done. Two midterms and two finals. Picks pricey supplies.

Since this class was taken for an elective course, the skill levels varied a lot, making it difficult to grade subjectively; but if effort is put in then an A is basically guaranteed. Prof Cox is really great at what he does (painting, go look him up), so he'll be able to give you great feedback on your paintings without being too critical.
Great Teacher.

Probably my favorite studio I've taken at wash u so for. Belinda is an excellent teacher who truly encourages free thinking and rewards innovation and hard work. As far as sophomore core goes I HIGHLY recommend you try to get into this class. Its a lot of fun; you can get by with little work, but its worth it to put in the extra time and do well.
One of the best teachers I've had, and an amazing person!
Belinda is an amazing teacher. She is a perfectionist and is very demanding, but I learned so much from her course and improved a great deal.
Wonderful teacher, light work load, though she does expect you to do a lot of sketchwork and show dedication in class. Very understanding. If you can, take her drawing class!

For a 3 credit class, she expects about 20-30 hours of work be put in outside of class (her words), which seems totally bogus if you're not into the class. If you're committed though it makes sense, and she'll whip you into shape for sure. Her students usually end up with the best work but she hardcore weeds out the weaker, less committed students.

She'll probably have you end with the best portfolio but she will wreck you along the way; she expects A LOT and for the amount of credit hours the class is worth, it might be wise to consider a different professor if possible. It depends what you're looking for in the class but if you're not 100% committed it won't seem worth it
This course is the most inspiring course I've ever had since elementary school. If you enjoy the process of designing and re-designing, then definitely take her course.
ELISA MUST BE AN ANGEL SENT BY GOD!!!!!!!!  In my life, I am most thankful for those two people: 1. Elisa 2. My Mom  Anyways, NO ELISA, NO LIFE.
Elisa is a god. A truly inspirational teacher, mentor, and friend. This class radically changed the course of my life.
Very polite. I love this guy.

Provides no help during critiques, and just speaks out the ass about random bull5hit when you ask for help. So much useless 5hit comes out his mouth, he probably was a failed poet. I'm convinced he knows nothing about architecture and is just a cad monkey corporate slave that somehow managed to get a teaching position.

I was really looking forward to this class, but with covid, he didn't really do a great job of actually teaching especially in large groups. Going in for one on ones helped, but still, for the amount of effort I put in, I didn't feel like it was worth it. Not a hard class, but I would probably not recommend it to others.
don't take this class, the professor is super cap and says the vaguest things -- you will get 100s and assignments and he will still say bad things

I came in to this class hearing that ninety percent of students who dropped out of architecture were from his section and now I believe it. He gives no constructive criticism but instead gives us vague and useless opinions.

The sweetest, most enthusiastic professor ever. Her redesign of 2960 evidently required enormous amounts of work, and she handled the new semester extremely well. Her teaching style is good, but the class structure is great. As long as you consistently actively engage with the material, it is very possible to do well in this course.
She's so sweet and amazing.
Elise is awesome, very accessible. Give very deep explanations.

Dr. Shannon really cares her students and explains concepts clearly during lab sections. Biology department should let Dr. Shannon to be the lecturer for Bio2970 rather than Dr. Barton whose brain often stops working. I would recommend others taking lab section with Dr. Shannon for Bio2970.
Very sweet prof.
She is extremely nice and assigns a ton of reading. I would highly recommend her and her courses to any WashU student.

Professor was very caring and nice. Be prepare to read 50 pages text before each class because this class is very discussion heavy. I learned a lot about the culture and different perspectives through this course. Very interesting points of views were discussed.

Very caring and inspiring. Topics are highly relevant and she is dedicated to involving everyone in conversation. Extremely validating, which encourages students to participate. Didn't love the readings she chose, and she assigned a ton of reading for homework. However, she integrates it well in class. Extremely personable, take a class with her!

Such a wonderful professor, I'm so glad I had her. Strongly would recommend taking any class she offers, she makes every student feel respected and teaches you a lot without making her class ridiculously stressful. You should definitely read and participate but if you have an off day it's nbd. Can't say enough good things about her.
Truly the nicest professor ever and makes you so comfortable discussing difficult topics.

I loved Dr. HC in the GCP class.  She is an incredibly strong woman, and clearly very passionate about Ireland and gender studies. Amy expects a lot as she should, but is available to help and clarify if needed.  I highly recommend getting to know Dr. HC if you have the opportunity.

Gender/IR/Irish History are clearly Dr. HC's passions and she is a phenomenal instructor, even though I had no experience in any field. Readings were often tough and class structure varied widely, but this was outlined in the syllabus and was to be expected. Dr. HC was always willing to explain concepts more and justify grades. HIGHLY recommend.

Dr. HC cares so much about both her students and gender studies. Her passion always shines through. I loved the course content and her, although the reading was way too much and the class was often a bit unorganized. Felt like a little community. Definitely have to put the effort in, but I learned a lot. She is available and talking to her helps.

I had Heath-Carpentier for Gender Analysis in IAS, which taught me so much. Heath-Carpentier is caring and open-minded, but I felt she was disorganized. Her lectures were hard to follow and she graded harshly without clear feedback. Thankfully there were opportunities to gain back points so I was able to receive an A. I recommend the class!
Amy HC is a lot of work and very theory focused but when you show up to class she explains things. Best prof I have had thus far. Cannot recommend enough. Also insanely good career advice!

I cannot begin to explain my hatred for this man.  He is a terrible teacher. He doesn't explain things well and the only I learned from this class is that it's okay to never go to lecture but read the recommended articles and still get a 98 on the midterm. Just proof that you don't need to listen to his BS to pass the class.

A lot of students don't like Klein because of his personality, and the fact that he spends much of his time setting up the context in which the artwork was created, but I found his technique both engaging and thorough.  Not for students looking for quick sound bytes of information that they can easily regurgitate in an exam.

I've taken 2 courses with Prof Klein and I've enjoyed both. The previous comments are unfair, though his style requires some getting used to. He tries very hard to phrase things in the most precise way possible, which might initially seem verbose but they were helpful for my own writing. Knowledgeable and approachable.

The most pretentious professor I have ever met, and undeservedly so; highly unwilling to help and unwilling to admit that he is anything less than perfect. Avoid this class if possible. Talks in monotone, makes things up.
ugh Intro to Modern Art was horrible

Professor Caddel often fails to give feedback on weekly assignments. His style of teaching is very stringent and does not allow for much creativity. He is too focused on his academic ways, which prevents students from expressing their ideas and discussing world events. He seems like a nice guy but he is unapproachable and talks down to students.

Professor Caddel is most valuable because of his decades of experience in the State Department. He provides personal anecdotes that make the class material more meaningful. In terms of assignments, quizzes and exams, they are largely based on doing the reading. They can be a little boring, but are also fairly easy.
Lectures could be more helpful with the main policy project.
so boring, lectures don't make sense with what we were learning

Dr. Caddel has worked as an attorney and a diplomat in the State Department (in Israel and Afghanistan, I believe) and he's serious about preparing students for careers in these fields. Class sessions are active, with lots of simulations and group projects. Very different experience from most Wash U classes and one I highly recommend.
I really like Lisa, she is super helpful and gives great feedback. I've had her for Capstone and Printmaking
Not exactly understanding of everyone's visions and problems.

Lisa is really sympathetic, understanding, and accommodating to each student's individual interests in terms of the materials she recommends (i.e. she doesn't push collagraphs as hard as some of her colleagues do). And her work is interesting.
Don't take this class if you want to be... taught. Or inspired. Everyone skips.
She is very rigid and kind of intimidating. I was very interested in the class but she hasn't made it something I enjoy too much.
Would recommend everyone take Fin340 with Todd

Todd is amazing! He explains difficult concepts without assuming any prior knowledge, making the class easy to learn from regardless of your academic background. Super funny and personable -- get ready for some swear words and funny stories. He is straightforward and descriptive with assignment requirements. Awesome class

Took class when he was transitioning to online hw/textbook use.  He didn't really know what he was doing because of the transition.  Funny and friendly, but grading was unfair and uncertain due to already mentioned website use.  Difficult material but he makes it bearable.
Overall a fantastic teacher.  Makes class interesting and engaging.  The class is hard and takes time, but is great.  I definitely think it is because of him.
Great guy, great lecturer. Very entertaining in class. He makes Finance 340 interesting and keeps people engaged. He is very helpful during office hours.

One of the finest professors at Washington University Law School.  He has high standards and you be better be prepared in his class.  He expects you to be prepared because if you are not you will be embarrassed.  He gives you a song and dance on the first day about class preparation.  He makes it very clear to you.  Exam was very fair.  Good guy.
Very helpful prof. Best I had in law school.

I had Legomsky for Immigration and Crim Law.  He's a very nice professor who is always willing to spend time with you outside the classroom to answer questions.  He's very particular but his tests are straightforward and fairly easy.  Mention statutory interpretation!
very enthusiastic, very nice, but he expects a lot from his students

Such a great professor. Keeps class interesting and fun. The material covered in class is great for those entering the bio or science world and has helped me in other classes. Super easy but you still learn quite a bit. Take his class if possible.

Great professor, he made the material very interesting, approachable, and applicable. The homeworks and exams were reasonable, although the grading scale is slightly harsher than average (93 is an A-). Still a great class to take. Work included 1 homework per class, 3 midterms (no final), and one short group project (mainly working by yourself)

I happen to love etymology, partly because it lets you easily remember or outright guess the meanings of even the most obscure words you might come across. That said, I think Platte is the shiiiit--he's highly engaging, helpful, and understanding. If you go to class and keep up with the easy, short (yet frequent) homeworks, you should do great.
Really interesting professor and the class has practical use. No final and the exams aren't bad at all. So glad I took this class

He's not a bad professor, but he also isn't exceptional. His lectures can be quite monotone, but the topics covered were interesting. I really disliked the class, but found the professors to be fine. Wouldn't recommend the class, but if there's a class you want to take that is taught by Dr. Williams, go for it.

I felt like lectures were really hard to get through, and it wasn't very clear what he wanted us to learn sometimes. It was hard to read his handwriting (both on the PowerPoint and the chalkboard). There were no breaks during lecture, and because it was hard to understand, I often felt lost and frustrated after. The HW helped though.

Brent isn't my favorite professor, but I'm also not a huge science person. His lectures were kinda boring, but he seems like a nice guy. I was in Earth's Future which mostly had guest lecturers. I learned a lot and enjoyed the class, but Brent wasn't the most engaging. If you love science/ engineering, disregard this comment.

The difficulty of the ChemE major really started to pick up from this class, but he tried pretty hard to help us. He was so cool everybody loved him. Workload is not low but manageable. It sucks to have the class at 8:30, but lectures definitely help with homework and exams. Just make sure you know how to do the homework sets, exams were similar.
She is a great prof. I really enjoyed her enthusiasm for the material.
One of my favorite teachers, and inspired me to practice.

Quite random and scatterbrained! In class, very hard to follow her train of thought. Seemed knowledgeable but unable to teach it to others. Good luck trying to follow her. Out of class, extremely helpful, esp for exams. Tells you what was wrong with your test bit by bit. Open door policy! forreal....she MAKES you come talk to her

Everything the admission office tells you turns out to be true with Prof Goldwasser. Some don't like her because she has her opinions and makes them known and might seem intolerant of other views. But really she presents the information without playing hide the ball and she has a truly open door policy. Extremely helpful, especially with exam advic

Shalev is a great guy - willing to help and pretty funny. However, it was sometimes hard to understand what he meant and he made it somewhat intimidating to ask questions. If you read the book and go to class it's not hard at all.

Prof Shalev makes accounting interesting and enjoyable --after all, it's not rocket science, as he puts it. He tries very hard to make sure students comprehend the material and takes our evaluation/suggestions seriously. Come to class prepared, or you'll be lost. Overall, highly recommended. Prof Shalev is talented and has a great sense of humor :)

This class=living hell. it is so boring in class it will seem as though you do not need to read. READ you must READ class is pretty useless but he counts your attendance and participation for 7 points. I suggest reading thoroughly and studying for quizzes. have a friend sign you into class
run away as fast as you can
Great instructor, loved class, truly cares about students.
Great instructor, played music, relaxed atmosphere, made lab fun.
Lab portion of the class, made science enjoyable again.
Required course, odd but funny sense of humor.
Amy is really devoted to the class and I'm deeply moved by her attitude. She can explain the messy tax laws well. However, the burden is still heavy.
It's pity that she is an adjunct lecturer! She is knowledgable and really devoted in teaching tax. Using plain English, she can make tax law easy to understand.

She is not capable to explain the materials she wants to cover in class. Very few examples and the way she put the quiz questions was just too hard and tricky. She did not convey the important points clearly in class.

Some professors teach students how to use tax software, but Amy doesn't believe in teaching students how to actually fill out forms. Surprisingly not that much to memorize as I expected, but still requires a lot of studying in order to pass the quizzes. She also allowed an option not to take the final, but the final will mostly improve your grade.

He may be the smartest guy you ever get to take a class with. Don't miss that opportunity. Not too tough a grader and gives very helpful feedback.
