He's leaving Wash U after this semester and is a huge loss for the history department. Great lecturer, brilliant guy, demands a high level of thinking and analysis but is very fair. You'll learn a ton.

He is a GREAT teacher, one of the best I've had here. He is a pretty forgiving grader. He is really an expert on his subject and does a good job of making it clear, even when the material is a bit intimidating. He is extremely helpful outside of class and is also super nice. Take his class!

Absolutely brilliant professor. Lectures are very clear and well organized, if occasionally a bit dry. He has a good sense of humor and and is generally an interesting lecturer. Not too tough a grader and gives the most helpful feedback on essays I've ever gotten. Take his class.

Fabulous lecturer and guides discussion very well. He's a little intimidating because you get the sense that he knows EVERYTHING, but he's a forgiving grader. Comes off as soft-spoken and rather proper at first, but he has a very dry sense of humor that comes out at the most random times. He's awesome.
Terrific guy!
With a spirit that will not die...
oh the humanity!
No Comments
Great teacher! He explains everything clearly and solves tons of example problems with you. He's really helpful and considerate.

Great lecturer, good humor in class. Enjoyed every aspect of the class. The only thing that kinda bothers me is that the Piazza response time is really slow and sometimes he just doesn't respond at all.

Ben Moseley is delightful in every way. He clearly cares about whether his students understand what he's saying, and is happy to reiterate a point until people get it. Clearly loves math. Usually very enthusiastic with lots of gesturing and smiling, so his lectures aren't dull. Also, he provides a lot of opportunities to do well (easy quizzes/hw)
Nice, cool guy. Explains clearly everything he says. Gives quizzes every week.
She cares about the students by relating the materials to the languages that the students are good at. She even did extra research for my class. It was my honor to take her class.

Something to say about workload. She does assign online homework every week and give handouts, however that doesn't add more workload; on the contrary, her handouts and class materials are so carefully and purposefully prepared so that completing them actually saves you a good amount of time going randomly through the textbook and getting lost.

she's super sweet and gives really good explanations; isn't afraid to admit when she isn't sure about something; easy to pay attention because lectures are interesting; you can tell she loves what she does

great professor, made intro to linguistics super easy. was readily available during office hours, super passionate about linguistics and her smile is infectious-- would definitely take a class from her again!

If you don't answer at least 2 questions a class, you will fail. Let's guess if there are 50 students in the class, each question takes 2 minutes to answer, if all students participate, he doesn't need to lecture at all! What an easy way to teach! He gives the worst exam and expect you to know everything, even things he never taught before.

Enjoyed going to classes. The assignments are tough mainly because they're usually based on real life data, making things not clear; the professor asks students to make assumptions and move on if stuck. But if you do go through all these, you will learn a lot. Go to classes and ask questions. It is where the professor actually gives you information

Extremely knowledge/intelligent. Puts a lot of work into preparing lectures and the class in general. The weekly problem sets are hard and will probably take a long time. Learned so much finance it's ridiculous. Teaches interesting and useful topics like Monte Carlo Simulation. Can't say enough praise.

Professor Manela isn't easy, but he's very upfront with his expectations. Very approachable for help. Surprisingly funny at times. Not an easy class, but doable. Come prepared and he'll get you where you need to go.

Kind and funny. Class time is him going through example problems, all the learning is done through textbook readings asynchronously. Clear, not super difficult. TA'd for him and he was nice to work with.

Lake is a great instructor/lecturer. Lake cares about his students and makes sure that everyone understands what is going on as we solve example problems step-by-step in class. Lake always brings a positive attitude to class and I appreciate his sense of humor. He also brought us doughnuts and lets us pick the pen color! #GalaxyPen
He is soooooo nice!!!! We can choose pen color every class! The lecture is clearly and he is a really nice teacher that don't want any one gets a B.
amazing professor that genuinely cares about his students, the content can definitely be challenging but he makes it the easiest it can possibly be
Great professor. Theory lectures can get complex/tricky, but the course content as a whole is fairly understandable.
Very good at breaking info down into bite-sized pieces. Also likes to give lots of one-on-one feedback.

I'm a writer and thought this class would be a breeze. Not so! He is not clear enough with his instructions and there are so many rules for the research paper at the end of the semester I was extremely frustrated and barely got it done the way he wanted it. Not clear enough as to what he wants / doesn't give us enough freedom to write what we want!

Andrew is a good teacher if you like freedom.  He doesn't give much direction and unless you've impressed him with your writing, he's very lenient.  For those he prefers, he grades on a much more difficult scale!!

(Grad student lecturer) Andrew is uptight in the beginning of class but he loosens up twds the end. You don't do a lot of writing in class and he didn't give enough prompts-- you're kind of on your own for coming up w stories/ideas. But there's not a lot of reading, unlike other Fic Wrkshps, so I rec the class w him even though he's not the best.

Monika is the BEST!! I've taken 2 classes with her and loved both. If you're very particular about what kind of art you want to make, this may not be for you, but if you want a weird, fun, art school journey into the bizarre, take her class!! She is so sweet and funny, and her class always brightens my day. Easy grader

Monika is very sweet and funny. She goes out of her way to make sure you have all the help you need. Her classes are not very pedagogically oriented, the class is more like having a mentorship with Monika with other students around
I took her 2d Design class and she taught us film for the whole semester. Basically you spent a whole semester with her learning nothing and waste your time.

I had Monika for 2D Design. She never learned our names (15 ppl in the studio), was rude to our TA, didn't teach us anything about 2D Design, and wanted to focus solely on her medium, filmography. If you wanted to do well in the course, you had to show up and make the kind of art she wanted to see, not the art you wanted to make.

Doug was a real gem to have during my first semester at WashU. Fun assignments, really approachable, and super caring. Even though the class was remote he offered students a couple of chances to do some in-person work which was so helpful. Sometimes the class critiques dragged on but everyone got something out of it. I'd highly recommend his class.
I don't understand why the first person on the page says that. Doug is a great professor and at least I find him approachable and kind.
He's great! But sometimes I find his feedbacks (and what he says in some lectures) a little vague. Overall great prof and I think as com des you will have to take his class.

He is fine, but he's definitely not my fav prof in com des. Doug gives good feedback which I really appreciate, but a lot of people including myself find Doug not approachable and also a little intimidating.
love him but intro film was tough (its intro!)

Great professor all around. Challenges you to your highest ability through his lectures and deep knowledge of Cinema studies. The class is difficult and make sure to start your papers not the night before the deadline. But strongly recommend because he's very passionate and is engaging within his lectures.

Prof. Fleury is the best professor I've ever had. Usually, I can never stay awake in lectures, but he makes class so fun that I was not only awake the whole time, but actively participating. He's extremely passionate about what he teaches and kind towards everyone. He lays out clear, fair expectations and if you follow them, you'll do great.

Taking his first-year seminar Superhero Media was one of my favorite courses of the semester. His interest alone makes you want to be interested, but regardless the content is great and easily engaging. There's some group projects, but they're a breeze. You literally get to watch superhero movies and shows for a class, what more could you want?

Professor Fleury is always prepared and friendly. His class is very organized, engaging, and entertaining. He grades quickly and efficiently with detailed feedback. I thoroughly enjoyed video game studies. Wish all professors were like this!

While the class content isn't difficult the work load is. Come semester's end we wrote 4 ten page papers. However the amount of questions he wants you to answer on these papers makes it much longer than just ten pages. Some of the writing feels unnecessary and irrelevant. However he's not a bad professor, his lectures aren't bad, but not great.

He is such a great professor. Really cares about his students and appreciates when students participate, even when classes are online. He is a very successful person and it was an honor to be in his class.

Unhelpful and unprofessional. Every test is graded by his TA and they are horribly inconsistent, then when you ask him for a regrade he will just meet with you and have you tell him how smart he is for an hour. After that he'll give you an A. I am happy that I had him during the pandemic so I didn't actually have to go to any lectures.

Very unique class. You show up, and he talks - basically fuels a discussion about really interesting topics. His lectures are so interesting that you don't even have to take notes. You will have to do about 2 papers and 3 homeworks then a final presentation and thats it. He even brings in professionals to meet you! Love this professor and class.

Prof. Griffith was caring and really wanted everyone to participate and engage. It was a heavy subject but she made a really wonderful class experience and most of the class was facilitated discussion. I'm sorry she's going on sabbatical in 2024.

Dr. Griffith is an amazing professor and I have really enjoyed taking her class. She is super supportive of her students and very responsive to emails, and wants to help you succeed. She does assign a lot of reading each week and expects participation in class disscusion, so be prepared to get called on and know what you're talking about.

Religions of St. Louis was one of my favorite classes I've taken at WashU  it's an amazing opportunity to learn so much more about the city and its religious mosaic. Dr. Griffith assigns a lot of reading and a lot of papers, she is an excellent scholar, teacher, and overall human. I learned so much and was constantly so inspired by her.
3 hour class that meets once a week, and for each class you need to read a book (think 200-500 pages each week). Class usually ended within two hours, but it was a rough time.

I found the lectures to be very enjoyable. A lot of them were discussion based but participation is not graded and she never put pressure on anyone to talk or cold called (even when the room was silent) which I appreciate. Super approachable in office hours and overall good classroom environment.

Dr. Shady did a great job teaching this new Writing Intensive course (Politics & Identity). Multiple checkpoints for the main paper of the semester helped groups stay on pace, although her feedback was sometimes contradictory for later reviews of the same content. Knowing terminology from the lectures is needed for the exams.

Her class was much harder than I expected for a 200 level class. I bombed the midterm exam (got a C) but then was forced to clutch up on the final to get an A-. Her exams are pulled straight from the slides, so just study them, which I learned a little late. There's a 10 page paper you have to write over the semester, so keep that in mind.

Lectures and content are really interesting, and she has you start working on your final papers at the beginning of the semester so you don't scramble at the end, great feedback on drafts too. Tests could be extremely detailed and difficult to do well on. I studied for days for the final and still didn't feel super confident. Study early for tests!

Freidel is a pretty nice guy, and this class is super easy. Lectures are all discussion and your grade is entirely based on homework assignments. Class could be kinda boring at times, but would absolutely recommend if you want an easy A.

I took this class online. It was an entirely discussion-based seminar class, with select readings that were not necessarily required, but encouraged. Professor Freidel did a great job balancing sharing his personal experiences/views on relevant issues and giving students time to discuss and share perspectives. Only grades were journal reflections.

I believe most people take this class with the intent to get an Easy A. Beware! This class will not yield an easy A! Tests & assignments require TONS of reading and writing. The professor provides very FEW, and UN-CLEAR instructions or guidance regarding homework, tests, and our major research paper. TAs do all of the grading. Nice guy though.

My favorite professor of the semester! He obviously cares about his students and made the studio a safe place to learn and grow. This class was challenging as someone who didn't know much about 3D, but I learned so much and ended up really enjoying it.

Professor Chavez has incredible ideas for projects that really expanded my technical and conceptual 3D ability, and artistic ability in general. He is really kind but also still pushes us to be the best we can be. I had done very little 3D work in the past, and 100% want to continuing pursuing 3D because I loved this class so much.

If I could take this class again, I would in heartbeat! Juan Chavez has been the best professor Ive had at Washu so far! If you are looking for a class that can be an introduction into sculpture, this class is amazing, if you are looking for someone to push you and help develop your craft, this class is amazing! I highly recommend this class!!!

Took three courses with Prof. Zeng. Definitely shaped my college trajectory, never thought I would enjoy applied math so much but he framed the subject as this fascinating puzzle I couldn't get enough of. I quite literally wouldn't have finished with the major I did without him. One of the best professors in the engineering school in my opinion.
amazing professor!
Best professor I've had so far. Very fun lectures. Taught me what it means to really know a subject.
If you're maximizing revenue, you're selling to much!
No Comments
Wow.  This guy is great.  His style is very animated (and very British).  He is a favorite among Wash U MBA's.  Make sure you take him to tea at the Ritz!

The class is so easy that it is difficult to stay awake. The textbook can be safely ignored, as can most of the notes. I learned nothing over the course of this class. Nonetheless, Bhattacharya was completely unhelpful and arbitrary in grading. Worse, he sometimes exploded with anger at the class and the students. Avoid him at all costs.

Unable to explain anything in an understandable manner and the way he grades things is incomprehensible. The class itself is relatively easy but he uses this to lull you into a false sense of confidence. The average for the final exam was 30/100 compared to 90's for everything else.

Don't take MEC 290 with Bhattacharya.  Brutally easy class. Felt like I was in 8th grade, but then he'll give you (and the entire class) a B on his imaginary grading scale.  I was informed that the class AV was a B+ (I received better than class AV on all but one assignment and had extra credit and received a B).  Dumbest professor I've ever met.
He's tough but fair. Definitely have to put the work in, but explanations are clear.

By the end of this class, I really felt confident about my grasp of the material. The homework assignments  and exams were well-designed; I learned the most from struggling with these questions. Dr. Chanthamontri is a hard but fair grader. If you get the grade you want in his class, you'll know that you earned it. I highly recommend him.

Overall, Ken is a dedicated professor who is concerned about his students' success. I took General Chemistry with him over the summer and felt that I had a good grasp of the material, despite the fast pace of the course. While our exams were challenging, he tried to ensure that the grading was fair. I would definitely recommend taking his class.

Was Ran the best lecturer I ever had? No. Did he have very clear handwriting when writing on the board? Absolutely not. However he's one of the funniest professors I've ever had and I was always laughing in class which made it easy to be invested in his lectures. Highlight was him leading us in a Shrek sing-a-long on the last day of class.

If you work with other people on assignments and go to lectures, totally possible to get an A. Lectures are required attendance- Ran is a pretty funny lecturer, I laughed quite a bit. However, the lectures can be a bit complex or unrelated to the subject at times.
Awesome professor. Pretty clear lecturing, very fun and entertaining. Clearly cares about his students.

I think that Professor Crain absolutely knows her stuff, has some great examples of how the law works, and brings a practical eye to what she teaches, but at the same time, her classes feel like a sort of soupy mess and the casebook that she assigns is bewilderingly disorganized. Very mixed feelings.
Professor Crain is terrific. Her Labor Law class was incredible.

Professor Crain is BY FAR the best professor I have had in the Law School.  There is an immense value in going to class, where she doesn't just walk through the facts and holding of a case (unlike so many other classes).  She is an extremely prominent professor in Labor / Employment Law, and it was a real honor to honestly learn labor law from her.
Professor Safe is an accomplished professional with solid practical experience.
High demand but very helpful
No Comments

Professor Stillman is one of the best professors/teachers I have ever had. She is extremely knowledgeable (Yale Law) and prepared for class. She connects the topics in class to current events and welcomes debate. The class will teach you a lot - stuff that matters in life. Go to her office hours and talk about life.

Professor Stillman is a great professor - very organized, passionate about law, and hilarious. Class is mostly discussion/debate oriented and very fun. Go to office hours and talk to her about life - great advice.
Gotta love her...

She's the best. She really makes the material interesting and prefers to help her students reach their own conclusions and then comment on that versus just lecture forever, which is what other art history professors tend to do. I want to take all of her classes!!!

Professor Bass is great - she didn't overwhelm by assigning multitudes of work but rather focused on helping students learn and love the material. All the writing assignments were creative and about engaging with art rather than research, which I loved.
Professor Bass was great. Not only was the class incredibly interesting and informative, her delivery is captivating. Never a dull moment.

Professor Cowsik is a truly accomplished genius (look up his Wikipedia) that you can learn a lot from and is chill. His notes are difficult to read, and you always don't know what is going on in the class. All tests are take home, which are slight extensions of the weekly homework sets. You have to fumble hard to not get an A in the class.

Professor Cowsik is very passionate about his subject, but speaks softly and is difficult to hear even with a mic and small classroom. His handwritten notes are difficult to read as well. Graded on homework only, once per week. Midterm and Final are the same as HW but a little longer, all take-home. Easy class with a bit of Physics background.
Nobody went to the lectures, and the TA gave the homework assignments. The midterms and the final are taken home, so that makes it easier. Great professor, super understanding

Very easy class. Professor Kim made an effort to be personal and interact with students. The class was pretty disorganized and you didn't always know what assignments were or when they were due. However, in the end it was a simple class with few expectations and help was available if needed.

Are you looking for a class where all the busywork you do gets dropped into a black hole? Look no further.
Feedback? None. 
Grades? Definitely not.
Informative and coherent lectures? You're asking for too much already.

From the bottom of my heart, don't take a class with Prof Kim. She has good intentions, but you will get nothing out of it.

Professor Kim has the most boring lectures I have ever participated in. They were actually painful to sit through. The class was not clearly structured, and professor Kim was very disorganized. The class was on writing skills, yet I learned very few tangible writing skills. Assignments are mostly boring busy work. Do not take.

Although ESE205 doesn't involve much lecturing, Professor Vaughan is always there for his students. He is always happy to help and is willing to work side by side with TAs. He would even dedicate extra office hours on weekends, especially toward the end of the semester when everyone's working overtime to finish their final projects.

Ken is new to teaching ESE205 and things were a bit rocky from a management perspective, but unlike other professors he completely recognizes any issues. Ken cares a lot about students and is never "out to get" anybody. Expect lots of extensions for labs when needed. Ken is the perfect type of professor for a lab style class like ESE205.

Professor Vaughan is a fantastic teacher and very kind person. Despite significant issues I had unrelated to course content, he was incredibly understanding and made sure accommodations were made to salvage my learning. I am extremely thankful for his support and highly recommend taking a class with him whenever possible.

Works hard, expects you to work hard. Briefs can be vague and confusing, but it's her first year as a studio lead so I'll give her grace. Exposed us to a lot of intense work very fast, but I liked that because it gave us great exposure to skills for the years ahead. She has a great sense of humor if you get to know her.

Zahra could be very intimidating during the first few encounters. But throughout the semester, she proved to be a great architect, professor, and just a person in general. Very work-heavy, so get ready to work on weekends a lot. She does use some difficult terms so you should ask for clarification but her feedbacks are very detailed and helpful.

I never understood anything that we were doing or how to do it. She would explain things in the weirdest ways with lots of pretentious design words in a first semester undergrad studio. The grading criteria was never clear, when I would read the brief overviews it felt like reading a different language. Seems nice but not for new architects.

Very kind and forgiving, only 3 essays all semester which was nice, but you don't get feedback on any of them until the end which makes writing the second 2 a little more difficult. Readings were pretty long and necessary for discussion, but interesting enough. Hou is pretty disorganized but it makes the class more chill.

Prof. Hou is kind and clearly cares a lot about the field and her students. However, I think she's a little overworked, between all the classes she teaches and being a mother of three little kids. As a result classes were often cancelled or sometimes we were just shown a video we could have watched at home.

Professor Hou as a person is very kind, understanding and passionate about the field of sociology and social justice. However, her lecturing style mostly consists of her rambling; her explanations are very unclear at times. The book helps explain a lot of concepts. She also tends to cancel class abruptly and takes a long time to grade assignments.

Louann is great at what she does and is super knowledgable.  She will leave you behind in the dust if you do not stop her and ask questions.  ASK QUESTIONS.  she is a wealth of knowledge and will give you all you need to know to make a pattern.  super strict grader.  treat her like a grandma and ive her hugs, she deserves them.

Doesn't actually teach.  Just starts making patterns and expects that students should be able to follow and understand.  She doesn't understand that what is second nature to her is totally new to us and may require some explaination.

She is great! Tests are easy, but labs are difficult and are basically quizzes. The information is very interesting and she is enthusiastic about her work. I loved this class and would recommend it for anyone!
Super nice，super friendly，just wants you to do well。

Amazing class. Learned a lot about the drama that goes on behind the scenes at American universities, and I thought it was all fascinating. We had great group discussions. Short writing responses and a fair amount of reading are required every week. A longer term paper is due at the end of the semester. Pretty standard workload.

Higher Education Administration was one of the best if not the best classes I have taken here at WashU. In fact, I made an account specifically just to write this review. Provost Thorp brings an amazing perspective to class and has some very interesting experiences to share. Lots of reading but very interesting discussions. Would take again.
The text book was rarely used. Cases are not even applied on exams. You're best off tattooing the Code of Federal Regulations to your arm. Would not recommend taking this class.
Really nice Professor! I liked her and the class a lot.

He is a great guy who is very passionate about genetics and drosophila! Albeit a bit disorganized at times and class time wasn't always efficient as a result, but he cares about how much we learn in the course.

Prof Ben-Shahar is really engaging and isn't a professor who throws his own research at you but instead discusses a wide array of genetics topics, sometimes bringing in guest lecturers who are the experts in their fields. He is interested in his students, very approachable, and wants everyone to succeed in the class. One of my favorite courses!

Fantasticenthusiastic lecturer. Culturally (NOT biomedically) focused. Participation is important! 20-30 pg of readingweek, each on which a 2-pg. paper is due (12 total) graded out of 5 pts (if you skimmed, etc. you will get a 4580%). Independent Project as well. If you really put in the work and do it well it is very manageable! 92+ is an A

The case studies Dr. G presents are fascinating, and the connection of music to broader social issues is quite amazing. While grading is quite easy, there is a fair amount of work involved (2 exams, four 2 page papers). My biggest piece of advice is to make note of authors' main points and page numbers! This helps during essay writing and exam prep
Good course. Requires attendance to 8:30 section. Very helpful. Course material is not difficult.
Great Professor. Funny, smart, discussion style teaching. Really great insight into companies and cases.

Incredibly dense textbook. We are quizzed on our understanding of the chapter before going over it in class, grade is based on how well you understood the book. Tests and quizzes are MUCH more difficult and specific than information given in lecture. She's so nice but our average exam scores for the class never went above 70 reflecting her lacking
Her seminar class is pretty interesting- I'd recommend. Participation counts though.
Friendly, helpful guy with a long background in composites. He's painstakingly pieced together a thorough powerpoint and is excited to pass his knowledge along. Homework is short, exams are easy.
Super easy.  Funny guy.  Homeworks are short and few.  Classes are always dismissed less than halfway through.
Keep asking “Do you follow me?" but not explaining proofs quite well. Only several students follow while others are confused

Professor Pelayo is a good professor who cares about his students. He is helpful during office hours or just asking him a quick question after class. Tests are not difficult and relatively fair. I would recommend taking Math 318 with him.
Great lecturer. Expects a comprehensive understanding of course material.
Fantastic professor. Course material was interesting and clearly explained. Always available for help if you need it.
Danielle is an amazing person and teacher. I recommend her highly.

Danielle is an awesome teacher and human being. I loved her writing class! She's helpful, clear, smart and clearly passionate about writing and helping other's write. I'd take any class possible with her.

He is a solid choice for studio. Eric is organized, clear, and cares about his students. He will not be unreasonable, and I genuinely enjoyed his class. However, he attributes towards younger versions of himself, so white male students do better with him. It was a consensus among half the students that he does this, through our various experiences.
Best teacher I have ever had...he is extremely knowledgeable and passionate about architecture.  He is organized and very helpful.  He is also handsome which made class much more enjoyable.
She is really nice and chill. I think it is inspiring to see a professor who makes material feel simple while building difficulty.

Martinez is young and relatable, but an unorganized grad student whose passion doesn't lie in teaching. The course was marketed as one that fulfilled Calc I credit, but it just covered precalculus concepts. Martinez's TAs were unavailable, and her own teaching was rote. Her class did not engage students. We also had 180+ modules of online homework.

Even though he says, "Call me Marty!" you will be terrified of him at first. He tends to shoot down students when they are wrong, but participation is important. Once you prove yourself to him, the class becomes much easier, so try to do this with the first big assignment. Super experienced in publishing, which makes related classes great.

His lectures are interesting and the assigned readings are too. I found him to be very intimidating--I was scared to ask and answer questions because I felt like I would be shot down if I was wrong. He's an amazing writer and knows what he's talking about, so if you want to take a class that he teaches, go for it.

This class was pretty easy, just show and pass but it was overall dry and boring, not really super interesting. However he does teach you some pretty practical skills to start your own business. You do a couple activities that he grades easily and one final project. I had to do an interview once and he was willing to answer all my questions! :)
Solid course designer (E4B course series). However, sketchy in how he recruits students to do work on his own personal ventures
Horrible! Kuai Pao!!

Professor Chen is a relatively new professor at WashU and she has done a fantastic job so far. While my class with here has not officially finished as of (11/20/21), I can tell she puts a lot of effort into her class content. She is open to feedback and does actually implement it in class as soon as possible. She genuinely cares about students.

You have to be engaged in class, lots of activities, talking, reading, presentations, etc. But she is super nice, is understanding if you need to leave (but do not abuse it), and is very, very, very passionate about what she is teaching.

Dr. Jones is amazing and understanding, by far my favorite professor. She cares about student success, gives a buffer time in the beginning of class, has short breaks throughout class, and listens to what students need. Class involves a short lecture, discussions, and a tiny group project.  assignments are reasonable and not harshly graded.
Amy is a tough grader for it to be a beginners drawing class. She grades as if all of her student are art majors, she offers zero flexibility. Not a easy beginners class.

I didn't really like the class or the feedback of the instructor. I think she was a bit harsh. I am not a art major. I was doing the best I knew how and I feel like she was a bit critical. I got a C. I am happy I passed but I was skeptical. I needed the class. I wouldn't take it again if I didn't have to. If you enjoy art then go for it.

Professor Jain Ji is incredibly sweet and caring to her students. intro Hindi 111 is relatively easy for most students with some background knowledge, for students like me who had no background in hindi it is alot of work. She gives a ton of homework every day though if you actually do it (and not google translate) it can be genuinely helpful

Jain ji is the kindest professor I have ever had. She strives to make her course content understandable. Her workload is easy to fulfill, so don't be daunted by the high credit count. My only complaint is that she sometimes has moments where she loses her train of thought or makes mistakes during her lectures, but she always promptly corrects them.

Really loved Prof. Meyer! Obviously very intelligent and knows a lot both inside and outside of his field! He is the first professor that I have come across that I believe sincerely respected the students in the room. That was refreshing! Really glad I took tech writing with him!

Very caring and funny guy. Reminds me of my grandpa. Attendance is required and instead of providing a typical rubric, he goes over the grading criteria during lecture, so you have to take notes and pay attention if you want to do well on the assignments. This class is relatively easy in terms of content but will take up a lot of time (6-12hrs/wk).

It was an interesting class, and more like a literature/communication class compared to other business classes.  The professor is very artistic/poetic and helped us develop a more artistic way to deliver narratives to others.

This professor's Business Communication course is disappointing, focusing excessively on writing format rather than content. Assignments must be exactly 300 words, with strict grading on format leading to 20%-30% deductions. Feedback on the actual substance is lacking, replaced by critiques on format, word count, and the number of paragraphs.
DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS!!!! HE IS SO BORING! HE CHANGE HIS MIND QUICKLY AND HE EVEN EXPECTED You to write something exactly 300 words! RUNNNNN!

He frequently cancelled class, so I felt less prepared for the exam and assignments than I would have liked to be. It was so bad that it became a running joke in my friend group about how he never showed up. Once, he cancelled class at 1:45 am the night before because he was in Chicago (since class was at 8:30, that was less than 7 hours notice).

This class angered me. I enjoyed the subject and the professor was knowledgeable, but seemed uninterested in actually teaching. Classes were regularly cancelled (few weeks where we had all planned classes). The syllabus was constantly updated without corresponding readings being posted. Overall, interesting subject matter but execution fell short.
Great Professor! One of the most empathetic and engaged professors I've had so far

Nice guy who's very knowledgable and the class is very interesting. Took class for NSM requirement. barely any homework and it's not graded harshly, but tests are the issue. The midterm and final both ask many extremely detailed and insignificant facts that he'll mention once in class and youre expected to memorize random planet densitites.

Professor Tomy is a very insightful educator. In the class, he never gives out answers; instead, he lets students think hard and debate with each other, so that the classroom allows different idea to exist, but also give students a chance to cross-examine their own ideas. As long as you write with effort you will receive a decent grade.

Professor Tomy is very funny and told a lot of stories during class. There was a lot of reading but he also used podcasts and youtube videos which made things easier. Most of class was talking about the readings and debating. His grading at the beginning was easier than at the end but I also got a lot of extra credit so still ended up with an A.
Really cool guy.
Worst professor ever! He made me play ultimate frisbee with him after class!
Amazing professor for an amazing course. Would highly recommend for any cs majors
He has a wide range of knowledge and is always happy to help with any project. He seems to really care about improving 437 and about preparing students for the "real world".

He is by far my favorite professor. Throughout the semester, we learned so much about different topics, and although we moved on to different topics each week, it did not seem overwhelming. He makes the class very engaging, which is super nice because it is not your typical lecture class. He is super understanding as well.

Professor Cortes Ferrandez has been my favorite Spanish professor at WashU so far! His assignments require you to think about the topics presented in class, but are designed in a way that does not cause as much stress as other classes. He is a fair grader and really cares about the students.
Had a great time taking this course. DeeDee made it a fun place to go to.
Amazing instructor for a course that could have been very boring! She cares about the students and made class very fun and interesting and applied lessons to real life scenarios.
Professor Filippou is a great professor and an even better person. If you take a class with him, make sure to visit him during office hours as he is always available and willing to help out.
Prof Filippou is very caring and accessible for help outside of class. He's not a professor who would try to trick students with unnecessarily complicated questions on exams and homework. Nice guys.

It was her first-semester teaching. It is a very easy class, although the lectures can get boring. There was not much work throughout the semester. This is probably the easiest class I have taken at WashU
She cares about her students and marketing. She is willing to make sure all her students understand her course and it's an easy class.
Grading criteria is not clear. No matter what you do or how hard you work in this class, it seems like he pulls a grade out of the air. He is also great at putting people to sleep.

The course is divided into two parts. The first part,. you go to classes and do the homework. Although the lecture is not sufficient for you to finish the homework, you can still do it. The second half is lab. There are three lab HW and a final group project. if you do not know verilog, DONT take the class. the HW/final project is not doable.

Sara stratton is quite literally the WOAT. couldn't even tell you how many students have dropped study abroad because of how poorly she's managed it all. no one knows what classes they're taking or even where we're living, and we're only a few weeks out from flying to our host cities. highly unresponsive - avoid at all costs.

Amazing professor. Class is extremely hard. You need to memorize just about everything said in the lectures for the tests so be sure to take good notes. While difficult, it's extremely interesting and rewarding and will definitely teach you how to learn things. McDermott is titanic in the field of memory - you couldn't find someone more qualified.
Really sweet professor. Really important to study off of powerpoint. taking notes during power points can sorta be helpful, but is unnecessary. memorize everything (in 1-2 night)  A
Challenging professor but cares about his students and is fair.

She is SO nice--probably my favorite professor I have had so far. The class does require work, but you can work with other students and ask IAs questions. You probably will have to put some time into it, but it doesn't feel overwhelming at all. (Also she is super good about getting student feedback and adjusting the course accordingly)

Professor Fischer is a fantastic professor. If you like to read, but would rather have in-depth discussions as opposed to lengthy tests and papers, Professor Fischer is your guy. Readings are long, but interesting and Fischer leads great discussions on a wide array of topics. Very knowledgeable, but values the opinions of his students.
Lectures were way too overbearing with over 50 slides being done in a day. Lot of points were in useless in class quizzes. Made very simple content as hard to digest as possible.
Nice person and excellent educator!
Fun, helpful, obviously knowledgeable. He's very in-touch and reasonable, and he's very flexible with helping you manage your work. Definitely the best professor I've had at this school :)

New/young professor...I think he tried to act tough and be hard because he's new to teaching. The tests were hard so he had to curve them. He just basically reads off the slides in class, and he hands out the slides in class beforehand but if you don't go to class you can't them them.
easy A class.
Great Professor, applies class lectures to real life applications
Pushes concept a lot, interested in the process just as much as the product. Liked his class

Strangely passive aggressive which makes for an uncomfortable classroom environment at times. To do well in the course, all you really need to do is agree with her and her viewpoints on certain topics. I've seen her attack students who even hint that they have opposing viewpoints to hers (especially on political topics), so proceed with caution.

Definitely the hardest MechE class at WashU. She is a great professor, especially when you go to OH. Required course, but really make sure you review thermo and go to office hours to ensure the design homeworks go better than horrible. Sorry, this class is hard, but it's college, and it's engineering.

She underestimates how much work her design homeworks actually are and doesn't believe us when we tell her. Everyone in this class has been miserable all semester. The class would actually be super interesting if it weren't for the completely unreasonable courseload. You can't enjoy or absorb content if you are drowning in it.

ryan is such a caring guy, and he is so helpful in machine shop. would absolutely recommend taking a class with him. he is exceptionally skilled, but is still down to earth, and he encourages all students and lifts u up if u make a mistake. 10/10

She is fine, very nice, and will meet with you if she wants (sometimes does not show up to her own office hours or answer your emails). She is a semi-interesting lecturer. I did not love her, but maybe you will.

Prof. Liang is knowledgeable and passionate about what she teaches. And she engages students with lots of activities, discussion questions, and business examples. Sometimes she can be intense and harsh, but she cares about students. She provides insightful feedback on your strengths and weaknesses based on your personality and career aspiration.

(Pleasure and Pain: European Fashion as Art History)
Might be my favorite class I've ever taken. Gabel's lectures are super interesting and engaging, and she is the nicest person, I totally recommend. You definitely have to put in some hours of studying for the exams, but they are very doable if you do, and you'll know what to expect.

Dr. McDowell is one of the best professors I've had at WashU. She is really passionate about what she teaches, and she really cares about her students. Attendance is mandatory since the class is mostly discussion-based. I learned so much in this class and could not recommend Dr. McDowell more!
has a really clear method of teaching, nice guy,helpful in office hours

A little outdated understanding of the field as it is now, but overall tons of experience in the field to learn from about general practice. He loves dicussion in his class. Overall nice guy, will learn a few things from him.

If you care about history, take a class with Dubé. His classes are hard - complex readings, abstract, sometimes awk but v engaging class discussions; I did what was, imo, my best academic work to date in his class. One prob I have tho is that he is not super clear about grading criteria/assignment expectations. Meeting outside of class helps tho.

Had him for DT. Not much help, truthfully, but I am extremely grateful that he is supportive of student ideas. He tends to take a step back from the process, unusual for Sam Fox profs, but I enjoyed the freedom to research and explore ideas. Not every DT professor gives you that space to be "half-right", i.e. supportive pedagogy (not directive).

Sri is an amazing professor. There is not a single textbook he likes on this subject, so he sends out lecture slides on each topic. I think he should make them into his own textbook. They are that good and organized. His quizzes are fair. If you show up to class, you will know the material. I only wish more of my classmates were more engaging.
The best professor I met in Olin. His class is very inspiring.

Claims "I don't like to grade based on exams" and then has us do 2 midterms that are worth 30% each, which he calls "quizzes" to make himself feel better. Thinks we are phd level and goes through dozens of growth/development models. Just take another class unless you really want to learn growth/development models.
New teacher and it shows. Seemed nervous when talking in front of students. No one got an A. Rarely prepared for lecture causing many starts and stops during class.

Everyone absolutely failed every exam, so the class was insanely curved. She projects her iPad onto the screen, then hovers over the iPad and doesn't look up at the class for the entire duration of the period.

Pretty dry lecturer, but has fair cold calls and lets you know in advance when you'll be on call and what you'll be asked. Models course content after MPRE, so very helpful to take this course right before you take MPRE in conjunction with other prep. Little to no variation year by year. Exam was fair and similar to MPRE as well.

Her writing is AWFUL. She is such a hard read! She uses passive voice constantly, makes up her own terms, uses improper grammar, beats EVERY subject to the ground, and when "summarizing" the point of someone she quotes, her summary is longer and more ambiguous. I have a master's and graduated with honors, and found Shakespeare easier to read!
Absolute legend.

I love her with all my heart. She know all the students names. Lectures are super clear and engaging, some of the best I've seen. Go to office hours, she will teach you everything and help with homework.

Cezareo truly cares about his students and their success. He is always available for student feedback and his lectures are taught with the assurance that all students understand the material. My favorite professor at WashU.
I took her Contemporary Issues in Psychology seminar. She is very caring and always gives encouraging feedback. The class involves a lot of writing, but is an easy A.
Very kind professor. Really cares about teaching you the foundations of singing.

I thought Prof Vashist was wonderful. Very experienced in the corporate/research world and enjoys connecting with students. I found her Negotiations class to be one of the most useful courses I've taken in Olin.

SangMok is a bit new, but he's getting the hang of things. He's responsive to student feedback, too. The key for his 503 course was to read the lecture notes independently, as we often didn't have time to discuss them thoroughly in class.
Straightforward and organized lecturer, a caring, approachable, and understanding guy.

Prof Essner is a good choice. It is a lot of work but you will get a lot better at writing. She gives really helpful and specific feedback on every single assignment and its clear that she puts a lot of effort into the lectures. If you aren't ready to work at the level required take a different prof, this definitely isn't the person to slack off on

Cell biology is a very difficult course, but Professor Wang is passionate about the subject which makes it more interesting and enjoyable. I only recommend taking this course if you are genuinely interested in the subject and are ready to study for hours each week. The tests are very difficult and studying is extremely necessary, but it is worth it

Very nice person and very caring about her students. As a teacher, she was ok. Didn't really learn that much or felt engaged. The class itself is not entertaining so that's not her fault. Outdated and unhelpful resumes and cover letters during the career unit. Don't procrastinate on the final report, or it'll be a pain to finish during finals week.

She managed to explain difficult chemistry concepts in a clear manner and seemed to really care about her students. I credit her help sessions to getting an A in chem 112 because Sobotka's teaching was so bad.

Frey is the worst of the Chem professors. She just plowed through the material, and she seemed to no care if we learned anything. Daschbach was much better. Frey was also useless at help session unless you asked a question about a specific hw problem, and even then she would just read the key, which we already have. Really frustrating semester.

Professor Frey had supported my friend and me through very tough times. She was very caring and I very much enjoyed her class. I am very happy to have taken my first chemistry class with Professor Frey. In a sense, she helped me grow a healthy mindset towards college. I couldn't thank her enough.

Frey is very awkward. She reads from her notes in a cold tone. It is not clear she understands everything she is saying. She does not answer questions well. Then she insists we work together on simple clicker questions. I strongly recommend you take someone else for Gen Chem. Overall the course is good, but she is not.

An incredibly sweet professor who genuinely cares about students' academics and their personal wellbeing. Her explanations are SO helpful and she breaks down the material so that you understand it very intuitively. Gives out candy during help sessions!

She is pretty organized, but it seems like she is reading a script when she lectures.  Her fake enthusiasm is annoying and condescending.  My father came to one of the lectures and left disgusted by her antics.  What is with the candy?  We are not 5 yrs old.

Amazing professor that makes lectures interesting. Dr. Frey is highly energetic, once pretending to be an electron by running around the lecture hall. Cares highly about her students.  Make sure to understand the material and do the problem sets. This is not a memorization- heavy class. Utilize all resources: RPM, POGIL, PLTL, office hours
Super enthusiastic! makes gen chem interesting. not boring like other professors who teach the same course. if you have the option, take it with her. she is also super nice and approachable.

I don't understand the praise for Frey.  She is ok, at best.  After the first class, I started going to Buhro's section-wow, he's much better.  They are absolutely opposites.  She seems to care only about test scores, and not how the students are learning the material.  She is also not patient with questions and way too rushed.

Her lectures are fine, a bit dry and I wish she would not just read from her notes.  She struggles with explaining things beyond what she has presented.  They preach that the exams will push you, but I didn't see it.  Just memorize the problems from POGIL or PLTL and you will do fine.

Professor Frey is a very good lecturer. She explains hard topics well and is approachable for questions after class. She also writes down almost everything she says which makes it much easier to take thorough notes. Her help sessions are not my favorite, but I do recommend her lecture.

Dr. Frey is always very energetic during her lectures, especially when she hops around and pretends to be an electron. I love it. She moves at a fast enough pace that the lectures don't get boring, but slowly enough to allow students time to process and understand what she is teaching.

Although this is a very difficult class, professor Frey is an excellent teacher.  She is usually very clear and always concise.  Her lectures are always the fullest, because many students enrolled in the other sections prefer Frey's teaching style.

She will tell you exactly what will be on the exams. Lectures are a bit boring though. She has a tough to e explaining several topics. I went to other's help sessions to get better explanations. Overall she's ok, and you can get a good grade, even if you don't understand the material.
Of the three gen chem teachers, she is the best. She teaches everything you need to know on the test in a clear and understandable manner and will answer your questions well.

Frey is very clear, and spells out everything you need to know.  I wish she would have pushed us a bit more in terms of applying the concepts.  Overall, not bad though.  It is a bit annoying that students talk throughout lecture.

One of the best teachers I've had. I had heard a lot of talk about how this course is the worst but she made it interesting and understandable. Strongly suggest taking her section, Lumus guest lectures did not compare and left some scratching their heads.

Professor Frey is a great professor who explains difficult concepts in an exceptionally clear way. Exams were very tricky, but as a whole, the class was not as difficult as I had expected based on what I had heard from other students/faculty.

Frey is a clear lecturer, and she makes it pretty obvious want she wants you to know for the exams.  She sometimes has a hard time giving alternative explanations of concepts.  Overall, don't be afraid of Chem 111 since they no longer curve.  They use a straight point system.

Frey is amazing! She is energetic during the lectures&keeps the class engaged & participatory-quite the feat w/ such a large lecture. She is always willing to take Q's during lecture or stay after. She formats her notes organizationally&simplifies the learning process. Some disliked her notes, as it took her time to write them, but it helped many!
Really liked having Blank for Calc II! He'll often go on tangents, but he's so funny. He once showed my class the comb he uses when he cuts his hair.

Very bad teacher. Rambles on about subjects not related to stats(such as cereal from the 1970s) Assigns coding homework that no other professors teaching 2200 assign. Very funny and nice guy if you go to his office hours but not very good at actually teaching the subject. Tests are not too hard if you make a good cheat sheet and use calculator

Not a good communicator whatsoever. In lecture and his lecture notes, you have a hard time figuring out what he's trying to say, and he goes off topic frequently. When he explains something you end up more confused than you were before.

I took AP Statistics in high school so i was lucky enough to fare well in this class with no help from Blank. He makes no sense when he lectures. His own notes are over complicated. The third test will hurt and if you have not done well on other tests you will not get a good grade. DO NOT take this class with him.

Clarification: textbook  his own 'class notes' textbook PDF's  Also, First President U  WashU.   Though he seems like a nice guy, his teaching style makes the class appear deceptively easy. Lectures, though not crystal clear, often made sense to some degree. But exams didn't reflect the lectures terribly well. He went on tangents in class often
Confusing and not very clear in lecture; often makes mistakes on exams. Avoid.

Brian Blank is a mess.  His lectures are all over the place, he didn't use a textbook, and his exams were unfocused and unrelated to class.  His grading can be lenient at times, but taking a class with Blank is the ultimate toss-up.  Funny and sweet guy, but overall don't recommend.

He's an all right professor overall. Unlike some math professors, he is a very approachable guy and responds to emails pretty quickly. His lectures get confusing, but he types his HW/practice exam solutions very thoroughly, which is where the bulk of my learning took place.

Professor Blank, while not the best at teaching, is by far the funniest professor I've had at WashU.  His exams are pretty easy so long as you put a bit of effort into the class (read his online textbook!).  He does make quite a few errors on his exams, however, which is frustrating.

Blank gets really off track, is extremely unclear, and is terrible at answering questions. I got an A in the class by reading, rereading and rereading his online textbook. Do every possible problem and try to write everything from his online lecture notes down on your note card. Don't worry about not having a textbook, use his online one.
Skip class, watch khan academy instead. Lecture notes are confusing. Lectures themselves are filled with unnecessary discussion of cereal boxes and 1970s references.
Blank gets a bad rap, but he's actually a pretty good teacher if you pay attention. He  rambles sometimes, but most of the times what he is saying is actually kind of interesting.

Lectures mainly on obscure material and does NOT focus on core concepts/problems. HW + Weekly Quizzes reflect NOTHING of the tests. Condescending and unhelpful in office hours; doesn't understand when students don't 'get' it. Emails are incomprehensible; test questions often have errors he insists students should've 'noticed and spent less time on'

Took his Calc II class this semester and it was much more difficult than it should have been. In lectures, Blank spends too much time on unessential background information and flies through the limited examples he does in class. His Webwork was much harder than any exam or quiz question and was basically useless to learn from. Not a great experienc

Blank isn't the best lecturer and often gets sidetracked during lectures and talks about irrelevant information. He does plenty of example problems but doesn't thoroughly explain concepts. You can get by without going to class. Do suggested exercises and old exams to study. If you can, avoid Blank for Calc 2.

Blank gives several example problems in his lectures, but does a poor job of explaining the steps. He also can get a little sidetracked when he rants on about the history of math. He's not the worst lecturer, but definitely not the best.

Generally a pretty nice guy, but his lectures are very vague and confusing. Makes a lot of mistakes, including on the exams. He doesn't assign a textbook but I would definitely recommend using one for the course, simply because his lectures/notes are not always very helpful. Expect lots of random tangents, too

Blank is a very good lecturer. He puts all relevant theory on the board in very clear handwriting. Also does good examples in class. Notes you take will be very helpful for homework. The class is hard but this is not his fault. If you take good notes, do practice problems, and SEE HIM FOR HELP IF YOU NEED IT (or email him), you will do fine.

Horrible. Ignores the textbook, teaches using his own personal longhand way of doing problems. quizzes+HW are long and count very little. tests are tricky, confusing, and test your 'reading through nonsense to find a question' ability rather then your knowledge of the material. Adds unnecessarily difficult integrals to complicate problems. Avoid.

Avoid at all costs barring a natural talent in statistics. Your interest in this very captivating subject and its powerful predictive powers will be in tatters by the second midterm exam. A textbook is not assigned - instead a horribly written PDF handbook that is about as clear as mud is given instead. If you must take this course, buy a textbook.

Bro is by far the biggest jackass I have had the displeasure of interacting with. You know it's bad when his first lesson of the year is him apologizing for being a "white, cis-gendered male." The only school supplies you need for this class are a bucket and a rope. Tell my family I love them. I think I might go bungee jumping in the Cornell gorge.

He was condescending and unfair to students, especially with his course policies which were very strict (no makeup exams for almost any reason). Also, many students felt uncomfortable around him. His TAs basically led the class and were amazing in answering all of the questions that he failed to teach us. Easy to do well with office hours.

Professor Shook is not a good lecturer. The TAs basically teach the course for him.  He also is not understanding or flexible at all, and will not accommodate personal situations in the classroom.  Tests and assignments are mostly easy for this course though.

Prof Shook don't even teach the class, he never showed up and there's no communication whatsoever. However, his TAs are amazing. The class was easy for me so I never had to ask questions, but if the content is new for you it might be hard.

The class itself is well designed in my opinion but Professor Shook doesn't really help much with it. Rather, the TAs are the ones who are helpful. I will say though that it is somewhat difficult to rank Professor Shook since you will interact with TAs far more than you will with him, but my few interactions with him were fine.

Shook doesn't really play a big role in his class. CS TA's will carry you and you have to appreciate them. My only interactions with him were through lectures where he was repeatedly passive aggressive towards students. I'll give him the benefit of the doubt since TA's have a high opinion of him. Reach out to him and I'm sure he'd be down to help.
Shook was quite condescending and didn't really take well to questions. The TAs pretty much run 131 and the exams are pretty easy so you'll be fine, though.

Prof Shook was nice enough, and the lectures were helpful when he did code writing problems. As others have stated the assignments are very difficult, but the tests are much easier. I didn't really enjoy the class, but it was easy enough to get an A.

He manages to turn even the simplest of classes into a scripture-reciting session. His class is an unparalleled cure for insomnia. It's a rare talent to amass such a collection of poor reviews for teaching what should be the easiest of all CS courses.

Shook didn't teach much at all. The lectures are a waste of time if you read the textbook, which is also terrible. I taught myself CS last semester, and it's embarrassing that WashU or Doug Shook might think they had anything to do with it.

I personally found it boring, but probably because I do not like CS, as it turns out. Assignments can be tough, but tests are easier and are pretty fair. Shook has days where he is super passionate and some days where he is kind of hard to follow and less passionate. He hates it if you ask about tests, but overall he is just fine.

Professor Shook was actually better than I expected. I found that doing the modules before going to lecture/while in lecture and going to his lectures was the most helpful for me. If you follow along during class and do not get distracted, you will notice the content isn't difficult. If you need help, you can go to TAs office hours.

Doug Shook's CSE 131 is a class run by TAs. The lectures are helpful to some people but most will just skip them. I highly recommend doing the code and not using chat gpt because you will fall behind otherwise. Pretty boring teacher honestly but atleast grading is clear and fair.

Prof. Shook is not the best prof to take your first computer science class as it might make you question your major. He's also rude and judgmental when answering students answer in lecture. I also want to take the time to thank the amazing TA that helped me for hw and pass this class but Prof. Shook...
This class definitely NOT meant for those who had no coding experience before. The homework assignments are hard and take very long time to complete.

I don't have much Java coding experience, but I thought the class was pretty easy and straightforward and you have a lot of time to think through your answers on tests. Shook doesn't go over anything useful in lectures though so just do the module readings and talk to TAs. He also is super stingy about talking about the test format/content

This class isn't made for those who've never taken a computer science course. It goes very quickly and there is little help to learn everything. The assignments are difficult and Shook's lectures are very poor. Often when you ask basic questions, he claims we should already know them and does not answer. This class is entirely run by TA's.

Professor Shook was extremely bad at teaching, I would not recommend attending the lectures at all for this course. I was already vaguely familiar with the content of the course and I found that when I went to lectures, I left more confused than when I started. He also is just kinda rude. At the end of the day class was extremely easy, but bad prof

I'd only take him again because the class is required. If you take this class, skip lectures he is incredibly condescending and won't go over anything useful. The weekly studios are required and a good place to learn and demo your work at with a TA you know well. Overall not a hard course, just do the assignments honestly and you will be fine.
Doug is so funny and the class is so fun!

He seems like a kind man, but is very scatterbrained and not good to teaching the material. I had to do a lot of studying and teaching myself the material outside of class to do well and thankfully my calculus from high school gave me a solid foundation. His is the last math class I ever took.

He is the reason I had to stop taking math classes at WashU. I graduated over 10 years ago, and still remember how awful this man was as a professor. His terrible instruction for both Calc 2 and Intro to Stats left me unprepared to take more advanced courses, so I moved on to other areas of study. If you actually care about learning, AVOID!!!

Spitznagel is your typical tenured professor. He's obviously very interested in the material, but mumbles & is difficult to understand in lecture(I ended up going to 2 lectures max the entire semester). Tests are each work 25% of your final grade, so as long as you self-study from the textbook & know the material, you should be able to get an A

Very nice guy and good at working-statistics. However, he is a terrible professor. He is absent minded in class, tells random stories, and does not teach the material. If you want an A, you better go to all the help sessions and read the book every night to learn the material for yourself, especially since he tests over things not covered in class.
Do not take any course with him. I do not think he knows the course materials well and he does not care for his job and respect his students at all.
Run for your life.
Hard to understand, mumbles words, unfair test questions, avoid at all costs.

tests arent hard. all questions from textbook with the same number so if u solve all questions on textbook you will get an A  i got an A on his 3200 class, but self-taught from the first chapter to the last. I personally think he is not a good lecturer, but his class is OK to get an good grade.

This guy is extremely soft-spoken, doesn't have productive lectures, goes off on thousands of tangents, and has no structure for the class. I personally think the material isn't extremely hard,  but his teaching style hasn't allowed me to learn anything (even though I took a stats class in high school that was very similar). AVOID HIM AT ALL COSTS.
This class was my biggest regret. Stopped going after the first month. I just read 4 chapters on the night before the exam. So yea --- don't take his class unless you hate yourself.
Don't take his classes. Please don't do that to yourself.  He's a nice person, but that sadly does not translate to being able to teach coherently
Can't hear him
He is a great lecturer!
AVOID ALL CLASSES TAUGHT BY HIM

Not a clear lecturer. Mostly goes over homework problems. Doesn't do a good job teaching how to use SAS. Goes on tangents easily, some classes you learn more about the history of the statisticians than the math itself.  Class content and old exams are not good predictors of exam content.

Hands down the worst class I've ever taken at WashU.. The lectures were completely useless as Spitznagel consistently went on tangents. Not to mention, he literally changed the material that would appear on the final 3 days before. Definitely avoid taking any courses taught by him.

Wait a semester and take the class with another professor. Doesn't cover much material in lectures. He just mumbles to himself in lecture and is hard to understand. Also doesn't respond much to emails, so it is hard to get help outside of class. This semester, he also changed the material covered on the last exam 3 days before the actual exam...

He barely explains anything in class except for maybe going through how to do a few of the homework problems.  Other than that he makes no effort to have a lesson for the class and so none of the material from the chapter is explained.  He spends most of the time on SAS (which he doesn't even teach well) and hardly any time on the actual material.

Do not take a class with him. You cannot understand anything he says, and when you can actually understand his words, all he talks about all random tangents. There is not curve and the class is abysmal.
Easy class to do well in if you do all the homework.

He is a great lecturer and funny. However, the intro to psych second semester is HARD. He seems a bit mean and hard to approach sometimes, but he is hilarious and super educational. Overall, he is super enthusiastic, and I enjoyed it for sure.
By far one of the best lecturers I've experienced. Never comes to class without pep.

He is the best professor I ever had! He's ADORABLE and his lectures are easy to follow.
Tests are straightforward and reasonable and he cares about his students a lot.
He is GOAT! His lectures are funny and he makes reasonable tests. Plus he's so adorable! Would take him again 100%

There were three professors teaching the course. When he lectured, he always did a good job engaging me in a long two hour class. The essays contain significant weight in the class so make sure to get ahead on those. Overall, it's a fun and interactive class that was made even more fun by Dr. Carpenter.

I think this class overall is good if you are interested in psychology but all around Carpenter is a good presenter so I think even more for his part, he is good at making class engaging and interactive with so many students.

Professor Carpenter is the kindest, kindest professor I've ever had. He is so understanding and genuinely cares about you. He is this and also HILARIOUS! Very hip, very cool. Love him. Would 1000% take another course with him.

Very fun and engaging professor! His lectures are clear and easy to follow, and he keeps you engaged through the use of videos and demonstrations. He also answers the Piazza clearly and consistently. His quizzes are written well but can be a bit confusing at times. However, he is overall a great professor who is truly passionate about the course.

I want to pick up minor in psych after taking this course with Dr. Carpenter. The lectures were really well done and clear. If you've never taken a psych class, I'd say taking thorough notes during lecture and for the reading makes it much easier to succeed in the class. Dr. Carpenter is a really funny, caring teacher that I'd highly recommend!
Gives really interesting lectures. For intro to psych as long as you take notes you'll be fine. No homework, no tests, just online quizzes every other week which aren't very difficult.

I loved his lectures! they were always very fun and engaging. Of the 3 professors in Intro to Psych, he had the weirdest combination of topics, but he did such a great job with the content. He was such an amazing professor and his quiz questions were generally straightforward. 10/10 amazing professor.

LOVED him! He was so engaging and made class fun. He even plays a song at the beginning of every class. Very knowledgeable and you can just tell that he loves what he does. I hope to have him again at WashU.

Best professor ever and super relatable to Gen Z. Teaches concepts using his own personal experiences. Because of him, I want to get a Psychology minor. Plays music before class and has explanations for several quiz questions that many people in the class got wrong. Clear quizzes and corrects his errors to make quizzes fairer for everyone.
Out of the three professors for psych, he was the best. He makes the lectures very enjoyable and keeps everyone engaged.

Professor Carpenter's lectures are long but pretty interesting. The quizzes during his unit were the hardest because they were so subjective and the wording was confusing. However, I like his teaching style and how he goes out of his way to explain things using real-life examples.
Had Carpenter for 100B and FYP123 and was great. FYP123 had no quizzes and so it was easy to do well in the class. Lectures were good for 100B

Out of the 3 profs for Psych 100B, his lectures are the longest with most unneeded information. Although he might be a nice person, his quiz questions are very subjective (really depends on how you look at the question), making it really difficult to secure an A because the cutoffs for the course are so high.
Professor Carpenter is very kind and gives the most interesting lectures! He has been gracious to accommodate the students during COVID by changing quiz dates, etc.

Out of the three professors for psych 100, he was by far my favorite! The way he teaches is very engaging and Carpenter himself is a well rounded professor. He went out of his way to teach us emotions through art! He also used other real life examples and applied them to lectures. Loved his part of the class! He really listens to students as well.

Prof. Carpenter is seriously the best. Loved this freshman seminar SO MUCH! Class got a bit boring at time when the other profs lectured, but when Dr. Carpenter lectured I was always engaged. Definitely have to show up to do well, but there are no exams (all short papers/projects) and not difficult to do well if you put in a small amount of effort.

Orgo 2 isn't as hard as Orgo 1 but it's still hard; though, Dr. Moeller is an excellent professor. In class, he provides the notes before hand, and goes at a decent pace. He also provides a lot of practice exams for practice, which personally helped me on exams. Was helpful outside of class as well.

Dr. Moeller is the best teacher I have ever had. He distills very complex chemistry down to a set of core rules that just make sense. The goal of this class is to help transition students to reading the chemical literature and provides a very solid foundation for doing so. His (take home) exams are challenging, but you learn a ton from them.

Orgo 3 is a fast paced class, definitely use his prewritten notes in class and add onto them. Grade is entirely exam based, but he will help you on the week long take home midterms in his office hours. If you enjoyed 262, then definitely take 451. It is intense, and the exams are tough, but you can easily do well without too much extra work.

Dr. Moeller is one of the best professors I have had at WashU. I started Chem 262 hating organic chemistry and by the end, I liked it enough to take his upper-level organic chem class (451). I recommend using his written notes instead of trying to keep up and then reviewing afterwards. Chem 262 and 451 were both very difficult but still doable!
This class is so hard. He is very kind, but not too helpful in office hours (he goes too fast)

Orgo is hard, but Prof Moeller does absolutely everything in his power to help you succeed. In addition to well paced and organized lectures, accompanied by notes before and after on Canvas, he is so kind and funny. He is very quick to respond to emails and very accessible and approachable during office hours.

Seemed easier than 261 since it was more memory-based with examples similar to previous exams. He provides fill in the blank notes and posts online lectures, making class flexible to learn on your own time, but don't slack since it is heavy material. Exams were relatively difficult, ranging from easy points to usually tough questions near the end.

Dr. Moeller is one of the top 3 profs I've had at WashU. Truly cares about his students in all aspects of their life and is extraordinarily accessible outside of class (office hours EVERY DAY). He helped to me truly understand and enjoy Orgo. Exams are nothing crazy and he gives hints. Extremely bright prof with a deep passion for teaching.
262 was fine, but not good. Moeller covered a ton of material, but not in much depth. I ended up memorizing old exam problems and did fine as they were nearly identical.

I found Moeller went way too fast and did not explain concepts well. I stopped going to help sessions since he just repeated what he said in lecture, and he made me feel more stupid. FYI, the Bio pitch for this section of 262 is over rated...it is organic chemistry.

Prof. Moeller really does care about his students, and you can tell. His lectures are fast-paced but clear to the point. This class is difficult, but it's orgo so that's expected. As long as you study ahead of time, it's not hard to do well.

Dr. Moeller is amazing, one of my favorite professors. He is very knowledgable and makes lectures engaging. He is a fast lecturer, and takes some time to get used to, but he publishes his notes in advance for your reference. He is always kind and accessible during office hours. Don't be afraid to ask him questions!

Great professor. Does a good job explaining concepts and his lectures are super engaging. It's clear that he wants to test you on your ability to learn the material and not your note-taking abilities since he gives you notes for each lecture. He's super friendly too and always makes you feel welcome during office hours.
I loved Dr. Moeller and he was an amazing professor

Professor Moeller has really got teaching Orgo down to a science! He's a fantastic teacher with clear expectations on what you should learn and ample amounts of practice problems to study with. I really loved going to his office hours, as he would write down any mechanisms or drawings that would answer my questions, then give me the sheet to keep!

First day of class, told a story about why orgo is important. Very inspirational teacher but material is of nightmares. You cannot even imagine. 80 hour a test to get an A. Extremely helpful during office hours (gives you hints about material on the test).  I got shrekted by this class. Orgo is love, orgo is life.  1110 would take again.

Moeller is a great professor in that his lectures are very clear, and he is super helpful during office hours.
