His office gets packed the week of his midterms and final, but other than that, his office is pretty empty. His tests are tough, but do-able if you pay attention to his notes and mechanisms.

262 is an extremely difficult class. You will have to work hard and study early to do well on the tests. I wish Dr. Moeller would make problem sets- I don't like the idea of  his old exams being used to practice. That being said, he is one of the most caring and approachable teachers I've had at WashU. EXTREMELY willing to help.

It's a tough class, no doubt. But you can tell he's passionate about the material, which is nice. I do wish he would place more emphasis on the stuff done outside his lab (most of his test questions refer to his own experimental workstuff he directly works with) but on the whole I've still learned a substantial amount.

It has been a very disappointing semester.  I was told KDM is one of the best.  I don't see it.  He reads from his notes and does not seem to pay attention to whether the students are getting the material.  Luckily, he does hand his notes out so you do not have to pay attention.

I adore Dean Dale! She's one of the most enthusiastic and hilarious professors you will ever have, and everything on the test is something she says in class, so if you go to class then you can do well. Don't expect it to be a breeze, but if you study you're fine. She's so much fun that it's totally worth it!

I loved Intro to Archaeology. Dean Dale was so easy to understand and you can tell she loves this subject. She was funny and added lots of real life examples and activities to class to make it interesting. The best professor I've had at Wash U.

Cont'ed: We can mention early stone tools & possible assoc w A habilis & plow thru to Neand burrials, & cave paintings...All Intrsting, but we need time for techniq & method. Thr IS World Arch, but beware: Its not an "SS"! Want to learn ab more thn just stone tool tech? Take smthng else. Or just take Hman Ev-Its same information anyways minus labs.

Nvr got on to historical arch. Don't think it was rly necessary to learn ab Ardi Garhi, Lucy, Turkana Boy, etc.;  enumerate theories concerning the evolution of bipedalism; & memorize specimen cranial capacities. Save that for a Physl Anthro course.
Dean Dale is very sweet and radiates enthusiasm for her subject. Go to class and pay attention; it's a fairly easy course, but you have to put in at least a little effort.

Dean Dale is so enthusiastic about this class and Archaeology in general that it just rubs off on you.  She makes a relatively boring subject come alive and grab you.  One of the best professors I have had in my 3.5 years here.

Dean Dale is hilarious!  She also really likes her subject, and teaches it in a fun way. You definately  have to go to class and take notes, cause she only uses powerpoint as an outline.  textbook use is minimal, tests are multiple choice and not bad if you study.  highly recommended.
Dean Dale is one the friendliest professors I had. She kept the class interesting. Not too difficult.
fun fun fun.... teacher.  course a little dry.
she doesn't really teach, and doesn't really say anything useful.
She sucks. It seemed like she prefers to tell a joke than actually teach. If you like bad jokes and relearning stuff you learned in the 6th grade, then sign up.

This is a fun class, no stress involved. You'll learn a lot & will actually want to show up. And even when Darla Dale's in a bad mood, she's in a good mood...there's always a smile involved. Just shut the hell up when she's talking.

This is the perfect class for people who need to lighten up a heavy schedule.  Go to class, pay attention (not difficult to do since she is actually fun to listen to), study about 1-2 hours the night before each exam, and there's no reason you shouldn't get an A.
Dean Dale is an AWESOME Dean and Professor.  One of the most influential, fun faculty members I had met at WashU.
Energetic, enthusiastic, and easy to contact
Who doesn't love Dean Darla Dale?

Had a 10:00 a.m. lecture with her and never missed a class. Great sense of humor, perky, keeps a light tone on a dull subject. Tests are multiple choice and can go up to letter K, so studying is essential, but grading is fair.
Easiest class I've had in college.  Lectures interesting, prof hilarious.  Very engaging -- actually enjoyed attending class.  Assignments easy.
Easy to follow lectures, fun and fast paced.
love her! she is a great professor who makes archaeology (a boring subject) interesting.

Professor Carey is a great teacher and person. She cares a lot about her students and wants them to succeed. Workload isn't bad at all as long as you stay on top of it, especially supersite. She's not the easiest grader when it comes to tests but she's definitely reasonable. One of the best Spanish teachers here, she deserves a much better rep!

Professor Carey is very fun and caring in a cool aunt way. But when it comes to classwork and homework, she don't play. There is VHL Supersite work that is always assigned in the class, but you're able to work ahead and it's not hardly difficult to get done as long as you're caught up in the class. Do your best everyday and the class will be good.

Carey is a good professor, she cares a lot about her students and she's very nice but she is a very tough grader. There are a lot of assignments in this class and although she gives clear explanations, it is hard to do well in this class.

She printed out textbook pages so buying looseleaf not necessary, but do need access to VHL. Grades very harshly but eases up at the end of the semester. Takes off a lot of participation points for being late to class. Some busy work. Hard to do well. Nice person. Takes long to respond to emails. Understanding if you talk to her in office hours.

She was very passive aggressive and made me dread going to class. She will get mad if you aren't giving her your undivided attention and leave rude comments to you on canvas and decrease your grade. Take the class pass fail, it'll save your GPA and reduce your stress.

extremely caring and allowed me exceptions and extensions because of mental health reasons. just had to communicate with her about those issues before the date of the assessment or assignment deadline. if you go to class and show you're trying she'll like you

Professor Carey is a nice woman for the most part. However, she is by no means my favorite professor. She WILL NOT give 2nd chances. Miss an assignment due to mental health issues? Too bad, no make-ups. She'll expect you to email back the day of, but won't do the same herself. Would not recommend.
Absolute terrible professor. Would never recommend

I absolutely enjoyed this class. Professor Carey was nothing like I expected. I did hear some negative things about her but this goes to show you that you dont always go by what people say. She was highly engaged with the class. Topics were wonderful. Assignments were meaningful. Kudos to you if you choose this one.

Profa. Carey is very passionate about Spanish and teaching young people. She is very engaged every class period. This class is only taught in Spanish (which can be intimidating at first) but it really allows you to solidify your understanding at a faster rate. I recommend taking Profa. Carey if you are interested in Spanish and ready to work!
I love her. Yes, the work is hard and there is always homework but that is simply the curriculum, she's just amazing at teaching it. 10/10 would recommend.
Profesora Carey genuinely cares about how her students perform in class. She assigns a ton of homework, but you learn a lot as a result.

Profesora Carey is an amazing professor who encourages students to go outside of their comfort zones with the language. She is a tough grader, but is always willing to meet students and is very understanding. The course is fast-paced, but so are all the sections of Spanish so it's not her fault.

She's a nice person but is out of touch with how to effectively teach this class. The material moves quickly and she doesn't go in depth on some topics that really matter on exams. Exams are not formatted uniformly and sometimes have surprises on them. There's never material learned in class, it's always just review of what was learned at home.

Dr. Carey has high expectations for her students and keeps them accountable. Essentially, she treats them like the adults that they are. So while the material in introductory Spanish classes is difficult, she is incredibly organized, thorough, and always available to help outside of class. She's also really funny. But yes, prepare to work hard!
One of the worst professors at WUSTL. Extremely harsh for no good reason. Seems like she doesnt really care about students. Would not recommend.

Dr. Carey is well organized and very clear with her expectations. If you are not an extremely self-disciplined person DO NOT take her class. She doesn't give second chances and expects everyone to act like an adult. All the HW & dues make you feel like you are chased by a bear.
Carey was so amazing. I felt like we created a real friendship. She is lovely. I learned a lot from her and she explains clearly.
Assigns a lot of work and very harsh. Not good at explaining concepts. Do not take her class.

She has a high rating from old scores. One of Worst professors I had at Wash U. She is not a native speaker, so her methods of teaching are very rote and do not reflect a natural language speaking/learning environment.  Grades harshly, quizzes and tests were often covered in red.

She's super kind and she's always happy to help. There is a lot of homework and her lectures cover lots of content really fast, but she teaches it very well given the short amount of time. Despite all that homework, it's a pretty easy class.
Super kind, breaks down the concepts really well. Pretty easy A in my opinion, just keep up with the homework and the tests'll be fine.
Icon. Learned more from her two classes than from the rest of the math department put together - wish the school had more professors like her!
Lectures are optional any easy to catch up on.

Dr. Jager possesses every quality you would want in a professor. Her lectures are clear and concise, her expectations for the class/assignments are laid out clearly, her exams and grading policies are more than reasonable, and she's an overall kind and caring person.

Jager is an absolute GEM. I had never taken stats before so was a little nervous for this course. She is gives exceptional lectures and is one of the best professors at explaining information in a digestible way. Even though I am not a stats major, I would 100% take another course just to have her as a professor again.
She's the GOAT in stats.

Best professor on campus. Def take her 2200 before taking 3200. The lectures are explained detailed and easy to understand, the hw are helpful. The final project is so easy can literally finish it in 1 hour.
The best teacher ever, and nothing to complain about. Just follow and listen to her class, and she will make your life easier. I wish she could teach more of my stats classes.

Definitely the best STEM professor I have had here at WashU so far. Though it is hard to point out a single highlight, she does just about everything you should expect from a great professor. Her lectures are perfectly clear, the exams are fair, and she is very helpful. I had a great experience and would strongly recommend taking a class of hers.

Dr. Jager is the standard. Crystal clear lectures with guided notes. Exams were overall straightforward. The final is a group project that should be relatively easy to complete and do well on. Respects the students and never talks down to them.
Just amazing idk what else to say. First math class I've enjoyed in a really long time and it ended up probably being my favorite class of the semester. I love you Abigail Jager

You can clearly tell she really knows her material and loves teaching. My biggest criticisms are sometimes it feels like we would learn a very theoretical concept and then go straight into challenging applications of that theory. Also, I wish we had more practice problems or some sort of a study guide for the exams.

Tests are 70% of the grade but are much easier than in class problems, and similar or easier than HW problems. Content slowly goes from a 2/10 to 8/10 in difficulty, but she is always eager to explain things and help. Weekly problem sets that take 1-7 hours. 60 minutes of required videos per week. I would take this class again if she is teaching
One of the best lecturers in the math department. Explains concepts extremely clearly and cares about the success of her students. Would highly recommend.
Amazing professor! I really liked that professor Jager included examples after to teaches a concept. Really helps to fully understand new ideas. If you need a math course this is the one!

Had a great experience w/ 1011! Easy enough class, especially for non-STEM majors. Office hours were incredibly helpful and she was very understanding. She records every lecture, so you're only required to go for in-class activities. I would recommend the class to everyone.

Professor Jager is the BEST math professor i've ever had! Maybe even best professor ever. She cares a lot about her students and their wellbeing. If you are confused with anything in the class, just go to her office hours she will make sure you understand all the material before you leave. I also love how organized her class is!! She's amazing!

Jager has been one of the best professors I've had at WashU. She delivers clear lectures, and always posts review for the exam. I personally liked her teaching style of having fill-in notes. It is a sort of grindy class with 3 midterms, daily hw and quizzes, and a final project, but you can definitely succeed in this class.

Prof. Jager is the best math professor I encounter at WashU. She gives good lectures and is very accessible outside of class. The workload for this class is very manageable. Exams are also not difficult. Definitely deserves a 5 star.
Superb if you have talent.  Terrible if you are uninterested or unintelligent.

Never have I heard a professor say "I don't know what I'm doing" so many times. I stopped going to class after the first test. The class isn't that bad, but if you're hoping to learn from Rochberg you will be disappointed. Be prepared to learn from the TAs/teach yourself from the book/Webwork. Hopefully the worst teacher I will have here.

I may only be a first semester freshman, but i am confident that Rochberg is the worst professor i will have throughout my college experience.  If you like reading a textbook and never attending class, then yes take this class. If you need hands on help, STAY AWAY.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS

This was the first college class I fell asleep in- and it wasn't just once, either.  Rochberg rambles on and does weird things, has weird pauses.  Also, the exams were very easy, as was the homework.  The only disappointment was that I expected to learn more than I did.

This guy is a horrible teacher. Sure, he went to Harvard and Princeton and is obviously brilliant, but teaching isn't his strong point. His tests weren't extremely hard, except for the multiple choice, which requires true understanding of the math. Anyway, if you want to do well you better make the textbook your teacher because this guy won't be.

terrible, awful, dreadful professor. went to class twice the whole semester and listened to him ramble on incomprehensibly. tests weren't too bad, other than ridiculous true-false questions which boiled down to luck. if you take him, be ready to teach yourself from the text. don't bother showing up to class.
This class is where I got my nap in everyday, other than that it wasn't worth the time to go to lecture unless totally lost on a subject

Overall, not bad but not great either.  He seems to know a lot about the subject material, and is obviously an expert in the field, but that doesn't necessarily make him qualified to teach people how to do it.  Its probably because hes so good at it that he has a harder time conveying the topics.  Oh yeah, he always has chalk on his face--its true.

This guy is the worst "teacher" I've ever encountered in my life.  This guy just knows math, but he is far from a teacher.  Going to class made me more confused, and I barely got through the class.  Avoid him if possible.
He doesn't teach - be ready to read the book.

The worst professor I've ever had.  His lectures are boring (and you don't expect much from math lecturers in the first place) and completely useless.  He fumbles through notes and writes inaccuracies on the board all hour long, taking so long to figure out what he did that he covers no substantive material. Avoid if at all possible.

Great prof. Helpful, up-to-the-point lectures. A lot of helpful examples in class. Exams and grading scheme are very reasonable. Learned a lot from this class. He is also very helpful outside of class and is very flexible.
great prof.. his lecture always make me smile silently :))
I love this guy! He is entertaining, and he makes Variable Calc an enjoyable experience. I recommend taking Math 318.
dry sense of humor, but does lots of examples.  not an overly difficult class if you do the webwork
He was always unprepared for class and often admitted to having incorrect notes, writing them on the board anyway.  Not a good teacher as a whole
Funny, sometimes confusing.
No Comments
Awesome guy, one of the nicest professors ever, very concerned with the student's success in his class.

I took a class with this guy in 2001 when I attended. Ive been waiting for this day that I can adequately bash him. He is a jerk, severely unqualified, and has no regard for the future of his students. My future was not shaped in any way by his teachings and I have never once had a use for it. The only thing he did was reduce my GPA.

I went to WUSTL back in the 1990s, and I wish we had a website like this back then. I remember taking one of Rosenzweig's classes, and it was the most boring, pointless, wastes of my time I can remember. All in all, one of the worst experiences I had at Wash U.

The worst professor at WashU. Will not listen to students and spends entire lecture on his ideas and his way of studying the literature, refusing any discourse on the matter. A disgrace to the Department. Does not provide constructive criticism, and often speaks to students in a derogatory way. Should be removed! WashU is aware and will not act!

Easily one of the worst academic experiences of my life. Rosenzweig seems incapable of grading essays that are not written in his own words and style. I took this class after having read the reviews on here and thinking that my passion for the subject would get me through his controversial teaching style. DO NOT MAKE MY MISTAKE. Avoid at all costs!

It has been proven that students do not benefit a lot from discussion-based classes. Rosenzweig wants us to learn from his deep reservoir of knowledge instead of students' own limited, shallow understanding of literature. He is an intellectual and he won't stoop to the mediocrity of masses. Take him if you are genuinely interested in literature.

Terrible class, awful professor.   Refuses to entertain viewpoints other than his own, classes are lectures rather than discussions.  Only saving grace of class was easiness of material.  In all fairness, occasionally had interesting points to make, but outweighed by his insistence that his was the one and only interpretation of a text

Half of his lectures are incomprehensible. The other half are contradictory. His writing is littered with typos. Often he'll start a sentence and just not finish it. He is never clear about what he wants or is trying to teach. Taking this class has made me a significantly worse writer. He tries to be accommodating, but the class isn't worth it.

He deserves a much higher rating than he is given here.  At times he can ramble, but overall I have found his lectures to be extremely interesting.  He really cares about students and is always willing to help with and discuss papers. Recommended!!

It honestly astounds me that Paul Rosenzweig is a professor of anything, anywhere.  He lectured my seminar class of 7 people and never let anyone express ideas or thoughts. The class time felt unbearable.  Though I met with him several times, I could never get a handle on what he wanted for essays. The whole experience was bewildering.  Avoid.

He's a very intelligent and friendly guy. However, he's a very poor professor. He teaches a literature class, but doesn't allow for discussions of ideas and theories. There's no ONE WAY to interpret a novel. Being lectured to for an hour and half about personal philosophy is not why I came to WashU. Seriously conisdering dropping the course.

I almost didn't take Prof. Rosenzweig's class because of the terrible reviews he's gotten. The truth is, the man is one of the nicest, most intelligent guys I've ever had as a professor. I'm honestly beginning to believe that those people that rated him so poorly must be talking about someone else... or they're liars. GIVE HIM A CHANCE AND SEE!
YOU ARE ALL IDIOTS. ROSENZWEIG IS AN AWESOME PROFESSOR. TRUST  IN THE ZWEIG.
Worst professor I've had in a long time. He basically lectured the 8-person class every day and then criticized the papers instead of grading them. Don't take his classes.

A disgrace to the English department. The worst prof. at Wash U. From what I understand, the only reason he is still around is because he is married to someone important in the eng. dept. Tried to unteach every principle the Eng. dept. tries to get across in every other class.
Okay guy, awful teacher.  I agree with most of what has already been said.

Very disorganized and does not stay on topic well.  Skips around topics when discussing literature.  Hard grader.  Talks more than discusses.  Extremely unclear and talking with him does not help much.  Forgetful.
No Comments

Rosenzweig will make you write clear meaningful criticism of literature, even if he is abrasive, repetative, and distant.  His classes are lectures, not discussions, but the novels he picks are premium.  You will become a better thinker, even if it hurts, with his classes.  He is an intellectual, albeit a wierd one.
No Comments
No Comments
No lecture. No studio. No exam. No discussion.

The biggest reason why people struggle in this class is they don't start early. I completed the majority of projects early on so I barely need to attend class/pay attention to the work for a semester. This would've been a great class 10 years ago when companies were still using these technologies since his videos haven't been updated since 2016.

Tough class because there isn't much downtime between labs: HW fatigue. My mental health did take a hit however the grading was a lot nicer than I realized. He's not really accessible--just go to TA office hours. I personally felt that he likes people who really get CS and not really those who struggle more, but idk. Biggest advice: start early!!!

If you value your mental health, don't take this class. All the Wikipedia/assignments are available online with a google search, so if you want to learn the material, just self pace. The workload on top of 4 other classes is unreasonable.
If there was a 0, I would give it.

As someone with prior swe internship experience the class was easy enough imo, but i've never seen a professor so disinterested in his class. A WashU cs prof who doesn't host office hours, reply to piazza posts, or answer students questions is surprising but reflective of the cs program here. If you have no prior experience you will likely struggle

Incredibly unreasonable and inconsiderate of students' situations. Group projects were required, yet there was no way of grading individual effort and no accommodation if there were issues. And even if an incredible amount of extra work was completed he adhered to unreasonable policies. Basically has 0 respect for students.

CSE438. 4 labs (2-3 weeks each) + Final project (5 weeks) for the grade. The resources provided for each project are about half of what you need and are reused videos. Lots of outside research is needed to do each project. Each project takes anywhere from 4 to 25 hours. Some are insanely tedious and difficult, so start atleast 4 days before due.

Fantastic 48+ working hours per week makes your summer terribly fulfilled, one project per week without a second extension for any assignment with mind blowing new materials and lecture notes made decades ago.
At most 50 hours of workload per week. Significant penalties for various minor points. No late submission is allowed even with the request before the deadline.
massive work load

Isn't accommodating, thinks of his students as robots and not humans. Unwilling to help out at all with anything happening outside of the classroom, I wouldn't be surprised if he has been the cause of multiple students' depression because I know he has certainly helped mine.

Yes, 330 is a very hard and time-consuming class (esp. if you've only taken 131), but Dr. Sproull is super kind and always happy to help whenever you get stuck. He is extremely knowledgeable about web development and can solve pretty much any problem you encounter in under 5 mins. You will undoubtedly come out a better developer
Insanely smart. Class is mostly just learning yourself and A LOT of work, but most useful class I've taken yet. Highly recommend, but take it with a friend.

330 has an intense workload, but the work is valuable and the class is built for success if you put the time in. 330 covers a wide range of languages in large, interesting projects with a partner. TA OH are available several hours daily on zoom. Sproull is helpful in class, which is work time for projects. Grading criteria is very clear in rubrics.

330S gets a bad wrap but it's the best class I've taken at WashU. There is a ton of work but it built my entire resume. You learn so much and the class isn't hard if you put the time in (the rubrics are very clear so it is easy to do well). Take the class during an easy semester to build up your CS skills. Sproull is way better than his 1.9 rating.

Seems like a friendly guy. But he doesn't teach at all. He just assigns. 

Wants you to just read the wiki. Videos do nothing. Group projects are hard to do on your own so you better know a good partner! I got stuck with a dumb one on module 3.
This is an online class, and about 10% students fail.

He'll give you 2 20-min videos every 2 weeks and expect you to complete huge 20 hr+ projects with no help. These videos are also many years old as well as the homemade wiki pages you get. How WashU prides themselves on being a top tier institute while employing professors that only teach via recycled videos and short wiki pages baffles me.
worst professor that i have ever met in my life. do avoid him at all cost. this guy does not teach plus never help solve any questions posted on piazza at all and his TA are all very unhelpful.

What a sweetheart! Dr. Canfield is absolutely adorable on top of being a genuinely amazing professor. He's positively brilliant and truly cares about his students and their learning. His class is easy if you do the readings, and the TAs were great.

Great teacher, very excited about class/ the material. Good pace, keeps things interesting; sometimes tries to cram a little too much info into one class, but if you do the readings (all very interesting), you're fine.

Anthropology and the Modern World.  Interesting material, but morning class coupled with his lecture/ppt style teaching was not good.  Very sweet.  TA did a lot of teaching and all the office hours, grading, creating the tests.

Loved his aside stories, full of cute Canfield stories. Did learn a lot of useful things, Leach, Barth, Bourdieu all very useful. One of the sweetest professors. Hope to take other classes that permit for more of his creativity to show.
What a nice guy. It's always a bonus day when he wears a sweater.

such a nice guy. His works and ideas class is really, really boring, but how fun can that material really be. He understands that the material is dry and does his best with it. Meet with him after class to really get anything out of him. Also, his higher level classes are great.
N/A

Very insightful man, his lectures were not engaging to some, but they really did hit hard at some very important ideas. If you're paying attention, you'll walk away with a lot from an experience with Prof. Canfield.

He is such a nice person and covers some really interesting and worthwhile topics, though the lectures do drag on a bit sometimes.  Attendance is required which can be painful at 9 am, but the material you learn makes it worth it.

He is a nice guy. That doesn't make up for the fact that he lapses in and out of sentences. It's IMPOSSIBLE to take notes because he changes his wording so often. Oh and about the grading. He doesn't grade the exams. His TA does and this year (fall 2006) the grading in my opinion was outrageous. Take this class only if you're an Anthro major.

this class is intolerably boring. but, canfield is really nice. and as long as you study really hard the night before the exams, and memorize a bunch of stuff, which is not very difficult, you will get an A.
this class is AWFUL.  he's really nice though.

While Canfield is obviously extremely intelligent, his class is very painful to sit through. He knows what he is talking about, but the class usually doesn't. The readings are pretty hard, the class is really confusing, but the tests/paper are suprisingly not difficult. He seems like a very nice person, but his class is pretty bad.

class is very easy but ridiculously boring. easy to get an A but you have to go to class and sit through the lectures. Canfield is a sweet guy that loves the material, it's not his fault most people hate it. take it for an easy A, but bring something to do in class

The subject material was interesting and the books for the class were great, but Prof. Canfield's lectures were often disorganized and more confusing than helpful.  He was more knowledgeable on some subjects than others, which made a big difference in the quality of lectures.

He is such a sweetie. GO AFTER CLASS because he's really nice adn helpful.  He's really into what he teaches and definitely knows his stuff.  But he is kinda boring to listen to, esp in the morning. If you're interested in the subject, take his classes (otherwise they might be kinda blah for you).

Granted, he is a really nice guy, but that doesn't make up for the fact that he teaches a really boring class. Because he is such an easy grader and makes no demands of the students, there is little motivation to work. Because of this, there are no class discussions and a slow, uninteresting class.
Great teacher, great class if you are into it
awful class that was poorly taught.  he should retire from teaching.  he was disorganized, very boring, and couldn't control the class. the class complained about his structure and teaching in class.
Very great teacher, very interesting, super fun material

Jennings teaches material at a pace which is often pretty unachievable to keep up with without spending hours outside of the classroom. Though he seems friendly at a surface level he is not very approachable - in fact he is slightly condescending when asking for help/questions.

ACCT 2620 is not an easy class. If things click, that's great, but if they don't, it's hard. Jennings goes fast during lectures, so you'll walk out confused. However, there is no homework - just do like 5-6 of the practice test exams he gives you the week or two before the exam comes and you'll be good. Highly recommend office hours too.

In spite of the large amount of material to memorize in Accounting, this class is not bad. Jennings gives the past ten exams to study and ends up basically copying and pasting one of the tests for the actual test. I really liked Jennings as a professor, as he would constantly tell jokes and stories to make the class more engaging. Highly recommend.

He will teach you accounting. Read the textbook sections he tells you to, pay some attention in class, and study well (he gives 10 years of past exams and picks one randomly to reuse for each exam w different numbers), and you will do well in the class. Tells funny stories sometimes.

Jennings made accounting interesting. As long as you do the past tests (which we have the last 10 years of) and participate in class, you will be fine. One thing to look out for is that 75% of your grade is based on exams, and there is no homework. Overall interesting lecturer and a very doable course.
Jennings is a nice guy, and the material is straight forward, but he's bad at explaining things and bad at lecturing.

Professor Jennings made his Intro to Financial Accounting class as engaging and interesting as possible for an accounting course. He did his best to make confusing concepts easy to understand in lectures by using plenty of examples. Tests and quizzes can be difficult but he provides plenty of practice exams as resources to prepare.

People tend to dislike 2610 and for fair enough reasons. It's a lot of memorization and learning via trial and error (almost like learning a language). That said, Jennings's lectures are very engaging, and his decks are thoughtful and informative. Biggest gripe is that he doesn't let students leave during class (even to use the bathroom).
Professor was nice; just make sure to show up to class and participate. His tests are super hard and you need to do practice exams from previous years to do well.

Jennings was a great professor. Lecture slides on Canvas. I needed to read the textbook to understand everything. Exams were fair and he posts a ton of practice exams that were very similar to the actual exams. Jennings was available in office hours, and he always wanted to help. He really cares about students. My favorite professors at WashU.

Overall a solid professor. He went too fast through the lectures in class and I often had to find what he flew through in class within the textbook later. Midterm Exams were fair but the final covered wayyyy too much material for one person to memorize.
I still can't understand why students are not allowed to use laptops in class. The material is boring and not related to the exam. U need to do practice the previous exams if want to get an A.

Don't listen to the reviews from last year (20-21). Jennings is great, great guy and has some interesting and funny stories in class. Exams are fair, only a bit difficult due to the breadth of material covered. Helpful in office hours. 3610 is obviously more difficult than 2610 but highly recommend even if you're not an accounting major.

Jennings is an amazing professor!!!! He has bro energy and is very chill. I also truly appreciated how patience and enthusiasm when he answered questions, both inside and outside of class! His grade breakdown is tough, but he gives you the exam questions in advance so it's very fair. He made a boring subject interesting and understandable. :)

I'm TIRED of all the Jennings slander. I was scared going into this semester after reading all of the negative reviews, but he was actually a great professor. He goes through plenty of examples in class, doesn't test on random details (all tested material was covered in class), made his exams extremely fair, and was overall a really cool guy.
The Material is extremely boring and most of people around me are regretful to take this course. The tests and quizzes are difficult too.
To be honest, he is not a good professor. Teaching is very awful with very crazy tests.

Professor Jennings definitely teaches difficult material. Your grade is entirely composed of tests/quizzes which definitely raises the stakes a little. My biggest issues with this class were that he tested specific things that he glossed over during class and the test questions were much more difficult than in-class examples.
Low quality class... stupid tests and quizzes. Avoid!

For 3610, his class lectures were pretty clear (with a few exceptions) and there were plenty of examples to study from for the tests/quizzes. His class is test-heavy, as is with any accounting class so that's within the norm. I don't love the material, but he made accounting at least somewhat interesting and he's a really nice and understanding guy

This class in general was a total mess. if anyone in the future just found out who the profs will be and is reading this DROP THE CLASS! There was no textbook and tests were insanely picky so you had to memorize everything on the slides and said by the prof. The only way I could study was rewatching every lecture. Learned absolutely nothing too.

His lectures are extremely boring-very uncharasmatic lecturer that makes you fall asleep. All he does is read off the slides, which are extremely content heavy. Exams are hard and test minute details. He is a very tough grader and only has office hours once a week for two hours. He also explains things in a very unclear way. Overall very confusing.

Dr. Balota gives good lectures and is good at explaining concepts. He is certainly the toughest grader of the three who teach this course, but there's only a 1/3 chance he'll be grading your essays. Exams can be a little hard, you will probably need to memorize 15-20 pgs of notes per unit. Sounds harder than it is, just start studying early.
His lectures are extremely fast and you may miss something important that will be on the exam, and he is also just not a very nice guy
Very negative demeanor and not a very inviting / encouraging professor. I'd avoid.

This class is quite different from other psych classes. You must not only memorize the experimental procedures and results, but also who did them. And even if you've memorized very clearly everything, you would fail his test if you don't understand fully implications of each experiment. His tests are very tricky and challenging.

I would intentionally give myself a concussion by smashing my head in the library at 2 am on the ridiculously priced textbook that Balota never (not once) uses to get out of taking an exam for this class.

As a psychology major, I can easily say that someone who may have only taken this class and not other psychology classes first would be inclined to never study psychology again. The textbook is hardly, hardly ever used. I don't think he's every referenced it. Lectures are both boring and rambling/confusing. He talks fast and will just skip slides.
Not hard to get an A if you actually study
He's awful and writes ridiculously hard tests. If you don't show up to his 9AM (AMMM) class, you will fail.  Don't take this class.

As a senior Psychology major, I can definitely say that Balota's Cognitive Psychology course is one of the most challenging courses I have taken. Although the material is difficult to wrap around, Balota's teaching methods are not encouraging and inconsistent. In addition, the book was unbelievably expensive for the little amount of it we covered.

Dr. Balota's approach to teaching his subject is the best I've encountered. Rather than provide a list of things to memorize, or only describe effects generally (the definition of priming is blah blah...), Dr. Balota instead teaches through the experimental paradigms which were used to study each phenomenon. You must take excellent notes!
So difficult and an absolute horrible professor. When someone asked him to repeat himself, he flat out said no.
Balota is incredibly difficult to understand and often seems very unhappy to be in class.  His tests are ridiculously difficult even though very little material is covered.

He was the psychology lecturer for intro to mind brain and behavior. Excellent lecturer, and clearly very smart; great at restating topics so they are easy to understand. The course is fast paced and requires a good mind for psych and neuroscience though.
Great professor; high expectations but takes plenty of time to explain the material.

He doesn't write much on the board, so you have to stay awake and take good notes. He likes experiments and graphs, so flash cards help a lot. Also his multiple choice are sometimes tricky, so pay attention. But very interesting information! And he's very easy to talk to after class.

smart guy, but doesn't write notes down so you have to constantly write down every word he says. strict on grading rules, missed a grade by one point, students don't get to keep exams. won't be taking any other psych classes after this.
exams are based on lecture;no slides or lecture notes;blackboard is as disorganized as it can be
good prof, horrible handwriting. good lab to work in too, if you can.

A delight to have as a college writing professor. A lot of people dread CW, but with her I highly enjoyed it and feel that I grew as a writer, thinker, researcher, and overall student under her professorship. She supports and challenges ideas in fascinating ways & gives INCREDIBLE feedback. With her as a professor, you're sure to be a better writer

G'Sell is an effective professor for skill improvement. Offers prompt and constructive feedback, is supportive during office hours, and fair in grading. Not an easy A, but reasonable. The readings in Power and Commodity are interesting; participation and effort are key for success despite the initial reading load.
If you actually interested in becoming a good writer take her class. If not drop the class.
Professor G'Sell helped me grow tremendously as a writer. She definitely does not let you take the easy was out, but makes you so much better for it. Such a caring teacher and very understandable.
Eileen G'sell is a nice professor; however, if you want an A in this class, I would recommend taking this class with a different professor.
I highly recommend switching out of this class if you have this teacher. Even if that means that you have take citizen scientist; don't have Eileen G'Sell as your teacher!
Also, her office hours were in-person at 8:30 in the engineering-part of campus. That says enough.
Eileen Gsell can not teach and is the harshest grader. Don't take any class with her.

G'sell cares a ton about her students and her work. You need to grind to get an A, so get ready to meet with her consistently in her office hours. But, if you put in the work, you will be rewarded. She is very attentive to students needs and super accessible outside of class. She has a habit of talking over students in class at times though.
She is a tough grader, but she is EXTREMELY HELPFUL and caring! Take Professor G'Sell's class, you will work your butt off, but it will be worth it!
Extremely caring. Super funny. I recommend Power & Commodity Culture to all.

Professor Eileen G'Sell is a highly respectable professor. She leaves paragraphs of meaningful feedback on every assignment you hand in and is always accessible outside of class to talk further. You need to WORK in her class. Professor G'Sell has made me think in ways I've never thought before and broadened my imagination through her wisdom.

She's obviously passionate and really believes that what she's doing is important. However, she constantly interrupts students and fails to listen to their ideas, asserting her own before she even truly understands what her students are trying to say. She needs a reality check so that she can use her passion for teaching in a more productive way.

Impossible to get a good grade if you have differing opinions from G'Sell. She will destroy your thoughts if she disagrees on a personal level and the grade represents this. Office hours are useless: she rarely answers the questions asked and will just criticise your individual opinions/thoughts. Prefers to talk over students than listen to them.

She constantly interrupts students, will destroy your opinion if it doesn't align with hers. Most unhelpful person during office hours, won't directly answer any questions/will talk about something unrelated. Will talk over you and is a harsh grader if your opinions aren't the same as hers. Has taught me nothing about being a good writer.

Professor G'Sell is truly one of a kind. She is incredibly accessible outside of the classroom and truly is invested in each of her student's success. I would not be the writer I am today without her guidance, support, and input. Her courses are the perfect mix of structure and independence. I wish I could take another course with her!

Professor G'Sell is a knowledgeable instructor who is willing to take the time to provide you with detailed, constructive feedback on your writing. Power and Commodity culture had some really interesting readings that were not inaccessibly difficult to grasp, and I would highly recommend it to freshmen for their writing requirement!
Great teacher. Tough grader and isn't afraid to critique students, but I genuinely cared about what I what I learned in her class. Learned a lot and overall had a great experience.

I think that Professor G'Sell's teaching style is tailored to those who intend to truly grow in their writing abilities from this course. Not only are her expectations and feedback clear, but she also rewards those who take an active effort to improve as the semester progresses. Put in the effort and attend office hours, and you'll be rewarded.

This is the worst class I have ever taken. I have always excelled in writing and came as an English major. She crushed this dream. I have never met someone so condescending, passive aggressive, and pretentious. If your ideas are even slightly off from hers, good luck doing well in this class. I would rate her a 0 if I could

GOODS: Rehfeld is an amazing speaker, very professional, and engages his class with a good mix of humor, anecdotes, and thought provoking questions. Fair grader, and very speedy email correspondence. BADS: A little unreasonable in the volume of reading assignments. Subject material is very abstrat and esoteric.

Professor Rehfeld is one of the best professors at WashU - extremely engaging, entertaining, very smart. You actually walk out of his class feeling like you thought critically about a topic instead of merely relaying your opinions.  On a more personal level, Professor Rehfeld is very caring and friendly, wonderful to talk to! TAKE MODELS.

Entertaining and and obviously knows his stuff. I felt like a learned a lot, in the class as a whole but also from him. Suggestion: I think it would be funny to use the phrase "and now for something completely different" when transitioning in class. I can't believe I've never heard a professor make that reference.

Smart guy, with a kind of Jean Luc Picard-esque baldness and an entertaining lecture style. Does an excellent job of making the material (Plato, Machiavelli, etc.) relevant to present issues. Class discussion highly encouraged and usually interesting. Draconian on plagiarizers. Highly recommend his class, with the caveat that it will be toughish.

Took Ethics and Politics from him in Fall 2009. One of the best classes I took at WashU, and phenomenal professor, funny and very concerned about students. One recommendation: Really make sure you are interested in the topic, as both the material and the assignments are hard. But if you are interested, you are in for a treat.

I've been hearing rave reviews about Rehfeld since I was a freshman.  Last semester I finally got the chance to take a class, and he definitely didn't dissapoint. He's funny, brilliant and really makes the material come alive. That class was HARD, though, so don't take his classes unless you can actually make time for the work.

This once great titan of teaching has fallen victim to the usual arrogance of veteran professors.  This class started off fine, then became overly burdensome and boring.  Disappointed as previous classes were met with great interest and admiration for the lecturer.  Now, focused on making students simply work instead of learn.

This is the worst class I have ever taken at Wash U. Rehfeld is overly demanding, arrogant, and incredibly condescending. I heard great things about him; unfortunately, none of it was true. I do not recommend taking this or any class with him unless you want to work harder than you have ever worked, on boring material, just to receive a B+.

History of Political Thought I: Rehfeld is definitely one of the top professors in PoliSci and at WashU.  I don't like political theory in general, but being in his class made it interesting and enjoyable.  He has a great sense of humor and encourages a lot of discussion in class. It is definitely a challenging class, but you get a lot out of it.

A professor who is truly knowledgeable and passionate about the material he is teaching.  Does not seek to drone on and hear his own voice, instead encourages students to work hard and find their own answers.  Decent amount of work, but truly a pleasure.  Take this course!
Very entertaining and funny

History of Political Thought I. Excellent class, hard but interesting. Essays are very hard because they were different than any other essays I have ever written. Lectures are really interesting and engaging. Professor is very open and will make you really think through and defend what you believe in.

FANTASTIC professor.  Very clear.  He might seem as if he is arrogant originally in class but he is extremely open to meeting with students.  If you go to his office hours you will not only learn a ton, but he will help you with your essays and you will see what a great guy he is.   A MUST TAKE!

Political Representation is a very challenging course, and the reading can be exceedingly dense at parts. I loved it. Dr. Rehfeld is a fantastic teacher, one of the very best. He is an expert in this field, but he is still willing to change his mind if a student puts forth a good argument. He is the kind of teacher that makes learning fun.

Political Representation was very challenging, but it's one of Rehfeld's academic interests, so he was very enthusiastic about it. Readings are challenging and class discussions very lively. Be sure you're interested in the topic because it's fairly specialized.
Rehfeld is one of the best professors I've had. His class is always interesting and he illuminates old texts that might otherwise be dry.
This guy is hilarious while at the same time engaging and incredibly intelligent. After taking his class as a freshman not knowing who he was going in, I now realize how lucky i was.
Smart and funny but totally arrogant. Good lecturer though.
an absolute standout in a school of already top-notch professors. take one of his classes if you have the opportunity, even if you have only moderate interest in political science.

I have had multiple classes with this professor, each one has been great.  It would be a shame to go through WashU and not have at least one class with Rehfeld.  He is probably the best professor I have ever seen, on any and all levels.
He is so nice...I wish I could take him for all my chemistry classes!

He is now teaching at UTD. I read this comment and I just wonder who did really give this man a job at UTD to create the same horrible experiences for students at UTD!! he is one of the WORST professors I have ever seen. No sympathy or respect towards students.
He shouldn't be in academia!!!
He is terrible and unapproachable.  He does not care about the students at all.  The whole semester was all about him entertaining himself.  Worst ever.
Horrible! Never again.

Thank God the semester is over.  Gelb is terrible and does not care at all about the students.  It is clear that he was counting the days until he leaves.  I hope he will not be teaching wherever he is going.  Gelb:  get rid of the notepad!

If you expect to learn any practical chemistry, you will be sorely disappointed. He knows his stuff, but even after 40 hours of listening to him, you probably wont. His test questions are seemingly meant to trick students and you'll have seen nothing like them in class. Truly horrible.

Pompous, bad teacher. Knows his stuff, but doesn't care about students. His lectures are boring and he spends the whole time deriving equations as opposed to actually teaching us anything useful for the exams.

He's not the most engaging professor but he is very passionate about his subject. As others have said, he's very interested in theory that no one else cares about which is pretty annoying. Going to class is a waste of time as he never adds anything of importance that isn't in the notes in lecture.(just copy someone's notes)
Gelb is horrific.  He does not care at all about if we are learning anything.  I am not sure he is talking to during lecture, but it is not the students.  Get rid of the notepad and derivations.
Awful. Derives formulas the entire class period. no interaction with the class whatesoever
Spends a ridiculous amount of time on theory rather than giving examples.

WEIRD.  He is off the charts weird.  I don't like just copying his notes as he writes them, but at least we know exactly what we have to know.  His tests are straight forward, and a bit too easy.  With so many students, I would rather harder exams to spread out scores, but also to better push us.  I'm not sure all the derivations are needed.
Quirky guy. Class merely consisted of us copying what he wrote on lecture slides onto our own copy. Tests were fairly straight forward (more than first semester).
Overall Gelb's lectures are well-organized and easy to follow but I found that he spent way too much time on derivations of equations rather than applications. His hair is awesome though.

the man you want for gen chem II. lectures are extremely well organized and clear. he makes it interesting as well. great hair, great sense of humor. exams are challenging but doable (not like 111 exams). quizzes pretty straightforward if you do the hw. the hw problems he makes up are real hard, but generally you get by with the text problems.
he tries to teach but tends to go off tangent of what we are supposed to be learning about. he seems pompous, but i think it might just be the hair.

This is an excellent professor. His lecture slides were very organized and his derivation of equations in class really helped me to connect and understand many chemical principles. His tests can be a little bit tricky initially, but by the end of the semester you'll be able to pick them out. Overall excellent professor!
He knows his stuff, and his exams are not impossible.. Not too many curve balls.
Lev Gelb is WONDERFUL. He's organized and clear. It's extremely evident that he really knows his stuff. Plus he's endlessly entertaining.
Far better than Buhro, whom I had first semester, Gelb was clear and prepared for class, though his expectations for the exams were often unclear.

shidal is rlly nice but the grading was so inconsistent bc its done all by TAs which can be annoying. if u go to shidal he will be helpful which is good tho. all the lectures r async on canvas which can be tricky if u r a procrastinator. the midterm was hard but if u stay on top of watching the videos and asking questions during studio its fine

Prof. Shidal is a great professor. His explanations are very clear if only a little drawn out. If you go to his office hours with a bug in your code he will likely find it in less than a minute and explain why you have the issue.

RMP doesn't give me the option to select CSE433 with Shidal (so putting this review under 422). But this is the second class I've taken with Shidal and he's just as caring, accessible, helpful, smart, precise, and illuminating as always. He remains my favorite prof in WashU's CS department.

Shidal is an amazing teacher. He is always available to talk to students and teaches the course really well. I left this course learning a lot and would love to be able to have him is my professor again. I recommend anyone to take a course with him if you can.

Lectures are certainly boring but mainly serve to reinforce the material presented in module recordings; they can be safely ignored during studio if you have a good understanding of the content before class. Studios themselves are super helpful and give great hands-on experience for C++ principles
A flipped class style with in-class studios. You mainly learn C++, which is not a super interesting topic. He is quite boring with the lecture, but a nice guy overall. Exam is hard but curved.

Shidal is a pretty good professor. My only complaint is that it's hard to understand his lectures in the beginning (not super good at dumbing things down) but once you understand how to code in c++, it's so much easier. Do the assignments early cause they can take some time to complete. He's really attentive to students and helps as much as he can

Best CS professor I've had so far at WashU. Genuinely cares for students. Class not super difficult, especially if you've already used C++. Assignment instructions are super clear, just need to follow those. Sometimes graders are a bit tough, but not excessively so. Can do studios asynchronously, which is nice.

The course is very straightforward and doable except for the one test which is disproportionately hard relative to the rest of the class, but it isn't that bad if you just focus on memorizing C++ syntax & understand pointers (you get a cheat sheet)

Jon is one of the best professors in the CS department. His lectures/explanations are clear, precise, and illuminating. He really understands how to make potentially daunting concepts incredibly digestible. Beyond that, he is caring, super nice, and approachable. I found 332 incredibly organized and an exceptionally valuable class.
Just a phenomenal professor and person.
Great professor. Great vibes
His CSE332 course is awesome. It's elective but I highly recommend this course.
i love jon he rules.

Shidal is knowledgeable and a good lecturer. Each class is a required studio, and sometimes a free day to catch up on studio or hw. The assignments can be large, but he will frequently accodomate for any difficulties the students may face. He is decently active on piazza. The 1 exam is the only flaw which does not suit the class. He curves it a lil

Shidal is a great professor, he is so passionate about the material and wants you to learn. The class is cool and you get to work on a variety of projects. It is very code heavy, so be ready to do a lot of coding, and it is not an easy A, he makes you work for it, but he is a great prof and loves to see you engaged, so make sure to make an effort.

Know his stuff, and seems alright as a guy. However, he is rarely around to help you and doesn't try to seem open. Lectures are informative, but the one test is easily the worst part of this class and you do not get prepared for it.

After taking this class, I also TA'd for him multiple semesters. I appreciate Prof Shidal not just as a lecturer but as a mentor. If you get the chance I recommend not only the class but coming back to TA, he's a great supervisor and really values feedback both from students and TAs.
Professor Shidal is very caring in the studio and will explain everything in the easiest way to let you understand.

Professor Shidal is very accessible outside of his office hours and was willing to meet with me just about anytime I emailed him. He gives really thorough explanations and actually cares about you as a student. He pretty much gave everyone extensions as long as you asked in a reasonable amount of time.

Terrible professor, always contradicts himself and incapable of answering questions. For such an important topic in CS, he hasn't updated the materials in years. The only takeaway from this course is that I should have self-studied.

Worst ever, hates questions , same exam same homeworks every year! All about how well u squeez info into ur cheatsheet! About how fast u can answer in the exam! You won't benefit much.

 Material is not up to date

The poorest choice of professor for such an important material in cs field. Avoid him. Super long exam with very short time window. Boring lectures where he is unable to answer any question clearly, while he forces students to ask them. Flipped class, with recorded lectures and mandatory zoom Q&A sessions. Unreasonable.

Super dry and boring. Flipped classroom (watch lecture at home, go to class for Q&A). Basically, he just reads from the slides. You probably learn much more and better from reading the textbooks. Hard test. Materials are definitely interesting and stuff that every cs major should know. Too bad we can't get a better prof to do such an important job
The best course in WashU that I enrolled for. A ver caring professor, expects all his students to do well. Would take his courses any day

One of the best courses that I have taken with this professor. Make sure to attend the class and participate. Lots of homework and labs, but really easy to do because it won't take more than two hours to do a bunch of them together. Clear grading criteria. Be aware of the exams timing and competition.

Horrible.  Unable to answer any questions in class.  Exams are ridiculously long and are given in a shortened period of time.  His explanations are vague and the best students are the ones that can fit every little detail onto their exam cheat sheets.

One of the worst profs in the CS department. Very unclear in lecture - he invites students to ask questions, but then can barely answer them. Tests on minutiae and acronyms without ensuring understanding of larger concepts (put a *lot* of detail on your cheat sheet to do well). Cuts off time on tests after 60 min. so that students won't finish...
He is really patient. To do well, you need to go for every class

You have to work hard. It is a very difficult class. You can tell that the professor really want the student to do well. But there are lot things to learn in this class. You have to work hard but it is definitely worth it.
Good course for those interested in going to the industry. Will cover a collection of concepts not known to many.
It is really helpful.

Very useful course especially for people who are seeking a job in industry. I believe the professor did a lot of preparation on this course to present high quality material. With his vast knowledge and expertise in this field, you will definitely learn a lot. The grading was generous, so you don't have to worry as long as you study fair enough.
Really helpful. Especially for those who wants to go to the industry right after graduation

I think it is a great class. The professor teaches everything from the fundamental level. The homework is quite related to the teaching material, and not very hard. Overall it is a good choice to chose it as an undergraduate course.

This was one of the best courses which I ever took. I went from not knowing anything about networking to deep appreciation to the field and looking for ways to be active in this field. The exams were more than fair. In a world were any device with a battery is networked I highly recommend this class.

Studying with Dr. Jain was an amazing experience. All that you need to know to start you on networking is in this class. Jain is very knowledgeable, he cares about the students. Lectures were very interesting and the book highly recommended. Dr. Jain will explain and go over any question that you may have, I wish more prof. were like this.

Professor Jain is one of the renown experts in the field of networking. He is very knowledgeable about the material. It is difficult to convey all of the material in only 2 hour and a half lectures a week, so most of the learning comes from the textbook.

I enrolled this course in FL2010, which motivates me enroll more courses taught by this professor. I really enjoyed this course absolutely. In common sense, this course is the best one in PhD core courses. Don't hesitate to enroll this course.

Very excellent course! I can't believe why some guys rated this teacher so terrible. This course teaches us a lot. The percentage of grade is very reasonable. Best of 2 mid-exams will be chosen as your final mid-exam grade. I've never seen other Profs provide this kind of mechanism. Also, the content covered in every class is awesome.
Mean

CCY is a genius and has a respectable portfolio, but she doesn't teach. She does bring students on field trips, but she doesn't give lessons. She bases the grade on essentially a 30-page paper written by two students and 2 take home exams that require around 10,000 words for a decent grade. BE ON TIME AND WRITE A TON ON ASSIGNMENTS if you take

I was very excited about this class at first but: CCY has very specific expectations for her students but does not make them clear at all. The class is based on 1 massive paper and 2 extremely long written tests and that's it. She is hard to reach, not communicative, did not give a single lesson/teach anything, and creates a hostile environment.

CCY is an absolutely atrocious teacher. She is rude, condescending towards students, and will literally make you cry with the amount of work she assigns. I will never hear the words "specificity" or "detail" ever again and not think of this class. DO NOT take ANY class with her.
I would never take this course again, even if I was paid.
CCY is just so mean. She will laugh at you for getting questions wrong, scold you for being two minutes late, and give you a laughable amount of reading and projects.
Ridiculous amount of papers for a 101 class. Doesn't bring up the textbook in class at all but the essays are all about textbook. Grade essays and projects extremely harsh. Avoid her at all cost.
UPDATE: I changed my entire major just to avoid this professor. And it was worth it. DO NOT TAKE CLASSES WITH HER.

Yeakey is clearly an academic first and an instructor second. Very blunt and condescending but will like you if you answer her questions clearly and with some sense that you've done the readings and know a little bit about what's being discussed. Exam answers are straight from the readings. Project is annoying but doable. Would not take again.

I agree with the rest of the comments below about Professor Yeakey. However, I will say if you do have to take this class, no matter what, make sure your course and personal workload are very light. This class should only be taken if you have light work in other classes. Not a fun class and is stressful.
Don't listen to the 15+ years worth of negative reviews. Take the class!

She is very condescending and constantly interrupts students who try to answer her questions. The class would have been very interesting if she wasn't so mean and made the class so unnecessarily hard (expects us to read hundreds of pages of textbook + novels each week).

Absolutely the worst professor I have ever seen in my entire life. She does not and is incompetent to teach, thinks she know a lot and never gives clear instructions. No mercy is given under any sort of circumstances if you have a conflict, and managed to be very demanding, demeaning, and condescending at the same time.
i need a therapist

By far the worst professor I have ever had in my entire life. I am considering changing my entire major just to avoid her. Do not, under any circumstances, take classes with Prof Camp-Yeakey. She is mean, bitter, cannot teach, hates her students, and is an excessively tough, punitive grader. Avoid the Urban Studies program AT ALL COSTS.
Professor will like you if you put in effort and participate

Read the reviews & decided to take it anyways. I went into the class very optimistic, tried to see the very best in her. I respect her as an academic but not at a all as a teacher. So condescending. All you do is watch movies in class and talk about current events but the tests are very specific and are on the hundreds of pages of textbook readings
Worst teacher I have ever had

Very difficult class with a lot of reading, but if you read it all and take good class notes you will be fine on tests. She is very blunt which can be off-putting, but she is also very straightforward in her grading. She tells you to study everything and you are tested on everything. POD projects are not difficult if you put in the work.

The absolute worst class I have taken at WashU. For starters, she spends many 2 1/2 hour class periods putting on videos because she is incompetent and cannot teach.  She barely touches upon  concepts and then creates midterms and finals solely based off topics never discussed in class.  Urban Studies was an interest of mine and no longer is.

His lectures were very fast and he barely went over details. He just wrote examples and never elaborated them. He stood in front of what he wrote, so it was hard to take notes during lectures. On the other hand, his exams were very easy, and the textbook for the class is very helpful. You could do well in the class just by reading the textbook.

His lectures go by stupidly fast and are pretty hard to pay attention in (so if your calculus is not very strong, you WILL suffer). If you can't keep up with his lectures, try to use the textbook-he wrote it, and his lectures tend to pretty much just be a copy of his textbook sections. However, his understanding of the content is second-to-none.
The lectures are so bad

Avoid taking any course with Krantz at all costs. He may be a great mathematician but a horrible teacher. You can cheese the exams really easily but don't expect to actually learn anything. Most days he ends class early, stands in front of the board, and writes extremely small. He doesn't care about his students at all.

Krantz is passionate about differential equations and math as subject. He is generous when grading exams and homework, but that is unfortunately a necessity. Krantz's lectures are paced too quickly and he frequently stands in front of the bord he is writing on, not allowing to you so take notes until he's already moved on to the next section.

Krantz class is very straight forward. His lectures are very example heavy which is amazing. He isn't the most elaborate lecturer but it suffices. His exams are near identical to the practice and aren't difficult. The homework isn't too much and it covers most everything you'll need to know for exams plus some. Try and you'll succeed.

Besides being a quite potent form of anesthesia, Krantz is extremely quiet when lecturing and writes very small on the board, making it impossible to glean any information whatsoever from lecture. But he sticks to the textbook pretty well, so just read that instead of going to class.

I took Professor Krantz's Math 310 in 2018, and he was very nice and easy, but Complex Variables is a mess. He teaches rigorous complex analysis so fast and vague that nobody could follow his writing on the board, and he breaks his "tradition" of easy exams by a tricky final that is very different from all the practices. I learned very few math.

There is no class at all. 5 min recorded audio for each lecture reading through his crappy lecture slides in a speed so fast that 0.5x won't help you. Spend 15 min in “lecture” and 1 hour in hw, and you pass with A.
My message is simple: if you have to take Calculus I as a major requirement but do not like Calculus and want to get good grades, take Calculus I Extended with Professor Steven Krantz.
Exams are not hard if you've taken the practice tests, and genuinely cares for students.
If he's teaching 233 or 310 or anything, just go for it. You would not regret

Lectures are a little boring and moves pretty fast. Could probably just skip lecture and read the textbook as he copies his lectures straight out of the book. Funny guy though and his exams are carbon copies of the practice exam so its easy to get a good grade

Professor Krantz made calc 3 easy to learn by presenting it in understandable and bite sized portions. In addition, he often told fun and or hysterical stories about his past life or made quips in class. He was frugal with time and often ended 15 or so min early. Take Calc 3 with him if you can! It made math fun again.

Krantz is honestly amazing. His lecture pace is perfect because he only tells you what you need to know. He regularly lets class out early because he's so efficient with time. Sometimes he will bless you with a joke or story. Also, his test never have surprises so if you go to class you're pretty much guaranteed an A.

Amazing teacher and very understandable and smart.  He always randomly gives us a 1-2 minute joke in the middle of lecture and is ready to answer any questions.  In order to do well on his exams just a simple run through of the practice exam will suffice.  He get's right to the point without any fluff, meaning class is usually let out in 45 minutes

Professor Krantz is a wonderful and brilliant professor -- he is extremely funny, and his exams are pretty easy as long as you pay attention in lecture.   His grading is generous.  He seems to really connect with the students.

Great teacher! Admittedly I would get lost in the lectures, as he is incredibly smart, but I wrote it all down and, come test time, I realized that I knew everything that was asked on an exam. Very reasonable exams and very helpful in office hours
Great lectures while also being one of the smartest professors here. Gives very reasonable tests. If you have the chance take his class

Really great prof! Lectures were interesting and engaging. Not really the warmest prof ever, kind of brutishly funny. Homeworks got to be incredibly difficult, but exams were ridiculously easy because he was just looking to see that you know the very basics (and he means it). Would definitely take another class with Krantz.
Not clear on paper feedback and is dismissive and condescending when you articulate views that are not his...

He basically just talks about his personal interpretation of the films and thinks that he's always right and everyone is is just "learning". Accessible after class, but doesn't give any good feedback and isn't helpful if you are struggling. Has 1 favorite and doesn't really like anyone else. Was obsessed with Freud (??? red flag).

This guy may be the worst professor I've ever had. He says things won't be on quizzes and then they are, he openly states that his subsection will be graded more harshly than the TAs, and he is inaccessible outside of class. It truly seems like he wants you to fail. How this man is a professor at Wash U stumps me. I hate him.

BEST PAPER FEEDBACK I'VE EVER GOTTEN IN MY ENTIRE LIFE. Lectures can get long and confusing because he covers everything from business to technology to artistic motivations. There's just a lot going on. Really improved my understanding of writing in film studies. Also helped me win a game of Trivial Pursuit with what I've learned in this class.

If you want to be in the film industry then stay away from this guy.  He is an arrogant  academic type whose is lost in his own semanticsemiotic pseudo intellect.  While real creatives are out there making it happen, Paul is aloof and removed.  Good class for a philosophy major.  Bad for anyone who wants to be in business.

The least I can say is that I haven't wasted my time with this course. Prof. Paul introduced me to incredible movies and my analytical skills have greatly improved. Paul is quite demanding but if you work, it will eventually pay off.

Sure he's pretentious, but he's a good professor. Definitly knows his subject matter. American Horrors was one of the best classes I took during my college career. Ridiculously hard grader but will bump up your final grade if he thinks you improved.

extremely arrogant and haughty. he comes across as an understanding instructor and is willing to "help", but will punish you in the end with a low grade and **** comments that oppose your interpretations and support his preconceived ideas of the particular films.

Prof. Paul is a great guy and a great teacher.  I've had him for five classes, and his impact on my college experience cannot be overstated.  If you don't like him, that's your problem.  And yes, he's a hard grader.  Wake up!  It's college!

At first I had the same impression as others here, but after speaking with him about these problems, he was understanding. He definitely has his own take on film and doesn't allow a lot of other interpretations. Now that I am in another cinema course, though, I am VERY glad he taught this way; he prepared me to go on to analyze film my own way.

History of World Cinema.  I think he is a little boring, but I found him to be pretty reasonable.  He usually pushed back the deadlines of our papers.  He gives excellent feedback on the papers too.  He is relatively organized and (usually) prepared for class, except that he is almost always late.

Paul is a knowledgeable and well-versed teacher, but he knows it, so he comes off as arrogant. Also, he has a fetish for following "rules" to the extreme and, although he does make exceptions for a special few, is often unwilling to assist students in any way (save meeting during his office hours, etc). If you can handle him, there is much to learn

Horrible class. Paul is arrogant and is not very accepting of views that he doesn't hold. Will write only good comments on paper and then give you a bad grade (worse than C). Class is boring and you fall asleep. Anything left to say?

He's the devil...all around jerk...mean to his TA, pretencious and belittling, he told me my ideas were great and original on  an essay and then gave me a C-...I just want to continuously poke him with a spork
Nice guy, not much of an orator, tho...

boring yes
 good class? depends on what your looking for
 american was much better than world
 good teacher, but voice that can put you to sleep

Bill Paul is misunderstood.  The majority of people slamming him on this site are the people I would look back at in class and ask to shut up because they were not paying attention.  Paul is well versed and intelligent. To do well with Paul you have to put forth effort.  All you haters are lazy.
worst teacher ever known to a student

He has a lot of stuff to say, and reads from his notes as lecture, while pulling film clips to support the interpretations he cites. One of the few profs I've met that will just tell people they're wrong (thank God!). Grades harshly, but well. Provides lots of historical background to the films.
avoid taking his classes.
I loved Professor Hale!! I feel as if she's super genuine and accesible outside of class. Interesting lectures with her mini-anecdotes as well.

While I think Hale is a really kind person, she's not a good teacher. She uses her life story to teach and goes on long tangents. With no attendance required, there's no motivation to hear her talk about herself more confidently than class content. She doesn't always post lectures. Also, if you don't do well on the 1st exam you're nearly screwed.

Hale is super nice as a person. That being said, she is not the most put-together professor. She often goes on tangents and tells irrelevant stories. Not to say that it wasn't entertaining at points. Though this does make it harder to focus in lecture. That's why I ended up watching lectures online at 2x speed. Interesting class material though.

Sandra is very interesting to listen to, you will learn literally every corner of her brain. You will know her family, her childhood, but all of this makes it very difficult to actually understand the information you need to know. I would love to have her as a friend however a teacher she makes it a tad difficult.
