Lots of reading and it's important to have a general idea of all content to get good scores on the weekly discussion boards and quizzes, but content is pretty interesting. Final project requires barely any class content, but really cool.

Either you love Nacho or you hate him. I found his lectures to be very informational and entertaining. There is a LOT of reading in this class, but the weekly assignments are simple and pretty open-ended in your favor. Nacho has a lot of passion for these topics, and it almost makes you care that much more about the class too. Overall a great guy!

Nacho is by far one of the most inspirational and knowledgeable people I have ever met. Though he is unafraid to express his own opinions or assign lengthy readings, he is receptive to feedback and respects the diversity of perspectives of his students. Making the commitment to engage with the materials will result in an unforgettable experience.
there are a lot of reading but it's nothing you can't skim through

this class made me hate college. when i tell you this class will literally take away your sanity and any chance at having a social life, i mean it. one assignment consists of multiple 45+ pages of readings, videos, a prep board, and a quiz all in one. please save yourself the trouble and find another class to take :)

IMO Nacho is impressive with extensive knowledge and does a great job of recounting L.A. history factually. My only complaint is he spoke the entire class and people zoned out. Easy to get an A if you do assignments + lots of extra credit opportunities. I read a life-changing book for this course and received wonderfully kind comments on my paper.

Nacho teaches opinion as fact and says some really inflammatory things. You have to agree with him in your discussion posts and papers in order to do well. He assigns more reading than I have ever had and most of it is redundant. He is interesting and has a lot of knowledge, but is hard to follow in lectures and mumbles a lot.

Nacho was, overall, a very good professor to learn about Latin American affairs with. The course is reading intensive, with a multiple-choice quiz and a 200-word prep board due at the beginning of each week--lots of extra credit is offered if you do happen to miss something though. Attendance is mandatory, so be prepared for the commitment.

I hate this class. Unless you hate America and are ignorant of politics you will also hate this class. Every lecture comes from a strong socialist bias, and the amount of reading is insane. It is a very one-sided class and other opinions are not acceptable. Sure, he can be a funny guy, but I walk out of class with anger every time.

I would highly recommend avoiding Nacho. His lectures are long and not easy to follow. He does not allow computers in class and is constantly berating students who don't study what he considers worthwhile fields. He assigns 20 page papers and is not at all helpful if you go to office hours. If you want a good grade, be ready to suck up to him.

Nacho is one of the best professors I have had at WashU.  He is one of the top people in his field, but still cares so much about his undergraduate students.  I have asked him for recommendations, gone to office hours, and overall could not recommend him or his classes more!

One of the most influential professors of my college experience. You'll do a lot of reading but he makes lectures incredibly interesting. Definitely take this class! It changed how I see the world. Also Nacho is fantastic and will write rec letters for you if you need it.

one of the best classes that i had at wash-u. although he's a hard core leftist, he has strong reasons for that and it is very worth learning such perspective. frequent quizes, A LOT of reading, need to cite all the books used in class to write a mini 8 page paper but his class is sooo inspiring.
Amazing Teacher! LOVED IT

I can't believe only 2 people have rated Nacho. He's an AMAZING professor and if you have the chance to take him... do! He's extremely smart and thought provoking and he really cares about getting to know all the students personally, even in a class of 120 people.
This is my favorite class. I've had a few health problems so far this semester and "Nacho" has been very flexible and understanding. He's a great guy, and his class always wakes me up in the morning.
This professor is awesome! He assigns A LOT of reading, but other than that his classes are always really interesting, he knows his stuff, and he's funny. I would definitely recommend this class.
Took for Portfolio Plus. Early is a fair grader and caring to students of different art levels.
Had him during the Portfolio Plus summer program as our drawing professor and was incredibly helpful and respectful.

Professor Early has a very logical and formal approach to teaching drawing which may not be the most engaging format for some students. However, I found his class to be very helpful in building upon my abilities as an artist and designer.
I mean, who doesn't like John? He's the most approachable professor in Sam Fox in my opinion. I always enjoyed my time in his class.

One of my fav professors - take his class if your schedule permits! I met over three people who have taken his classes in the past, and once they heard I'm taking John's class they all congratulated me. Always answer every email outside class. Organized. Fun projects with a sense of accomplishment. Helpful and considerate. Absolutely fair grader.

I had 2D design with John and he really pushed us to think about design concepts. His projects force you to think critically and he emphasizes process. His 3 projects each took about 1-2 months to complete, though we were all pressed for time during the week of the deadline. Good about replying to emails. Pretty ok feedback. Fair grader.

I think John is famous for being nice. He gives good feedbacks even after class, and he's always accessible if you email him with any questions. He is caring and fair towards every student. His projects are interesting and inspiring. It's a pleasure to take his classes; I've already signed up for another drawing class that he'll teach next year.

He's very clear on what he wants to see from the projects he gives out- but even if you stick with what you want, as in not take his advice, he still respects you and your work. He's very enthusiastic about teaching and has a chill personality.

I really like John's class because he gave us a lot of freedom during the creative process but guided us in the right direction. He gave us a lot of time for each project which made it easier to make a complete piece in the end. would recommend

He thinks art can be taught through only logic and the whole time in his class it felt like math. Step A, Step B, and so forth. I just really did not like his teaching style. Many other professors I had were on the contrary. It was just him.

John's very into creative process, his feedback is good if you're willing to talk to him as you work (not the night before). Sometimes John can change his mind on you which is confusing, so be willing to change your ideas as well. Be ready to talk about your work. You really get out of the class what you put into it. Overall John's great. #lovejohn

The projects we did were extremely limiting and elementary. They actually  made me like art even less because they took a lot of work and were a huge chore with little to no personal voice/exploration. His grading criteria is extremely questionable and not consistent. Vague feedback. Likes concepts that are very elementary and overdone. Hated it

John always helped me pick out little mistakes and suggest changes that helped make my pieces much better. He is very supportive of students of all skill levels, and can help make even a beginner student an amazing artist.

John is eager to help students make their projects more successful, but often his advice is rather vague and unwelcome at first. But once you start thinking about it and applying it to your project, all becomes clear and you find yourself with an amazing end product.

Would highly recommend him as a teacher! I loved his 2D design class, and I learned a lot from him. If you ask questions and use the in-class work days he gives you to check in with him you won't have a problem. Missing critiques will kill your grades. The assignments can take awhile, but overall the workload is reasonable. Expect to work hard.

John is a pretty good professor but can be ambiguous with his instructions, which can be tough because he can be very particular about what he expects in a project. If you are clearly putting a lot of effort in and ask lots of questions, you will do well. Projects can be complex but he gives a very reasonable amount of time to complete them.

I love John!!He is amazing! Though sometimes he is ambiguous( well he kindda likes subtle things and is too nice to say bad things) which confuses people, he is a great instructor who is there for you! And the class was really interesting!

I had John for 2-D design, and he was a really relatable, easy to interact with professor.  He is really engaged with his students and can be helpful in developing ideas and working through your process.  It can be a little hard to understand how to succeedget an A on a project, and his lectures on design concepts can be a little simplistic.

John tells you what you don't want to hear, which can be hard but usually results in a better final work. He doesn't really take into consideration how much time you've devoted to a piece, and will try to steer you in another direction if he doesn't like where you're going. Sometimes it can get frustrating but you definitely learn a lot from it.

One of my fav profs at WashU. Readings can be a bit difficult to understand sometimes, & pop quizzes are specific on certain details of the readings. If you read closely, you should have no problems. Exams are also quite difficult, so make sure you study. His classes aren't easy A's, but what you gain from his classes is invaluable.

Rosas was great, really strong and coherent lectures, which helps for an intro class. Made material accessible but also engaging and interesting in a large classroom setting. Definitely my favorite of the poli sci intro classes at WashU, I'd recommend if you're looking for an accessible poli sci course about a wide range of various political topics

I liked Rosas a lot. He's very well spoken and gives great lectures with a lot of detail. He's super knowledgeable and loves talking to students outside of class and getting to know them. Felt like I had a good relationship with him, even though it was a big lecture class. He's a pretty bad exam writer TBH, but he curves a lot so it's all good.

I learned a LOT from Rosas. He has a depth of knowledge and teaches with nuance and detail. The tradeoff is that he is a harsh grader. Students will not get an A unless they study well for his tests and pay close attention to readings and lectures. Overall, I think taking his class is definitely valuable, but be ready to work hard.

The material isn't too difficult to understand, but it's definitely important that you keep up with the reading and study your notes well in advance of each exam. Test questions were mostly focused on small details from lectures/the book, so good memorization is key. You definitely need to attend class because he gives frequent pop quizzes.

Doesn't technically take attendance but gives very frequent pop quizzes, and missing those quizzes can kill your grade. Definitely important to keep up with the reading for the sake of the quizzes and exams. The material is very simple to understand if you do the reading, but it can be kinda time-consuming to keep up with it.

For his first time teaching comparative politics, he did a fine job. Lectures were pretty bland and restated what the textbook said for the most part. He explained confusing topics well. The pop quizzes were annoying and hurt a lot of peoples' grades. The exams were straightforward. Basically if you read the textbook and take notes, you'll be fine

Readings are tough but Rosas is flexible and lets us know what he's looking for with each assignment he gives us. He's a great practitioner of empirical-style polisci. You walk away from each lecture feeling smarter about this style of academia and in understanding politics. Very, very helpful in giving feedback & direction with your research.

Excellent, passionate, and insightful. He asks challenging questions which makes discussion engaging and has a nuanced, economic lens of examining Latin American politics. inspired my interest in the region's politics! Lots of homework and reading, but not too difficult to stay on track if you take good notes and pay attention in class.

Class was artificially rigorous in the sense that material was not hard but assignments were time consuming because of verbal trick question type approach and because they were uploaded to give 5 days to complete them and not 7 in majority of classes. Also, evaluation stressed homework more than exams for some reason. I hope it is same for everyone

Interesting material and a very smart guy, but his lectures were not very engaging.  There was tons of information and it was difficult to process everything in class at a pace that could keep up with the lecture.  Also, really tough exams.  Rosas clearly has a very firm and detailed grasp of the material, but was not great at transmitting it.

Rosas singlehandedly turned me off of Political Science... go to class and you'll be bombarded by 90 minutes of solid information without any time to breathe.  Tests were ridiculously inconsistent, and class was murderously hard to sit through. He is a great guy though!
He speaks to quickly and can't be understood. His tests ask unfair questions and his review guides are useless.

When he speaks quickly, he can be a little difficult to understand.  You must do the readings to do well on the pop quizzes.  I thought the grading was fine, only because i got a 100s on both exams though :-).
He makes you work for your grade but you learn a ton of really interesting stuff.  Definitely recommend his Latin American Politics course.  He has a cute accent and all the girls think he is hot!!
He is from Mexico, so a little hard to understand.  He is an incredible guy and a thorough scholar, however.

To his credit, it is a difficult class to teach...so many countries.  He made it clear that the concepts were more important than the details, but it's still kind of difficult to prepare for the exams because you don't know what to study.  Really nice and helpful, but lectures droned on and on...
great professor, very smart, but class was too big and grading is a little weird.

Guillermo is soooo nice (and some would say hot). His grading scale is weird and he's not the best lecturer. Kind of disorganized. I guess just take it if you like good looking, nice, 30 something Mexican guys
Take his class if you can. Chill guy, assignments and exams are easy, and free pizza last week of lecture.
Very knowledgeable prof with loads of practical experience that he shares with his class.
Awesome prof with loads of practical examples. Loves how he taught us to work with live data. Do all the assignments before exam. Helped with job search.
By far the best teacher I have met. Answered my questions patiently during office hours.

This professor does a lot for his students to succeed in his class. The subject is tough and the tests are hard but the professor is knowledgeable and able to explain these concepts clearly. His lectures are also engaging.
Awesome prof! Thanks for the recommendation letter.
Professor Sarkar is knowledgeable, patient, and caring. His lecture is well-structured and very informative. If you wanna learn more about Investments, then Sarkar's course is just right for you.
Sakya is awesome. If you go to class and take good notes, tests will be a breeze.
Great Professor; he prepares you for the quizzes and the final exam. Moves fast but he will slow down if you have questions.
The course material is interesting and the professor is easy to understand. I learnt a lot.
Always almost late for everything, and asked for leave for too many classes. And if you are taking an online final, be ready for an noisy professor keeps talking to you.

He constantly kept talking during the final, and acted indifferently to students. He over-squeezed the test time just to make it purely harder in a timely manner instead of testing your true understanding of materials. He grades you not by the score you earn, but by comparisons to peer students, and his class was tedious and lengthy! Unbelievable!
when I was taking the final exam, I could always hear noise coming from the zoom, which is very disturbing

I felt frustrated by his class. I excel in my stuff, but his class was still dull to me. It was lifeless, lifeless, lack of interactions at all. And you can't believe he often publicly played with his feet during the class! (my friends have recorded it) He often played with his phone during lecture! And he doesn't reply to emails whatsoever.
please just be nice
Great prof taking Fixed Income with him again.
Sarkar gives great lectures. He has clear grading criteria. Quick response through emails.

Prof. Sarkar seemed as thought he was genuinely interested in the success of the students and was clearly passionate about the material. He was also very encouraging during lectures, in class exercises, and with regards to homework. He also was very open to identifying areas of improvement.

Dr. Gais is, without question, the best professor I have had over the course of my college career (I am a senior). When you engage in the class, you will learn so much, be exposed to new and different ideas, and be challenged. You have to put in the work and be on top of things, and if you are, you will succeed without a doubt.

TAKE THIS CLASS! Learned so much and felt like a much better student coming out of it. You have to go to class and participate consistently to get a good participation grade, but you'll feel smarter after doing it. Prof is super great and so intelligent. You get what you put into this class for sure. The students control the experience. No screens.
Show up to class. You have to participate frequently its discussion based. Gais is awesome. No screens.

Absolutely no criteria for grading, very subjective. She grades things based on her own opinion of them and doesn't tell students how she is going to grade projects until after students turn it in. Be prepared to participate, participation is heavily weighted, though she does prefer to call on male students. Nice professor, but doesn't grade well.

Extremely interesting material and class discussions. King and willing to work with students. Very manageable workload. However, she strongly favors people and voices that agree with her own as well as non-outspoken individuals, and this is reflected in the grade you get.

Class is exceptionally easy, but the only grade with actual stakes is participation, and there is no actual indication of your participation until you get the final grade back. Stay on her good side or else you'll be in trouble with no discernible warning.
Fantastic teacher, with really in-depth knowledge on a lot of issues and very interaction/discussion-focused classroom environment.

Really chill professor, sometimes maybe a little too chill. Facilitates really insightful class discussions, however, depending on who your classmates are and how interested you are in the subject matter, you might find her classes to be a waste of time or one of the best classes you have ever taken.
All around amazing. All her classes are super low stress and enjoyable if you love political theory. Would highly recommend.
I didn't feel like I learned anything and it was boring
Very kind and understanding professor. Her class is a treat compared to my other more lecture-based classes. Reading and homeworks are all very manageable.

Dr. Gais teaches the most chill Poli Sci class at WashU. No tests/quizzes, just weekly annotations and brief written reflections. The class itself also entails lots of intellectual discussions about exigent issues in politics today. Would highly recommend taking her class if you are a Political Science major.
This is one of the easiest courses I have taken thus far. There are readings, discussions, and weekly reflections. No exams.

Professor Gais is definitely my favorite professor. The class is discussion-based and super engaging. She is not a harsh grader, and she doesn't give a lot of pressure on the students. Will recommend it to everyone.

Amy is a fantastic professor. She makes sure her students get the most out of her class but never makes us feel stressed. Her Text and Tradition classes are mostly discussion based, which is great if you're more of a collaborative learner. She really makes you think before you speak, and she makes you think better, if that makes sense. 10/10.
Dr. Gais is an amazing professor. You'll be excited to show up to class. Would 100% recommend taking a class with her at some point. You won't regret it

Professor Gais isthe best professor I have had here at WashU. I took modern political thought with her and got so much for the class. Highly recommend. Taking another one of her classes next semester.
One of the most kind and caring professors at WashU. Dr. Gais genuinely wants you to succeed and become a better writer and thinker, and she will help you out as much as possible along the way.
Super awesome, highly recommend.

Professor Cuille is great. His syllabus and instructions for assignments are very clear. He also includes short presentation activites in pairs in addition to his discussions. He also has some funny moments where he speaks French randomly and makes some lighthearted jokes.
favorite professor at washu. you can literally revise your essays to get higher grades. he really cares for the mental health of students and wants every student to succeed.

As a Comp Lit Class expect to do some reading. He was very knowledgeable about the texts, very keen eye. As a seminar class, he guided the discussion quite a bit, having us focus on certain aspects of text, compared to more free form discussion that I've had in previous classes. An enjoyable, stimulating class.

What a perfect way to end my undergraduate career! Cuille was hilarious and charming. Knew a lot about French culture (actually being from France probably helped) and it was a pretty chill atmosphere. Also learned a lot.

Dr CuillÃ© stands out as one of the best professors i have had in my time at Wash U. From professors I have had in PNP, IHP, and the pre-med requirements, he is one of the best. He teaches a great literary modernites course. Wash U would be hard pressed to find a better professor to lead such an in-depth and intellectual course.

Wonderful professor! One of my absolute favorite classes of my undergraduate career. He is personable, flexible, extremely knowledgeable, and one learns much in his class. Wish I could take more classes with him - the passion and learning in the classroom is wonderful.
I've taken both French and literature classes with this professor and I must say that he teaches both fields very well. His lectures and engaging and in depth. He is also very accesible and helpful,
Excellent instructor. He cares about his students and tailors class time based upon their interests. I am not a Humanities major but I started taking literature classes because of his class.

Course on Literature & War Extremely knowledgeable professor who has a passion for the subject matter that is contagious.  He also very mirthful and makes class time go by quickly, using a multimedia approach.
Great teacher!! My passion for French has grown considerably because of him. So helpful, and very nice. Grades easy.
great prof. not very clear but very entertaining, so the class goes by very quickly. grades easy, always willing to help. a lot easier to understand when he speaks French rather than English

T and T class on emergence of modern identity.  Very interesting class.  Prof. does have trouble at times expressing his ideas but don't let it fool you is very smart and well versed.  Grading is fair to easy.  Great guy solid prof

I take my words back. Prof Cuille indeed has trouble conveying his ideas because of his broken English. Though extremely amiable and approachable, Prof Cuille has a poorly organized course plan and  sometimes comes to class poorly prepared.

Took a humanities course Prof. Cuille should not have been teaching--he was unclear and poorly versed in the subject matter much of the time.  He also had unrealistic expectations for students--kept adding to the workload at the last minute, for example mandatory "makeup class" during finals?!  Redeeming factor: a VERY easy A.

Freshman Seminar on the Ideology of Speed. Very interesting class with relevant topics at first, but began to lose focus once we got out of the defined topic of futurism. Very multimedia. I liked this class even though most didn't. He gave us a book to read that turned out to be ****graphic; he hadn't read it before assigning it. Interesting.

Took a humanity class with him and have to say he is fantastic. Although he struggles a bit with his English because he is from France, he presents the difficult material with thoughtful clarity and complete patience. He is knowledgable in many areas like philosophy, history and western literature. He is a fair grader and your hard work will pay of
good class, good teacher

Really great professor and funny guy. He's super into the subject so he goes on rants, and sometimes is not the the most straightforward when he explains how to calculate things. But he is very smart and knows his stuff. He's pretty accessible, puts out a lot of study materials, and made class interesting sometimes even though I kinda hate micro.

Natenzon is one of my favorite professor's at WashU. His lectures were extremely engaging in my opinion and he always listened to student feedback. I do think the class could use more TAs but he hosts some office hours and you can ask him questions right after class. If you understand the content and do all the practice exams, you will do fine!

Incredibly nice professor, really enjoyed his lectures, and I thought he was fairly good at teaching the material. However, his exams are immensely difficult, and the bi-weekly problem sets are not especially easy, either.

The difficulty of this class is overrated in my opinion. Its true that Paulo is not a very good instructor (although a nice guy), I never paid any attention to his lectures, I think online videos and self-studying are more efficient for this class, and if you do his practice exams, the actual exams are very fair and manageable.

Paulo is a fine teacher. He cares more about his academic papers than actually teaching the class. He is a nice and funny guy but not the best teacher. His class is easier than the other MEC 290 Professors, so I would still recommend it over CDC's class. The class should be fine if you have already taken MGT 100

Office hours were useful but weren't sufficient enough for me. I did not learn from his lectures. Additionally the exams were difficult. Either learn MEC290 from another prof or be prepared to devote ## hours a week to his class. Again, Paulo is probably an amiable prof to do research with but his teaching isn't accessible for beginning microecon.
Very nice professor, but the class is definitely hard. I feel like I only did well because I had taken AP Micro and Macro in high school. I would recommend taking advantage of office hours

Prof. Natenzon is a REALLY nice guy. I have nothing bad to say about him as a person at all. He really cares about his students and is super personable. Regarding the content of his class, it can be really hard to understand and I think he does not always have the best approach in lectures when trying to explain the material. Great guy, hard class.

Great professor. Clearly very intelligent, and mixes humor into his lectures. Exams can be hard but certainly aren't impossible, just study. Some say stay away, but I like him and would like to take more classes with him.

Would not recommend this professor for MEC 290. I gave it shot even after having read the negative reviews and it backfired. Simply put, his style of teaching makes content unnecessarily difficult to understand. Often, I'm easily able to grasp what he attempted to explain in an 80-minute session in a single 20-minute youtube video. Nice guy though.

I feel like he just does not understand student learning in the slightest. He just gives the formula or idea behind an economic concept with a little explanation and then expects the students to comprehend the application of the formula or rule with absolutely zero practice.

Brilliant teacher, but failed to acknowledge the lack of knowledge many students came into the class with. Homework was too hard and took a large group to understand what to do. Test averages were extremely low, but the curve did surprise me in the end with a much better grade.

Natenzon was extremely disrespectful and unfair towards us, especially as we transitioned to online learning in the time of Covid. When all other teachers were extremely accomodating, he made the class even harder and gave us less information. He did not even tell us our final exam scores, and released final grades with no curve information.

Class was structured as an experiment - exams varied greatly in difficulty especially once transitioned to online and multiple people received 0s. Grading scale was unclear - many students were surprised by their final grade. Lectures were less helpful than just self-studying for exams. He is resilient but was not prepared to teach the course.
Really smart. But his exams and homework so hard!!! if you don't want too much psychological pressure, or there is high opportunity cost, just don't take it.
Great professor, easy to talk to and helpful, interesting class, not an easy A though.
Great prof, but you need to keep up with readings because he will keep going forward even if you don't!

He's a tough teacher, and very dry.  He's brilliant and sometimes that makes it difficult to understand him, but he's also perfectly willing to help and once he warms up he's very fun to talk to. If you're not REALLY interested in chemistry, don't take a class with Schaefer, because he'll leave you in the dust; if you like chemistry you'll be fine.
The text book is seldom used (and expensive). Also he has a huge interest in irrelevant science history (seriously).

Just as other people say, Schaefer is back to Gen-Chem. Is that a good move? Probably not. He is to smart and bored for a 101 class. Neither him nor the students have the energy to deal with it at 9AM.

Schaefer may be smart, but he does not know how to teach or communicate with his students.  There is no connection between topics and he makes soooo many mistakes.  I also stopped going, and am now in the Loomis lecture.  Now he is a great professor.  Take Loomis if he teaches it again.

This is the first time in 17 years that Schaefer has taught Gen Chem...and it really shows. He obviously knows his stuff, but talks in an incomprehensible monotone and uses chemical jargon that even TA's can't decipher. Like many others, I stopped going to his lectures after the first exam. I started going to Loomis's class which really helped

Nice guy, dry (funny!) sense of humor.  While it is clear that he cares that the class learn the material, his lectures can at times be unclear, with the main points being difficult to discern.  While the course material is very difficult, his expectations are almost always reasonable.
N/A

This material is too hard to have a dry, monotone lecturer.  Schaefer should retire.  He seems tired of teaching and I don't think he cares if we get the material.  Loomis filled in for a couple of lectures and wow, he is night & day better.  I bet Loomis is hard, but you would learn a lot more.

great course overall. 2 criticisms: he emphasizes intuition but then makes u mem. some eqns. he can speak with monotone and it's hard to discern what's most imptIthe big pic) if you aren't already familiar with the material.  clear and well taught though. a great prof and guy! exams are fair.

Dry sense of humor and an ok guy.  But, I did not learn a thing in there and got an A.  I know his course has a lot less work than Loomis', but I would rather do more work and learn the material.  Bring back Loomis.  Schaefer should stick with NMR
Great guy that makes great jokes. Makes lots of mistakes but is always willing to be corrected. Quizzes and tests are tough, but if you study the recommeded problems, you'll do fine.

He should have notes from the textbook rather than some other books, but otherwise he's the greatest chem prof. after Woooley. He's funny, interesting and cares for his students. Don't dream of working in his lab.. no way he'll accept you. On the other hand he does chat with anybody very humbly.

Schaef's the man- didnt do well at all on the tests, ended up with a B. Dry sense of humor makes for unexpectedly humorous parts of lecture. Good prof...material is hogwash though. Seems humble for a man who  invented C-13 NMR, one time even showed us his lab in help session. Highly recommended.
He's one of the most interesting chemistry professors especially because he teaches you the way things were discovered instead of just teaching the book like Frey does.
No Comments
Hause is easily the sexiest and best professor I've had at wash u. He makes everything interesting, and knows what keeps students engaged and tells great stories.

Take a class with Prof. Hause! He is like your kind, incredibly intelligent uncle who tells you fun stories. I have taken two classes with him, and have not heard a boring lecture yet. Always willing to meet outside of class and answers questions with pure intellectual crystalline clarity. Hands down the best lecturer I have encountered.

BRILLIANT! Take a class with him before he leaves, you will NOT regret it. Very talented lecturer-as people have said before, he weaves each class into a story, and more-less acts it out, too (which can also be unintentionally hilarious). End of story, he is awesome, if you're wondering whether to take a class with him... DO IT!!!!
HAUSE IS AMAZING, take any class with him. He is retiring after Spring '11 so get his classes before he's gone. He's so personable and willing to help.

Hause is the man. The stories he tells in class are usually both amusing and educational. basically for 3 hours a week you get story time and you learn a ton without even realizing it. He is also incredibly open to questions even in a big lecture class
Knows everything that ever happened to anyone anywhere. Fantastic stories, eager to help students, slightly dirty sense of humor. Basically your grandfather, if your grandfather were a genius.

Amazing lecturer, he weaves a story (that is relevant and usefull) from a few keywords.  Totally separated from the class but willing to talk to students, the TAs make the grade, he knew no ones name.  Brilliant
Extremely knowledgable
Fascinating, makes boring subjects interesting and fun. His lectures are great, I look forward to going to class. He is a little bit hard to meet with outside of class, however.

He was one of the best professors I have ever had. Friendly, helpful, and extremely clear. He wants to see you succeed. He also seems to know everything about everything! Always willing to talk outside of class. I hope to take him again!

I have never had a better lecturer in all of my scholarly years. Makes every topic interesting, and you can't help but laugh when he gets satirical about serious topics. Very unfortunate that he is not connected to the class outside of lectures. Highly recommended.
Always entertaining. The best part of the class were the random tangents about monarchial sex scandals and fairy tales.
Always entertaining, extremely knowledgeable.
Amazing speaker. Never missed a class. Makes even boring topics like the economic history of Europe fascinating. Hightly recommend taking any of his classes. You'll do well if you do the work.
Awesome. Very, very interesting, funny, and very reasonable. Best Teacher at Wash U.

torture during the pandemic. multiple answers questions are insanely difficult. A nice person but barely knew how to teach.
Jeff Zacks is the worst. A horrible, horrible person. Stay away.

After taking this class, I have no earthly idea what all the negative reviews about Zacks being "arrogant and rude" were about. I had a great experience in Zacks' class--his lectures were clear and engaging, he's very approachable, and seems like someone who genuinely cares for his students!

I generally struggle in lecture classes (especially those that involve biology or neuroscience) but Prof Zacks organizes his lectures well and does a good job of explaining them in ways that I could wrap my head around. He was very helpful (as was the TA), and friendly/approachable!
sure, his scholastic pedigree is impeccable, but he's the biggest ****bag I've ever come across

There are several great professors in our department, many that are competent and teach well, and a few that waste everyone's time.  Jeff Zacks is at the bottom of the entire list. In a class based on discussion, Prof. Zacks was awful at leading discussion. He talks for too long, capitalizing the conversation for an inappropriate amount of time. This teacher has no coherent teaching method and just likes to talk on and on about nothing. Very harsh grader and has little interest in helping students understand the topic. He is condescending and belittling when questions he deems unworthy are asked.

Arrogant, condescending and belittling. He has no interest in teaching or making his concepts clear. His teacher's pets are obvious. He was unhelpful and outright mean. He made sure the class knew he was from Stanford and that most of this was beneath him, especially those who needed help.

omg i could write a book on how much i hate this man, he picks on students unfairly, tries to humiliate and embarrass people, unfair and unclear grading, rude and disrespectful outside of class, has obvious teachers pets, should not be allowed to teach
VERY good teacher. lots of work, but you will leave knowing your stuff. hands down my favorite psych prof.
The best professor in the department.
he really likes it when people are late to class
Don Not like his class.
I Do not think that his grading is fair.

His class is fun to take! I had zero knowledge about this topic, and he encouraged me "You are born to learn this." All assignments can be done by Internet (namely ChatGPT), even if he found out, he will only talk to you without any penalty or redo for the assignment. Overall, it is a great pleasure to take his class!!!

Professor Ma is amazing. There are so many extra credit opportunities and he responds to emails super fast. Pay attention to class and do well on quizzes and attend the subsection to ensure good grades.

Amazing lecturer, I'm so happy I took him for Chinese Civilization. This class goes through a deep history from ancient civilization all the way up to today. Interesting discussion sections once a week. Lots of extra credit opportunities. Keep up with the material (don't procrastinate) and you'll do good on the quizzes. I hope to take Prof Ma again
Professor Ma is one of the nicest people I have met at WashU. Try to get as many extra credits as you can. The lecture is enjoyable to listen to and the material is easy to learn.

Dr. Ma is a great guy and a very well-respected scholar in his field. He lectures for a while with pretty generic slides but has interesting things to say. One day per week is a discussion section on the readings. Each week there is a short-answer essay quiz based off the readings. Also has outside of class events you have to attend. Cool class

Awesome professor. Like another comment said, my only qualm was that his lecture slides didn't have much on them so note-taking was just scribbling down passing phrases. Very interesting class though, and he's a super funny person.

Professor Ma is such a kind man! Expert on US-China relations and Chinese history in general. He also oversees the undergraduate Chinese major. Super nice guy. Chinese Civ class had a lot of readings, but the grading was easy (drop 3 lowest weekly quizzes, participation, and attend 3 outside events). No final. Lots of extra credit opportunities too

Professor Ma is both hilarious and incredibly knowledgeable. Class is always pretty engaging and the homework was doable, although sometimes reading-heavy. The only problem was he did not have good lecture notes, he just talks the entire time. Otherwise a great professor.

Knowledgeable about Chinese studies. Perhaps one of the most engaging professors I've met at WashU. Cold-calls students in class, but in a way that's not intimidating. Expectations for assignments could be clearer, though. In general, the class's topic (U.S.-China relations) is pretty important.
The scale of grading is weird

The problem with Professor Ma was that he was, to a degree, unclear about the expectations of his class. His lectures tended to ramble, and it was never obvious what he expected us to know from the texts or derive from his presentations in class. The guest lecturer who taught about half the course was even more disorganized. I learned very little.

Very funny and sarcastic teacher. Likes to lecture a lot but other than that the class is a lot of fun. Very insightful about modern China since he experienced it himself. I?wouldn't say the class is easy it has a decent amount of workload. But professor Ma is a very fair grader. You will do fine as long as you do all the work.
He is a great professor. He lectures A LOT but if you can deal with that then take his class. IT's very fun.

Great teacher! Has a bit of an accent but is always willing to help his students excel. He's a great lecturer and knows what he's talking about. Understands that we have other commitments and is willing to make deadlines work for us. Very sarcastic and funny.
This dude wild fr.

Frustrating when you do the readings and the quizzes are on details from the beginning of the semester. Class is great if you have prior knowledge/can cram. Too much of class spent making fun of students/cursing/trying to be funny that it is hindering a person's learning at that point. I don't understand the positive ratings on here, honestly.

Keith is a great professor. He is really passionate about lecture material which makes the course super engaging. PNS is one of the best classes I've taken at Washu, and it has made me more excited about Neuroscience.

Keith was an awesome lecturer. His humor makes the class much more enjoyable and the content makes you want to pursue a career in Neuroscience. All information is given to you through lecture and pre-lecture readings, and the Friday subsections really help you learn to read scientific papers.

Very approachable, engaging, gives interesting lectures. People complain about having a quiz every class, but I would much rather study some material constantly than be stressed about a huge exam. Feel like I learned more in this course than any other course at WashU (concepts are applicable to real life) and he gives EC to boost your grade.

Makes me question my motivation for school, interest in neuroscience, and passion for science in general. Is constantly discouraging and arguing with students and always brings an arrogant attitude to class. Would never take this class again.

Genuinely one of the worst profs I've ever had. He is extremely disorganized and condescending. Frequently calls the entire class "mega introverts, nerds, etc". Your entire grade will be based off of your ability to comprehend textbooks and scientific papers with no help. He gives terrible and unorganized lectures and expects his students to be experts on material he didn't teach them. Get ready to be cold called during lecture, and participate in discussions. Mans is always in a pissy mood and dislikes students in general. Condescending attitude during lecture, extremely paranoid about cheating.

Has a personal vendetta against pre-med students and will say so in class. Aggressively on the lookout for AI violations. Class is hard enough on its own, but it feels like the prof is actively rooting against you.

Dr. Mustakeem obviously cares a lot about what she is teaching. However, the class felt very student led. Passionate and caring professor.  100% would take one of her classes again.  At times is a little bit disorganized (e.g. her books were not available at the bookstore) but you will not find classes like hers anywhere else, VERY important subject matters.

This class was very informational and overall fun to take. There are some heavy readings but prof Mustakeem cared for and made efforts to listen to all her students. I absolutely recommend taking this class, you will enjoy it!
100% would take another one of her classes

Prof. Mustakeem is very energetic and compassionate, and she shows up every day very enthusiastic about the course material! With that said, she's pretty disorganized and it's evident. She gets behind the syllabus, and you won't know what your grade is until final grades are posted. BUT! If you do the assignments and participate, you'll get an A.

Dr. Mustakeem is the best professor I have had at WashU. She encourages her students to do well, she comes to class every day with a smile on her face, and she really wants every person to do well. She is so passionate about her work, and she genuinely respects every student that walks through her door. WashU is lucky to have her on their campus.

DO NOT TAKE. Professor Mustakeem is extremely vague, disorganized, and narcissistic (she cannot take criticism period). She could barely make it to class half the time! She is passionate, but not in substantive ways. This course was an enormous waste. Here's hoping she doesn't get tenure.

Worst teacher I have EVER had. Didn't show up to class frequently. She's awful. She is the most incompetent, unorganized professor I have ever had. Save yourself the trouble of spending time being yelled at by a narcissistic woman. She's gross.

She is by far the most amazing professor I've ever had. Her classes are never easy, but they are very rewarding. The readings are always interesting and relevant. She usually assigns a semester-long research project - and she pushes you to a higher level throughout
One of the best professor's I've had at Wash U. Challenging in terms of work load but you get so so so much out of it. Take one of her classes.

Professor Mustakeem is extremely passionate about her subject matter. Maintains great rapport with students and facilitates thought-provoking discussions. At times a bit disorganized and rarely available to meet outside of class. Overall an extremely rewarding class experience

She assigns a lot of reading, some of which is last minute or not on the syllabus, but overall Dr. Mustakeem was one of the best teachers I have ever had! She is passionate and down to earth and always there if you need guidance. Take a class with her!

Dr. Mustakeem is amazing at leading discussions. She assigns a heavy reading and writing load, but she makes you WANT to complete everything. Make sure you do the reading, because she will call you out.

College Writing is a well-recognized grinding course at WashU, but taking the course with Dr. Iler would surely alleviate such pain to some degree. Everyone has to take CWP, so be nice to yourself and choose Dr. Iler as your instructor.
DUDE'S SO NICE JUST TAKE HIM
around 30% of the work is graded on completion. Needs to participate in class. Easy to reach out outside the classroom

Hes overall good. he grades pretty tough compared to other college writing professors but a better professor to relate to and become close with. I have largely no complaints other than the lack of rubrics for assignments and no clear guidelines for what is considered enough participation

This is my second course with Iler, I also had Tech and Self w/ him and knew I wanted him again. Argumentation was more than I ever expected - the course is open ended and I found myself writing about a range of topics and exploring new techniques. Iler pushes you to do your best and is very helpful on feedback. Facilitates such amazing discussions

Dr. Iler is one of my favorite professors at WashU. He really cares about his students and helped me become a much better writer. He's an honest grader but is helpful and if he sees improvement will give you good grades. Participation is important but he will not call you out. If you're taking college writing I highly highly recommend Dr. Iler!!

Professor Iler was an amazing professor who truly cares about what he is teaching. He is also very open to you coming up with your own topic - I ended up writing about memes for one paper and Minecraft for another. He wants to make the material engaging for everyone. Readings can be a bit long but all were interesting. He will help if you reach out

Such an amazing professor! He really helped me with my writing coming to WashU. There are a lot of readings but theyre interesting in my opinion. Very helpful at office hours & encouraging. He really listened to me during the lecture when we discussed readings, different from the large classes I was enrolled in. I wish every class was like this!!
Dr. Iler is great. Writing Technology is a really interesting class and his feedback on papers is very helpful. Revisions/drafts for papers are a good experience.
Dustin is a great dude. Really cares about the students and gives great feedback on papers.

So glad I met Dr. Iler. He made my summer amazing. I knew that he is a great person on the first day of class because he remembered everyone's name immediately and never forgot or called a wrong name. During lectures, he asks lots of thought-provoking questions, encourages discussions and provides insightful opinions. Love him a lot!
Awesome writing teacher; gives you the freedom you need to find your own writing voice while keeping the class structured enough to develop your skills.

Dr. Iler was the best English teachers I have ever had! Writing has always been a struggle for me and he made it so understandable and very helpful towards his students I could email him at anytime and get a respond great professor!

I love Dr. Iler! He is so sweet and helpful, I was dreading Writing 1 but he made that class my favorite my first semester. He managed to make the readings interesting, and he never forced participation but still got to know you well. An easy grader but also helped me improve my writing significantly, and he is hilarious. Take him if you can!

I came into college very intimidated by writing and dreaded taking writing 1, yet Dr. Iler made this class my favorite class my first semester freshman year. He makes a mandatory course fun, interesting, and actually taught me how to significantly improve my writing. Would love to take another course he teaches.

Colin Bassett chillest college writing professor out there. Super lax deadlines for everything. He's really approachable and cares about his students. I feel like I didn't learn a lot from this course but he does provide really good feedback on essays. I know so many people who hated college writing and I didn't!

Colin is super approachable and an easy grader! I'm an engineering student with a heavy courseload, and I still managed to get 100s on 2 papers. A lot of assignment deadlines are super flexible, and he's super kind and easy to talk to if you're struggling. HIGHLY recommend

Pretty easy to get an A if you turn in your work. Colin is very accommodating and understanding. I needed to meet with him urgently when I was working on my research paper, and he made himself available for a meeting immediately. He gives very good feedback which has improved my writing, and he's very knowledgeable in general.

Super sweet guy and very approachable for help. The topic was rather boring but he did a good job incorporating lots of different activities that didn't have to do with writing. Also gave lots of room for revision and feedback and was just super caring and understanding. Assignments were very open-ended and flexible.

Good guy, forgiving with deadlines. Still, he seemed not to care about teaching. Showed up late, scrolled on his phone, let the class chat for the first 20 mins of a 50 min class. A tough mix because he wastes class but expects good writing on essays. Very nice, though; I wonder if this was just an off semester for him.

One of the best teachers I have ever had. I was a strong writer going into this class, but Colin Bassett provided great feedback on every one of my submissions. He was a really nice, kind, and approachable guy too. I miss his class terribly.

Such a great guy and a great professor. He genuinely cares about his students and gives good, detailed feedback so you know exactly what to work on. He is a really relaxed guy, approachable, and understanding. Asynchronous format reduced the workload and he taught CWP really well and did not make it boring. 100% would take his class again.
Nicest guy I've ever met and made me not hate writing classes. Really clear and good feedback on what he expects and how he thinks you can improve. Loved Writing Identity.

It's always a dance, - trying to find good TA's for larger lecture classes. Colin's way of speech and conducting the classroom is strange at first, but grows on you. He cares - and that is what matters the most from a teacher. Highly, highly recommend for Writing Identity. Made the University Writing requirement actually one of my favorite classes.
Colin is so nice! If you are struggling with papers and go to his office hours, he is always willing to help and your grade will improve. Highly recommend him for Writing Identity!

Prof Basset was an amazing Writing 1 teacher. He was a fair but hard grader so you have to work for a good grade. However, he was very easy to approach and cared for his student's. I really enjoyed the class.

He has high standards, but gives the best feedback I've ever gotten on any essay before. Really helped me as a writer, and is always prompt and open to student opinions during discussion. Classes are always interesting, and you end up learning significantly just from reading his comments on your papers. Truly, someone with great insight who cares.

LOVED THIS CLASS!!! He was so helpful and encouraging. I feel like it's definitely doable to get a good grade, but it hard to get a great grade. He makes class so fun and interesting, and he's really funny.

Colin is a really, REALLY nice guy, but an immensely hard grader. Do not expect an A in this class. Getting B's or C's on essays is very common. His grading is very arbitrary, and though he's very insightful, I hated how hard I would work just to receive another fat B. Try to get another Writing I teacher if you want this class to be a GPA booster

He is amazing. He is really interested in all of the students and provides deep and helpful feedback. My fiction improved immensely through the semester, largely in part to his excellent advice. It doesn't hurt that he's gorgeous either :)

This class definitely pushes you, but is so worth it! I learned so much and Karen is the reason for that. She is so kind and really wants to help her students. Her feedback is extensive and incredibly helpful. She really puts the time in to help us succeed. One of my favorite professors so far.

Karen was an amazing professor. Although the class was a LOT of work and veryyy time consuming and stressful at times, i learned a lot and it made me interested in GIS. very responsive over email and encourages students to challenge themselves. Difficult course but overall i think it is very rewarding

Karen might seem scary, but she's just being real. She is an amazing professor who gives you honest feedback and is always willing to help you with your projects. She truly cares about each and everyone of her students. The projects were a lot of work, but it is a class really wants you to be able to apply what you have learned.

Go into this class knowing there will be a lot of work. It definitely is not easy, but if you ask questions, Karen is willing to help and she is accessible outside of class. The assignments were graded fairly, and she clearly cares about her students.
Sister Run.

I've been a TA for this professor multiple times and it made me sad to see such a low rating. Professor DeMatteo can be known as a tougher grader or "scary," but she's probably the most fair professor when it comes to grading assignments and being a helpful resource. If you're a regular student and can pay attention in class, I highly recommend her

Class is doable but it really is a lot of work, especially when the projects are due. Expect it to be your most time-consuming class (not necessarily hardest) bc GIS takes awhile. Karen is not my favorite prof but she might be a little overhated. Her grading seemed a little subjective but she'll give advice if you ask far in advance so do that.

DeMatteo is an incredible professor - one of my favorites - and this was my favorite class last semester. While she can grade strictly, her feedback is clear. She obviously cares deeply about the students in her class and is only strict or tough because she wants you to succeed. Please don't let poor overall ratings keep you from her class.

Avoid this class at all costs. The content is definetly interesting but not worth the hassle. Work is super demanding and graded super strictly. I've found the class incredibly unsatisfying. Also, I think Professor DeMatteo is not very understanding of her students.
no tests. class is basically based on your homework performance. would NEVER recommend taking this class. The class is interesting but I would not take Dematteo again. I ended up dropping it.

Prof. DeMatteo is one of those professors who will -rip- you apart with her feedback on assignments, but she does it so that you know exactly what you need to do to improve. She clearly wants nothing more than for her students to improve and succeed. A very "tough love" kind of teacher, but she's -so- nice and is very understanding and caring.
lemme just say kids withdraw the last week of class people think it's worth more to them to throw an entire semester down the drain than to spend another week in class
if you lose focus for a mere second you might as well have not gone to class. you end up having to teach everything to yourself. she'll belittle you for asking questions.

Professor DeMatteo is not very friendly. She does not like when students ask questions during class. She rushes so quickly though exercises that it is nearly impossible to learn anything. If you plan on taking a class with her, prepare to teach yourself all the material. Her projects are hard and she is a tough, extremely nit-picky grader.

Very rude when reaching out for help. No rubrics given for very subjective assignments, so you have to go to office hours to know what is expected. Not understanding of the fact that it's a pandemic and failed to adjust her teaching style to assist students learning in an online format.

Dr. Craver is passionate about discussing with students. He understands you and will point you to the materials for further inquiry. Moreover, Dr. Craver gives very detailed remarks on papers,  from concept structures to phrasing and clarity. Writing makes thoughts organized. The skills I learned from this course are beneficial in the long term.

Great Professor! You could tell he was really passionate about the class content. The course material wasn't the easiest to grasp, but he did a good job of hitting the key points. Encouraged discussions and was easy to reach outside of class. He was also flexible and responsive to feedback from the class.

Carl Craver is extremely enthusiastic and knowledgeable in his field, and is a great person. You can tell he genuinely cares for his students. He does go on tangents sometimes, but his lectures helped me understand many advanced topics. Very effective in the high-level seminar I took, but may be difficult for students newer to the field.

Craver's Philosophy of Science class is a really worthwhile experience if you're interested in philosophy or science.  It's not explicitly stated, but you should probably take some sort of philosophy or logic class as a background. There's also a fair amount of reading outside of the textbooks.

I also was in Philosophy of Science class.  Yeah, the amount of work's not too bad, but he's not the easiest grader either.  His lectures are very fast-paced but he often goes off on tangents that are so confusing.  He also wasn't very accommodating and wasn't so nice outside of class.  Definitely a helpful class and I learned a lot though too bad.

Carl Craver is hands down the most engaging lecturer I've ever had! I don't know about his PNP 201 class, but his Philosophy of Science class was one of the highlights of my academic career. Two in-class essay exams and two out of class papers. If you have any interest in science or just want to be a better thinker, TAKE THIS CLASS!

I do not recommend him at all. While he means well, he is too pretentious and smug to actually know if you're getting it. He is all over the place, disorganized in every way and is not especially all that smart to act how he does. I would consider waiting and taking any class he teaches with someone else.
