No Comments
Will absolutely be the student of his class again.
A well respected professor.
Excellent professor
Great!
Nice guy. Turn your work in on time and show up to class.
Accomplished professor and a funny guy, but does not teach the class.  Uses overheads strait from book.  Impossible to learn anything from.
Not a nice person... Im gettin an A in the course but still think he is an A$$.  He has no interest in teaching this course and lets everyone know it...
No enthusiasm whatsoever. Quite accomplished in his field but terrible at instruction. It is apparent that he does not enjoy teaching this course.

Good guy - sharp, very precise.  Wants things done his way - not hard though.  You should be quick to keep up with him.  People complain that he's too hard or that he's an @ss - but they just are slackers.
Very good Professor and, more importantly, very, very, very hot! I am having a crash on him! The class is difficult but it is worth the effort!

Galiani is the man. He is a total expert on development and anyone remotely interested in the subject should take this class. Some of the material covered in class involves pretty intimidating  mathematics but he doesn't hold students to those standards for the exams and homework. His Argentinian accent can be difficult to understand sometimes.
From Growth theory, institutions, policies, to experiments. The whole field is covered coherently! Very good class.
Very good class.
Best econometric class I have ever had. I now understand how to do research.
Great class. Very good teacher.
Overall, very good class.

Well prepared class. Full of interesting lectures on Economic Development, covering groth theory, institutions, democracy, policies, poverty, inequality, and markets. Heavy use of maths and specially, econometrics.
This guy is crazy, does not care about teaching...just wants to write papers.  The TA is useless, and is a strange grader...stay away
I absolutely loved intro to WGSS with Dr. Cislo. They are very passionate, knowledgable, and patient. They seem to genuinely care about students. There are many opportunities to get your grade up!

I've taken a couple of classes with Professor Cislo and enjoyed both. She cultivates a welcoming atmosphere and is clearly passionate about what she teaches. The courses were both discussion-based which keeps you engaged. Professor Cislo's grading criteria are very clear so you're set up for success as long as you do the readings and assignments.

Cislo was really good at helping me understand the material and apply it to other readings. Very homework heavy but still very interesting to learn about. In-class was more discussion (about the readings) based than lecture based, especially as the semester went by. 
There were a lot of ways to raise your grade and tests were pretty easy
Dr. Cislo facilitates great discussion!

Amy is a truly caring and down-to-earth professor. One of the best Intro WGSS professors you can have. However, they do assign quite a lot of readings, so be prepared to dedicate 1-3 hours on each assignment. But, every reading makes you think. Very discussion-based, but definitely a class every WashU student should take, regardless of major.

Her class is easy, and she's a chill grader, so if that's what you're looking for, go ahead. But, I don't think I gained anything from her class. She's rather close-minded, and for her, a lot of things she considers to be black and white, and not at all grey (doesn't consider context). Very condescending toward people she doesn't agree with.

This is an incredibly easy class, especially if you take it as an upperclassman. Amy is wonderful and I learned a TON! This class really helped me to identify as even more of a feminist and stand up for myself.

Cislo is so amazing and understanding.  She really is there for all of her students and wants you to succeed.  The class is really discussion based and she perfectly splits putting her own opinion and letting you and the class form your own.  Also, she does a good job of bringing everything together and explaining new terms and connections clearly.

If you have the chance to take a class with Cislo, DO IT. She is fantastic. She doesn't dominate the lectures, yet she provides her opinions and makes them extremely interesting to listen to. She is hilarious and does a good job covering the material and making it interesting. Would love to take another class with her.

CogNeuro was a pretty easy class. 30% of the grade is online homework which is easy (and 1/3 are dropped), 30% is tests on Canvas (open notes, 1 of 4 is dropped, and mostly multiple choice questions), 30% was quizzes on Canvas (during class, open notes), the other 10% are pretty much free points. Unfortunately, I can't say I learned a ton, but 5/5.
Professor Peelle was the nicest professor I have ever had. He truly cares about his students and wants everyone to succeed. I highly recommend taking a class with him!!
Dr. Peelle was an amazing professor. He truly cares about his students and his expectations are very clear. I can't recommend this professor enough!!

Dr. Peele is straightforward. All expectations are clearly outlined in the syllabus and followed. Grading criteria are explained and you can argue back points. Gives pop quizzes that arent really pop quizzes because he tells you about them. Topic isnt necessarily riveting, but hes a good professor and engaging enough.

He is one of the most easy-going and fair professors. He tries to engage his students and make the class more enjoyable. His assignments are quite simple and can be done quickly and the exams are straightforward. As long as you go to lectures and pay attention you will be fine in this class.

Peelle gives a lot of assignments, so even if you do bad on one thing, you can still do really well in the class. And even though he gives pop quizzes, he drops the lowest 1/3rd of your scores. Overall, I recommended this class. It will definitely not be the most exciting course you ever take at WashU but it is pretty easy and decently interesting.

My best favorite professor in college so far. Clear lecture and many good examples from his knowledge & his research. Though I took the course as a freshman and felt a bit challenged at first, his lectures are so great that I recommend this course to every one interested in neuro. Very insightful course if you wanna conduct neuro research in future

He is always happy to speak to his students during office hours or by appointment, if you ever have questions. explains things simply and makes the subject easy to understand. if you put in effort, you will definitely get an A. he is my favorite teacher in college so far

Dr. Peelle was an exceptionally fair and understanding professor. He integrated research studies into his lectures, which helped give me a better understanding of not only what cognitive neuroscience is, but how people move the field forward. Peelle also has a great sense of humor.

Professor Trevor is great. The class is purely discussion based, so make sure you read the readings and some group projects. They assign the readings on Canvas (about 20-30 pages long). The homework assignment are weekly reflection essays, which are super short and easy. Pay attention in class and you will do well on the quizzes.

I absolutely loved this class and Prof. Trevor. They are extremely knowledgable, caring, and engaging. Classes were incredibly interesting. Participation is important but the assignments were interesting. I would take this class again in a heartbeat.
Amazing Prof. I've taken two classes with Trevor and they are both a great teacher and very understanding of the college student mindset.
Very participation based. Great prof tho

Sometimes, Trevor made queer studies easy and fun, but they speak w/ long sentences, big words, & very abstract ideas. Appreciates participation, but its easy to feel like they think your contributions are stupid. Can get angry if the whole class read but doesn't understand a reading. Look up "Think, Mark, Think!" meme. They're Omniman, you're Mark

Great professor and great class. You get A as long as you work hard. The only thing is that you need to be politically correct in your paper and class discussion, so be cautious with what you say. But overall it's a great class and you get to read a lot of great readings.

Here's my issue with Trevor: she's nice and she's not a harsh grader (she gives extensions no questions asked as long as you ask her 48 hours before the due date) but she doesn't allow much room for Devil's Advocate. You can tell when she's hearing what she wants to hear and when someone's not being quite PC enough.
Great class, great professor. Changing the world one reading at a time.

Trevor's awesome. Everyone should take her intro classes, honestly. The grading seems a little rough but a lot of the overall class grade is participation, attendance, and completing some ungraded assignments consistently. Attendance is absolutely mandatory.
Kappy

Kaplan is a nicer grader. Don't worry too much about grades in this class. His lectures I really like because he does the accounting problems with us in class. One thing I don't like is the evening tests we need to take. It ranges from 6:30pm or 7pm. Personally, around nighttime, my attention is not there, so this makes test-taking harder.
Fantastic professor and makes the course content accessible and manageable for students. Would 1000% recommend taking his section for 2620.
Dr. Kaplan was a stellar. I found his input inspiring and his energy electic. I think every student could benefit from a class with Dr. Kaplan.

He's a really nice guy and is good at teaching the subject. He brings in a lot of practice problems and examples during class and has plenty of extra problems available during review sessions or online. Tests are straightforward and very similar to the problems he gives. I would definitely recommend taking him for 2620.

clear lecturer, fair (really easy) exams, accessible even outside of office hours. goes over practice problems in class, which is very helpful for learning the material really wants to see students succeed

As a new professor, he is trying to do well but I'd say there is huge space for improvement. typos in examquiz;the tendency to read slides;randomly changes grading criteria sometimes comes not so prepared(but may improve after he teaches this class more)

This semester is the first time that Professor Kaplan teaches this course, but he's absolutely doing an amazing job as a new lecturer! The material is explained very clearly, and a lot of in-class practice and review sessions are provided to help us catch up. Also, he always maintains a lively class atmosphere.
Professor Kaplan is great. This class is not the most interesting or entertaining but he makes the material relatable and always applies real life problems. He also offers lots of outside help.

Her class is discussion-heavy, but the students have to lead the discussion. In class, she doesn't really teach. In individual meetings, she's intelligent and helpful. She knows what she's doing, she just doesn't teach in class most of the time. Really smart and friendly in individual discussions, but wouldn't take her for a class again.

Do not take courses with her. She takes insanely long to respond to students emails. Responded an hour before the final was due despite being contacted more than a week earlier. Prepare to lead more than half the class as the students, the professor doesnt even teach half the total amount of classes. Assignments are posted hours before theyre due

This class was one of the most boring I have taken. In addition, though the professor is passionate about her work and research, she doesn't realize that she is not getting the lecture across to her students. The readings are dull and long, the discussions are forced, and there are no clear directions for the assignments.

This class was lecture heavy, but it was to be expected in a class of 30ish people. She is clearly passionate about the topic, and gives good feedback on papers. She genuinely wants to help her students succeed.
Her small, upper-level classes are great- her helpfulness in answering questions, which some have been saying is a negative in a big intro class, is a definite strength in a class with 10 students.

A demanding but not unfair professor.  She assigned readings that were very long and challenging, but once you were finished you felt kind of accomplished.  Class discussions helped a lot in terms of having a greater understanding of the texts, although sometimes it was hard to stay focused.
She's easy. If you're in architecture READ THE BOOK. Half the class will be architecture students at least. What she focuses the lectures on are on the tests.

Professor is incredibly incompetent. The class is easy but you will not learn a thing from her. 99% of the material learned is from the book. Takes up lecture time by asking vague, unimportant questions in a 200 person class. She actually bases her lectures around these questions and answers. Class is a joke. Take for an easy A, nothing more.
Very enthusiastic, nice, easy grader
An incredibly patient and understanding professor. Had great conversations outside of class time and is always willing to talk.
His class is not as good as his reputation as a economist.
Teaches an undergrad class like it's a grad class.  And to make it worse he is rude.
One of the best Professor's at Wash U. Take this class if possible. I have heard rumors that he can see the matrix...
Levine clearly wrote the last post himself, none of that is true.  He taught Price Theory like it was a graduate course, and he was rude and dismissive of questions in class.
Great professor, one of the best I ever had. Crystal clear lectures, really emphasizes important points. Helpful too. Do take 401 from him if you can. Tests actually weren't that hard.
Dr. Levine is NOT a bad professor. He is coherent, friendly, and takes time to answer questions. Unfortunately his tests are extremely difficult.
Avoid Levine at all costs, the man is out to get you.  Hardest A I've gotten in my life.
No Comments
Stan seems really nice at first, but then his true colors come out and he is incredibly disrespectful. Not recommended.
Stan was a great teacher
Knows his stuff. Always seems preoccupied with other things though.

Stan knows his stuff.  He is one of the most knowledgeable Photo I teachers you will ever find.  He makes the class fun, adventurous even, and shows his students the magic of Photo I.  His personal experience as a photographer and artist is vast, therefore he is quite the resource.  The photo daddy if there ever was one.
doesn't give any real feedback in crits, and definatly plays favorites.
Stan is intimidated by students and unnecessarily (and unhelpfully) harsh when it comes to critiques.
Simply the best!  Part of the reason I went to Wash U.

Stan is great. Definitely the best photo 1 teacher. He's laidback, lenient and funny. Very helpful and really likes teaching photo 1. His is probably the least stressful photo 1 section, but still requires work and effort

Gleaves is one of the best professors I've ever had at WashU. The fundamentals of materials science can be a dry topic, but Gleaves livens up the class with anecdotes of his experiences in industry, in Vietnam, and in academia. We covered a lot of material, and yet everything made sense. AFAIK, most of the class got an A on the final exam.

Well.. He taught us the most important and essential concept of Materials. This course is a very unique course. But you need to know fundamentals of chemical engineering or to read the textbook before the class. Easy A, if you do your assignments.
Greatest professor I've had in college.

He did not care about teaching at all.  He just wrote equations on the board all day without explaining anything.  Always held us late.  Couldn't remember what topics he had even covered so far -- gave us homework on things we'd never talked about at all.  So disorganized.  The only good thing was his stories.
No Comments
No Comments

MTC-Modern Technological Challenges, it was too long for the class box.  I love Prof. Gleaves, he is one of the most interesting and engaging teachers I've had.  I look forward to taking more of his classes.
No Comments
Really interesting lectures and not a harsh grader.

QPM is a necessary evil - Martin certainly knows his stuff, but his teaching method can take some getting used to. You need to rely on the TA's and learn a lot on your own (and before class) - his lectures aren't especially useful or organized. Not too hard though.
Clearly very smart, but also quite arrogant and seems to look down on students.
Very difficult material but he does a good job explaining it. Make sure you get to know the TA to help you with all the details. Boring class, but decent if you have to take it as a requirement.
i have no idea what everyone is talking about. this man refuses to answer questions in class and consistently mocks the sad kid who always raises his hand. boring class, but who likes math anyways.
I liked his class a lot even though it wasn't the most exciting material. The class went very smoothly and he was always ready to answer questions.
Good class. Relatively easy and straightforward.
Good class.  Altough stats are a little boring.

Professor Gildner's lectures were very organized. This class is mostly centered around big concepts and events throughout the history of global health. Grading is very exam-heavy, and how well you do on the exam is mostly on how well you memorize the slides' info; that being said it is pretty easy to do so; would recommend this class

Dr. Gildner was very nice and her lectures were clear, so I still gave her 4. However, I personally found the course content very boring and repetitive at times. Some units were more interesting but others were very stale. If you know the powerpoints it should be relatively easy to get an A.

Great lecturer and nice person overall, but class was kind of common-sense based (expect when learning about the diseases). Tests were all memorization and pretty easy (the final short answers were the same as the practice test) but grading can be a bit harsh

This class is relatively unnecessary and boring. We cover SOOO many topics at a surface-level explanation. Most of it is common sense. It is very content, memorization, and PowerPoint-heavy. She gives you LITERALLY 30 seconds to answer the Poll Everywhere for participation and gets angry if you do not pay close attention to this INTRO class.

Dr.Gildner is an okay lecturer. If you do not need to take global health, I would not. It's a very surface level class and you can get back by doing minimal work/memorization. Dr.Gilder is very nervous and it impacts her delivery of the material both in its validity and passion.

Introduction to Global Health is very lecture-heavy, and attendance is required. However, Dr. Gildner tries to make her lectures interesting with videos and graphs. Tests are easy as they are rote memorization and the SciComm project replaces a second midterm. In general, she is super caring and wants her students to succeed.

Class is largely "death-by-powerpoint," with exams being based on pure memorization. However, they are incredibly easy and can be reviewed through simple flashcards. Her lectures can be a bit boring, but Gildner is a very polite professor who will answer your questions clearly and is approachable. Very manageable class.
Super nice, lectures can be boring at times
I simply don't get the hype. She is a very nice professor and very knowledgeable, but she's not very clear and her teaching could certainly use some improvement.

Took for Employ Discrim. Not sure if the subject was easy or if Professor Kim is such an incredible professor it felt easy. Honestly one of the best professors I've taken. Would go out of my way to take another of her courses. Material was explained so clearly and I feel I actually learned the subject 100%. Also super caring & LGBTQ friendly.

Impossible to say enough good things about Professor Kim. She's super clear and to-the-point in laying out the law, and also manages to weave in policy discussions and students' opinions on the law. She's friendly, funny, and extremely smart. If only all law professors could be so lucid.

Professor Kim is definitely one of the best lecturers in the law school. She knows the material cold and is an absolute genius in the field of employment law. She does not play hide the ball like some of the older professors, and will always make it a point to give you the black letter law while easing into the more abstract doctrinal concepts.

I really liked Kim. She taught things I use all the time in my jobs and internships (that people who had other teachers don't always seem to be familiar with). She is nice, fair and straightforward. no laptops are allowed which is always annoying to me but i do get it i guess since its a big class and she tries to facilitate conversation.

Pauline Kim is brilliant and literally no one dislikes her.  It's spooky how many people I've talked to who will volunteer the exact phrase "I want to BE Pauline Kim."  I had her during 1L, and I'm not necessarily interested in employment law, but I'm going to take it, of course.

Professor Kim is a wonderful instructor.  She calls on students regularly but never puts them down.  She worked as a attorney for California migrant workers for many years, and aside from being brillant, brings a unique perspective and insight to the subject matter.  Plus, she encourages you to stop by her office ANY TIME!
A good professor. Shes professional, organized, and well-informed.
Changes how you think about humanity every class. Batten is a muse and is incredible. Take the dang class with her if you can. Best lit theory teacher by far. She's just real. What a dang gem.
She was a wonderful teacher and I would highly recommend her class! Outstanding!

Prof Batten is incredibly passionate, intelligent, and caring. She does not explain herself very often, but if you read and digest the materials carefully you'll realize just how smart and complex her observations are. Readings themselves are pretty tough and she never straightforwardly summarizes it, so you'll have to read to pass.

Really sweet woman, is pasionate about the material and cares about the students. If you just try, you'll get a good grade. However, the lectures were basically incomprehensible because she gets caught up in academic jargon and forgets to explain herself to the students. Lectures were hard to get through, but the reading material was good.

Very sweet and understanding.  She really wants her students to enjoy and understand the material.  Her comments on papers are helpful and thorough, and she's always available to help.  Loved her class.
seconded! great teacher. a lot of heart. teaches with passion, which you sometimes don't get at the wash u english department. definitely recommended!
Excellent, though can get carried away in her lectures and be boring. But a very forgiving and easy-going teacher; if you show you care and put in the time, you'll get a good grade.
Amazing.  She is smart, she is funny,she is a leader and an inspirer.  I recommend you TAKE HER CLASSES!

Prof Smith is really funny and walks through each lab clearly so everyone understands. However, I think his lab AI does all the grading for the lab reports, so I found that the grading was weird/random. I would get points off for calculations where my friends would not get points off even when they used the same equations and got the same numbers.

I was generally interested to learn biology, but Craig Smith made enjoying learning it so difficult. I was often one of the last people to finish, and he would make you feel dumb for this. His lab lectures were very poorly executed and not well explainedwhich is typical for freshman weed-out classes at WashU. Disparaging, intimidating, and awful.

Dr. Smith might pause sometimes in class, but he's funny and you can tell he cares about his students learning the material. I took his UCollege Biochem class and you needed to study for his tests, but if you did, you would do fine.
Really confusing lectures that often contain mistakes and are very hard to understand. I don't understand the course material at all.
Great professor! Great class!
Dr. Smith is a great professor. He really cares about his students. He's funny and good at explaining things. He makes biology make sense.

His lectures are extremely dry, and he has an speech impediment (slurring, a slight stutter) that makes him hard to understand. Though I'm sure he's a nice guy,  I absolutely would not recommend taking a class with him.

Dr. Smith is hands down my favorite professor ever at WUSTL! He plays awesome music in lab, he's super helpful and caring, and he's an amazing teacher. One of the nicest people I've ever met. Jokes around with students and makes the lab section something to look forward to weekly! I can't recommend his subsection enough!

Received so many nice and helpful emails from Sandra throughout the semester. The class is heavily dependent on the quality of your other group members which you have no control over. That said, even if you have a terrible group it is probably still better than taking the single credit ethics. Just such a pleasure to take such an impactful class.
A good professor that makes engineering ethics interesting and thought-provoking. Never create difficulties for her students and give fair grades. Strongly recommended!
Awesome interesting class that is not very stressful. Professor Matteucci is caring and nice. I recommend this course.
Professor Matteucci gives students one-on-one attention throughout Tech Writing. She is an easy grader, and her comments are helpful and constructive.
One of the most amazing professors at WashU
Great class, easy grading!
No Comments
She is good.  And very easy.
I liked all the times when he wrote on the board and when he wore slippers to class!

Best math professor I've had at WashU. Explains things extremely clearly and very accessible in office hours. Homework is graded on a combination of completion and accuracy, so it's very manageable. Tests are also pretty similar to homework questions and lecture examples, so they are not too difficult. Highly recommend to to anyone!
Great at explaining things. You need to go to class, but that's okay, because class is really interesting!

Song is a pretty decent professor. He teaches concepts fairly well in class but can be a bit unclear if you ask him a clarifying question. The class is only graded on homework and exams; there's very little homework, and the tests are short and not too difficult. He doesn't post any lecture notes though, so it's up to you to make class

Prof. Song really cares about making sure that every student in the lecture understands the content he goes over. 310 can get to be an abstract, confusing class at points but he always welcomed questions and answered them thoroughly, even if it took more time than he was expecting. There's really clear expectations and he wants everyone to do well.
He goes through concepts pretty thoroughly which helped me really understand the progression of the course. Do not hesitate to ask questions!

The class is difficult if you are not great at math like me. Professor Song is sweet and pretty funny. The tests are fair, and he is overall a good teacher. He is a good choice if you can learn from examples, but maybe watch videos on the topic before (or after) so you can process his examples better.

Pros: - Very straightforward/methodical - Teaches well and explains well - Very knowledgeable - Often relates back to old material Cons: - Accent (not too bad tho) - Sometimes hard to read writing (sit in front) - Lots of homework but it keeps you on track - Only way to get material is from lecture no online help - Exams are a bittricky
On point. I understood most of the things he says
very good
poor clarity. He can't express himself better for students to learn. Question marks over his qualifications.
One of the best professors.
N/A
Best Law Teacher Ever, I hear you are now in malawi? Your brother is forcing you on malawians? Tell him to try joyce banda.
BORING BORING
You can't understand what he's saying, the lectures are unhelpful, and the exam is completely random.  Might as well let a random number generator create all the final grades.
I barely learned anything in his class. He has a very thick African accent, so it is very hard to understand what he is saying. He is also very slow and barely covers any material.

Took both mandatory classes - 20c. Architecture History and History of Furniture. Awful classes ever! Lack of information about subject she fills with "discussions". She uses almost the same Power Point presentations for both classes. Obsessed with "Conspicuous Consumption" and "The Chair"... Expect additional reading...

Smart, funny, really compelling in a small seminar setting. She had great ideas and follow-through for group projects and had us keep a reading precis for individual texts. A great teacher and a strong scholar.

I'm sitting in her lecture right now and it's literally one of the most painful experiences of my life.  She makes Architectural history feel like death, and an excruciating one at that.  Honestly this must violate some sort of law against mandatory torture
Worst art history teacher in existence
Professor Lupkin was not a good lecturer caused by her constant lack of knowledge of the subject. Did this woman get a degree? Very boring class. Taught the pointless facts.
What an insightful and real lady...she's teriffic teaching smaller groups, and she's one of the most down to earth people I have ever met. Her class was truly broadening.
bad lectures (can't stay awake at all)...harsh grading... hate this class but it's f**king required!
No Comments

Very knowledgable about the authors and works we read in class, and helpful in office hours. However, he often interrupts students when they are making an observation, and usually only responds positively/productively when you express something he agrees with. We read an interesting range of novels, although I only enjoyed a few of them.

Not recommended.  He is not open to ideas that he doesn't agree with.  Doesn't know how to respond to students, and often shuts down ideas in class.  He gives somewhat interesting lectures, but "discussion" is more trying to guess what he's thinking about than expressing your own thoughts.  Essay expectations and guidelines were often not clear.

Very even and open to real thinking from students.  As other commenters note--very responsive to students.  Also, it must be said that he is preeminent in the field.  You are lucky to take a class with him.
very interesting and intelligent.

Very hard grader, very intelligent. The subject matter is very interesting, but when half of the class is either sleeping, eating lunch or reading materials for another class, something is definitely wrong.

Milder is absolutely brilliant. His knowledge of Melville, Hawthorne, Thoreau, or for that matter, any author from the 19th century is astounding. He is a very tough grader, but is very helpful during office hours.

EXTREMELY knowledgeable in his field.  Class is interesting but make sure you keep up with the reading.  Though he may be a hard grader, he is very concerned for the well-being of his students.  He was so understanding when I was sick and had no problem giving me extensions.
Excellent professor, very intelligent and interesting.

Loved John's class. Im not sure if hes teaching next year but I would 100% take again. He is the professor you read about in books, knowledgeable, kind, and caring. He has funny comebacks in class that make you know hes on top and engaged while still relaxing and letting conversation flow. I would recommend taking the class just to talk to Bowen.
Bowen is a passionate and talented anthropologist, and an excellent professor sensitive to his students' views.

Professor Bowen is great! He does assign A LOT of reading, but they are usually fun and you don't necessarily have to do them all to get a good grade. You will learn a lot without too much work. The grade is all participation and one final grade. 100% would suggest.

This professor thinks very highly of himself. As a student with lots of experience growing up overseas in Europe and Middle East, I found it difficult to connect with him. He always wants to tell you that he knows more about every foreign culture than you do- even when his knowledge is superficial or only partial.Arrogant dude!
Very knowledgeable and genuinely interested in his students' input and observations! A great class to take outside of your major. Essay tests with a final research paper.
Pretty easy, the workload is light, and two of the tests are open-book and open-note. He is actually pretty interesting.

Reading intensive but grade based on participation and final paper entirely. Very knowledgable and when he explains things he's great, but doesn't always put everything into a framework. Very busy and prefers to answer questions and get papers over email.
He's really great and helpful.

The Writing 1 curriculum is horrible, but that's not his fault. He does his best with it, to some success. He seems to be pretty fair with grading. Go to his office hours, it really helps with your grade. I really enjoyed his class, but mostly because he brought his dogs in and occasionally brought us treats!
Simply the man. Try to get him for Writing I.

Ehhh...he's alright. The class was decently interesting, considering it was a writing I class. I do think he is genuinely invested in his student's advancement. I would encourage future students to go to his office hours whenever you need more clarification.
He's awesome. Way better than any of the other teachers that are out there. He grades fairly and genuinely helps you as much as possible. He also brings treats sometimes.

It's really not Mastro's fault that the writing one curriculum is so terrible, but this class really did make me want to jump off my balcony.  He grades easier as the semester goes on.  Talks about his dogs a lot.

Mastro's class was easy save his belief that writing comments on your papers is bad, because it will affect your writing.  The curriculum as a whole, however, is not conducive to good teaching anyway.

Definitely take a course with Prof. Vaughan if you get the chance.  He's pretty passionate about film, particularly marginalized groups in film, and it's conveyed in his lectures.  Also, he's very approachable.  Word of advice: meet with him before papers are due; he can be a tough grader.
An awesome professor! I actually enjoyed going to class. He kept the material interesting and had a great (though sometimes odd) sense of humor. Tests weren't too hard, essays were graded by the TAs.
A gentleman and a scholar.
A good man

Vaughan is a genuinely nice guy and knows his stuff. He's approachable for help but is a somewhat tough grader. Ladies in the class tend to fawn over him, and he definitely lets some inappropriate behavior slide. However, the class was interesting so I'd recommend it.
No Comments

Although he is new to school and is still working on developing his teaching style, he is one of the most flexible and approachable professors I've had. He is very dedicated to working with you to develop your ideas and work through the material. Despite being personable, he is still a tough grader and has high standards for his papers.
Very nice and caring. She ensures you learn without imposing loads of homework on you. Her grading is generous, but it is not a blow off class. If you try you will succeed. Nicest professor I've had.

Qin laoshi was super nice, understanding, flexible. She really focused on in-class participation, making class chill and fun to be at. There were 2-3 quizzes/test a week that were cumulative of vocabulary but weren't too bad + generous grading for lvl1. Class requires a lot of work/prep if no prior experience. Would take her lvl2 class if I could!

Qin Lash is the best teacher, and everyone will say so! She is making sure you are participating, and I cannot wait to take her course next semester, She also loves to join us in our awesome laughing sessions, and it's fun! You will learn the most Chinese you will ever learn in this course, and I highly recommend it to anyone excited about Chinese
Qin Laoshi is a fantastic professor at WashU. If you put in the effort, you will get an A, but outside of that, she really cares about the students and genuinely wants you to succeed.

Qin Laoshi is amazing!! So nice and caring. Class is rigorous, but if you do what you're asked, you'll come away with a great knowledge of Chinese. Very reasonable expectations and wants to help you succeed, but there's homework EVERY night. Classtime is very engaging and participative.

Qin laoshi is amazing!! Absolutely love this class. The class is rigorous, there is quite a bit of homework (something due every day), and you have to participate all the time. However, this has all only helped me learn so much Chinese. Definitely take this class with her!

Another great class from the Chinese department. Qin laoshi is funny, entertaining, understanding, and engaging. I definitely continued to expand on my Chinese after taking Chinese 1. There is even more vocabulary than in Chinese I, but if you took Chinese I at WashU then you know what to expect. I sometimes felt the class moved too fast, however.

He is ok, I think we just didn't vibe super well. He talks SO SLOW and I think is definitely more suited to teaching middle school or younger grades. He is kind of funny and can be helpful if you ask questions but otherwise critiques didn't really provide any feedback and nothing was on Canvas so instructions/due dates were often unclear.

Pike is the best. Pike is a god. He's so funny and entertaining, but very professional, organised, and helpful in critiques. He gives you a lot of freedom to draw what you want and in whatever style, and he makes you feel passionate about art. I learned a lot, had a lot of fun. You can tell he loves his job, art, and his students.

Prof pike is such an amazing professor, he gives really good feedback and is always willing to meet w/students outside of class to help them in any way possible. He is also super accommodating, where if ur unable to turn something in on time you just need to email him and explain the situation and he's super chill. Class pace is super manageable.

Professor Pike is extremely caring about everyones work and developmental process. He is also hilarious. The course was extremely engaging and effectively met all of the goals mentioned in the syllabus. This was my favorite class of the semester.
Super helpful and friendly. Always looking to help you improve.

2D design with Pike is great. He's really respectful and helpful to students, and makes the class bearable even to people who have no interest in it. He tells a lot of stories about his life and is just a very nice person and cares about his students' well being, not just their art. Take a class with him!
Professor Pike is very friendly and helpful. He is also open to learning new ideas from students and his main interest is helping all the students succeed.

Not sure if it was just the class or the teacher but this could have possibly been the worst organized class I've ever taken. Was expecting an architecture theory type class but instead got a boring random lecture class that nobody needed this semester. I never learned anything and the only thing we had to do for the class was make a 5 min podcast.

Heather is a terrible teacher. She designs your project and then forces you to do that. At the same time she is so insecure that she changes her mind all the time so you are constantly redoing your work.
hard to communicate

She is a really nice person, no matter in class or out. She has an absolutely solid architectural background. She is always helpful of guiding student to complete their projects from really abstract concepts to extremely logical spaces. She is more like a translator between ideas and architecture when she teaches in school. I like her studio.

Guidance is quite scattered. Attitude seems to be to work harder than smarter. Not well grounded as a responsible practicing Architect. Do only what she asks and in the amount x10 and you will get a good grade. Knows how to see a good student, but not an innovative designer/architect.
She's willing to help at all hours.. it gets to be too much. She's much more interested in the conceptual  than the realizable. Expect to make some great physical models.

She's really good at what she does, and she expects a lot from her sudents. you have to push yourself. she's available outside of class anytime (within reason) if you only ask. she's the best prof at this school hands down.

Probably one of the best professors at Washu. Lots of reading and seems tough/intimidating, but very down to earth if you go to office hours. He will call on you the whole class, but he has a purpose in doing it and doesn't do it just to scare you. Never goes on tangents/off topic, has a rigid plan each class. Also, absolutely brilliant.

Hardest and most intimidating professor throughout law school but one of my favorites. He is brilliant and I felt like I was able to take on law school after his class. Take his class, its a right of passage as a law student.

HE'S AMAZING. May seem intimidating because he cold calls you for an entire class period and if you're wrong he cuts you off, but it's very efficient & makes the class move along faster. Every useful thing I've learned in law school so far has been from him. Lots of reading but do it, it's very rewarding. Very energetic and fun!

He assigns a LOT of reading, but after a few weeks you get a sense of what he's looking for and it's easier to get through the work. Really fun enthusiastic guy - it's clear he loves doing this. He's tough and sometimes scary, but fair. Would definitely take another class with him.

This guy is straightforward and a stellar teacher. He breaks down complex concepts into understandable fragments and then ties the fragments together with boy-scout-knot strength. The allegations of intimidation and mean-spiritedness are likely a function of discomfort with proper application of the socratic method.    Take Tamanaha's classes.

Professor Tamanaha was the reason I received such good grades my first year. He was an amazing professor and the analytical skills I developed in his class was applicable in most other courses as well. He can be tough and even downright mean at times, but works with a brutal honesty not often seen in other professors.

Tamanaha is a fantastic professor. He can be very demanding but is committed to making sure you understand the black letter law; you will learn a huge amount. This was definitely the best class in fall!
Engaging lecturer and lenient grader.
This is one of the few times where I felt like more than just a number. He wants you to succeed. If you have a chance to take a class with him, do it.

I liked the content, but I was frequently annoyed by the copious amounts and repetitiveness of direction giving. Not a bad experience, but I just didn't jive with Steve like I would've wanted to for a wgss class. I really appreciated the amount of feedback given on draft essays, as well as his understanding when it came to missing class/extensions.

Your professor ultimately makes or breaks your college writing class, and Steve was pretty incredible. The class was interesting and rigorous without feeling overwhelming. Steve is incredibly accessible outside of class and gives good, clear feedback on papers. Be proactive about reaching out, and you'll be fine. I'd 100% recommend Steve.

I enjoyed my class with Stephen (Examining America Ampersand). He gives kinda harsh but constructive feedback on papers, and is always accessible by email or to meet. He is super kind and overall great at leading class discussions.
The class itself has a lot of boring readings but Steve makes it all more bearable. He's very understanding and will offer extensions/be flexible with you!
he gives harsh/helpful feedback, but he's not the worst grader. he really tries to accommodate to the students and gives extensions always. (I had him for power and commodity culture). go steve!

He's a super nice guy, gets to know students personally and is available outside of class. Class starts off easy and then picks up, with the last unit being pretty difficult, so start strong at the beginning and you should do well. I wouldn't take this class if you have 0 familiarity w stats but if you know some of the basics you should be good.

I was really disappointed in this class, I had read previous reviews hoping to find a caring professor, but I am still looking. Ted was not helpful with questions on Piazza, even kind of rude at points. You basically have to teach yourself in QPM. Not a great lecturer, left much to be desired. If given a second chance, I would avoid this class.

Ted is the GOAT. Whoever mentions that he is not caring is lying. I took this during a very difficult time in my life and he was there the entire way making sure I was on top of things and got my assignments in. QPM is a bit tough, but Ted's a great professor to help you weather through it.

He might give the facade that he cares a lot about his students, but you would be mistaken. Not a great lecturer and would never actually answer questions. Lots of material on tests and problem sets, you're expected to know, were not discussed in class or in the book. Try to avoid this class, instead, take Data science for politics if possible.

Ted's kind of a legend. Legit the nicest dude around, he's always there to support his students and cares about everyone. He definitely makes you work for it though; QPM is not an easy class, and you've gotta do a lot of learning if you want to succeed. Take CSE 131 or a stats class before QPM if you want to have an easier time. Ted's the GOAT tho.

I just finished the final and because were in Covid it took me 25-30 hours and Im not kidding. DO NOT TAKE THIS IN A PANDEMIC. Thats a mistake you dont make twice. I came in to this zoom class not being able to code and Im leaving the same way. To be perfectly honest because everythings online, if you wanna learn, you gotta teach yourself.

An extremely accessible professor who is always open for questions and discussion, especially via Piazza. He responds to Piazza posts + emails quickly. Knows his stuff and a kind soul; he wants students to succeed, does what he can to help. Lectures sometimes drag but material is important to internalize. Lab sections essential for learning coding.
Best professor at WashU! Hope she becomes a billionaire so she doesn't have to work ever again :)
cool

Dr. Parai is so cool! Great intro course for planetary science. She always makes sure the lecture is understood and provides us with tools and notes that help us prepare for tests and projects. 10/10 recommend.

Habitable Planets was an easy, interesting class. I enjoyed all of the little facts Dr. Parai would sprinkle in, just like how she casually mentioned she had smelled the moon. 

The grades did come back quite slow, but overall this class doesn't add much workload. I would recommend it as a fun class.

She is very passionate about what she teaches and it comes off during the lecture. She cares for students and wants to know their names even in a class of 120 students. Explanations are clear and the class is pretty easy. Definitely recommend her and this class if you need a science class.
Really sweet and very intelligent. Definitely a good choice.

If you're a stem major looking for an easy language credit do not take this class. It's definitely super interesting and the teacher is super motivating, but it's a lot of work. I had to switch to pass-fail. The exams are really hard and you have to write three essays.
Prof. Keane is super nice. Her choice of the textbook is brilliant. I really enjoy her lectures on Juvenal and martial. Weekly quizzes and essays are not very difficult to handle.
Tough grader. Really knows her stuff--passionate about classics. Dry sense of humor that can sometimes be a bit harsh. Ultimately a good professor. Discussion-based humanities class
Very passionate about subject matter, I found myself looking forward to class.

Prof Keane's really sweet, the translations move slowly and classes can be boring--most days we only translate or have a short grammar lesson.  Not completely interesting, but Professor Keane definitely has a deep interest in her students doing well and being interested.  If you've got time take the class. If you're decent at latin, you'll do well.
You don't used textbooks, but you read a lot of other books.

I took her Italian Food Italian Culture class. Was a lot of history and culture. Some parts of the class were pretty heavy in work. She likes you to log on every day and participate in the discussion board. Other than that the class was fun and I would take it again.

Conti was an amazing professor. As you get more advanced in Italian it's essential to be as much in Italian - listening, speaking, reading, and writing - in class as possible. Conti was great at pretty much always speaking in Italian and providing feedback politely in class and on assignments when we slipped up. She's so caring and quite funny.
She is kind and helpful. You will need to put in plenty of time outside of class but if you use the textbook to study you will do fine. Tests/quizzes are very reasonable.

I was hoping that the class would be fun since it was a Italian Food course. I felt like everything I responded to regarding the discussions werent good enough. I understand the Professor is looking for certain things but I don't think I deserved a D. This brought my GPA down tremendously. I have been on the Deans List several times. Never a D.

Professor Conti is so sweet and truly wants all of her students to succeed. Definitely a learning curve having to communicate 100% in a foreign language, but she ensures that everyone understands what is going on. As long as you stay on top of the homework assignments, the class itself is very manageable.

Professor Conti is very clear and extremely helpful. She is open to scheduling outside office hours and offers information about italian groups and activities on campus that add to learning a new language.

This professor is amazing!! 10/10. He always made sure to provide extra help and go the extra mile to make sure the class understood. Participation was also very important! It also helped a lot especially because his class was based on zoom. My spanish definitely improved and it was such an easy, motivating class!
Good guy, but is a stickler for attendance. Also class participation counts more than exams.
Very nice, stickler for attendance; wants you to do well- appreciates it when you come to office hours and when you ask questions that help not only you but the entire group
Really nice, smart, and helpful. Really wants his students to do well and enjoy Spanish

Probably one of the easiest spanish teachers. Very willing to help and nice. I'd say that his teaching abilities might not be amazing, but still strong.  If you want to for sure do well at, perhaps, the cost of learning a lot, take this class.  BTW the class is COMPLETELY grammar-based. don't worry about material (at least with this teacher)
really great guy, super nice. Kind of hard to meet with him one on one. Easy to understand, makes material interesting.
N/A

Jen was engaging, kind, and extremely helpful. The class is very reading heavy and at times can be a lot of work, but she gives very helpful feedback and wants her students to succeed. She's extremely understanding and flexible. She expects each student to try their best, but is flexible in how final drafts/pieces look. Learned a lot overall

Professor Jen Meyer is amazing and clearly wants her students to succeed. She offers extensions as long as you ask in a timely manner and gives extra credit opportunities. However, there is a lot of reading for the class, and the class is very discussion based. So, make sure to be prepared and participate, she enjoys that.
Not strict at all; wants students to do good. Simply try and you'll be good.

One of my favorite teachers. I switched into her class the first day of school because she truly loves her job and students. She is extremely helpful with papers and is extremely easy to talk to. She understands the stress students go through. She most definitely adapted to Covid in the most efficient way and better than any other teacher.
Totally hot and really helpful. Sometimes she can be hard on you, but I know its because she really cares about the students. If anyone can get you a job, it is her!
The worse ever.

He is so mean and you feel tired to attend his class. He will not give any comment to your design and you will be confused after every class. He and his TA will laugh at you on your design and they have no patience to tell you how to improve. He is the worst professor I've ever met.

I have no idea why Steve has been rated so low. What I know is he is super knowledgable and helpful. Very gentleman and respectful to his students. I took his studio and a class about film. Definitely one of the best professors I've ever had

I thought he was really great at the graduate level. Has a strict idea of what he wants the projects to be, but is really helpful in getting creative past that point. Has good ideas. Really laid back and never degrades the student. He is very supportive and I learned a lot.
No Comments

clearly he is the one rating himself this guy was the most selfish, irresponsible prof I have ever had.  bottom lined, I learned nothing, and he made no effort to teach at all He actually has less education than I do. What gives him the right to even be there? He has no imagination, and no desire to teach. the guy only talks about cars. HE SUCKS!
The GOAT
He is a great professor. He is a hard worker and very kind to helping his students. That was a great experience to take the course from him...

An absolute unit of a professor. If you ask for help, his response time is no less than 20 business minutes and will help you with pretty much anything, whether that be conceptual or a question about a specific question on the exam.
Great class, interesting lectures.
Great professor!
He is one of the professors I see by now. He was very nice and help a lot with each process.
I think he was a solid lecturer. The class moved quickly at points but overall he was just a typical math professor.

Professor Nguyen is a sweet, funny guy and you can tell he cares about his students, but he isn't very good at teaching. His lectures were incredibly hard to follow and a lot of people, including myself, would attend other lecturers whenever possible. He also didn't cover everything on the tests in lectures, aside from reviewing the study guide.

Professor Nguyen makes jokes in class, and he is a really sweet person, but he isn't a great professor. He makes many computational and conceptual errors when he is working through problems which makes it hard to follow examples. His lectures are generally hard to follow and even harder to comprehend. Many people went to other professors' lectures

He's a pretty funny and sweet guy but not the best lecturer. Isn't that great at explaining the material, and most of my real learning came from outside the class. I found the exams to be pretty easy, though I skipped the vast majority of lectures by the end of the semester.

The other lecturers I went to (Thornton and Wick) spent significantly more time explaining the concepts of this course and for most of Nguyen's lectures it felt like he would spend about 5 minutes explaining the concept and the next 45 minutes doing practice problems with little explanation as to what he was doing. Seems nice but not a good prof

I love this man with all of my heart and I know he feels the same about each and every one of us. Just don't try to correct something super trivial or he will make you feel foolish. But he is a massive jokester and always makes us laugh and warms our hearts with his. Please take a class with this man, you will not regret it.

Probably the most content-heavy class I've ever taken at WashU, prepare for a time commitment! However, content was extremely helpful for MCAT prep, exams were never unfair though they required a lot of memorization. Dr. Osdoby is also very kind.

Speaking now as a 2nd year med student, I very much recommend taking endocrinology. Even though the information is relatively simple, theres so much that it can seem overwhelming. Keep up with it! It prepared me so much for the MCAT and has been one of the most helpful classes for med school.

This was a tough class. Dr. Osdoby has a very dry lecture style and dry lecture material, so 90 minute classes test your ability to focus. His tests were fair, and he offered some extra credit, but still was v. difficult. Even so, I remember more from this class than my previous biology courses. Be ready to memorize lots of pathways.

Loved Dr. Osdoby! He offers a LOT of information which can seem overwhelming, but the exams are extremely fair and offer extra credit. He's obviously passionate about his field and always personable/ready to help.

Fun class, you learn alot! But there's so much information, I wish i took it with a friend. Class was based on 3 non-cumulative exams (no final), a paper, and 3 quizes.  He is very willing to answer questions, wish is awesome.

This class had a LOT of information in each lecture. He only gave us two exams for the semester, so there's a TON of material on each one. You'll have to cover it all to some degree for the multiple choice section, but he does let you pick which essays you want to answer. Nice guy to talk to, definitely willing to answer questions and help out.

Prof seemed nice, but he doesn't grade your papers/projects. Your grade depends on how nit-picky your TA is. Long lectures with interesting topics, but the small group discussions with the TAs can feel dragged out at times. Lots of reading, but it's interesting stuff. None of it really matters though if you have a harsh grader.

I learned a lot in this class, but not from the professor. He assigned some extremely interesting readings, but he never explained what we were supposed to take away from them or organized the lectures well. We were always discussing the readings a few lectures after we actually did them, which was confusing. Also, the grading criteria was unclear.

David is an amazing lecturer and a leading scholar in sociology. It is so cool being taught by an expert like him. Soc2020 was so interesting and we grappled with some difficult concepts, but his lectures clarified all concepts. He facilitates tons of class discussion and genuinely cares about his students. I cannot recommend him enough.

David is so caring about students and is always willing to meet. He is a genius, a top scholar in the field. However, the class is very theoretical and lacks a clear structure. His lectures are hard to follow and often boring. Lots of difficult readings. It was definitely a challenging course, but the grading was pretty kind.

Really great professor but very theoretical and hard to follow sometimes. Asking questions and demonstrating engagement are key. A lot of reading and you have to do it or you will be confused in class and the papers will be hard to write. Very doable papers although vague grading criteria.

I took Order and Change in Society as my first intro to Sociology course and Dr. Cunningham did a great job with it. He's enthusiastic about sociology and teaching and it really shows in class. His "lectures" are more in the style of a facilitated discussion and he encourages participation. If you do the reading and show up, you'll do well.

Dr. Sommers is an amazing professor. I have taken two classes with her and loved them both - she always finds a way to make dense material fun and engaging and she is super approachable. Definitely take a class with her!

Dr. Sommers is incredibly passionate about what she teaches and goes above and beyond to care for her students. She has fantastic ratings from all her university positions for a reason. Her feedback and grading are clear, and discussions feel lighthearted and enjoyable. I'd take another class with her in a heartbeat!

The words I have for this woman are invariably good! Dr. Sommers is an amazing professor who really does care about her students. Her classes are so interesting and disscussion is mandatory, but as long as you have done the readings you'll be fine. Assignments are relatively easy as well! For reference I had her for an Ampersand program.

Dr. Sommers is such a lovely person who is so passionate about literature! Her Shakespeare class was super enjoyable and she brought kindness and humor to class discussions. She genuinely cares about her students and will meet with you to discuss essays. If you are going to take a Shakespeare class, take it with Sommers.

Super sweet, lovely person. Her lectures were not the most engaging I have ever had, but she also didn't grade too harshly and was pretty accommodating whenever I needed something. Passionate about her subject!

Dr. Sommers' class was amazing! Her grading on papers and things is on the tougher side, but definitely fair. Like most literature classes, her class was entirely discussion based. She was super engaging and made class very interesting and enjoyable. Some readings were a little on the long side, but most everything else was very manageable.

Diallo was a pleasure to take class with. His grading is lenient and easy, and he appreciates when you go beyond in the reading responses. Readings can be a bit much on top of other classes at times, but it is manageable if you set aside a day or two to really sit down and do it. Overall, I would take this course again and again. Exam was easy!

Professor Diallo is very passionate about the subjects he teaches, he is very kind, and he cares for his students. But, he assigns a LOT of reading in this class. While they were interesting, the load was overwhelmingly large some weeks. He is also very unclear about guidelines for big projects such as papers and exams. Thankfully he grades easily.

African Immigration was one of my favorite college classes. Prof Diallo chose excellent readings, guest lecturers, and shared so much about his own immigration experience. This class is not hard and prioritizes learning about what immigration from Africa entails. I took away and reflected a lot from this course and couldn't recommend it more!
Professor Diallo is amazing. He is very clear with his projects and homework and the actual work itself is not too bad, with 1-2 hours of reading per week. His grading is also easy and lenient.

Prof. Diallo is a fun instructor and so passionate about the topic he teaches. The class is easy with 1-3 hours outside of class commitment for reading response. He's also very understanding and cares about his students. Will definitely take all other courses he teaches

Professor Diallo is very knowledgeable on the topic of African history and the youth in Africa. This class is fairly easy and interesting. Do not stress about the reading response, final project, or the country presentations they were very low maintenance and did not take much of your time. He's an amazing professor and you learn a lot

Prof. Diallo is clearly very passionate, but it sometimes felt like the class was taught at a high school level. We only read one text a week and then were graded on writing a one-page regurgitation of the information. Discussions were fine but I didn't feel like I learned that much. Liked the map test and student presentations

Was just stepping in for another professor for 190B and it showed. Clearly didn't write the lectures, and they were delivered quite blandly. 190B honestly has an excessive number of assignments for an intro course. The assignments are all quite easy, at least, but several of them are essentially pointless. Expected way too much out of TAs.
Olson was awesome, very caring and knowledgeable guy. Lots of extra credit opportunities and the assignments were extremely easy. Take this to knock off a HUM req and to boost your GPA
A true expert and a really nice guy. Class is incredibly easy and chill, but lectures were interesting! Great guy all around.

lowkey goated and overqualified for his job. Is very knowledgeable, likes to adapt the course to what people are interested in and get feedback from students. Course was a little unstructured but I got the sense it was because the class is brand new and he was testing things out. If you do the work you should have no concerns about your grade

I took Anthro 3261 and the class was amazing! I would 100% recommend taking this class or taking any class by Kyle Olson. He is very laid back and approachable if you need help. Overall the most interesting 300 level course I have taken.

Olson does a great job with World Archeology. It is one of the best classes to take for your HUM. Pretty much everyone got an A. Attendance at guest lectures is required but you can watch on zoom or in person or check the recording after the fact to find the answer to the attendance question. Definitely take this class, very little workload.

Incredible teacher! I had Rami for Ethnomusicology and it was one of my favorite courses I've had at WashU so far. He clearly is passionate about both teaching and making sure students are enjoying the course, and customized the syllabus to topics that would be of interest to our particular class. Readings can be lengthy, but incredibly insightful.

One of my favorite classes at WashU. Such an interesting class that delves into so many aspects and areas of African music. A mix of history, analysis, and a little bit of music theory. Show up to class and take good notes and you'll do great on the exams. The readings are sometimes a little lengthy but always interesting. Also a really nice guy.

Do the readings (skimming is encouraged) because he has everyone write a 300-word "reverse abstract" on what they read for each class. Written/oral quizzes/exams are open note and are graded based on your discusssion of the sociohistorical context, musical style, and links between that question's subject and other material. Overall, a great class!

Even if you have never taken a music class before, you should absolutely take a class with Rami. His classes are unique and engaging and its very clear that he is truly passionate about what he teaches. The quizzes and graded work are also straightforward.

The most important thing for me when it comes to professors is whether they care about the students' learning or whether they just want to give everyone a bunch of content to memorize. Rami's teaching style makes it clear he's the latter, with custom syllabi for each class based on their interests. 1021 and 3021 are both amazing and informative.

Hands down one of the best professors at WashU! I had him for Music of the African Diaspora and the class did not disappoint. He knows how to make class fun and the material was interesting ranging from Samba, Ghanaian Highlife, Reggae, etc. Only had 1 paragraph reading reflections per class and 3 papers. I recommend taking a class with him

I had Stucky for Musics of the World and it was one of my favorite classes. The content is fascinating and it's clear Stucky enjoys what he teaches. Assignments are very manageable, class is collaborative, and he's extremely accommodating/understanding. I told all of my friends about this class. 10/10 would recommend!!

Food, power, and culture was easily my favorite class of the semester. It's kind of amazing how Richardson can talk for so long without slides or full outlines about various topics. She does say some out of pocket things though, and is very clear about her political stance (not in a forceful or necessarily bad way), but conservatives take heed

Professor Richardson clearly cares about her students and is passionate about her work. However, the lectures could get monotonous, and there were readings due before every class. The class isn't too hard, and the grading is mostly based on writing papers.

Professor Richardson is thoughtful and caring. The class was interesting and rigorous without being too overwhelming with work. She's accessible to meet with outside of class if you have essay questions. 100% would recommend.

So Professor Richardson is rather new to teaching but she's trying really hard to adapt and improve. She really cares about her students and she is really accessible. She's also pretty resourceful so reach out if you need any. In short, one of the most thoughtful professors at washu but do not expect really organized lectures.

Professor Richardson is clearly a brilliant academic, but she is not a good lecturer at all. She cares about her students, but her lectures are so incredibly boring that it's actually difficult to stay awake. The course content is also not that engaging due to the types of readings she chooses to assign. Overall, not worth your time.

Lectures are a little monotonous and boring. Her comments about student work can come off as abrasive. That being said, she seems to be trying to improve and adapt the class as best she can with student feedback. Her Canvas page is also very organized and it's easy to understand the course structure.

She moves very quickly, WAY too much work for a 2 credit class.  She also seems angry a lot of the time...maybe because her students don't practice enough. You really need to practice and learn the material - she gives pop piano quizzes and long written mid terms and finals

As others said before, she moves VERY quickly. She plays absolutely beautifully herself, but expects the students to be able to keep up. I had to practice a TON outside of class in order to barely get the assignments down, and then we'd be on to the next topic. If you are looking to learn piano just for fun, I wouldn't suggest her.
She will move fast, so keep up. Practice an hour before if you are very good at another instrument. If not, couple times over at least two days before the next class.

She is an amazing piano player, but she moves very, very quickly.  She expects a lot of outside practice out of her students, and she assigns a lot of work for each class.  If you've never taken piano before, I would suggest taking private lessons in concurrence with this course.

Gives extremely clear and detailed feedback on papers, which is not something that a lot of professors are willing to do. It does take him a long time to grade papers. Provides some interesting readings, and so long as you read them and attend class, you'll do fine.

Professor was good in class. He stayed neutral with arguments and did not force people to talk or anything. When it comes to papers, he will critique your rough draft and give great notes. If you turn in a rough draft you usually get a good grade. Exam was ridiculous. Created a study guide and then used nothing from it for the actual questions.

Gotta agree with the below post-- took this class P/F and very glad I did so. Got a C- on my first essay which admittedly wasn't very good, tried a different approach on the second one and got a C+. Exams are better, but only slightly so, and focus on specifics from arguments rather than main takeaways. Pretty aggravating.

Very interesting class material, but professor was underwhelming. Took months to get exams and papers back and despite this, graded extremely harshly. A goofy guy, but he leads discussions well, even when people are shy to talk (or haven't read). He's not my favorite professor, but it's well worth taking Present Moral Problems with him: he'll let the debate happen, expectations are clear, and it covers important topics

The HW and Exams that she designed contained frequent errors and ill-defined parameters.  Resultantly,I frequently had to seek clarification via email.  Her response was often VERY delayed. Lectures were very helpful. Her notes are very organized. She talks pretty fast, but to some extend that gives the class more energy(which I appreciate since thermo is a pretty boring topic). Learned a lot of Matlab from this class too, didn't anticipate it. The class is by no means easy, and it requires a lot of work. But it was very rewarding.
Dr. Lo is really enthusiastic about teaching and her concern for students learning shines through.

I agree with the previous comment. The class is boring and it's hard, but Lo is always willing to help. You can tell she's passionate about the material and tries really hard to get us excited about it too.
Class is unbelievably boring but she really does try to make things interesting.  She's ALWAYS available to answer questions and tries to be fair with her grading.

Julia is a gift. Get coffee before class, because I had trouble participating before noon, and discussions are a lot better if you're prepared and engaged. She can be a tough essay grader, but ask her for help and she's usually more clear about what she's looking for. Her laugh is so awesome.

She makes class interesting if you go, a lot of people didn't go to our class. I definitely liked listening to her talk if I could stay awake at 8:30. Grades can be a little tough just because there are only 4 essays, and doing below average on one can really ruin your grade for the course. I would suggest going to class, it can def. help grades.
She's awesome. Take a class with her if possible.  She's very clear.  Her laugh is amazing.
She's awesome. Can be difficult if you don't show enough interest in class. She can be boring at times but overall if you're interested in the subject she's a good teacher. Pretty easy if you try.
Awesome teacher.  She cares a lot and really helped.  Take any course you can with her.
Expects well developed argument and critical skills for participation in class

She taught Spanish 200 at SCSU and is amazing. I am not good at spanish but if you take her you will pass!! She is a little difficult to understand because she is also chinese. But that makes the class even more fun because she knows so much! She is very nice and helpful! Easy to make laugh! Passed STAMP with her! Take her!! She will be stress free
Miaowei is very nice, but difficult to understand and communicate with.

Weng is amazing. She's very helpful and always willing to go an extra mile. She's also likeable and understanding. I loved her and will miss her class very much. This woman was a great teacher for intro-level Spanish.  She was very clear and spoke with a perfect accent and not too fast.  She was always willing to go the extra step for the students and was always willing to joke around and even have class outside.  I learned a lot from her.

Marshall offers a lot of guidance on writing and specific points where you can improve your story. He offers feedback and gives areas on where stakes can be added and where it can be expanded. Overall, Marshall really cares about his students, and his writing exercises and comments have made me a better writer.

Got great feedback on my work. Read a lot of interesting pieces. Was readily available to help me think through my pieces outside of class. Frequently interrupted the women in the class, but let the men ramble on without interruption. Kind of a red flag. But a nice guy otherwise.

I've never received so much feedback on my writing ever by a long shot. He'll write 6 pages single spaced feedback on your workshop piece. A really nice guy, clearly cares about helping you improve your writing. Participation-heavy and a lot of work for a 200-level class, but overall worth taking with him if you want someone to push you to be a better writer. I learned a lot.

The professor is very nice in person but gives really tough grades. Participation heavy and he keeps track all the time! A lot of readings and writings. If you want an easier pass, his classes aren't the best choices.

You'll read a tough paper for every lecture (+quiz) and write difficult algorithms in a language you may have never seen before. But, you'll learn a lot. His class is doable as an undergrad. Just stay on top of reading and dont assume you can knock out the exercises in a 4 hour binge session.

(1) Kind but lack of teaching skill. (2) Too many reading yet almost no supplement reading materials. (3) The explanation of readings are not insigntful. the best

fabulous teacher.

Basically, Rachel is amazing. And she is like 24 and awesome and is more like a peer than an authority figure. And I got an A+. I love Rachel Sullivan.
Grad student lecturer. She is pretty easy-going and actually lets you workshop your work, unlike other Poetry Workshop teachers. If you're interested  in workshop, try and get her class.
Ogilvie is the man... His lectures can be a little long-winded, but he is one of the nicest teachers I have had at Wash U.

Very entertaining. Positive attitude. Definately one of the kindest and most understanding professors. Class covers some interesting material. Its kind of an environmental ethics class. If you attend, and put in some effort, you will do well.

Dr Ogilvie is the coolest prof. ever. He gives tons of extra credit and will go to great lenghts to ensure that you understand. He's also very funny and cracks many jokes. Very enthusiastic and friendly instructor.  Class may appear to be heavy on the math for non-science majors, but it is definitely possible to do well on the tests regardless of previous experience in science classes.  If you attend class, you'll do fine.

One of my favorite teacher's I've ever had. Is a clear, concise lecturer that uses practical examples from the real world to reinforce the material. Cares about his students and has a great sense of sarcastic humor.

Nice guy, a ok teacher, however if you have the option of Sterling or him take Sterling. She is a better lecturer and doesnt let a select few ppl dominate the class. He really cares about his students and does a good job of explaining things most of the time. He was very reasonable. Tests can be hard, but he takes a lot of liberty when it comes to moving up final grades a few points.
Decent professor. Nice guy.
Good lecturer but trying to talk to him was miserable.

Meeting and learning with professor Tikhonov is a fortune of mine. Professor successfully showed us physics' basic idea and its close relationship with math through the course. He designed everything well! He was caring! He authentically tried to help the students.

HE IS SO HOT (respectfully) TAKE HIS CLASS

Go to Office Hours.

Professor Fields is awesome...but can we have some cases that aren't from the 1980s?

Professor Larsen is extremely knowledgeable about psychology, especially his specialty in personality psychology. Dr. Larsen is a terrible teacher. He spends most of the lectures talking about himself and how "awesome" he is, while spouting off many problematic and offensive comments. idk if its bc shes from new zealand but her tests r different.

Tests averages are low, but they are not hard. People probably don't study but if you do it is easy to score high. Some of the other stuff is graded a little harshly.
