Dr. Sun has no empathy for her students, especially those in DR. Her lectures are straight-forward, but memorize every detail to do well on the exams - all 4 of them, plus the final. Confident in her teaching. Direct, no-nonsense professor that values her time and yours. Caring and amicable.
Prof. Perfecto gives amazing lectures. She's incredibly knowledgeable, and you can really tell that she's passionate about teaching. And she makes beautiful, neat slides!

Very solid class overall. Well structured course with clear expectations. Prof does her best to make lectures engaging and encourages class participation. You need to go to class. Assignments are fair and there aren't too many other than exams/group project.

Good instructor and very personable. Lecture handouts were not very useful reference for studying for tests (perhaps by design?). Tests were definitely challenging. Took this course during Covid so perhaps in-person will be different.

Prof Perfecto is super sweet and hilarious. She's very understanding and a fair grader. She made marketing research relevant and interesting to learn about. Perfecto is a great professor, she is engaging and makes the course relevant for students. She cares for students and if you go to office hours she will answer your questions and give you lots of support. She is super understanding of students and will do her best to help you do your best.

One of my favorite professors at WashU! I took Consumer Behavior with her and learned so much. She teaches in a way that's super digestible, so I've found that I remember a lot from her class. Elanor was one of the friendliest professors I have had at WashU. Her lectures were full of personal examples, interesting stories, and funny sidelines. I would love to take another class with her, so if you are thinking of taking Consumer Behavior, wait to take it with her!

Professor Williams is the sweetest woman alive! She is such a wonderful lady. Prof. Williams is new but she's already proven to be a great professor. Her lectures strike a solid balance of humor & intellect. Prof. De Roo is a sweetheart. She is very thoughtful and direct during her lectures. Lectures are interesting and easy to follow, but there is attendance in a 200 person class, and a ton of reading. Sections were painful and not helpful. If you go to class and turn in your papers on time you're fine. Deroo is very concerned with making sure you understand the material & is great.
Helpful, interesting topics, lectures, films, etc. A  little bit TOO concerned about what students think of her courses. Nice if you talk to her outside of class.

awesome guy. human ev.'s a very interesting class

Really nice guy and very helpful. Wants students to succeed.  Come to class, review notes/practice problems before exams, and that's all you really need. This is the kind of class where if you get it, you get it.
He knows his stuff and is really nice.

So good XD

Dr. Brennan cares about her students. Though the material for this class isn't riveting, she makes you WANT to like it (though it is hard to like numerical methods IMO). Her homeworks take a lot of time but they set you up nicely for exams. It's definitely time consuming but worth the effort.

Dr. Brennan is one of the strongest professors in the department. She clearly cares about teaching and it shows in her engaging lectures and willingness to talk to students outside of class through both Piazza and office hours.

Dr. Brennan is the best professor I have ever met. The material is well prepared. She cares about students. HWs take reasonable amount of time and they are very enlightening. The exam difficulty is fair. She is an easy-grader. The course is graded on a holistic understanding of the material instead of memorizing. I will take more courses with her.

McCune is an open book, very funny. Knows the material well. But he's EXTREMELY hard to contact outside of class. Flag him in class to talk after b/c he rarely answers emails. Homework is graded by completion, with a mix of difficult and  easier problems covered in class. No midterms or finals. And all quizzes can be redone!

Shankar is a really cool professor. He is understanding, passionate, and very intelligent. Sometime his answers to questions weren't stellar and it was a bit confusing. Nevertheless, if you pay attention, his lectures can be thrilling and you can learn a ton. Recommend!

Professor Shankar is a great professor who does his upmost to explain the conceptually difficult concepts of electricity and magnetism in an understandable way to a class of freshman with little background experience in the field. He made the class very not stressful, while still providing challenging problems that inspired me to learn. A+ teacher

Dr. Mukherji is clearly a very smart, caring guy, but his lectures are unnecessarily confusing. Considering this was an intro physics course, the concepts and the explanations behind the concepts taught in the course should be relatively straightforward, but he over-explained everything he covered this semester.

Shankar is a solid professor. Though at times he can be a little wordy and overcomplex in his writing, he is always willing to help and explain--even on the homework. He loves this subject and wants his students to do well. Would reccomend.

I adore Dr. Kravchenko. She's truly one of the most caring professors at WashU, and her class is so interesting. I struggled with a lot of health and personal problems this semester, and she was so understanding and allowed me to complete the work on a timeline that worked for me. Additionally, the course is fascinating and thought provoking,
Not a hard class but I was very unengaged with the material, classmates, and her. Lacked communication and seemed like she did not want to be there most of the time. Interesting content though.

One of the best humanities courses I've taken. You're not forced to participate vocally, which is great for people who hate speaking in discussions (like I do). You can get participation credit by just filling out worksheets she gives out. She very clearly outlines what she wants you to know, pretty interesting class too.

One of my favorite teachers I've ever had! She cares a lot about her students and their understanding. She gives a good amount of reading, but for tests, she gives you everything you need to know and there are no surprises.

She is an interesting professor, but a hard grader who never specifies what she wants and almost seems to want students to get B's instead of A's. Exams are example heavy. She makes you take pop quizzes every class so they're just quizzes. And she takes notes on who participates every day.

She had the best class structure I have ever seen. Everything is clearly organized in the modules and very easy to follow. The exams are a little tough, got a D on the first one but still ended the class with an A. She emphasizes group projects a lot and is very knowledgeable. One of the best professors I have had.

Selin is nice and cares a lot about marketing, but her grading criteria was set up to where you get full points or nothing. Tests questions are also difficult so getting an A is a challenge with her (Consumer Behavior 4101 - Ohio State University)

Great professor overall. Tries to make even the most abstract research concepts interesting, and leads lectures very well. Makes it very clear what she expects on exams and cases. Grading procedures are very demanding, but the curve makes  it bearable.
Intro to Archaeology has a lot of material, but if you study the tests are not that hard.  Labs are very fun and a good way to boost your grade if needed.

Not easy, but fair and always tried to help you improve. Lots of study material. Lectures cool - one was in 3D - and better than the readings, which are not as important to study. Always tries hard in lecture to add current discoveries. She's good, take her.
Learned more in this intro class than any other. She works you, but is fair and gives lots of study materials for the test.

Makes no sense. She is a horrible lecturer and is a pain to listen to. Unrealistic with her grades and exams. Her class alone made me reconsider my anthropology major so that I wouldn't have to take the class

i personally liked her a lot, the lectures were pretty easy to follow but not all the information was on the powerpoint. you HAVE to attend lectures and actually read the textbook in order to pass. daily quizzes are really easy if you do the reading
Overall great person and interesting lecturer, however can be unclear at times

Personality psychology seemed like such an interesting class, but trust me, it couldn't get more boring. She doesn't seem to definitively define or explain any concept, but dances around it. The worst is when she tries to force participation- she just waits for an answer, sometimes for minutes of tortured silence. It's 9 am, give it up.

I had her for personality, could have been interesting but lectures are boring, rushes through regurgitated material, cannot speak well or lead discussion, bad at answering questions, doesn't seem to truly understand material or is just so nervous that she gets confused

The man is a total genius, and it shows. His knowledge of the material is extensive, almost encyclopedic, and his ability to peel through the substance and give you a simple recitation of the law is unrivaled by any other professor I've taken. That said, he will often lose his train of thought making connections to other concepts, so follow along.
Great lecturer. Really really knows the topics and is enthusiastic about them.  Can be less helpful outside of class.
Decent prof if you can get past his elitism and recycled jokes. Straightforward presentation of material.
Class was interesting and concepts explained well.  I found the final unfairly weighted towards RAP concepts.  It comprised a far larger percentage of exam material than it did of class time.
Class content somewhat difficult but she is very supportive.

Professor Rashkova is one of the kindest and helpful professors in WashU's business school. Yes, SCOT458 is inherently difficult, but Professor Rashkova does her best to make sure students understand what she is teaching in-class. I needed extra help sometimes and she was always ready to assist! Very intelligent and interesting person to talk to :)
good prof

Goes through content in class as if we already know it, doesn't teach us the equations properly and how to apply it, homework is way harder than class content, exams take hours. Professor Rashkova is a nice person, but not a great teacher/professor.

He can be helpful when he isn't playing favorites. Tends to ignore students who aren't sucking up to him.  Has quite an ego.  I do think he knows what he is talking about, but he doesn't meet the requirements of a graduate studio professor because he doesn't take an interest in each student's project equally in moving forward through semesters.
extremely thoughtful. passionate and knows what the hell he´s talking about. hes a baller!
Wasn't really helpful. Has a bit of an ego.
Cares about the students. Makes you think about writing.
Not the most exciting professor. Understanding and caring of students, but often comes off as judgmental.
Some of the best help with poetry I have ever gotten, even though I was a first semester freshman when I took the class. Professor Salli is an execellent person to talk to about papers as well.

Mat Smith is a really interested professor. He provides useful feedback on workshop stories, and you get the sense that he has a lot of experience with writing and analysis. I wish he was a better lecturer sometimes, as class sometimes would falter because of his quiet style and discussions would have to be lead by other students.
Amazing writing 1 teacher. Take any class you can with this teacher. He has a dry sense of humor but is hilarious in class. He can make a dull, required class pretty fun.
great writing 1 TEACHER!!!!!!!!!!!
Great guy!
best help sessions- entertaining and you will learn.  Dr. Spees is the man
GREAT RECITATION LEADER....this guy was hilarious
Don gives valuable, specific advice and really cares about his students. He rewards hard work and fearlessness. Great professor and guy.
Don is an awesome teacher and person! His assignments were fun which made you want to work hard in his class.
Has really good feedback and teaches you to recognize ways to make your work better.

Absolutely love Gina. She's a bit intimidating at first but once you start turning in work she's nothing but kind and ready to help you. She really lets you work on what you're interested in, and helps you to develop your vision and with the technical side. 100%  recommend.

So great! She really tries to make everyone grow, and she pushes you in the direction that you really have an eye for (even before you realize you have that aesthetic to your photos). Incredibly knowledgeable and so much fun. I could think of no better teacher I could have had from 6 to 9pm on Mondays and Wednesdays. A Gem! Grade reflects effort.
Simply the best. You're into photography, take her class. Inspirational, and will give extensive feedback on your artwork without obsessing over homework requirements. Art  school. Real contemporary.

Hardcore, real world experience.  Walked us through his own very public background and what he learned, then brought in speakers to do the same.  Amazed that his "Iron Laws" kept coming up - both in class and outside.  I started the class like one of my friend - wanting to drop and then stuck with it and learned so much.  My friend missed out.

"PW" acted as more of a coach than a traditional Professor.  He clearly turned his class into a 'working lab' for professional success using the topic of Entrepreneurship.  I credit my job interview success with his class - far more practical than any others I have taken.

Prof.  Weinhaus is a mix of academic insight (his "Iron Laws"), real world experience, and total one-on-one attention to our individual performances.  He gave extra credit  just w/in our grasp, intended to lead us toward further improvement.  He constantly told us that he drove us so hard so that he knew he could rely on us "in the real world."
John Hoal was in compliance with those who launched powerful initiatives to 'gut' Kiel Opera House in 1998. Those initiatives were stopped. 'Wait for what the ULI recommends'. Nope the were stopped.
teaches with passion

He has extensive expertise in Urban Planning, but his mean spirted attitude is unbearable. I learned more from him during his lectures than I ever would talking to the guy. I would advise taking a seminar with him before taking his studio. If you can get along with him during the seminar than take his studio.
No Comments
No Comments

Lecturing could be clearer. Mid Term and Final consist of in class essay where you research the answer (yuck) and also a 3 and 8 page paper respectively.  All due w/in 1 week of exams.  Too much. Also a 10 page paper for 33% of your grade.

This guy is awesome!! Great real world examples from his past like consulting for fortune 500 companies guiding them through events I remember! His lecturing is really good and he was able to keep my attention for hours. very rare for me :)  Also,  he really seems to care about others, super nice guy!

Taking this class right now and it's incredible!!! Not only is the content enriching but the professor makes it come alive with real-time examples in social media and examples from his past Fortune 100 experiences.  He knows big data and he knows how it applies to solving management issues.
Awesome professor!  He made the concepts applicable to my everyday work.  As a business major this is everything.  Bridged complex academics with real world examples.
Really funny guy and does a good job keeping the room engaged during class.  Necessary to come to class because of iclicker.

Great lectures! Professor Zu seems genuinely enthusiastic about teaching physics, even to those who aren't particularly interested in learning. Ended up watching lecture videos for most of the semester but he was definitely very personable and good at answering questions when I did attend lectures in person.
clear lectures; nice guy; easy with grading and grades; fully understand the materials; care about students' understanding and questions...
Dr Sovich is extremely smart and clear when teaching but the class was so hard. Everyone I know in it struggled on the exams but did well on the quizzes. The class covers a ton of content as well
He's a good professor and explains concepts very clearly. I would take his class again!
do the work, you'll get an A

I had the chance to talk to him outside of class and he is honestly one of the most genuine professors I've had so far. The class is very discussion-based with several papers. It is pretty easy-going.

Great relevant topics. Really gets into the nitty gritty on policies cities can implement. One of the few local-level focus classes in Poli Sci. Highly recommend for public policy, urban studies, or environmental studies people.
He assigned lots of readings you don't have to do them. His lectures were interesting, but relatively lacking discussion given the size of the class.

Very knowledgeable and helpful professor. Homework/readings aren't terribly long, but can be a little bit dense. Fair grader and gives great feedback on assignments/papers. I definitely learned a lot from her class.
Julia is an absolutely wonderful professor who comes to class prepared and ready to share her vast knowledge with her students. Prepare for her classes, and you will learn a lot. Wonderful.

Professor Walker is an absolute Joy. I had absolutely no interest in 19th cent. drama when I got into the class but she made it incredibly interesting and worthwhile. She is smart, funny, kind, and a fair grader. Very good mix of history and Drama. Take a class with her if you can!
Professor Milich did an incredible job with the pandemic course. She was always approachable and did a good job organizing the material which was very impressive given the size of the class.

A wonderful teacher who inspired me to take classes in more fields. Her pandemic course was very well put together and I enjoyed taking it. The assignments were easy and fun, and although the course was only three weeks long, it really expanded my knowledge and understanding of COVID-19. I would definitely take a course with her again.

Professor Milich was always there greeting us with a smile, encouraging us to be compassionate in all situations, and wishing us well as lectures ended each day. She was always approachable when we had questions was quick to respond to emails (provided that they were during the week). Overall, the class was great!

Fuzhong is not a honorable person. He does not respect your time. In his world, he is a tenured professor and he earns a pre-set salary anyway. So he would assume you are the same. He does not care whether you achieve anything beyond drifting and passing the time. He has a dark character. If you do not satisfy him in his way, he would sabotage.

The lectures are long by being a 4 credit class, but he is fantastic at teaching. His notes and lectures are well organized and easy to understand, and he makes it very clear what concepts are necessary both through the lectures and the homework. I was never shocked by an exam. Never miss a lecture, that's at least how to pass.

Throughout the entirety of this course I was very impressed with how clearly Professor Zhang was able to communicate his ideas. We did a ton of practice problems in class which were very helpful on the homework assignments.

I would actually die for Prof Brown. I took Queering Citizenship: Gender/Abolition with her and it was a perfect balance of her incredible knowledge and well-facilitated student discussion. She cares an unbelievable amount about her students and it's reflected in the great classroom environment. Don't graduate without taking a class with her.

Prof. Brown is the professor everyone wants to have; patient, generous with grading, and accessible. She definitely has a WGSS teaching style, but it worked great and clearly inspired every student in the class. She is very knowledgable on the course material and incredibly intelligent, but tempers that with humility and kindness. A great class!
Dr. Brown is one of my favorite professors I have had so far, and I hope to take one of her classes again. She teaches with a genuine desire to listen and learn alongside her students.

I have taken all of Professor Keating's classes in Law School. I could not recommend him more. He is the sweetest guy who really takes time to go through what is often fairly dense material. You should certainly take at least one class with him before you graduate.

Literally the nicest academic I have ever met. His presentation of the (admittedly very dense and confusing) material could be slightly better, but he will take as much time as you need to answer questions outside of class.

A great Prof that makes you love a boring subject. How does this mild mannered man make bankruptcy exciting? I admire a Prof who doesn't pretend to be superman, admits that a subject is confusing, and that sometimes he doesn't have all the answers. Why couldn't I have had this guy 1L year rather than 3L? Wulaw might have seemed less than miserable.
He explains ML concepts with simple thought process and nailed them with demos and visualization. And thanks for uploading videos on youtube. Best ever ML introductory content.

The class is amazing, although fast and sometimes challenging discussion. Requires hard work. A lot of coding, competitions, and sometimes self-learn. If you follow the class, it's a huge fun. Never noticed the 80 minute length of the class.

Good teacher, keeps class very interesting and lighthearted, projects were fun. Definitely a lot of work, but very enjoyable. Tests were probably weighted too heavily given the amount of work involved with the projects.

fairly easy class, but she takes herself much too seriously. She often seems cranky and gets short with students easily. also forgets what she's trying to say a lot. One of the worst teachers I've had at WashU
friendly, easy, helpful.  only thing that irritated me was that when we were talking in small groups, she'd hover.  other than that, a good teacher.
A very helpful and understanding Spanish teacher.  Not a difficult grader at all.  I would take her again if I had the chance.

I really liked Professor Chun. She was very kind and funny and made lectures engaging by making us participate constantly. Contrary to the previous ratings, I never found Professor Chun intimidating at all. Because class sizes are extremely small, make sure to be prepared in class as you will participate a lot.

Heayoung Chun is a fantastic professor. She ensures everyone is involved in class and understands well how to teach the Korean language using proven-effective tactics. She may seem intimidating at first but know that this view of her may come to pass. The class, however, can be challenging at times, with 2-3 quizzes per week, so stay on top of it!

Professor Chun can be intimidating at times, but she truly cares about her students' learning. Professor Chun works actively to make sure each student is engaged during class. This 5 credit course is fast-paced, with 1-2 quizzes a week, a chapter exam every other week, oral presentations, a midterm, and a final. Be prepared to learn a lot!

Professor Dalla Torre creates a lively classroom setting where I always felt comfortable and excited to participate in class. For language learning it's important to actively practice and I felt like the classroom facilitated this. There is VHL homework due before every class and attendance is important but this should expected for a language class

Prof Dalla Torre is not a good professor and constantly displays her arbitrary grading system she uses for oral exams and tests/quizzes where comments are unclear and unhelpful for work done. She also insinuated that one of her students was sexist because he asked a course policy question. I would avoid her as she makes claims like this frequently.

Unreasonable amount of work: online HW due before class (3x/week), 4 quizzes, 2 exams, 2 oral exams, and a final project. 

More importantly, with 50 minute classes, students could barely finish quizzes and 12-part exams left people scrambling. If you can't finish in time, she says to contact Disability Resources. Enthusiastic but very dismissive.

I really like Prof Ward as a person, but was really disappointed with his class. Expectations are incredibly unclear as to grading, what he wants out of assignments (essays don't have prompts), and class structure is very unorganized. He seems to not know what he wants to do with the course and improvises class time.

Does not have clear grading criteria. Lacks clear instructions, engagement skills, and structure when it comes to lecture and often rambles. Not very great at giving critical and productive feedback in a manner that doesn't delegitimize work. Has a syllabus but jumps around A LOT and this makes it hard to figure out what material should be prepared

I really enjoyed this class with Eyal. He teaches Hebrew well and actually helps you understand the structural conventions of the language rather than making you brute-force memorize vocabulary. The only thing is that he assigns A LOT of homework, so get ready to take on that workload if you take this class with Eyal.
Eyal is an absolutely amazing and caring professor and I thoroughly recommend taking intro hebrew with him

Never cried so much from a professor. She expects her students to be perfect and if you fall short in any capacity she will penalize you to the fullest extent. Homeworks have nothing to do with the lectures and are not easy while being a huge chunk of the grade. Exams had nothing to do with homeworks or lectures. She should not be a teacher.
I wish I took it with the SLU professor. Did not like how she ran classes. Went to lectures but not required

When there was a challenging homework due, students would gather in his office way past his office hours, and Russ was always patient and encouraging. His photo was the background picture on the math department computer for the two semesters.  That's how much fun we had!
Much harder than other semesters of calc 2.

Great Course on Robotics. Got me interested in the topic. It was really helpful to have real life applications and easy to follow lectures. I wish more such courses were offered. Needed to have some background in Control Systems but he was willing to spend time on background material.
No Comments

Very unclear in assignments and what you should study for testsquizzes. Classes are confusing. A lot of reading, and you WILL be tested on specifics on quizzes. Projects are simple and not such a bad grader for those. Overall a bit of a confusing course.

This class as an absolute joke. Professor Xu was very disorganized and confusing. She would assign the wrong readings and blame students and simply be unwilling to help. She is also a tough grader. Stay away if you can.
Really engaging and will challenge your thinking with his classic: "ACTUALLY..." Also, he's super hard to prove wrong!
Great professor! Learned so much!
Doesn't seem like she really cared to be there ever and acts stuck up for some reason. Not someone I would recommend to other students

This review probably isn't worth much as she's leaving soon, but for whoever's curious: Dr. Solanki is hands down my favorite professor I've ever had. Perhaps the best person I've ever met. Pretty much anyone who's met her would say the same. Smart, funny, helpful, really wants her students to succeed and have fun in her class.

I've taken Dr. Hanes for both Bio 2960 and 2970 and she's amazing. Very clear with expectations for lab, grading, and participation. She's really good at rewording content from general lectures to be more accessible and easier to understand for lab application.

Dr. Hanes was the best bio lab instructor! She was incredibly accessible outside of class and effectively and efficiently led our lab sessions which was very impressive given the difficulties of lab on zoom. I didn't learn quite as much as I would have hoped as the labs were kept fairly simple, but the concepts it did cover were very helpful.

Definitely the most organized professor I've had in the engineering program yet. You have a lot of resources given to you to pass the class. His exams are very similar to the work he does in his lectures and homework. He's very good at making his exams moderate in difficulty. He even let us earn back credit on our exams when the class did poorly.

He really cared about how we did/took to the material: lots of partial credit, made the final optional, dropped constant hints exam probs, etc. He was also not finicky about late submissions, unlike his terrible TA. Go to his office hours when you're stuck or need clarification about grades. He actually regraded my assignment once. Super nice.

Craig Lincoln is not a good physics lecturer. His lectures are clear enough but he assumes that students know where than most do, he's condescending when asked questions, and he takes forever to answer emails. Take a Physics lecture with Hynes or Medioros.

Professor Lincoln is a talented educator and presenter. He is clearly passionate about the field of physics and while his lectures are not as high-energy as some may desire, he is very effective at building foundational understanding and answering student questions or concerns whenever they arise. Would absolutely take a higher-level class with him

I came into this class not knowing what it would be about or how much I would like it. However, I am really happy I took it. I learned a lot! Professor Liang has been one of my favorite professors at WashU. She is very engaging in class. And she makes it easy to understand and apply concepts.

Professor Liang is the best professor I've had at WashU. Her classes are really engaging, with lots of peer discussions, group activities, and case examples. And she deeply cares about her students. She makes sure everyone feels safe to participate in class, and she provides individualized feedback to everyone. I highly recommend her course!

Nice guy, class was boring at times (polisci isn't my area) but he made it interesting with small tidbits. Other review is tripping because I checked the quiz average (a 95) and the final essay average (a 92), with even higher medians. Doesn't mean the class was easy per se but I think the expectations were clear and he was also willing to help.

Dr. Olson is a believer in in-class essays and then offers very little feedback and grades harshly. Class was boring and assessments did not fit the material. He did not care about the individual success of students.

I had Rob as a trainer for being a tutor at the Writing Center. He is kind and sometimes tells stories or jokes, but also expects dedication and commitment to the class. I don't know if it would differ for other more traditional classes but his grading was not overly harsh and he seems to want the best for all his students.

The syllabus states if you are 30 minutes late or leave early from his 2 1/5 hour course, you will be marked absent. At 3 absences you fail the course. If you're late to class and turn in a paper, you lose a letter grade automatically.

Dr. Bai is an extraordinary lecturer. While in class, you feel like you are learning a ton. However, in hindsight, he really didn't explain the concepts that well and the problem sets that he gave were too surface-level and easy. This class is designed as a "break" from our rigorous ChemE curriculum, but I don't feel prepared for the future.

Dr. Bai makes materials science interesting. His lectures are very clear and his practice is very helpful to learn what he covers during lectures. Some materials might be confusing towards the end of the semester but he tries his best to help us understand. However, exams cover a lot so be prepared for everything.
Cannot believe I pay 80k a year to be taught by this man.

I took the class a year ago. I heard different feedbacks for him. Prof Thimsen is very smart. I heard that year SP2018 were insanely hard which the homework is 24 hours/week. In our year(SP2019), he teaches clearly on the concepts, some of which overlaps with GenChem. However, his homework and final are really hard. Ready to learn during exams.

I took 1313 and plan on taking another class with him in the spring. Absolutely my favorite class I've taken! I was genuinely looking forward to class days. We just get to learn about music and spend time making it, and his passion for the subject really shows. Quizzes are easy enough, and his feedback is incredible.

If your goal is to hone your creative mind this course is for you. Class sizes are small (10-12). He always graded based on the vision of the artist. His genre was a lot different from mine but that never stopped him from helping me improve in the ways I wanted!

She is a really engaging lecturer and it's great to have a professor from the Caribbean at WashU. Her grading system is harsh though, and it felt like no matter how hard I worked it was impossible to get an A. She also didn't provide much feedback from assignments or lay out specific expectations she had for grading.
Tons of reading, was not necessary to complete all of it. A little lecture heavy, but she is really great, very nice and laid back

She tends to seek a lot of compliments and recognition from fellow students and can be quite demanding in that regard. Additionally, her rubrics for assignments often lack clarity or specificity. She is known for being a strict grader, and students who go above and beyond to gain her favor tend to receive better grades.

Course content and grading was inconsistent with the syllabus. I found that even as an experienced hand knitter with a no knowledge of machine knitting, Prof. often focused on basic skills and swatches leaving little to no time for unrealistic final projects. She taught basic concepts wrong and asked advanced students to explain regularly.

Professor Precup explains things well and is so nice. Generally, the examples she goes over are easier than the ones on the quizzes but just do some extra practice outside of class and you'll be ok. The Calc II workbook is really helpful because it has practice problems and equation summary sheets. Webwork problems are a good grade booster too.
Excellent lecturer. Clearly understands material and is able to effectively communicate it. Homework and exams is challenging but certainly doable. Accessible outside of class.

Wonderful professor, so knowledgable, insightful, and thoughtful. Always available for more discussion on complicated and sensitive issues. Is genuinely interested in students learning and their well-being.

Fantastic teacher! Don't take this class if you are looking for an easy A. She will challenge you to constantly improve and expects a lot. She will give you extra help and extra reading if you're interested and is very accessible and friendly. Very thorough class and extremely interesting. Highly recommend!!
Mosi is the sweetest person you'll ever meet. He's super helpful and willing to talk to students. There is a slight language barrier, but he's such a nice person, and his class is really great.
He's slightly tough to understand due to his access, but if you actually work for your grade he would help you.
She's a great teacher. Very clear and really likes to discuss ethics! So interesting.
She's a really laid back teacher. I recommend the class if you're into ethics.

If you actually want to learn Italian, try not to take an elementary Italian class with Brody. She seems passionate about teaching the language, but she is used to teaching upper level classes, so she assumed we knew a lot more than we actually did. This meant she was speaking fluent Italian to us by week two, even when we had zero experience.
Her lectures are very interactive and interesting. The professor is passionate about the Italian language and culture. She is also very approachable and hilarious.

Ken was awesome. He's an extremely friendly/personable guy and very easy to talk to. Many of the topics covered in class are useful in terms of applications outside of class and in a career. Most of the work is longer assignments that can be time consuming, but if you follow the grading rubric, you'll get most of the points pretty easily.

She is a great teacher and is very understanding. She can be funny at times and her lectures were not boring. I do wish the class wasn't graded so heavily with tests. Personally, I never had enough time when it came to finishing the tests, but I still did very well in the class. Homework is easy but can take some time if you are new to Korean.

Greg Ott is one of those teachers who so genuinely cares about what he teaches and his students. Always tries to make the class fun. There was a day where he just brought us a bunch of foods of St. Louis and we talked about places to see there. Show up, participate, and work hard and the mandatory class you dreaded taking could become a favorite
Always willing to help and is very nice in class, respect her and she'll help you out. Homework was stupid though, she just posts the answer keys online and you copy it.
Really, really helpful and cares a lot about her students. The class is easy if you do the work and make an effort to talk in workshops. Definitely recommend the class.

I was interested in engineering project management, prior.  I did not expect for the class to focused heavily on construction management.  The assignments were very specific to construction management which I felt strayed from the courses intent.  Professor Gross is very vague on content but specific per assignment.  Stay away if at all possible.
Wonderful professor! Very helpful during office hours, and engaging during class. I loved constitutional politics.
She is an amazing teacher who cares more about her students understanding the material rather then their ability to memorize facts

I took "Game of Thrones, Game of Tones" with Professor Bokulich, and it ended up being one of my favorite classes ever taken in University! There were a lot of assigned readings, but they were fascinating and facilitated really engaging discussions during class meetings. Professor Bokulich is super approachable, and her lectures are so interesting.

Took for ARCH3248, Architecture History. Easy class, took it during COVID so on Zoom. Learned a lot about architecture that I still remember. Passionate instructor, can't speak too much about grading difficulty but I put in moderate effort and got an A. Just a major paper and architecture sketch as the main assignments.

Avoid at all costs.

This professor cares little for teaching, suffers selective amnesia, and demonstrates astonishly little empathy for students. She will be incredibly late to class if she shows up at all, and she does not prepare beforehand. She grades arbitrarily. She only sees through her own limited perspective and WILL NOT LISTEN TO YOU.

Professor Thimson and his Thermo II class literally had me a day away from switching majors. Literally dropping out of engineering. This class was hell. Thimson doesn't care about his students and it shows. His class is unavoidable for many, but good luck to all who have him.

I enjoyed his class. He was clear and knowledgeable on alot of things. He was funny. His class wasn't stressful at all. I can tell he loves his profession and cares about people. I would definitely suggest taking his class. You have to be interested in Psychology to get it.
Took in Summer 2021. The lectures were very useful and the videos he showed us were informative and hilarious at the same time. Dr. Polites is such a cool person and he is always open for questions.
Huge workload and difficult exam.

Suri was my calculus subsection leader. She was unresponsive to student questions and unwilling to provide answers to our class practice problem sets. Additionally, her methods of explaining math problems caused further confusion. Subsection can be very fruitful if your leader is effective. Suri was not and her class became a waste of time.

This was my first class in the Jewish studies department, so I didn't know what to expect but Professor Greenberg was very open/caring to students and passionate about the subject. Hands down, one of the best courses I've taken here at WashU as it really made me think deeper about not only the material we're learning but the world in general.

Made us buy HIS OWN textbook to do homeworks. Just reads off of slides, not very clear. Literally tells us what is going to be on the exam and has open book exams. Very easy class, don't feel like I learned anything hence my low rating.

Dynamics and Vibrations itself had a lot of hard concepts but swami gave a lot of examples and practice exams to help prepare. He also gave hints on what would be on the exam so it made it easier to study for. During grading, he was very generous with partial credit so showing work was very important. Overall great class, would take again.

Dr. Evans is relatively young, which is nice because she understands what the college student experience is and is good with technology. Her expectations on papers are super clear-cut and she tries to make philosophy as little intimidating as possible. If you want an introduction to philosophy, Phil 120F is a great place to start.

A great course which is accessible for students no only in the STEM field. She makes sure the course is truly an intro course and allows students to choose where they want to focus their learning. There isn't too much work (only an essay and presentation and quizzes after each class). Definitely worth taking. Lots of guest lecturers and field trips

Really sweet and understanding. She knows that design is subjective and would give criticism as suggestions and knowing her word wasn't the end all be all. Sometimes would suggest a revision and then next critique she would say she didn't like it but she's one of the better arch professors and at least doesn't make people cry during critique.

I had Professor Burton for two semesters and he's great! Although we had a lot of VHL homework and quizzes, the class was fun because of his engaging lectures. He always understood when we struggled a bit and encouraged us to participate even if we weren't confident.
RUN AWAY! Her lecture is illogical and boring. She is not nice and not supportive.

Nice guy, seems like he wants to be helpful. But he's so terrible with his organizational skills that he is not able to effectively teach. You read so much and talk about maybe 20% of it. He's constantly changing due dates and expectations. Not cool.
Great class, learned a lot.

Dr. A is funny, caring, and great at what she does. Even the music she picks out reflects her passion for social justice for all people. There were also great opportunities to see famous groups like The King's Singers, and fun things to do like caroling.
Good energy and sense of humor. Great at explaining things.

I liked how the class focused on three different units to give us the best understanding of the rise of populism in Europe. The economics got a little complicated at times, but Weldzius explained it well. Very lecture-heavy. The class only had a midterm, final and a paper. The tests were fair. The essay a little complicated, but graded easily

Professor Kita is hands down the most caring and kind professor I've ever had. She's genuine, her lectures and class discussions are interesting and edifying, she takes feedback to heart, and the homework was basically just reading + a short reflection paragraph. If you have a chance to, take a class with her. I'm certain you won't regret it.

Class is Pass/Fail Just show up and do the labs. For the most part Qato understands the difficulty in becoming proficient at MATLAB with only a 1 credit course. Grading is easy and determined on minimal understanding and overall effort. If all labs are done, it's nearly impossible to fail the course; If you miss 2 labs tho, high test scores needed.

As far as the math dept. at WashU goes, Escobar is a solid choice. Her lectures are clear, notes are well organized (she likes to color code), she often stops and takes questions, and she's available outside of class. Yea Calc 3 is always gonna be a hard class and you're gonna have to put in work, but Escobar does her best to simplify the concepts.

Probably the best teacher I've ever had in college. She is so kind, caring, thoughtful, and creative. A dance teacher AND neuroscientist, she is insanely intelligent but manages to explain it to me who had no experience in scientific fields. As a dance teacher she pushes you to grow in a nurturing, expansive way. Take any class with her if you can!

It is a difficult class, but Dr. Kelton tries his best to help his students understand the material. Try your best to preview the material and read the notes he sends out, as that will make the lectures easier to follow.

This was truly a train wreck of a class - very few people did well and it was all the fault of the professor. The material was not difficult in the slightest, but the professor and her assistant made the exams almost completely impossible. I have never been more frustrated with a class in my life. Do not take anything with this joke of a professor.

Dr. Lahiri's lectures were very hard to follow. He frequently made major mistakes in example problems and would have to redo them. He only covered half of the material in the syllabus partially due to the resulting slow pace of lectures. He did not give clear answers in office hours either. I highly recommend AI and peer mentor hours and the book.
The class is composed of discussions from readings and watching films. The readings are sometimes dense but most of the films are super interesting. All in all, a solid class.

Unorganized. Did not know when anything was due and what was due. Did not know what I was graded for. It has become so bad that whatever promise she states, I do not trust. Easy class though, it's Ecology.
He almost reads off the slides with a heavy accent. Not a very intelligent guy but is unusually condescending.

Very unenjoyable class. Mandatory attendence, with two group meetings required for most weeks. Other lectures feature slides that he didn't make and he isn't very engaging in the classroom. Grading procedures were extremely unclear for the case write-ups, which were a major part of the class. It seemed impossible to get high scores on them.

Banerjee knows and cares a lot about the field of nanofabrication, and it shows through his helpfulness and dedication to each and every student in the class. Banerjee pushes you to go beyond your comfort zone to make a truly original research plan for the final project and to develop your skills as a scientist.

Chris is a funny guy and a great teacher.  His comments on papers are helpful and very clear.  The only thing is that he tends to cancel or push back meetings a lot, and can be kind of unreliable.  He also really likes to talk, which prevents group discussion.  Generally though, he's a good guy and really fun to listen to.
Lectures really fast. Need to read books after class to comprehend the material taught in class. I have never thought that math could be so hard before I take this class. lol
Super nice and extremely lenient. Classes were mostly discussion-based. Very enthusiastic about course content.

Canna was very sweet & chill. Show up to class and listen to the slides/maybe take some notes, you will be just fine. Grades based on tests but they were basically the slides & the grading was extremely easy and curved--MC + a couple free response. For MC, if you erased the correct answer, you still got full points! She took feedback seriously!

Took History during Alexander the Great. Very kind professor. Very passionate about the topic. The map quizzes are really difficult. A LOT of difficult reading because of the translations and the not super exciting content. Take if you're a history buff. If looking for an engineering elective (as I chose to), pick an easier class.

Prof. Schult's lectures are incredibly interesting and a must at WashU - you'll gain a thorough overview of modern Europe and surprising insights from her unique demographic perspective. The readings are all excerpts from primary sources, which she reviews thoroughly in class, making the material engaging and the class overall very manageable.

Quantum Nanostructures IS NOT EASY. The topics are super difficult and require a good understanding of quantum physics. Homeworks were ridiculously hard, and the exams impossible. That being said, amazing lecturer, very engaging. Going to office hours saved my life. Very accessible via email. Can't miss class or else you're behind. 100% take class!

The GOAT!!
I took 111A w/ Loomis 2 years ago, and I am still realizing how much I learned from him and that class. He just knows how to teach and connect with students.
sir you saved my grade,love ur classes, still hot in 2024

You do not realize how good Loombach is until you take other courses. The team is incredible and passionate. They just know how to teach. They have remained my biggest mentors at WU. I will miss them.

I have now had Prof. Loomis for 3 classes. It does not matter what he teaches, he is the GOAT. Prof. Loomis strives to make sure every single student in the class walks out understanding the material. He is hard, but we students end up loving the material.
He inspired my whole approach to learning in undergrad... This person is one of the best professors you'll ever get to know in your career.

Great instructor, great mentor, and great human. 111 prepared me for my academic career at Wash U, without Loomis and 111, I am sure I would not have made it through. He has given me so much invaluable career advice since my first year, and he always pitched it straight and respectful.

Loomis just knows how to teach. I did not know what to expect with a grad course, but I learned so much. He brought together many aspects from Gen Chem, Inorg, and Phys Chem and tied them together to show us properties of semiconductor nanomaterials. I am so glad I opted to take this course as an elective.

I took Loomis 3 yrs ago as a freshman. I liked him then, but I am realizing now that he is the best at WU. Yes, he prepared us well for science, but he also taught us how to think, how to ask questions, how to understand problems, and how to study. I hope to be a smaller, female version of him down the road.

I once approached Dr. Loomis at his office to inquire about a question relating to semantics: "If hydrolysis is the breaking of a bond, shouldn't analysis be the breakage of the anus?" Let's just say, he answered via nucleophilic backside attack. WOULD DEFINITELY RETAKE!! SO KNOWLEDGEABLE! BEST PROFESSOR AT WASHU. HE IS A MUST-TAKE!!!!

Loomis has genuinely been one of the most devoted and caring professors I've ever known. He would often stay at his student help sessions until midnight to ensure that each student gets the most complete understanding of the material. His lectures are never boring as he is a hilarious person whose passion for chemistry is infectious. He's the best!

111 is a very very challenging course, but the fact that I received basically the first B of my life and still love the man says a lot. He was incredible in so many ways and truly cared not only about the subject but about the students. In a lecture hall of 250 kids, he knew my name and many other students' names. He is amazing!
Funniest and most caring professor. He pushes each student to do better in an encouraging way and does his best to set you up to succeed.
The LEGEND. He will challenge you and help you. There is no better teacher.

Context: I took a lot of hs Chem, and was going to skip 111/112. 
Loomis and 111 are incredible. I had so many ahah moments where my hs material became clear. It was a bunch of work, but so rewarding. Loomis did not just teach to me though. He worked hard to help all of us. Incredible semester.

Professor Loomis is one of the best professors I have ever had. Chem 111 is really tough, but he is very passionate about the subject and makes chemistry super fun to learn. Highly recommend this class if you want to learn a lot with incredible professors.
Just theee best. be ready to work, but he will help you.

Loomis + Daschbach = Best class ever!! They will push you, but you will learn. D is great w/ notes and details. L is great w/ explaining concepts and real life connections. If they ever teach together again, you must take this class.

Definitely the best professor in WashU I've seen so far. Passionate. Funny. Cares about the students. I can't believe he is leaving but I can only wish him the best. He's an amazing professor and you should always take his class no matter what it is, he will make it worthwhile!

Just an incredible teacher and an awesome course. Loomis changed my mindset and expectations for myself, WU, and learning. I almost placed out, but my RA convinced me to take 111 - thrilled I listened. So much more of Chemistry makes sense now.

I am an engineering major. I had Mike tutor me in Calculus 1,2,3, differential equations, engineering mathematics and engineering statistics. His degree was in nuclear physics, after graduation he went to business. I am writing this in response to the people who tell you he doesn't know math. Let me tell you without a doubt, Mike knows Math!
great
You don't need to go to class, but exams are based on lectures not textbook. Very good w/ concepts, a little difficult to understand the details.
If you're any sort of a math person, he's unbearable because he butchers the theory. Tests can be tricky. Don't go to class, just learn the formulas. Don't buy the textbook, you don't really need it.

Don't take him if you like math. He rarely goes into any underlying mathematical principles ("I go to my math dept friend and he told me to go to lunch & come back. Half an hr later he gave me this equation"). He makes you think he is bored in class or something (like "so you put sigma of x into this junk"). THE WORST EVER.
Amazing professor - best I've ever had.  Go to office hours and ask to see magic, literally.

one of the best teachers at the B-school; lectures full with real-life examples; extremely coherent and detailed; if you want more than B, you have to go to class and to the Fri sessions with the TAs!!!   tests are hard, so it is not enough to understand the material, YOU MUST SOLVE ADDITIONAL PROBLEMS FROM THE BOOK ON YOUR OWN!!!

This is a monopoly! Gordinier is the only professor who teaches both business statistics I and II.  It's not fair that b-school students must take 2 classes with  him which frankly are boring and unbearable.  He doesn't give us the change to use excel in class and instead we are forced to take notes on it which is impossible!
Gordiner is a thug, he is great, a top rating!

Great teacher, uses lots of examples. Test questions come mostly from homeworks and practice exams (numbers usually get changed, but same question). If you go to class and take notes, you'll do very well

very systematic-goes over a topic in class, gives an example or two, and the exact same type of problem shows up on the hw/exam. don't need to go to class but learning from the book would be wasting your time with TONS of non-exam/hw stuff. no partial credit on exams-why he has a hard rep. tests are EXTREMELY PREDICTABLE and everything is curved.

Great at teaching, but gives really hard exams. I enjoy his lecture and learn a lot from them, but have to work hard and study a lot to do decently. He gives lots of examples, and is overall pretty fair. You must go to class to do well.

Personal Finance:
 Very good lecturer, entertaining (funny, cynical but good natured), knowledgeable...one of the best I've had. Very useful material (esp. for the future). Difficult exams but if you study hard and take his advice, you will do well. DO THE PRACTICE PROBLEMS AND EXAMS!
 Overall, excellent professor.

Took him for personal finance. Clear lectures, hard exams. You need the textbook to be fully prepared for exams. His political "agenda" reflects a cynical disillusionment with government; what he really pushes is using the life insurance agency for all your investing needs, while ignoring the overpromising and underdelivering of such activity.

Prof. Gordinier is one of the smartest person I have ever meet. he is very hardworking as well considering the amount of courses that he teaches every semester. His tests can be very difficult but as long as you keep on top of the maerial and homeworks you should be fine. Exams on QBA 1 seems to be a lot harder than QBA 2 though.

I think the class is not terribly difficult. Gordinier is a pretty good teacher and he makes things pretty clear, homeworks are not too difficult if you take your time and check your work a few times. Exams are pretty tough but are made a bit easier by the provision of a formula sheet. Pretty good class overall, required but still worth taking.

He's terribly boring and seems to think that what he is teaching is far more important than anything else we have or will ever learn.  His general attitude is off-putting and the majority of the class swings a B without attending.    You don't have a choice but to take with him, so just don't go and get a B.  If you want an A... good luck.

Had him for QBA I/II and he is probably my favorite professor at Wash U.  He is very clear in his explanations.  Class is not that hard if you just come to class and study (the tests are very hard for QBA I, but they are on a curve).  Love his how he is not PC and libertarian.  Very funny.

Listen, the guys pompous. He's a brilliant guy and a professor at a university. He's going to have an ego. his tests are incredibly hard, but definitely fair. You see problems like them before (if you bother to do the practice midterm/final). Some of the questions on the final were word for word from the practice. Go to class, you'll do fine.

Personal Finance.  He is a really funny guy.  He throws in some very quoteworthy things into almost every lecture.  I learned a lot in this class.  Unfortunately, his tests were very tricky.  There is a lot on them that is only in the book or not even there at all!  My grade does not reflect how much I actually learned.  Take it, but pass/fail.
This guys a certified chiller, my dawg fr
easy teacher cse 107 is free

This class is unfailable. He teaches reasonable well, and sparks interesting conversations about data science and CS at large. Clearly very passionate about his subject especially when a guest is there.

Cytron is one of the best professors in WashU's CS department. He gives major dad vibes and is a very nice and chill person. His lectures can get confusing sometimes but attendance isn't mandatory and he offers a lot of extra credit to make up for that. His slides are also very comprehensive. This class is easy A if you do the work.
Ver fun and engaging prof (and nice lectures). Class itself was pass/fail and attendance base, but no homework

I also took him for 131. I'm not sure where people got a sense of rudeness from him. He offered 101 as an easy, extra in-person seminar option when most classes were online (COVID). He offers incredible perspective and intelligence. He will hold a discussion about anything you want to talk about. Also has the typical wit/humor of a CS guy

Good at explaining concepts and answering questions, but he was pretty rude and condescending. Although the workload is heavy and constant, 131 isn't too difficult to do well in as long as you pay attention and there are so many chances for extra credit so take them. I ended up TAing for this class in the spring after taking it in the fall.

Cytron was good at answering questions and giving feedback, but he does say things that can come off as rude. He teaches Java concepts really well and offers chances for extra credit (going to come in handy if you bomb a test). I think taking his class does pay off and if you're interested in comp sci you should give it a try!
Good teacher but not a nice guy
Gives good feedback and does help during sessions but is kinda rude.
-great class but professor was extremely condescending towards students -would not take cytron again but should definitely take cs
He is really mean...

A lot of what's needed to be said has been said. He's a good lecturer, will answer questions promptly, and responds to advice (people this semester complained the lecture speed was too fast, so he slowed it down).

Flipped classroom. At first the videos were very helpful/informative. By lesson 7 everything went downhill, the videos stopped teaching the material well & by week 9 all hope was lost for learning. THERE WAS SO MUCH HOMEWORK. Towards the end it was completely overwhelmng. Learned a lot of java. Need good time mgt. Exams were fine. Easy to get an A

I really regret taking this class, I thought Cytron was good before taking this class because he was pretty good for 131 and 247. But he just doesn't know how to teach this class, and very messy syllabus, the average for the final was around 60, but he does not do anything about it...
Mundane lectures and does not stick to the syllabus at all. Accessible to meet outside of class but looks down on you if you don't know the material. Not good at answering questions.

Professor Cytron was one of the rudest professors I have ever met. He was incredibly disrespectful to both students and other faculty members. I witnessed him many times yell at students during office hours for not understanding the materials and I have also seen him be rude to his fellow faculty. No adult should behave the way he does.

The reason why he has gotten such good reviews is because people know him as a celebrity from the videos or have had very, surface-level conversations with him!Ron Cytron is maybe the most horrible human being I know; frequently yells at people for not understanding, talks down to students and adults, and makes life hell for people he dislikes! NO

He is really good at explaining CS concepts to beginners. He made somewhat complicated things simple and he is so far the best CS professor I have met in WashU(I have not taken many CS classes). Midterms are fair and doable, the course itself as an introduction to CS is an easy GPA booster as long as you complete all labs and studio on time.

Professor Cytron is great! He's very nice and you can tell he wants you to learn. Had him for both 131 and 247. 131 has his online lectures, along with weekly labs and studio with classmates. 247 was hard - labs and exams were very difficult. But Cytron as a professor is awesome.

I'm glad to see recent reviews about Pegg are positive and I hope he has turned over a new leaf. But back in 2012 this man was a complete SOB. I withdrew from a senior thesis with him due to beratements and belittling. Rather than give feedback on how to improve he thought insults and putdowns were the way to motivate people. I hope he has changed

I don't think the Lecture really helps. And the reading requirement is quite demanding.
His assistant is actually more important than him for teaching and grading. Because it seems that all gradings of paper and exams come from his assistant.
By the way he says it's mandatory to take lectures but it actually doesn't matter if you skip one or two.
Fantastic professor. Lots of reading, two papers that he gives good feedback on. Love him.

Mark is such a great dude, literally one of the most passionate professors I have ever had. There is a lot of reading, but you don't really need to do all of it. The two essays were pretty long, but he gives great feedback on them and does not grade them hard. His lectures were very interesting and informative, with his clear passion for the topic.

Professor Pegg is an incredibly intellegent and passionate professor who cares deeply for his classes and wants everyone to succeed. That being said, get ready to read until your eyes turn red and show up to class, because whatever he says in lecture and in reading assignments

Excellent professor who will work to build a living picture of the time period he's teaching. The reading is difficult for sure, but very much worth it. If you put in the time and interest you can get an A reliably, since he will meet with you and discuss your draft in depth before submission. The Australian touch and suits are on point.
Pegg is quite possibly my favorite professor at WashU. Have never had a professor take such an interest in my work.

Never have had a professor more passionate about his topic than Pegg. He not only is hysterical (sit up front or you will miss his jokes), but is incredibly smart. Get ready to read, because you will have two long papers assigned to you on a topic of your choosing that stems from the readings. I would take this class again if I could.

Super hot, smart, and laid-back. He is so charismatic and attractive that  you feel sorry not to work hard. Two 10+ page papers during the semester analyzing a second-hand historical account. More manageable than exams in my opinion, since you are allowed to have your original and biased opinions in your paper as long as you can elaborate them.

Assigns a motherlode of reading that noone can finish. Showed up to nearly all lectures late, some very late. Can put people off with bristling, abrasive threats re: attendance policy, etc. Lectures are very interesting if you dig history though; filled with info you would see in textbook.

Fantastic.  He will help you become a better writer.  Meets with you on your papers and tells you what to fix or change.  If you make the changes it will get you at least an A-.  If you are lazy he isn't the guy for you.  The only problem is you can't count on him to make every meeting outside of class.  Tough to get a hold of sometimes.

He is brillant.  Bit rambling, but fantastic in a small setting. He actually cares about his students, which is rare. If you are willing to read, take his class. Easy A. If you are lazy, don't bother.

This man was a horrible teacher. The only things I learned were from my T.A. who could have taught the class better. He was incredibly arrogant, rude, and offensive. He also taught the class completely based around the history of religion, not like your standard introhist class. I wouldn't recommend taking this class unless it's required.
Knows his stuff; excellent lecturer. Unfortunately, is an arrogant **** with a god complex.
No Comments
No Comments

He has this super inflated ego-- he has written a book or two and thinks he's the king of the world. He makes a horrible teacher. Rambles on and on. The reading material was unrealistic. I agree with the previous comment-- go back to Australia, and never return.

This class should have been called histoy of religion because that's all he talked about.  His weekly readings are way too long and boring for anyone to actually do.  He thinks he is the most knowledgable person ever and wears his suits a size too small.  He should go back to Australia and never return!
awful, just awful. i have no idea what actually happened in this class. i've taken grad-level classes with less reading. pompous @sshole. clearly brilliant man, TERRIBLE teacher.

Awesome professor.  Challening at times, goes off on tangents, but very interesting.  Offers new perspectives on topics, makes you think in a new way.  Readings were a bit long at times, but lectures incredibely interesting.  If you're at all interesting in history, take his class when he comes back in 2 years.  Oh, and he's really hot, too.

He is a good lecturer who knows the material well and is likable. He often dives in deeper than necessary into proving equations the class doesn't actually test us on. Overall, the main thing is that he makes the exams extremely difficult with a heavy time crunch, so I'd definitely suggest doing a lot of practice before exams.

Professor Bleeke is the most down-to-earth professor I've had this freshman year. He makes Chemistry enjoyable to learn and his enthusiasm for science is contagious. Whenever we encounter new learning, my friends and I enjoy referencing Professor Bleeke's "equation of the day" catchphrase.

He seems like a very passionate guy who cares about his students, but his lectures are so boring and monotone. I fell asleep almost every day in class. However, his lectures are easy to follow and he is accessible to students outside of class. He does a good job of communicating all the necessary material when able to engage students.
I like Bleeke as a person; he is very nice and caring. However, his lectures do get confusing oftentimes.

Prof Bleeke is awesome! He's very nice and approachable. He makes the material easy to understand, and the exams are not too challenging, although you need to really understand the material. Absolutely recommend to any chem majors who need an elective.

Bleeke is a pretty chill professor and teaches inorganic chemistry at a good pace. My tips for the class is rewatch the lectures if needed and do the homework and ask questions, and you'll be fine for the class. Averages were pretty high for this course. Bleeke understands what is out of reach for the course and makes very fair exams.

Dr. Bleeke is a specialist in inorganic chemistry. He's the best fit to teach this inorganic chemistry class. The class was well-organized given that it's Dr. Bleeke's first teaching this class, which is amazing. As long as you are keeping up with the reading assignments, attending all lectures, and clarifying all problem sets, you will be fine.
The greatest teacher I've ever had.

Bleeke's "equation of the day" was always awesome and a great way of getting everyone excited. Lectures could feel monotonous sometimes, but all the information is really important for tests and quizzes. Bleeke genuinely cares about the material and you can tell he finds it interesting and is trying to make it fun. Highly recommend!

Equations of the day were always the highlights of my day. Lecture could sometimes become a bit dull (lots of scribbling numbers and equations) but for the most part, he seemed like he genuinely cared for his students and wanted us to learn and enjoy learning the material. He put a lot of effort into polleverywhere questions for us to practice.
Nice person, very knowledgeable, but be ready to completely fall asleep by how painfully monotone and boring he is as a lecturer
Nicest professor at WashU

We love Bleeke!!!!!!! He is genuinely a good lecturer and it doesn't hurt that he is one of the sweetest people ever. Consistent with every other professor in the GenChem department, a great professor.
I didn't love chemistry, but I loved him. Funny, engaging, clearly passionate about what he does. A bit dry every so often, but overall, pretty much all you could ask for in a professor.

Professor Bleeke was a great teacher! For me, his explanations of the material are very clear. The material, compared to General Chemistry 111 may be easier if you have previously taken AP Chemistry. Even if you haven't, the class supports students in every way possible (POGIL, PLTL, etc).

Bleeke is the real gem of the Chemistry department. Take 112A with him, you won't regret it. Sure the class can be boring at times and it's not always the most interesting material, but with equation of the day and his usual sense of humor, Bleeke got me through it!! 10/10 recommend.

Chem 112 is basically AP Chem with a little more depth in all of the topics. You can easily tell that Bleeke loves chemistry and teaching, although he can be a little bland at times. His equation of the day is always amusing. You definitely have to study for exams, but there is A LOT of practice material posted on blackboard/backfiles.

Bleeke is a great professor! His lectures are very straightforward, and he keeps the class engaged with his sense of humor and his equation of the day. As long as you put in the work, doing well in the class is achievable.
Professor Bleeke does a great job teaching some tough material, though he can be very bland at times. Equation of the day was by far the best part of his class.  Overall the exams were fair.
Bleeke is great. Every lecture was very clear and well-organized. Kept students engaged with his famous equation of the day.

I took both Calc I and II with Friewald and the grading was super favorable - I'm not great at math, but got A's both semesters with a moderate of work (nothing compared to Gen Chem!). I agree that lectures could get boring, but cut him some slack- what do you expect from math? He's obviously passionate about calc and is eager to help his students.

I had Prof. Freiwald for both Calc 131 and 132.  His lectures were boring and it often seemed like he was just talking to himself.  The webwork was 10x more difficult than what was taught in class.  The exams were tough, but he allowed notecards.  He dropped a fair amount of quizzes.  Go to class for clicker points.
Simply put, he cannot teach math to others. Going to lecture actually makes things more confusing. Would not recommend to my worst enemy

His exams were very T/F heavy and he weighted the final very heavily. Also, his exams were super theoretical and exams were tricky. Not clear during lecture and unable to explain things in multiple ways.

One of the worst professors from of all WashU. I have had my fair shares of professor taught courses but Freiwald tops the bunch as the worst. Can't teach, tough exams. You'll need not only the book but Advil to not suffer a stroke as result of this class. Not to mention, who teaches integrals a week before the final and then comprises 50% final...
:( a combination of torture and deathly boredom.

I cannot speak for other math courses Freiwald has taught as I have only taken Calc I with him. That said, he is by far the worst math teacher I have ever had--and I have had some really bad ones. The only reason I knew what I was doing was because of high school math knowledge. His lectures are so confusing to follow and he is WAY TOO THEORETICAL!
Had to watch youtube videos which gave me my grade. Worst professor till date. All you suck ups are ranking him well.

He is one of the worst professors I've had at WashU. WAYYY too theoretical. He was so interested in every little detail that he missed the main point. The exams are true false trick questions. Take this class with anyone else.  Several of homework each week, not counting the suggested problems.

As someone who considers themselves pretty good at math, this was a hard class. Lectures are somewhat all over the place, exams are T/F and multiple choice on theory rather than application. HW and webwork are easy, but exams are 70% of the grade. He does write nice notes for every lecture, but I wouldn't take Matrix with him unless you have to.
One of the worst classes I've taken. Lectures are not helpful. Read the textbook. Webwork and homeworks are easy, and then he wrecks you with multiple choice and true false tests. Really poorly done.
Too theoretical. Interested in every minute detail but never helps you get the overarching principals. One of the worst at this school.
He is extremely helpful. In fact, he is the best Math professor  I met in Wash U!
Best prof in Washu. take it if you can learn 309 with him. Most interesting prof i have ever met. Exams are easy and he is a generous grader. Always helpful at office hours.
Best math teacher/ teacher I have ever had! His class taught me so much not only about topology but the mathematical/ scientific way of thinking.

Professor Freiwald is to this date the best professor I have had in the Math department and possibly all of Wash U. He is extremely helpful and his lectures are interesting. I would definitely take Math 309 with him.
Great class. Great professor. He really cares about his students and helps you if necessary. Although attendance is mediocre, definitely go to class, since he explains things extremely well in class.
I took Calc 1 in high school, and I still couldn't understand anything he said. He didn't explain anything clearly; I went entirely off what I learned in high school.

Make sure to keep up with suggested readings and practice problems, or you may get lost in lectures (especially if you haven't taken calc before.)  It's fairly easy if you take the initiative to utilize help resources and ask questions outside of class. DON'T FALL BEHIND.

Wow, this guy is incredibly organized. He follows the syllabus perfectly and his lectures are fluid and informative. The material isn't easy, but he clarifies the difficult topics well. Never mention formulas like the chain rule until you actually get to them in the lecture...he'll know that you've taken calculus already (which everyone has done)

Took it for an easy A, was very humbled. However the issue is despite Parks being a nice and engaging Professor, he makes roughly 85% of his class grade on 3-open note exams that test on small  details. Furthermore some of the multiple choice answers are subjective, and can be interpretive (especially bc the quiz q's are from student-submissions)

Parks has a lot of passion for the subject but he doesn't do well teaching it to students. The class is all lecture which are rambles of seemingly random topics. Open book exams but they're still difficult to do well on because he will test you on tiny random details or something he mentioned weeks ago for 3 minutes.

I loved animal behavior with Dr. Parks! You must go to class because he tests you on things he says in lectures (and his lectures are random tangents), but it is easy to pay attention because he tells lots of funny and interesting stories and makes the topics interesting. Open-note exams so just take notes on things he says and you will do well!

Rambles instead of lectures. If you take Environmental issues 110, he will stand up in front of the class and show you videos, talk about random things, then test you on small, unimportant details instead of themes. Does not respond to emails AT ALL. It seems easy but if you just need the requirement take something else to save ur GPA and sanity.

John Parks is a nice man and passionate about his subject. That said, all the other comments are true his tests are almost impossible to study for since there are not many key points. The questions of the test are of random bits of lecture or from videos we watch in class. Open note or not, the tests are very challenging and they the entire grade.

This class was a joke. Parks is obviously tenured and knows this is the easiest NSM. The class has no structure, the syllabus didn't even have a schedule, and his lectures were just random tangents. He's a really nice guy though and wants everyone to succeed, so there is a ton of extra credit. Easy A if you even sort of pay attention in class.
If youre taking this class for your major, take something else lol this class is so poorly organized I wish I didnt take it

This was my second class with Parks and I know that he gets a bad rap. He's disorganized and don't even try to reach out to him outside of class. But the man really cares about the environment. He's a really great guy. This class specifically was really easy, a sophomore seminar with no tests, just discussions but I learned alot. Highly recommend.
