i took schvey's freshman seminar and he was great at leading discussions and is extremely knowledgeable and a great mentor. he connects with his students very well and is passionate about teaching and learning from his students as well.
Seems like a nice guy, but things are not always what they seem. . .
One of the nicest men I have ever met.  So helpful and knowledgeable and kind and everything good.  Can't wait to work with him in the years to come.

Henry was so supportive and knowledgable about the subject matter.  He took specific notice of each student's talent and supported all our ventures in and out of the classroom.  I actually enjoyed reading and writing papers for this class!
Henry's a great guy. Very smart and helpful, and almost everyone I know loves the guy.

At first, I thought Dr. Schvey could have given us a little more direction or structure, but in the end I was able to make my own conclusions. Dr. Schvey is great!  He is very helpful, compassionate, and knowledgable!

Henry obviously knows his stuff, but is interested in the students discovering things on their own. The class was easy and you can even get by fine without reading all the plays, although I would at least be familiar with them. He cares more about his students than any other professor I've had
This man is fabulous in every sense - He is kind, caring, a fabulous teacher! I cannot say enough good things about this man except if you haven't had him, you're definitely missing something.
He's a great guy.  So caring and helpful.  Though the class dragged a little at times, I learned a lot and loved getting the chance to know Dr. Schvey.
he was very nice but the discussions were not all interesting.  he is not such a bad grader.  however, the final is a little hard because it includes everything!
Dr. Schvey is such a great man, and brings intelligence to all class discussions!

He has no control of the class speed (three weeks on only one slide). The whole class basically based on answering students' questions. He tries hard to explain each one, but the more he says the more confusing it gets. The homework is not too much. Haven't take any test yet, but for sure not good at explaining stuff.

Professor Antinolfi is very accessible outside class and really cares about the students. Homework and tests are very similar and they are pretty manageable. Tests should be easy if you go over the past exams and understand the homework. Lectures are insightful and makes me more interested in this subject.

Antinolfi is a solid choice because he is super nice, always willing to answer your questions, and getting an A in the class is not hard. However, he is a boring lecturer. He goes into good detail explaining everything, but it was hard for me to pay attention because he did nothing to spice up lengthy lecturing.

Antinolfi is a very caring professor but the content is very dense. Make sure you have a good understanding of things going into the exam. Grading criteria is unclear but somehow I and many others got As. He seems to care more about you showing that are trying rather than getting everything perfect.

The pandemic complicated how lectures were taught this year, so Professor Antinolfi didn't assign any exams. The lectures were extremely dry, so much so that it was impossible to focus, and the textbook didn't outline the course material well. He is clearly a very nice guy and grades leniently, but I had to learn the course material through YouTube

Antinolfi is a great guy and a decent lecturer. He knows the material he has to cover is dry, and he does his best to make it engaging with decent results. He wants you to get a good grade, and most people do, although his tests can range in difficulty. He's genuinely funny, too, and is more than willing to answer any question you ask, so ask away.

Antinolfi is a mystery.  His grading scale is unknown and his tests range from impossible to easy, yet most of the class magically receives As.  He's a very boring lecturer, answering questions with convoluted responses, yet is a super friendly guy who seems to care about his students.  Take this class for the A, not to learn Macroeconomics...

Insanely nice professor that genuinely cares about his students. Lectures are really boring though and the tests can be pretty difficult. He's decent at explaining concepts and uses real-world analogies to do so. Homework is optional and he only posts it as a study aid. Very helpful outside of class too. Take 4021 with Antinolfi.

Antinolfi really cares about his students. The Economist articles he gave us every week really made the class feel relevant. If there is anything you do not understand, go to his office.! Office hours are usually empty --even before tests-- so don't miss out on the opportunity because he can explain things very clearly if you ask! Great prof!

My favorite Economics professor at Wash U.  Antinolfi gives clear lectures (nice Italian accent), and will help you understand more about economics in terms of ice cream and coconuts than you would believe. Understand the homework and you'll do well on exams. Took 402 and then 437 with him because of his great teaching style.

Prof. Antinolfi is awesome. He raised my interest in actually understanding the materials and he made lectures fun. I learned so much from his clear explanations and examples. Take his class or you will regret.
He is a great teacher, tells stories in class from his great deal of experience, and he is always willing to help you understand the material.

Gaetano is the man! ICE CREAMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMM! Seriously though, Antinolfi is probably the nicest teacher I've had at Wash U and he truly cares about his students. He is an extremely funny guy who tells jokes during lecture to lighten the mood. Take 402 and 435 with him if possible.

Antinolfi is a really nice guy with true concern for his students.  He is really intelligent and focuses real life implications for economics instead of only focusing on theory and math. As far as econ profs go, he's about as personable as they come.
His class was set up in a straightforward way.  Lectures were a bit dry, but alright.  Nice guy, helpful if you ask.  He has an understated sense of humor but if you pay attention hes really funny.
He is incredibly knowledgeable about the material, and he does a good job of answering questions/making his point clear when students are confused.
He is extraordinarily helpful and very enthusiastic over the material.

I.... could...not...bear...being...in..this..class.  The elipses are to show you how he talks, at 2 miles an hour with breaks in between.  SOOOO boring, half the class stops coming after the first month.  Is required course for Econ, but need a teacher to spice it up a little
great teacher. cares a lot about students. fun class, funny man.
Italian accent a bit difficult to understand. Extremely helpful, very concerned for students, dry sense of humor, straight forward and smart
he is very organized in class, and I really like his teaching style. He also carefully asks if everyone understands when explaining concepts. totally amazing professor.

He is funny in an awkward way, tries to crack jokes throughout lectures. Attending the lecture and doing the weekly homework really helps with understanding the theoretical concepts and prepare for the exam. He gives enough materials and support to succeed, and exams are curved.

Pros: Very straightforward with concepts, tells you all the formulas/methods you need to know. Curves so 50% of the class gets A- or above.

Cons: Super boring, monotone. Doesn't explain concepts well or at all. The second test out of 3 was very unfair/difficult given what we learned. Tests are worth 70% of grade. Homework is pretty difficult.
Straightforward professor who gives you all the info you need to know, but requires strong grasp of key concepts in order to succeed
Kinda awkward but in an extremely sweet way. Cares about students and the workload/exams are fair. Not an easy A tho; you'll definitely have to work for it.

Weinstein is a good teacher and uses real world examples to get his point across. I can tell he had a few difficulties showing us his mathematical steps over zoom, but overall I never felt he failed to teach us the material. He is also a nice person and happy to answer questions. If you know the homework, you will do alright on tests.

Class content is challenging but not impossible to follow, the problem is (maybe now since all classes are online) none of what is presented in pre-recorded lectures ever matches up the content of homework. Despite him always saying to reach out for help or questions and seeming like a genuinely nice guy, I never got any emails back when I wrote.
Definitely an awkward guy, but he gives clear and straightforward lectures and tries to keep things humorous.

He is a great professor. His explanation of the materials are thorough and dense. He makes sure that everyone understands the course material by going through homework problems. Exams and the amount of homework are fair. If you go to lectures and do the homework problems, getting an A is super easy. Amazing professor.

Weinstein gives very boring lectures. You could just read his PowerPoint slides. His TA's grade all of his exams, and he does not care if they take off many points for little errors. If you mess up anything on an exam, expect a lot of points off.

he is a fantastic human being, very funny and passionate about Econ. he makes dad jokes. I would recommend his class, he makes it easy enough to succeed (even though I did not do well, which was my fault!!!!) -- just do the homework, pay attention in lecture and you can get an A

Content can be a little theoretical while in class, but the homework always clears it up. Weinstein also does a great job going over homework afterward in class and being available during office hours to help you through it. Wouldn't say too much of it, 1 hour a week. Exams are very fair and often match the practice exams- definitely do them!

The class material is dense. There are weekly assignment which can take many hours. The lectures are well taught and the tests are fair. He is a fun person and very accessible outside of classroom. If you study hard you can pretty much get an A, but definitely not an easy class.
LOTS OF HOMEWORK

Professor Weinstein is a hidden gem of the econ department. The class I had with him was well taught and fairly graded. I learned a lot of material without ever feeling too stressed. Attending class does help your grade, but I actually really enjoyed attending class, he makes econ fun and exciting.

Great Prof. if you get a chance to take a class with him. Grading is fair, and expectations are clear. Lectures are great. Just be sure to do the practice exams: they're practically identical to the real ones. Overall, great class, couldn't ask for much more.

Jonathan Weinstein is not only a great professor:  He's a great man.  But in all seriousness he's the sweetest, nerdiest guy who wants his students to succeed.  Exams are all easy; mostly identical to the homework and past exams.  Definitely take a class with him.

Class I was the most likely to fall asleep in. Very boring and only gets a little bit better when you get to game theory. Tests are pretty straightforward if you take the time to learn the concepts though. Extremely nice teacher.

Keeps class as engaging as possible. Like a lot of Econ lectures, the material can be a bit dull, but he makes up for it. Homework is often challenging, but he makes up for it by counting it as a large chunk of the grade. Exams are reasonable, with the occasional curve ball thrown in. A bit awkward in one-on-one interactions, but a very nice guy.
Clear lecturer. Makes topics interesting. Slides are very useful. Old exams and problem sets are very useful. Review sessions before exams are also very useful.

Genuinely is one of the gems of the WashU languages department. Even for just an intro level Italian class, she puts a lot of care into the lectures and is extremely interactive with the class. Not just talking at us, but engaging with us. As for homework, VHL is as helpful as you make it; reading the textbook coincides with doing the homework.
Professor Messbarger is the BEST! She is so kind and accommodating to her students. Her lectures and assigned readings are very engaging. Minimal assignments, but participation is important.

Messbarger is very passionate about historical analysis, art history, & Italian culture, which composes much of Art of Med. She's very email-responsive & caring outside of class too. However, the Art of Med experience can become stressful if you get a biased TA. If interested in medical humanities, you should definitely get to know Messbarger!

Prof. Messbarger is a great professor. She really cares about her students and is always willing to give extensions/work with you outside of class, understands freshman year is hard, etc. Always answers emails. Her grading criteria and assignment directions can be unclear, so you'll need to ask for clarifications or settle with a decent grade.

Prof messbarger is a stereotypical humanities teacher. Says nothing but says a lot. Lots of weird hand gestures. Art of medicine class was really an art history class. Talks a lot about Italy for no reason. Easy a though if you really want to suffer through losing lectures that have no point to them.

I took a class with her years ago and decided to take another class with her. She is incredibly powerful and knowledgable. She really strives to make you a better writer. On a first paper, I did not do well and she really wanted to help me improve. You must work hard to earn a grade so that you leave the class with lifelong skills. Love her!

Italian use is very frequent in class so the lectures are sometimes confusing. The lecture slides are not aesthetically appealing, but do contain enough information for review. Memorization-heavy. Also, the lectures are sometimes arbitrary on historical facts and analysis, and do not invite much argument from students.

I took The Art of Medicine with Professor Messbarger, and it is easily my favorite class for far. professor Messbarger is extremely passionate, and can make almost everything interesting.  She is an amazing person outside of class, and very easy to talk to. Get to know her!

The Art of Medicine is one of the best classes I have taken at WashU. Professor Messbarger is so passionate about teaching this class and she takes an interest in each individual student. The class brings a new perspective to the medical field that was so interesting to explore. 100% recommend.

Dr. Messbarger is absolutely one of the best professors I have had at WashU. Her Italian Women Writer's class was amazing. I was extremely hesitant to take it, but she was inspiring and knowledgable. The readings, though sometimes boring, came to life in our discussions.

I'm in a freshman seminar about Italian museums, and you can certainly tell this is her area of expertise. She keeps lectures relatively interesting though the readings are terrible. I have to agree that she's very conceited. She had us read her own book, and I don't think I've ever seen someone so excited to talk about themselves.

I ADORED Professor Messbarger. Passionate, funny, down to earth, caring, and generally awesome. I could come to class after a bad day and leave class in a great mood. Her energy is infectious!! She made the course so interesting and the final project was actually fun. You must take a class with her if you get the chance. It'll change your life.

Had Messbarger waaaay back in '97.  I already loved the Romance Lanaguages and she helped me get my start in Italian.  I'm now married to an Italian and spend at least a month per year there.  I'm also fluent. Thanks for the great foundation Dr. M!!
Passionate about Italian and her students.  Also a cool fashionista.

an excellent professor in all categories.  everyone please ignore comments about her being arrogant.  rather i would say she has an air of authority about her, but she is definitely very accommodating to other people's interpretations and points of view.

Mrs. Messbarger was one of the best professors I've ever had.  She was very easy-going but she expected a lot from her students.  Her classes were always fun and the discussions were lively.  I wish I had taken more classes with her.

I found this professor to be extremely conceited and I am not the only one. Of course, the fact that she studies the anatomy of the **** as her research doesn't help much (No I am no kidding or insulting, that's what she does, people told me).
 I wouldn't reccommend taking her class. Better to spend  summer in Italy.
I liked Prof. Messbarger.  She was very enthusiastic and she spoke very clearly.
Boring and conceited. Many people think she's arrogant and I agree
N/A
She's amazing at helping you learn from your mistakes. Although the quizzes are quite challenging, I think she is an amazing professor who involves everyone in her lecture.
Marcus-sensei holds her students to a high standard; however, her feedback is excellent and gives one an idea of what needs to improve. The Wash U Japanese Program is excellent.
She demands a lot to students and is really harsh on the grades. I am just holding on to this class for my culture credits and I would never want to face her again after this class.
She seems intimidating af at first, but she's actually the bomb dot com???? I'm love her?????
an awful experience.

Provided handout/online schedule with what she would expect from her student each day. As long as you prepare what you are asked for she will give solid A (its not alot either). Some people can find her attitude assertive or demanding respect, but that is more like her trying to recreate what japanese class would be like, not being strict or mean

While this style of language learning does take a week or two to get used to, it is engaging and very useful when you become more comfortable with it. Marcus has an intimidating presence at times, but she does take into account effort, not just results, sotell her if you are struggling and ask for advice. The homework load is not bad at all either.

I'm going to have to agree with the previous reviewMarcus-sensei can come across as strict and scary, but really, she's a great person! Her grades are fair; you don't have to ask and answer questions promptly and perfectly to get a five for the daily ACT classes, as long as you show that you know what you're doing.

I'll admit initially that she comes off as an intimidating person, but she's an intelligent, at times humorous, teacher. You don't have to do perfectly to get a five in ACT class, just as long as you can correct your mistakes.

I will never take her class again. I guess she thinks she is the most important person in the world so that she deserves GREAT respects from all her students. Never question her. She has a condescending attitude towards everybody in the class and plays favouritism.
A very good course.

She is one of the best teachers I've ever had.  She is very demanding, but if you let her, she can be a great advocate and truly cares about her students.  I don't know where everyone got her rudeness or favorites-playing from, but I wasn't her favorite and still loved the class.  I did well the first year, but did worse in subsequent semesters.
Rude and plays favorites. Makes you feel like an idiot if you get the tiniest thing wrong. The other Japanese teachers are great though
Her gradings are extremely unfair. It is hard to earn good grades in her class. EXCELLENT performance without mistakes usually gives you only a 4.5/5 which is a 90 in the class.
Insecure and patronizing. The worst educational experience of my life and I got an A. Plays favorites

DONT EVER TAKE A CLASS WITH HER. She is the worst teacher I have ever had. She is condescending and plays favorites. She must be insecure about something because she has a god complex. The other Japanese teachers are ok. Knowing what I know now I would never take a class with her again!
This is the worst teachers I have ever had. She was also the most mean spirited people I have know. I did fairly well in her Japanese class. Avoid her like the plague!
Doesn't have the humor of the other Japanese teacher (Aridome), but genuinely trys to help. Occasionally livens up the room by discussing stories of her major Japanese errors
very demanding and will jump to assumptions. Takes multiple times to state your questions since she jumps to what she assums your question is. she wont help you succeed if she doesnt like you
biased and snappy
Dr. Faust is awesome. My class is really unconventional. No tests. One project. Some reading. I love it and would definitely recommend it to anyone interested culture or different thought processes.
Excellent class. Faust does a great job showing how Linguistics is relevant.
Worst class I've ever taken. Absolute waste of time, Faust taught nothing then left all assessments until the end. Nice guy just isn't effective as a teacher at all.

He takes attendance so must go to class (3 absences permitted). Flexible if you talk to him: let half of the class move the final. Easy if you listen in class & learn the vocabulary list. Speak up in class. However, set in his opinions and not open to views that contradict his own. Thinks his word is the law.

I like Faust, he's a great guy and really likes his students.  It may not seem true, but he wants his students to learn.  He doesn't accept opinions easily that aren't consistent with his own.  Take linguistics with Faust for a fun, easier class.  Maybe not the most informing, but it was fun to go to ad there wasn't much work.

Faust's class may have been an easy A, but I learned nothing about linguistics, and did not enjoy hearing him ramble in circles every day. Extremely boring. He preaches open-mindedness but is surprisingly close-minded, and judges your emails closely. He also penalized people for missing class during important holidays.

Very nice guy, absolutely awful professor. There was no structure, no clarity, and no solid foundation for the class. I learned very little, and what I did learn was a constant struggle between Faust and I, and I was never sure if we actually understood each other. I agree with the other person- he is very sensitive and gets offended easily.

He's funny and nice, but I didn't feel that the information presented had enough structure for me to take helpful notes.  I didn't think I learned anything because the class would jump from one thing to the next without Prof. Faust acknowledging the transition.

Faust is an extremely engaging professor who loves Linguistics and encourages you to as well.  His teaching isn't just the traditional sense with tests and quizzes and essays.  Faust motivates you to think and analyze everyday examples in a wonderful way.

Although the professor often rambles on about unrelated (but interesting) topics, he applies concepts from the text in an engaging way through in-class thought experiments.  However, BEWARE: on at least one occasion he adamantly stated (in a showish kind of way!) INACCURATE INFORMATION.  I corrected him later, but he never admitted his error.
I bet you he's going to show this page on the 1st day of class and complaining he's not being hard enough in terms of grading. WATCH OUT!!!
Best linguistics professor ever! He keeps the class very engaged. I got a good grade for a good work.
Great teacher who truly cares about his students. He is brilliant and open minded. This class is a must take if you love  studying cultures. Its not for everyone but if you like it you love it.

The professor does not know how to set clear expectations in the beginning of class and proceeds to lecture on something other than the class topic throughout the semester from lack of setting clear expectations.  Very sensitive about everything, so be careful when you participate in class.
The undisputed man.  You need to take his class.  That is all.
Interesting, good, intro class. REALLY nice guy, pretty easy course. Class is fun and not boring. REALLY well-educated and has a lot of knowledge about a broad range of subjects and languages.
He's great, incredibly knowledgable.  made me love linguistics :)
No Comments
No Comments

Professor Faust (and Dean Laurita) are two of the best teachers at Wash. U.  They are unconventional and make me want to come to class, yet I still learn a lot from them.  They have knowledge on a wide range of stuff and are quite approachable.  If you can take a class with either of them, do so.
Really good prof. loves the material he teaches and constantly engages with the class. One of the best profs. i've ever had.
Stoner really cares about his students, and if you take 141, you'll pass. The lectures are interesting, and he is very passionate about what he does. Convinced me to major in Anthro.
Anthro and PH has been the best class that I've taken at WashU!! Dr. Stoner is a very knowledgeable and engaging professor. 10/10 would recommend taking this class.
He is my favorite professor so far. All around awesome teacher.
Dr. Stoner was my supervisor at Washington University and the best I ever had. The first day I met him I said to myself, "this guy is a teacher." He truly is a gifted teacher and special individual.

He's AMAZING! He wants so badly for all his students to do well and enjoy themselves. He's cares more about his subject and students than I've seen any other professor do. Interesting material taught in a captivating way. Phenomenal teach all 'round.
Really great professor! The class was very relaxed. The exams covered material taught in class, not book material. Very engaging class.

I'm taking anthropology and public health with him and I really like it. Material can be a bit dull but he makes it engaging and interesting! One of the most sincere profs I've has. You can just tell that he lives what he teaches.
very engaging.

very great teacher! i got an a in his class and have learned very much. his last name is phunky too. i recommend this class to anyone and everyone, its amazing x3! he has a barbeque at the end of the year and everyone should go for the food, fun, and not to mention his adorable daughters. laid back, tidy and neat, very good teacher and  class!

Smart, funny, and helpful. He's a wonderful guy who makes class fun. Makes the class worth getting up for, and I recommend it to anyone. Easy to get a good grade if you go to the class and pay attention and very willing to help his students.

Took Med&Soc w Stoner and Lester. Stoner taught well and is very laid back -- how could he not be with a last name like that? The class joked about it and he was cool. Easy A - just take notes from his lectures/reviews, readings are not necessary, and chat with him once in a while. He loves talking about his WHO work and kids. Stoner's a great guy.
He is a funny guy - cares about his students, especially those applying to medical school. He is a bit busy, but still a great advisor and good lecturer.

My favorite class this semester! Medical anthro is a fascinating subject that prompts you to question and evalutate. Fun assignments like controversial issues in health, position paper, and of course, the class trip to the Indian restuarant. Dr. Stoner is a cool, laid-back, hyper-intelligent person who faciliates and encourages class discussion.
Absolutely great class!  Lectures are interesting and informative.  Worth getting up early for.  Tests seem to be focused more on details from readings than conceptual understanding.

everyone should atake this class! i learned so much from this wonderful guy, and his kids are adorable! make sure to remind him about the barbeque at the end of the year. very organized, very smart, VERY good teacher!

I LOVE THIS CLASS. It is the one class at Wash U which is manageable and Dr. Stoner (although he still doesn't know anyone's name in my class) really tries hard to engage the class. I would definitely try to take any class that he teaches. He is one of the few laid-back professors here at Wash U!

Took Medicine and Society and Anthro and Medicine. Stoner takes the most god-awfully written texts on anthropology theory and makes them comprehensible, which is impressive because anthropological theorists are the worst writers in the world. Definitely take his classes, you will learn a lot.

excellent professor. very organized, lectures presented clearly and in interesting manner, very concerned with students, helpful outside of class. recommend taking any class he offers, you will learn new things from an excellent and intelligent professor.  washu needs more professors like him.

I loved this class. He was a really good teacher for both this class and Anthro 260. For everyone who is interested in Medical Anthropology, you should apply for medicine and society. At the end of the year he invites everyone over for a barbecue. He's really smart.. a doctor and a teacher.

Pierce is so great!! His lectures are so interesting, and he is so knowledgable!! It was such a cool experience to learn from someone with so much experience and who is so respected in the field. This course was especially awesome because we had a chance to travel as a class! 10/10 would recommend this course and this professor!

Lamar is an interesting and engaging lecturer. It was great to learn from someone with as much field experience as he does. The issue with him is that he is great at explaining concepts, but when you get practice problems, it seems like he just expects you to figure it out. He is available, but his office hours are packed. Overall AMAZING professor
Lamar is an arrogant professor who doesn't teach much. He just likes to hear himself talk.
Best professor I had freshman year.
GREAT PROFESSOR! I love him

This class is kind of interesting, but it is hard for some freshmen. Make sure to read everything and print the slides before class. (Many freshmen are not prepared.) In general, the outline of this class is not very clear and many freshmen feel uncomforateble to study without a textbook.
Great professor, albeit intimidating.  Is accommodating and helpful outside of class.  Rewards those who speak up and contribute often and intelligently in class.
he makes things clear and most of all he is so SMART in the first place~

Good teacher.  Easy concepts. The class takes some getting used to, but once you figure out what is asked (especially on tests) it is quite easy.  Make sure you participate in class. Study all assignments and practice material.

I personally did not like him that much because I thought he was a bit boring. He goes off on tangents though, and those are interesting. Otherwise, GO TO REVIEW SESSION, REVIEW NOTES/READ EVERYTHING and try hard. It's a difficult class, but not impossible.

It's a pretty tough class. It get's less difficult by the end as you begin to understand what the professor asks for. He's a great guy, and an extremely knowledgeable professor. I definitely recommend him.
very easy class. keeping with the b school tradition of not having any challenging concepts and tests are easy along with huge boosts for participation
This was my favorite class of the semester by far, I had a really great time and I am entirely confident that it was because of this professor.
Good professor. Tests are mostly trick questions and aren't very well written, but he will give points back on regrades.

His class is pretty tough, and requires work.  The tests are challenging.  But he REALLY teaches you the material, and I am pretty convinced he knows everything about everything. You can ask him any business question about any market, and he will not only have an answer, but will have a 15 minute answer.  I really recommend him,this guy is the MAN!

Lamar is the man.  He's very straightforward in his teaching and answers questions well.  He's is extremely funny, but not everyone seems to understand his humor.  His tests are very fair and the class is not especially hard.  You don't really have to do all those readings.
Great prof! Awesome lectures and a very fair grader.
No Comments
awesome prof- tough class but he explains it well and lays it out in a way that is understandable.

Professor Pierce is an incredible teacher! I highly highly suggest taking his class! All his lectures are extremely interesting and he uses class participation very well. But, don't kid yourself, management is a very tough class with tons of reading. The tests are very hard and random. He will raise grades up to a letter based on participation!!!

Madame Allen was an ok professor. She's very sweet and caring, and she's one of the best French professors I've had in the WU French department (although the others suck). She assigns a lot of busy work, she's very unclear about project guidelines, and she's overall very disorganized (especially with Canvas).

Prof. Allen is one of the kindest and caring professors you will have during college. Though I won't be continuing french I enjoyed her class. Coming to class on time is a MUST and there's a lot of homework & some rather tough grading. Nonetheless if you p/f the class or don't care much for your grade you will have a lot of fun and meet new people.

Madame Allen is the sweetest professor you are ever gonna meet. She cares about her students and loves having conversations and helping us understand the language better. Her class can be difficult just because it is very fast, but she tries her best to help anyone falling behind.

The class material is interesting & helpful, but Prof. Allen can get very unorganized which makes things harder. She also can be a bit condescending and rude when grading/giving feedback, which ruins the class experience. The class is definitely worth taking if you want to study French, but go for a different professor if you have the option.

Class overall is not hard but the disorganization made it truly unbearable. Prof Allen frequently posted homework the night before class at 8 pm or later for a class that took place at 10 am the next day and never had a schedule planned.

Professor Allen was sweet and caring, but a bit disorganized during lectures. Most learning came from online assignments, with class time primarily consisting of review and group activities. She spoke in rapid French for most of her instructions/lectures, leading to some misunderstandings. Overall, the class was manageable but not my favorite.
Dr. Allen is great! Her course is a little disorganized but overall interesting. She is a tough grader but will work with you and give good feedback.
I would recommend taking Allen one of the best professors I've had at Washu. She's willing to go above for those who put in effort.

Allen very rarely seemed to know what she was doing. She often posted homework the night before on canvas without notice. Very unclear on expectations, and somewhat condescending. We usually spent the time before she came to class complaining about her.

She was a very difficult professor. Does not explain things, was no help to students. Very disorganized on Blackboard that she didn't even know what was on there. I got an A, but it was with the help of my classmates. We had to teach ourselves throughout the entire course. Do not take her unless you have no other choice. I recommend making friends.

She is literally the most caring and passionate professor I've met so far in WashU. The class is work-heavy but if you really work your ass off, you will get a A. She gives a lot of extra credit work which definitely helped me to get an A. I would love to meet her again in higher level FR classes. She made learning FR interesting and I loved it.

Madame Allen is by far the best professor I've had at WashU. I told her I was *thinking* about being a French minor, and without any hesitation she created a schedule of all the classes I would have to take throughout college in order to achieve that. She's so caring and sweet- I would love to have her again.

Professor Allen was amazing! She is so hilarious, kind, caring, and really a wonderful teacher. She was always available for help outside of class and gives opportunities for extra credit! She has made me love French and has helped me learn so much in just one semester! I highly recommend taking a class with her!
She is nice but acted like it was a 300 level class and graded kind of unfairly, in my opinion.
So nice, I loved her, she got me really interested in french
really nice, def. take a class with her
She keeps the class interesting and has the class's interest at heart.  Tres sympa! Je l'adore!
Great Professor!  Extremely interested in students and material.
So cool.
Very caring about students

Prof. Slaughter is a fantastic college writing teacher. While other students complained about their different CWP class/prof, I never shared that sentiment. I enjoyed the creative control and discussions within class. There's a lot of readings, and I can tell you that the more you read and engage in class, the better/easier your papers will be.

She is super passionate and has lots of energy. She gives good feedback. If you ask questions during office hours if you are struggling, she will be super helpful. Sometimes, the readings can be long and boring, but you should be fine if you skim them, as she leads the discussion. She is nice and makes you feel comfortable!

Although I hated power and commodity culture I'm glad I had it with Dr. Slaughter. I don't know if I would've been able to get through the class if she wasn't the one leading it. She's very understanding and gives back great feedback on assignments.

Great teacher and facilitated discussion well. Class was mainly discussion and workshop based. Slaughter gives extensions for anything (as long as she's told a little bit in advanced) and is very helpful with feedback

Slaughter is great. She is understanding of students' needs and gives great feedback on essays. The readings assigned are really interesting and I have learned how to become a better writer. Surprisingly made college writing my favorite class of freshman year.
A helpful and kind professor. Strongly recommend!

Slaughter is awesome! Gives great feedback on essays and I found the readings to be very interesting. She is such a sweet person and very understanding. Try your best on the essays, participate in class, and go in for help if you need it and getting an A shouldn't be difficult.

Slaughter is a great professor and I enjoyed taking power and commodity with her. The grade is only made up of a few big papers, but you get plenty of time and help with them. Can't stress enough: GO TO OFFICE HOURS! This is how I got the majority of my help with my essays.

She was a good professor. I was lost on the feedback here and there only because I don't feel like one should correct or try to change a opinion. She didn't make assignments complicated. There were multi choice quizzes. She had you engage with classmates on discussions twice a week before taking the quiz, which you only had to respond to one post.
She's fine for the most of the times, but her essay feedback can be subjective and not helpful

I'm not a strong writer and I dreaded taking this class but honestly, Rachel made it as good as it possibly could've been. I even considered taking another writing course next semester if she was teaching one! never thought i'd say that.

Professor Slaughter was a joy to have for my College Writing: Power and Commodity Culture class. She was very understanding of our transition to college and never gave a lot of work. Usually, she assigned readings for every class, along with 3 papers throughout the semester. Fairly easy grader and wanted the best for us. Highly recommend!

Dr. Slaughter was a wonderful writing professor. Her course grade is composed of four papers, and portfolios of preparation. She gives great, extensive feedback on papers, and is available to help you a good amount if you need it. Every student is mandated to take a college writing course, so take it with Slaughter!
Will help you improve your writing.
This is the best English teacher I ever had. I learned so much! She is extremely helpful and well prepared. I definitely recommend taking her class if you have the option.
great writing 1 teacher
super sweet
No Comments
Very nice and friendly. Tries to make class fun. You can tell she's trying very hard. Quotation is a noun. Quote is a verb. She'll make a good professor.
Rachel Slaughter helps your self esteem in a way that only a kindergarten teacher can.  Any effort is rewarded.

Guys, she is the best writing prof you will get at WashU. As an English major, I can tell you it gets worse and significantly harder, so thank the gods if you get Terri. She's perfect. So funny, intellectual, and entertaining. Can't say enough good things about her. Def ask to hang out socially

She places a great deal of emphasis on in-class participation, so be sure to contribute regularly if you want to be on her good side. Although feedback on drafts can come off as harsh, it clearly outlines how she wants you to revise your essay to get an A. Also, prepare for heavy readings until there is a quarter of the semester left. She has cats!

Proff. Taylor is an extremely nice person and the grading scale makes it very easy to get a good grade in the class. If you can just show up, add to the discussion, and turn in things on time, she will reward you with more forgiving grading and a better class grade. DO TAKE.

Cannot express how wonderful this professor is. So funny. I along with other girls from my class still maintain a relationship with her over a year later. You would be so lucky to have prof taylor for college writing!

Terri Taylor is an absolute gem. She cares about her students and picks out exciting content while being hilarious and fun. I continue to recommend her class to every first-year student! The WashU College Writing requirement shouldn't feel like a burden. Thanks to Professor Taylor, it was my favorite class that semester.

I LOVE Professor Taylor. The amount of times I brag that she's my college writing teacher is astronomical. Discussions are lively, engaging, and the readings are all very interesting. Taylor is hilarious and makes class so much fun. 10/10 would recommend. 

P.S ask her about her pet cobra
Terri Taylor is the best person I have ever met. Her hair is so silky. Outfits 10/10. So helpful. The reason I switched my major to English. MUST TAKE.

At first the class can be intimidating, as it's very heavy in readings and the emphasis on participation through discussion boards and during in class lectures. Make sure to meet with her at least once to discuss your paper. If you put in the work you''ll be fine, she loves students that are active in class.

Prof Taylor liked me very much (except for when I would not attend classwhich I would not recommend to do) because I participated so much. The readings were all short (10-25 page) essays, with assignments being discussion boards for every class. We had a total of maybe 3 papers, with a 8-10 page final research paper. Put in effort, and you're good

Easy grader with effort into your essays. She gives great comments on your rough drafts (graded on your peer comments for other students). Essentially, the rough drafts serve as a test run before you get an actual grade. However, she completely break down your rough draft. More readings than other classes (but front semester heavy) hard CW class

Pretty solid college writing professor. Beginning of the semester is super busy with a lot of readings but eases out towards the end. Only three large papers to write and you have a lot of time/help with them. A very fair grader.

If you're taking Commodity Culture, I recommend taking it with someone else. I had friends with other professors that gave much less work who seemed more interesting. Class is discussion-based, but Taylor sucks all the time out for herself. She will completely monopolize the discussion so it's hard to cut in and earn points. Grading not too rough

Professor Taylor's class wasn't particularly interesting, but it also wasn't too difficult for someone with a decent amount of writing experience. This is an adequate option for meeting the college writing requirement, but I do know other students who had a more enjoyable experience with other courses and professors.

Though college writing is often hated by freshmen, this class was great. Terri, though maybe initially intimidating, is quite affable if you show up and do the readings and engage in discussions. Overall a good experience and one of my only As from first semester.

Grading in college writing is extremely subjective and I found that I didn't agree with a lot of her grading/reasoning. She grades very hard but has a weird curve so it's not too difficult to get a decent grade.

College writing at WashU really depends on the teacher. If you get Terri, I would recommend dropping or switching immediately. If you talk a lot in class and love meeting teachers in office hours, Terri might be right for you. Generally ruined my first semester.

Love this woman. If you pay attention and talk, it's an easy A. Honestly my friends and I ended up sharing memes with her after class, she's really laid back and fun. Once you prove that you can do the work, you can take it easy and it's a walk in the park.

Her class is fun if you have done the reading. The discussions would be helpful to collect ideas for your own paper. However, she is very, and I mean, very specific with what she wants. I would recommend meeting up with her for your paper as much as possible if you want to have good grades. However, she is also not on campus three out of five days.

Professor Taylor loves interacting with her students and our classes would get wrapped up in alot of fun conversations. Writing 1 curriculum is standardized by English department and there are lot of papers (usually one a week). This resulted in poor quality of work due to burnout in my case, but that's more a general issue and not Professor Taylor
I think the class wasn't exceedingly difficult, but she is definitely a hard grader. I think the most hard aspect was the papers but she is willing to help.
N/A

Fantastic professor. She explains concepts clearly, notifies about upcoming assignments, and grades fairly. SHe is a very pleasant professor despite the class being a lot of new and challenging material. Prof. Doran makes it seem less stressful.

Prof is friendly and I think she truly cares about the subject but lectures are very dry and confusing. The textbook isn't much help either. Tests were quite hard and often left most of the class feeling pretty upset. Having previous exposure to linguistics would be very helpful.

Coming from a well-prepared Spanish background, the class was pretty much what I expected it to be. It is a very reading-intensive course and although the material wasn't the most interesting to me, Profa. Doran is a very nice person who is a relatively lenient grader. Although she rarely gives 95+ on big assignments, she is not as strict as others

I highly disagree with a lot of the reviews for Prof. Doran. I thought she was lovely. I thought her course was interesting, challenging, and fair. Yes, you had to go to class. Yes, you had to understand the material and demonstrate that. We had several take home exams, ungraded homework assignments, assigned readings, and a few group projects.

Although already graduated, I still remember this class! Professor Doran is very kind but a very tough grader. I was really struggling to get the grade I wanted in this class, and therefore, ended up switching to P/F. If you're still into Spanish though, don't give up. I aced every other Spanish class required for the major besides this one.

Doran is not by any means a bad professor. She is typically congenial and nice, but sometimes I think her grading is a bit harsh. She will give a 90-95% on any assignment that is near perfect, but not provide feedback on why she took off points; this makes getting above the 93% A mark a bit difficult. Participate 3-4 times per class.

Although Professor Doran is very kind and clearly cares about her students, her class is ridiculously difficult and the tests/projects take an ungodly amount of time and effort to complete.  The material is very confusing and tested very in-depth on.  She is extremely nice, but I would never recommend taking this difficult of a Spanish class.

Professor Doran was my elementary spanish professor, and coming into this class, I thought I wouldn't have to know much spansih, but she does not warn you of this. She lectures on all of the easy parts of grammar but when the quizzes come she will test you on material she has never gone over. For an elementary class this was very difficult.

Prof. Doran is a really sweet person who truly cares about her students. She never makes anyone feel bad about misspeaking or anything like that, and I always felt happy logging onto our Zoom calls. She also did a great job of keeping our Canvas page organized and used technology well - a trait that I find is hard to come by in many Spanish profs.
She truly cares for her students above everything else and promotes individual improvement. I wasn't sure about taking 308 and I'm so glad I did with Doran!

Doran is a kind person, and she makes at effort to get to know students.  The class is pretty straight forward, and she has a lot of resources to help you understand the material and write the essays. Pleasant class.
She's not a great lecturer and doesn't explain the material well.  She is also a relatively tough grader. AVOID.

doesn't speak loud, slow or clear enough. If you want to have a solid foundation in Spanish don't take her. She tends to be used to teaching upper level classes and expects you to know the alphabet and pronunciations by yourself. Hard-grader, pretty dogmatic about the trivia.

She used to teach upper level classes so if you are a total beginner and want to have a solid foundation in Spanish don't take her. She expects you to know the alphabet, the pronunciations( including those exceptions) by yourself. Quite dogmatic, deducts essay points for margin requirement; asks you to leave the room if you have soup before class.
Profa. Doran is one of the best Spanish teachers I've ever had. She is extremely friendly and always willing to help. She is an easy grader and if you participate in class she will love you!

She is clear, helpful, and pretty easy. he first essay was graded harshly, but everyone after that was graded much easier. She is just not that engaging. Class moves slowly, but the material is interesting and she does her best.

Even though the topics in lecture are very dense and usually abstract, Hayward will come up with a simple everyday example of it in lecture to make it easier to understand which is helpful. Readings are hard, but you honestly don't even need to do them if you go to lecture. Going to lecture is way more important for the tests.

I would not recommend taking this as an intro course. The class is graded largely on three tests over the course of the semester. The grading scale is awful especially for an intro course. She is not very receptive to students asking for help. The readings are long and don't actually prepare you for the test. Choose another intro class.

The tests are a lot of memorization, which is nice when you have good TAs like we did. TAs and Hayward hold a lot of office hours, so use them if you're struggling! I got more out of the TA labs than the lecture ngl, but that's because I don't like how she sets up her slides. Def still go to lecture though. If you study, you'll be totally fine.

I really did not like Prof Hayward, she doesn't understand that this is an intro class, she was expecting too much from students. Grading scale was also ridiculous considering the difficulty of the class. The readings are ridiculously long and complex, and she brags about the difficulty of her class. If you want an intro class, stay away from this.

Hayward is an incredible lecturer. The reading can often become annoying and a little too long, but if you get the basics, and the lectures will be on the test. The tests are mainly memorization of the readings. She is a really nice person and obviously cares about the topics.

Although attendance is not mandatory GO TO LECTURE.  The readings are incredible dense (although interesting) but the lectures are directly representative of the content on the exams.  I also recommend going to subsection.  Hayward is a great professor and as long as you put in effort, the class is doable and rewarding.

Great professor! Go to lectures, everything will make so much more sense. There are reading quizzes before each lecture, but they are super helpful when studying for the exams. Other than that, you are graded on 3 midterms (mcq/short response: 1-2 sentences) and recitation participation.

Hayward is an excellent professor. While the assigned readings are very long (and can probably be skimmed), doing them will lead to a greater understanding of tricky concepts and thus greater success on the exams. Her lectures are very to-the-point, and she has an entertaining way of delivering them. Attending lecture is a must. Great class.

Hayward is a great lecturer. She makes all the lectures engaging and humorous. The grading of the class is extremely unforgiving for an intro class. Homework is half credit for being a few hours late and no credit for being after class. Exams are too short and one mistake is 5% of your grade. They're phrased for you to pick the only wrong answer.
Exams are tough but amazing lecturer. Super interesting topics.

Definitely would recommend her Political Theory class. Easy A if you attend lectures and take notes. A lot of reading, but she sums them all up very well in class. Plus the subject material is interesting if interested in political science.
FANTASTIC. Clear lecturer who makes complex political theory more understandable. Definitely take her into to political theory class!

Took Power, Justice, and the City with Hayward - not all the material was interesting but there were some lectures/readings that were actually really eye opening. Would recommend if you're looking for a fairly interesting course with a cool field trip at the end

Her class is not an easy A by a long shot. However, she is a very good lecturer and attending class works wonders for understanding the material she discusses (which is very dense). There is a lot of reading in the course too, but as long as you get a general idea from it and pay attention during lecture, you should be fine.

Distills and explains readings so clearly that actually reading the material becomes unnecessary.  Generally well prepared for class; unhelpful lectures here and there but overall good preparation for exams and essays.

Is a fairly clear lecturer. Is accessible outside of class to discuss material with. Seems nice enough. Lectures and her examples do a fair job of summarizing and clarifying readings, but can ultimately be too vague; if you want the most accurate understanding of readings to do best on tests, do the readings, etc.
Great Prof, her class is really tough. Great class to take if you like theory, stay away if you're just fulfilling requirements. She's nice, helpful, and pretty damn cool.

Power, Justice, and the City.  It was writing intensive and since this professor is new to WU, her idea of writing intensive was spending just as much time rewriting each of the 5 papers, and all of them were worth the same! Very long, boring reading. Half the class pretended to understand.  Check out ratings from Ohio State University.
Such a kind and understanding Professor! Professor Lawton made class enjoyable. He was super flexible with due dates and very accommodating to student needs.

i adore the way that professor lawton teaches and i learned so much! rly lovely classroom environment/sense of intellectual community, even in zoom lectures. this online semester has been really difficult, personally as well as academically, but prof lawton's class is still sth i can look forward to and engage in despite that. 11/10

Prof. Lawton is an amazing and engaging lecturer. He is so knowledgeable about the Bible and taught me so much. He supplements his classes/discussions with really interesting anecdotes from his life. He's a fair grader, very accessible outside of class, and is very lenient about extensions on papers. He genuinely cares for all of his students.

Professor Lawton is one of a kind.  He is smart, funny, and genuinely cares about his students.  If you put effort in (speaking up in class, seeing him outside of class) you will get a B+ to an A.  If you have an opportunity, take a class with him.  No matter what the subject is he will make it fun.

Professor Lawton is by far my favorite professor. He's extremely intelligent, down-to-earth and funny. He genuinely cares about the progress of his students and is always available to answer questions and offer assistance. He's most definitely the one professor that I will never forget!
Lawton class was unlike any other. It was as spiritual as experience as it was intellectual. An artist with academic titles. Be prepared to me charmed and changed. Long life to David Lawton.

Great professor, he cares so much about what he teaches and is an incredibly smart and interesting person. I had little to no interest in the subject of the class before taking it and I still really liked it.  He's also not too hard and really wants you to do well - every student gets one free extension, no questions asked. Take his classes!
Brilliant professor! Thorough knowledge of subject matter and his accent makes lectures all the more interesting. I learned so much from him!

Amazing prof.  He presents topics in such a great way- I learned so much!  Willing to work with you on any topic, and he's brilliant.  Able to speak on any topic with considerable authority.  Definitely take his class!

So knowledgeable about the Bible and teaches in a completely unbiased, literary way. Also always willing to meet and help with assignments. Take his class if you're at all interested in learning about the Bible as literature.

Professor Lawton is awesome.  Chaucer is his specialty and that shows in his lively and entertaining lectures and discussions.  He is very laid back and completely open to crazy ideas - he also has an amazing sense of humor.  Take any class you can with him.

Professor Lawton's class on pain is extremely interesting, though not what it initially appeared on the syllabus.  Lawton teaches in a very conversational style that allows each student to express whatever ideas he or she has.  Your grade is based on one final project that consists mainly  of creative work.  Not very difficult, and Lawton is great!
The Smartest Prof Ever!!! I loved the class and wish I could take more.  If you take him, you won't regret it.
He is great - sweet, interesting, genuinely cares about his students emotional and academic well-being. The English accent doesn't hurt either
Entertaining and kind.  Seminars flew by.  Understands that people have a lot to do and sets realistic goals and expectations.  Willing to provide tons of input and advice for papers.  AWESOME class.
Great guy, very funny, amazing teacher

His accent is reason enough to take a course. Melodic voice that will, at times, sing one to sleep. Delightful sense of humor. Unfathomably experienced. Enjoys relating (often humorous) personal anecdotes. All-around likeable.

Sweet man with a Cheshire accent, very approachable for concerns about class, life, whatever. One of the most brilliant men I've ever met, great sense of humor, more than willing to listen to ideas and encourages independence in areas of interest, challenging course work but worth it.

I absolutely loved professor Wick! Super clear and well-paced lectures and he is so accessible for office hours. Willing to answer as many questions as you have and makes an effort to make lectures more engaging so you feel compelled to participate. No complaints at all.

He was a nice lecturer and pretty clear. He would make mistakes sometimes since he's going so fast, but they'd be pretty quickly fixed. Would recommend his lectures if you plan on taking any calc courses.

What I can respect is that any question you ask Wick will answer it no matter what (even if it may seem redundant). He won't make you feel stupid when learning and I appreciate that a lot. I would argue he is the best lecturer out of the three (Thorton and Nguyen). Exams are whatever just study for them.
Better lecturer than Thornton- would strongly recommend going to his lectures even if you register for a section with a different professor.
Favorite calculus professor. Taught by example, which happens to be my learning style. Lectures really helped my understanding of material but some find the lectures unnecessary.
Prof Wick is a reasonable good professor to have for Calc 3. He explains the concepts pretty clearly, and sometimes adds some dry humor. Not much to hate.
Wick is great at teaching Calc 3 and explains process better than Thornton
Professor Wick is very responsible for teaching lectures as he would meticulously explain every step of a problem. He is very chill to talk to. I would recommend take his CALC III

Not very accessible outside of class, but that is okay because there are videos online. I would suggest joining PLTL if you are struggling with course concepts. Class is not mandatory, but being there in person is better because you get to be engaged and listen to other students ask occasional questions.

Calc 2 is difficult for most, but I found Wick's lectures to be pretty dull and monotonous. I sat within the first few rows every day and could barely read the tiny writing on the board. Eventually gave up and switched to going to/watching Schaefer's lectures, which worked MUCH better for me personally. Not a bad guy at all, just not for everyone

Personally, I thought Wick was a fine teacher. Many others did not agree. On the first day, the class was full, but at the end, only about 30 kids max would go. Everyone started going to Schaefer's lectures instead. Wick frequently made mistakes and seemed very rusty on trig at the beginning of the year (hello??) but not terrible.

Very straightforward and clear during lectures. He only teaches what you need, and that's all. It is easy to take notes and follow his examples. Exams are easy as long as you understand the key concepts from the lectures.

Professor Wick is one of the nicest and most caring professors I have met! He accommodates conflicts in students' schedules so if you need an extension he will give it. He never tricks us on exams and exam questions are all covered and emphasized in class, so if you attend lectures and take notes (his notes are very well organized) it's an easy A.

Professor Wick is one of the most patient and friendly people I have ever met. Matrix Algebra is hard for freshmen to take, especially when taking it concurrently with Cal III. But he makes the contents easier to understand. Definitely enthusiastic and an expert on what he is teaching. The test is not hard as long as you pay attention in class.

It's a tough subject, and Prof Wick is exactly the kind of teacher you want for it- he's incredibly prepared, precise, and thorough. Very very helpful in office hours- no question is too dumb. Homeworks are big and take a very long time, and there are many exams, but the problems on them are very fair. Got a great grasp of the material from him.

He did a very good job explaining the concepts with examples. He has a hight expectation, but he won't trick us on the exams: everything is from lecture or homework, and most of the final is from previous exams. However, he is not accommodating individuals and we had both a midterm and a cumulative final at the end of semester, which is a lot.

Professor Wick is really kind and nice. Though the course material is kind of difficult, Professor Wick explained everything carefully. And he is always welcomed for every students' questions at any time. He replied to questions really fast and clearly. I am personally really grateful for the chance he could teach the probability course.

Professor Wick is the man. First professor I had in class at Washu, and he was engaging from the first day of class until the end despite the fact that the subject could get dry at times. 10/10 would recommend.

Absolutely AMAZING professor. Super knowledgable about WGSS and grades super easy on papers. Definitely pretty unorganized and takes forever to grade, but seriously such a kind and amazing human being. Would 100% recommend into to WGSS with her!

I'm kinda shocked to see that people gave Brumbaugh below a 4. I took ENG 310 and 4502 with her, and I loved both. Lectures aren't super useful, but I strongly believe that to be a product of the material, rather than Brumbaugh's ability as a teacher. She's knowledgable, articulate, and the nicest professor I've had at WUSTL. Highly recommend.

Professor Brumbaugh is by far the best professor I have had at Wash U--she is brilliant, kind, personal, and overall a fantastic person. Her Intro to WGGS class is absolutely phenomenal, and she pushes students to think outside the box while remaining comfortable and confident in their ideas.

Nice professor. Class is very useless and takes too much time. At least two assignments per week, half of which she won't even grade. They are very confusing too. There is something different every week. Some people might find that fun but I didn't like it. Don't take this class if you have a busy semester. Your other classes deserves more time.

I didn't mind her for Tech Writing.  I enjoyed going to class because she's very nice and has a good personality, but the class itself is TOTALLY USELESS.  I learned absolutely nothing.  Doesn't explain assignments too well.  Also, she taught so many classes this semester that she clearly didn't have time to thoroughly read anything we turned in.
I enjoyed having Brumbaugh for tech writing. She was fun and pretty laid back.  Sometimes she would see the class was stressed and cancel class, etc.  Pretty easy grader too.

She was the worst professor I have ever had.  She is very inconsistent and does not explain assignments well.  I did not learn anything useful from this class.  If you can take Tech Writing with any other professor you should.  Then you might actually learn something.

This class is super easy, all you have to do is show up and she'll at least give you a B+.  She even told the class that she has never given anyone below a B+....after the first couple of weeks it is really had to get to class since it is so boring and completely useless!
Awesome teacher.  Very understanding and she gives assignments that let students be creative.  I loved her class.
No Comments

you guys are all on crazy pills.  lynnea is an awefully tough and picky grader and the class itself is more or less worthless without the virtue of being a blowoff.  i suffered all semester doing pointless projects while sitting through her haphazard lectures, and i urge you to avoid the same fate
she rocks!
GREAT class. I loved the material and the way she taught was fantastic. I learned more in that class than in any other class I have taken.  Extremely helpful material!
Brilliant teacher and her class is really great but the assignments are essentially worthless. The material is wonderful and the class is fun.
There is a lot of work, especially as you get to the end of the semester, but Lynnea is incredibly flexible and very effective in her teaching.
Incredible teacher, very helpful and knowledgeable, makes things interesting and fun, goes above and beyond
Lynnea is hands down the best teacher at Wash U. Her classes are time consuming, but very rewarding and very useful as well.  Possibly one of my favorite professors of all time

Agrawal runs 347 like she's going through the motions. "Lectures" are not lectures. You watch years-old lecture videos, some made by other teachers, and then watch examples on the board during the lecture slot. The syllabus has no grade scale, and she avoided all questions about what it is. Would never take this class if it wasn't required. 0/5

Professor Agrawal makes lectures interactive and clearly walks though multiple examples for every topic. The course schedule with videos, lectures, and recitation is tough, but it helped me learn the material well for exams.

Not really a fan of Agrawal. Her lectures were all over the place, with convoluted explanations of content and poorly organized structure. Her grading criteria was often unclear, and I never really felt comfortable approaching her for help outside the class. Her only saving grace is that her exams weren't terribly difficult.

Easily the most heartless prof Ive ever had. Lectures were so confusing, and she makes notoriously hard exams. Not to mention, so many people were confused that they had to resort to using extra materials or working with each other and instead of passing people (in a pandemic???), she reported them all for academic integrity, so theyre facing Fs.

She is one of the best cs prof I've ever met. She explains the concepts clearly and very responsive to students. She doesn't rly reply to emails but very helpful in piazza and question sessions. Also, overall class structure was fair. Homeworks and exams were doable if u put some effort, and grading system was also fair. Really enjoyed this class.
Professor Agrawal is such an amazing instructor. She gives very clear lectures and very doable homework & exams. Really enjoyed taking her class!
Just reading MIT papers and so on. She cannot remember whole concepts that she uses in class. I did all my homework and paper reviews as best as I could but she graded me B.
She explains things well and is responsive to questions in class.
She's very good about answering questions in lecture and explains algorithms quite clearly.

This professor cannot clearly explain the knowledge. She used many MIT course materials, even for the homework. And she seldom replied students' email. I highly recommend MIT online courses instead of her course.
Completely unavailable to students. She should forward her paycheck to MIT because their videos were the only way I learned the material.

She basically follows the MIT open course for her lecture, and most of her homework. The lab instructions are not clear, which makes students hard to test the correctness of their code. The average test scores is about 50%.  Just avoid her section if you are not really necessary to take her class.
Terrible professor. Incoherent accent, primarily talks to the board. Spends too much time on complex proofs. Seems nice enough as a person, but she has moments where you question that as well
Loved her! great professor, requires you to focus a lot but its so worth it!

I think 241 is one of the hardest classes you can take at WashU. It's really interesting material, just hard. She does a pretty good job explaining it and she covers everything, but it's heavy stuff and you have to work hard in the class. I tried taking 241 with Bueller and she was far better.

I took her class two years ago and quickly dropped it. She has improved a lot. Her lectures now make way more sense and she is better at explaining material. The class is really hard but incredibly curved. I would avoid the class unless you have to take it, but it is less painful than I feared.

Unhelpful, class average was in the 40s, didn't seem to care whether anyone was failing or not, took weeks to respond to emails.  She only seems to be here because she does "exciting" research, as she really can't teach.

Great professor, and really enjoyable course -- especially if you enjoy public speaking. Lots of hands on work in class, but pretty minimal amount of work outside of class (except for writing 3 major speeches throughout the semester). Bill creates a really fun learning environment.

One of the best classes I've taken at WashU. Bill is an amazing professor who makes each class fun to attend. He expects a lot from his students and you need to do work outside of class, but he's a reasonable grader. Definitely take Bill's classes if possible!
great class for those that already have some public speaking experience because he fine tunes your ability, but as an introductory class he expects a lot.

Simply put, Bill's great. He has a way of guiding you and helping you think about your work in many different ways without micromanaging it. He's a big fan of the actor's impulse and puts a lot of trust in his students to work together in order to produce the best work possible. He asks a lot, but if you're willing to do the work, you'll be fine.
Bill is my favorite. Seriously, he's the man.

Bill is a great director, but not a particularly great directing professor - he has a hard time codifiying what he wants from you and how he's responding to your piece, his comments are often along the lines of "I don't know what it is about that music that is making me go 'hmmmm,' you know?"  He grades easily and does not read your dir. notebook.
Wonderful caring man. I looked forward to his class.
Absolutely fantastic.  Working with him is the highlight of my day.
No Comments

The warm ups and exercizes were fun, but it's not the safest environment for growing as an actor.  His comments on scenework were often vague, along the lines of "it's not working for me."  Good if you're a spontaneous actor.  Was always very available to help outside of class.

He's good at explaining things and obviously really cares about what he's teaching. He cares so much, apparently, that he screamed at and berated students in front of the class more than once for not coming on time or not having memorized. Take the class, but watch yourself.
Great guy, lots of fun
No Comments
Bill is fabulous.  His does not sugar coat but is never too harsh.  He is fun and interesting, and a pure joy to be around.
No Comments
Very good at what he does.  His energy is unbelievable, especially for an 8:30 class.  If you get the chance, take a class with him.
No Comments
nice guy. expert. easy going but has appropriate expectations.

He's obviously a very smart guy, and offers a lot of humorous/interesting anecdotes from his life. The biggest problem is more with the class itself and the material. In short, he's a good professor (though a pretty hard grader especially on papers) but the class itself could be improved.

Crisp is not a bad teacher at all. He tries to keep things interesting, tries to tell jokes.  The problem is that the curriculum for Comparative Politics is terrible, as is the textbook.  When dealing with such vague and useless material, no one can teach well.  He's really not "mean" nor "boring".

Although it is clear that he himself knows the material very well, is probably a good researcher and seems like a nice enough guy, he was never meant to be a teacher. His biggest flaw is that what he lectures is a different topic to what is on the powerpoint (which is also overly convoluted), so it's impossible to write anything down. Confusing!

Honestly, he wasn't the greatest teacher ever, but if you do the readings ( approx 20-30 pages due per class) and study the slides after class, it's a very easy A. I missed a lot of classes, but since most classes were focused on the reading, I didn't have too much trouble.

Do not take a class with Crisp.  The workload in comparative politics was more work than I have done in any poly sci class and I am a senior, poly sci major.  Not only is it work intensive, but the work is not interesting.  Heavy reading too.  Lectures are boring and discussion sections pointless.  Crisp is really really really bad.
Very interesting if you're a polisci major. Lets lectures be dominated by student questions. Though he comes across as a huge jerk, he is very approachable outside of class.
No Comments

Basically the biggest jerk alive.  He wouldn't give me an extension on a Monday test after I had just gotten mono that weekend even with the shs head physicians note.  Besides being an ass his lectures were boring as well that the course materials.  Dont take this class

This class was terrible.  He assigns way too much reading and one of the course books was not available thru the bookstore until half way thru the semester.  He always made a power point presentation but never put it up long enough for people to actually write down what it said.  Very bad professor.

Very heavy reading for an intro class. Crisp is interesting enough and intelligent. But he isn't the best teacher in the world. If you're just taking a political science class for fun, avoid this one. If you're majoring in it, then go for it. The content is useful for future courses. Plus Crisp has a good Polsci mind and is respected in the field.
awful

Doesn't teach very well, and gives very vague explanations.  Is practically worthless if you meet with him for help.  Creates tons of slides, skips through most of them, and keeps the worthless ones up for too long.

Worst Wash U class I have taken so far. Course description very misleading. This class is all about rational choice b.s. A lot of work for an intro class. Crisp obviously had no interest in teaching and had TA's do everything. Only cares about his research. Thank god the TA's graded.
No Comments
No Comments

Oren is an amazing and very engaging lecturer. Participation matters in this class and the people who regularly participated tended to do better. At times, Oren can be inaccessible outside of class, however he genuinely seems like a caring, very smart guy.

Oren is a great and engaging lecturer and is respected within class. While he joked around, his condescending and "knows better" attitude made class tense. The content was fine, but because scarce practice (esp. math based questions) and abstract exams, learning was difficult. Worse, the grade scale and cumulative grades were never given.

Oren has good lectures where he explains the material thoroughly and gives good examples. Tests were hard because the material was presented in a different way than it was taught in class, so students can work toward developing "intuition", but there's always a curve.

His lectures are super engaging and fun! However, he is kind of condescending (a lot of people describe him that way) when he calls on people in class and gives you basically no validation at all when you answer questions. The tests are kind of hard, but the curve is good and if you do well on the case competition your grade will be fine.

Oren is very smart, and very good at lecturing, but condescending. If you're not a favorite, it's much harder to succeed in the class. Tests are designed for failure and he acts like they are very easy, which can be frustrating. While presenting for case comp, he was laughing at us, not paying attention, and asking questions we'd already answered.

Oren was okay. He came off as condescending, and I didn't find him to be a compelling professor. The class is not fun: The content isn't complex, but the tests are seemingly crafted to discourage students. That said, be prepared to encounter oddly-worded questions. Worth noting: I was "on track to receive an A-" all semester but received a B.

Oren was great. MGT 100 puts a lot of content and a case comp all into 3 exams and a presentation.
