He made class as fun as it reasonably could be, and pretty much made sure everyone participated. doing so was a bit scary as you'd get called out, but it forced me to actually prep a bit for class. First exam is HARD, after that it isn't too much work
Super engaging and fun class. Tough tests but the curve helps out with that

Reshef is good at lecturing and responding to student questions. He is not afraid to call a comment stupid and tries to sneak some comedy into class. The class is fairly easy, but the grading system is unclear, which can cause unnecessary stress. The exam questions don't match up with practice questions, but the grading is generous.

MGT 100 was my hardest class this sem, but also my favorite. Reshef is super intimidating at first, but I would recommend going to office hours because you can get to know him and find out that he cares a lot about the subject and the students. Tests are kind of worded poorly but the curve is life saving. Enjoy this class, embrace the challenge!

Although Professor Oren shows much care and passion for his class, he came off as very condescending to me especially if you don't have the right answers. Also, the class is very test heavy so three exams in the class are worth a lot in terms of your grade. Make sure to come prepared and do the readings! Also participate as much as you can.

Oren is a great guy with a great personality. It's clear how intelligent he is and he is a good lecturer. You definitely have to put in the work in this class but it's rewarding. Take a class with him if you get a chance!

MGT 100 is a very mundane and useless class, but Oren makes the best of what he has. He's a hilarious personality, explains concepts very well, and gives very helpful feedback for the case comp. He's a bit intimidating at first, but don't be afraid to ask questions because he's very helpful.

I really liked Oren. He is very intimidating at first, but the class is really not that difficult even though they do try to scare you at first. If you put in effort your guaranteed a B+. Also don't use the online reading. I didn't read once and did fine.
Good lectures - easy to understand the content. The course is difficult but TA and professor help will go a long way. Oren means well and is a good teacher.

Professor Reshef can be a bit rough around the edges but is a really great guy once you get to know him. He's incredibly intelligent and a great lecturer. He genuinely cares about his students and takes students' feedback seriously. Highly recommend taking classes with him.
So easy

He's a great lecturer but somehow manages to be boring.  I can't explain it.  I just brought sudoku to class every day.  It helped me deal with the boredom while still being able to focus on the lecture.

Great professor. Even though I slowly learned over the course of the semester that philosophy isn't a subject I particularly enjoy, the class discussions were always very interesting. The workload for the course was quite manageable (something that any science major will appreciate).
Very enthusiastic about class. Tells great stories and, even if subject isn't interesting, he finds a way to entertain you.

great guy and great lecturer. subject is pretty boring, but the workload is light. my only complaint was that every piece of work we did was read/graded by the TAs and none of it was read by j. claude himself.
One of the best professors I've had at Wash. U. He's a really nice guy, an interesting lecturer, a very fair grader, and makes very complex concepts easy to understand. Go to his office hours!!

a descent professor. Sometimes a bit unclear but the subject he teaches is fascinating. You don't have to be genuinely interested in philosophy to take his class. Very easy grading very lax about attendance and super nice.

Nothing terribly wrong with this professor. A fair grader, sometimes a little bit unclear about his teaching but overall very lucid explanation of difficult concepts. Take his class if you have patience and are seriously interested in philosophy. He is very lax about the paper deadline and also does not care too much about attendence.
Cute, but unprepared and too repetative. He does not seem to have an agenda, and class drags.
Really interested in his subjects, helpful, friendly, but you must like the topic already to be interested in his lectures.
actually going to class was pointless... and the ta's graded everything based on their own opinions. overall, skip this one and take bioeth if you're looking for a good phil class.
Really liked his subject. But, like all philosophy teachers, there were many times that he sounded like a pompous ass.
he made me interested in the environment...i recommend him and this class to all
No Comments
Super guy, great teacher.
A southern gentleman.  Genuinely concerned for students, encourages shy students to become vocal.  Much more of a "guide" through the readings than a lecturer on a topic.

She is one of the nicest professors on campus and truly a cool person. She prefers concise work and graded pretty easily. Her exams were easy I got an A on the midterm from just listening in class - no additional studying. Loved hearing her stories more than the class, she is so fun. There was a relaxed atmosphere in class; I never dreaded going.
Good Professor

Professor Parikh is super nice and an exciting teacher. Sometimes I felt like the lectures were unorganized but the reading material was interesting (sometimes really long though). Attendance is required, but you can finesse that if you have friends in the class to sign you in. The class felt "chaotic" at times, with not a ton of direction.
Parikh was by far my favorite professor at WashU

She's clearly a cool person, but disorganized and never seemed to have a clear plan. Lectures were just her going over the readings in depth which meant I didn't have to read them but also made class boring. It was interesting when she would open for discussion, though.

Prof assumes an unrealistic level of backgroud knowledge in lectures. Lectures sometimes dry but integrate well with the readingstests.  Exams are easy provided you learn all the termsconcepts from readinglecture. A solid but not exceptional polisci course.

As a lecturer, she's mediocre. Most people generally skip except for "project days" which are mandatory, and understandably so. She often rambles on during lectures, but makes up for it in her willingness to help students who ask questions. She's very nice, just not very clear. Tests were a breeze though.
Very good teacher. A bit disorganized but lectures are really informative and interesting.

Amazing teacher, learned an enormous amount from her. Sometimes is a bit disorganized, but the genuine caring and her enormous knowledge of the subject is always obvious. Going to office hours is amazing, she is willing to discuss any and everything about her class candidly, and you can learn SOOO much from her, highly recommended.

Professor Parikh is an engaging and passionate lecturer.  Her examples and her ability to draw students into her lectures make attending class a joy.  She is truly caring and amazingly interesting in office hours as well.  I would suggest attending class, especially because she draws most tests from lectures (which are much clearer than the book).
Most disorganized instructor ever.  Good luck following her lectures.  Can be entertaining though if you're aiming to get personal stories out of her to get off-topic.  Very easy.
She's a really fun lady and is extremely knowledgable about India and Pakistan. The Sri Lanka and Bangladesh portions of the class are pretty weak. She's a good lecturer but gets off track easily.

Very knowledgeable in her area of expertise, but lectures lack organization. She is entertaining at times, but not always the clearest lecturer.  Her exams are relatively easy, but at times ID questions come completely out of left field. If you are fairly knowledgeable about subject material, take her- otherwise, be prepared to do all the readings.

I took comparative politics from her, but it basically became a survey of her research in India compared to the United States political system, which was marginally interesting but not so helpful when it came to the tests on the actually material.

terrible professor, gets off topic ALL the time, goes off on long tangents, repeats herself, i could have slept all semester and not missed anything, i dont understand why people seem to like her so much.  doesnt teach about relevant or educational things
Very good professor herself; unfortunately her illness has forced her to relegate many of her teaching duties to Alfred Darnell (um, yeah) and Jeff Smith (full of it).
She's a very light grader. You'll get an A if you show up, but the content is very challenging. She tries to push you beyond where your German currently is and does a pretty good job at it.

I ADORE carol jenkins with my whole heart. she is definitely one of my favorite professors at washu. the class has a bunch of homework but she is really understanding with extensions and turning in work late. the class is really entertaining and fun and we chat a lot

Professor Jenkins is really fun and really good at teaching german. There was a lot of homework but I think that they are switching up how homework is done in the german department. Overall great experience and would take her courses again

One of the best professors I've had at WashU. I looked forward to her class everyday and loved her enthusiasm and kindness toward students. She genuinely wanted you to learn and improve on your skills. Definitely take her class!!

Prof. Jenkins made German my favorite course taken this semester. She made classes very interesting, encouraged a ton of participation, and has a very fun personality. I really loved learning the content. I wish I could continue taking German with her!

BEST GERMAN PROFESSOR I'VE EVER MET. Personality great and knows everything there is to know about German. All you need to do is review the work that has been covered and you'll do fine. One person in the class never studied and he was the only one that struggled. Participation is main part of the class. I plan on minoring in German with her.
She has a fantastic sense of humor, which makes her classes very entertaining.
2 classes (1998, 2000), both excellent. She's a fantastic teacher, I'm glad she's still at it!
Sooo cool, really nice, great feedback, very positive.  Who knew ecomp would be fun?
Fun class and she is very nice.
SHe is awesome. If you dont have her for Ecomp, switch into her class. She is easily side tracked ;)

For once I had an English teacher who cared about what I wrote and how well I did in her class.  She has a quirky sense of humor that kept me laughing all semster.  She grades well and gives great feedback.  She made EComp fun, period.
She was a wonderful e comp teacher. She took a very boring sylabus and still made the class entertaining. She was very laid back too.
She definitley puts in a lot of effort and makes the class as enjoyable as possible.
I guess she belongs to the German dept but she taught my Ecomp class.  She was very entertaining, and a fair grader.

Ho is very frank and understandibly this can be interpreted as sasiness. He is by far the best bio2960 lecturer. He explains tough concepts extremely well and has a great sense of humor. Ho makes himself very available outside of class, he wants everyone to suceed in the rather difficult course. "Don't give it up, I will not give you up" - Dr. Ho

This class is a nightmare. Ho emphasized a lot of concepts during his help sessions that were nowhere to be seen on his exams. Exam 2 was very hard so if by chance you can do well on it it'll help your grade. I can't wait to take my final and be done.
Though Dr. Ho can be sassy sometimes, he's hilarious. He's an expert in biology, and admits to being condescending, but he's a GREAT professor in a sense that he shares his knowledge with the class.

He is very condescending to all of his students and rarely says much that is actually helpful. He will tell you that there is no necessity to memorize concepts, because they're "intuitive and obvious," but for anyone who can't figure out complex chemical structures, it isn't. His tests are graded very harshly; he expects students to match the key.

His half of the class is much harder than majors. I ended up doing well in the class, but I wish I'd known earlier that the key is to memorize (basically sentence-by-sentence) the problem sets and test keys. He goes over things way too quickly in class though. It's a hard class and I really hated it and him. Only take it if you have to.

Exams are very straightforward, uses the book and lecture slide material, nothing "tricky." He is very engaging, and you really appreciate his humor and character once you have other personality-less professors.
Nice guy, despite what some say.  You like him alot less after exams.

His English is fine, but his accent is heavy. It is hard to catch the important things he says because he talks quickly. He also can be a bit sadistic, but he is a better teacher than Majors. Make sure to go over the old exams and problem sets.

His English is near perfect. I honestly don't know how you couldn't understand him.  I found him to be much better than Majors and actually explains the concepts, though sometimes he just skims over some things.  The book does a great job of in-depth explanations though.  He's always willing to help, and I found his class to be really interesting!

He's earnest and wants to be a good teacher, but his class notes are a mess and he spends a lot of time trying to correct his own mistakes. Class was useless. I taught myself this class entirely from the textbook. Would not recommend.

Hot take: I did not like this class. Professor Baer walks through the aisles and speaks really intensely, probably in an effort to keep students engaged, but in reality it was just overbearing and made it hard to relax in class. My head was on a swivel trying to watch him. He also took an hour to discuss 20 mins worth of reading material.

I can see why some people love him, but if you're someone who dislikes raising their hand in class, get ready to tank a hit to your grade bc participation REALLY matters. His lectures are pretty interesting and engaging, but the group project was hell bc we had to find time to meet across 8 people, and had little time to complete.

Prof. Baer is an amazing lecturer. He makes the content exciting and applicable to real life. The creative mindset steps he teaches comes in handy for case interviews and other group projects. This is a must take class!
His class is bad. In general OB 360 sucks.
N/A

Extremely overbearing, but a very interesting lecturer. He cold calls and is very rough with grading. Sometimes what he wants isn't clear. Mandatory attendance with cold calling, so do the reading. He can be annoying sometimes as well, in terms of making students feel stupid or acting superior for knowing the answers.
Great professor and lecturer.  He makes class very interesting and I enjoy attending class.  He can be a hardass and his tests are by no means easy.  Participation in class is key.

The guy is an incredibly nice and interesting individual. There is a lot of reading involved, but his grading is more than fair. He definitely makes lectures interesting from time to time, and some of the information is pretty pertinent.
He makes the material interesting, he brings in fun real world examples and current research. He does a good job of remembering everyone's name, even outside of class, and he has fun teaching

very entertaining during class but somewhat of a jerk when it's one-on-one or in small groups...extremely intense when he lectures, i feel like no matter if i did the readings i still looked like an idiot when he talked to me

Great professor. He makes class interesting and doesn't just lecture the whole time. We watch good videos and work in groups a lot. It's a lot of common sense and people tend to think it's a joke, but this stuff is useful.
Easy class, as long as you go to class, pay attention and learn all the right buzz words to use in case studies.  There wasn't a class average grade below 85, ever.
Nice if he likes you.  Lots of reading and work, but not too hard to do well on last test.

Samira is a pretty good stats professor. The class is pretty easy as long as you pay attention. Lectures are recorded so attendance isn't really important but she likes participation. All the quizzes are online and open note and she gives lots of time to do them. The only thing is that an A is an 95% (not 93% like other classes)

Office hours and textbooks fill in learning gaps. Be careful of participation grade, because there's a quick assignment every lecture! Another user seems to imply Fazel's non-American accent is hard to understand. In my experience Fazel slows down her speaking, has very clear slides to follow, which accomodates many types of learners. Caring prof!

Look, Samira is a really sweet professor who tries her best to keep her students engaged. However, her class is extremely dull and it's almost impossible to pay attention in her class. Material is pretty easy.

The professor is sweet, always answers emails, and is available during office hours. Paying attention in lectures isn't necessary since the slides will be more than enough to do the quizzes and homework. However, there is a class participation quick assignment every lecture, so paying attention in class won't hurt.

She is incredibly sweet, and perhaps it's just the format of the class, but I don't think I learned much in the course. I came in with strong excel skills that allowed me to handle all the course's content, and they didn't really improve throughout the semester. Lectures were boring, and quizzes were so easy you don't need to pay attention.
Samira is the nicest ever. She will make sure you get a good grade on things if you ask for help.
Good person but so incredibly hard to understand what she is saying. The content of the class is not hard, but I have to spend the entire class trying to decipher what she is saying.
Such a nice person, but not a good prof. So incredibly boring.

Dr. Fazel is amazing! All you have to do is pay attention in class and fill in the guided notes, and you will do great. She explains the concepts extremely well, and her lectures were very organized and straightforward. If you have any questions definitely go to her office hours. She is always willing to help! This class is an easy A/A+.

Prof. Samira is such a kind, caring, and respectful professor. Yes, the statistic topics can become a bit dry at times, but she does her best to engage students in class, as well as give plenty of opportunities to get help. The exam is very easy if you use the past exams and study guides to help. Don't take an easy professor for granted! So kind!!

A really nice professor. Always willing to answer questions. Lots of practice quizzes and exams available and the course content was extremely easy so it's pretty much an easy A. Definitely take DAT 120 with her.

The class content is boring, but Samira does her best to make it engaging. She always finds ways to relate it to the real world. Lots of practice problems in class. She fills in guided notes that have everything you need to get 100% on exams and quizzes. Homework is very easy, usually just using Excel to solve a problem. Very kind and caring prof.

Professor Kidder himself is a great and caring person. The class has lectures and group assignments. All of the assignments were graded by TA's who were strict. I got points taken off for minuscule things that seemed excessive at times. Interesting class, but beware of the TA's and put in more effort than you think on the assignments.

I personally found the Earth's Future lectures quite difficult to get through because they were very long and not the most engaging at times. The Canvas page could be very disorganized, with assignments posted late, links in confusing places, etc. Kidder himself is very, very kind and understanding though. A great person, just didn't like the class

The class and Professor Kidder weren't too bad. The lecturing can get a bit dull at times, but there aren't many assignments. The concepts can be interesting to consider, but once again, it is boring.
Incredibly nice person and interesting lecturer. Earth's Future is a very easy class; if you participate in section and give effort on projects then you will do well.

I heavily disliked this class, but Dr. Kidder is a nice professor and gives great lectures. You can tell he has a passion for teaching and climate change, and this energy translates well in class. We had group assignments every week, and projects were graded very harshly. Wouldn't recommend the class, would recommend the professor.

Would highly recommend Earth's Future: Causes and Consequences of Global Climate Change to any incoming first year. Both Prof Kidder and the other prof Brent Williams are super caring (i.e. cancelling class when its nice out for mental health) and you will learn a lot about climate change (which is super important!) Considering changing my major

TR is an amazing professor to have. I had him for Earth's Future and it's probably one of the easiest classes I've ever taken. The information is really interesting and the group projects in class will help keep you engaged.

TR is amazing. I took Earth's Future which has mostly guest lecturers, but TR was the most caring, funny, and smart professor. I would absolutely take another class with him. It wasn't hard, but I learned a lot, and he was great. Super nice and always willing to chat: he really wants to get to know his students, even in a bigger class.
Great professor.  Knows his stuff.  Show up do the reading and you will do great.  Likes to open up for discussion.  Made the class enjoyable.
Cool dude.

This professor means well but only take the class if you're not interested in science. He tries to teach some stuff he doesn't know enough about, like chemical cycling (he wrote CO2 with a small O and the 2 like it was a power). grading is wack too, goes from hard to easy over course of the semester for everyone, no matter what the quality of work.
Tough class (warning: unofficial "writing intensive" class...) but he's really helpful. take it if you don't mind challeges. you will get rewarded for your hard work.

Dr. Taylor is incredibly kind and understanding. I would recommend her writing identity course for sure for college writing. However, you will get very little feedback and wont learn much most likely.
Dr. Taylor is a really supportive instructor. Her courses are intriguing and the work loads are fine. The feedback she gave is very constructive. She is a nice and easygoing person.

An outstanding professor. She is kind and caring, checking in with her students to make sure they're alright, and she only assigns a couple of larger writing assignments. Just know that there will be readings and discussion posts for each class, but they can all usually be done in under 30 minutes. Cannot recommend her class enough.
She was the kindest teacher. Very understanding and gave me an indefinite extension for no points off. Surprisingly enjoyed the class and I think she grades easy (no grades are in yet lol).

She has been one of the most lenient but also attentive professors I've ever had. She always understand when I have other things going on and always checks up on us. Discussions are well lead and I always learn a lot but it is also very low stress.
So sweet and a great, kindest teacher ever. She genuinely cares about each student.

i have dr. taylor for my college writing class (writing identity) for this semester and she is simply amazing. she's engaging and wants to hear about what we do outside of class. one time i accidentally showed up an hour early and she let me stay through that class instead of waiting for my own. plus she plays pokemon go

Prof. Taylor showed up to class late for the majority of the semester. It is the last day of finals and I have received one grade from her all semester. Nothing was graded out of the probably 10 major assignments that we had until after the last week of classes (the day before the final paper was due). She is kind, but her class is poorly organized

Let me just say that after arriving late to every single class her peer review reminded the class to "keep in mind I have two children to feed"... returned all papers super late. Only one or two essays were graded before the last week of class :(

Tough grader. Wants your paper to flow well + provide some creative insight. A b+ is a above average paper while an A-/A is an exceptional paper. Overall, nice teacher and understanding but you must put in work to get a good grade. She is very flexible to different ideas and writing styles.

Basically, writing one is boring and there isn't really anyway to get around that. Dr. Taylor is super nice and of all the writing one profs she probably gives the least amount of work. With that said, she is a hard grader and she nitpicks. If there is any flaw in your paper you best believe she will find it.

She is one of the most helpful teachers i have encountered. She also likes to push students to do their personal best. Since i love to write she really helped me reach my goals even if they exceeded the ones listed in the syllabus. She makes writing 1 enjoyable unlike many of the writing 1 teachers at wash u.

Professor Hegel is truly an awesome professor. He has so much passion for Chinese Civilization, and the class is really interesting provided that you're excited to show up and listen during the lectures. He is realistic with work load/reading, and is very understanding if you need an extension on an assignment. You should definitely take his class!

Hegel gives terribly boring lectures, but attendance doesn't count towards your grade, so you don't technically have to go. The workload really isn't a lot, but your grade is almost entirely based on discussion posts and papers. Getting an A on the papers can be tricky though... beware of the TAs, who are tougher graders than Hegel himself.
Terrible! Didn't learn anything! Very unstructured class
Very boring lectures, but easy to get an A if you do the work required.

An amazing professor! Kind, incredibly knowledgeable in his field (possesses not only breadth of knowledge, but a very nuanced depth that is rarely achieved by one outside the culture), and very much willing to help students.  The best professor I've had in my 4 years at WU, truly inspiring.

He's a great teacher, but be careful of his TAs! When you're writing response papers, don't do outside research or bring in your own knowledge because they will invariably mark your grade down. The best strategy is to just write down what he says on his powerpoint slides. Besides that, I think his class is excellent!

Take Chinese Civ with Hegel. Incredibly nice guy, very passionate on the subject, very knowledgeable and has a good sense of humor. Flexible on due dates and not a tough grader either. The final is incredibly easy and with a little work this class can be an easy A. Lectures aren't mandatory but I found I didn't want to miss the. Hegel is the man!

Professor Hegel is amazing! Chinese Civilization is not the most interesting topic to me, but Hegel was a great teacher who the fact that not all his students care as much as he does. He works around this by hoping you get as much out of his class as possible. The deadlines are not enforced for this reason - he wants you to learn the material, rath
Wonderful professor and a great human being.  Very smart, always has time for students, classes are interesting.

Took this class awhile ago, but still remember it as one of the best I've taken here.  He's very knowledgable about the subject, and makes class interesting.  Also, assignments/grading are very reasonable.
Great teacher with a great attitude -- very helpful, and flexible with due dates. Always wants to work with/for students, never 'against' (like some others.)
No Comments

[Rating for Individual & Community SOC301B/AMCS L98-301B. A ton of readings for each class. If you do them and participate in class discussion, that's the only way to get anything out of the class. Can be somewhat boring if class doesn't talk so definitely depends on class dynamic. Interesting topics covered.
sometimes can be group discussion heavy but tests are always take home which is a huge plus. if you listen during lecture you will learn a lot

Professor may come off quiet but he is actually funny. classes are either lecture or group discussion, depends on the day. it's easy to get distracted but his insights are interesting if you do listen. VERY forgiving for extensions even though essays seem like it is a lot.  if you participate in class he will like you

Darnell is a great prof in general-- would recommend any of his classes if you need to fulfill ArtSci requirements, I've heard none are particularly difficult. I found him to be a very forgiving grader, and was able to do well on the in-class exams simply by keeping up with the reading outside of class. Very minimal work outside of the readings.
His syllabus is put together in a haphazard way and he explains simple concepts and ideas in an unnecessarily confusing way.

Darnell is one of my favorite professors at WUSTL. I've taken a number of classes with him and would highly recommend him to anyone looking for interesting material presented in a laid back fashion. He is very helpful to meet with for essays and is a really great guy. You put into his class what you get out of them, take advantage of office hours

I really liked Darnell and his Race and Ethnic Relations in the US course. Do the readings and you'll do fine on the tests. He's a fair grader and a guy who seems genuinely interested in the subject, would definitely recommend this class.

I took the immigrant experience class with him. He never came prepared or on time to class. The readings seemed random,  very shallow and with no chronilogical order. No flow from class to class or even within a single lecture.  He likes to use big words to overstate very, very simple arguments. Waste of time and money.

I think his course was useful to me. He will not answer your emails, and he does not lecture in this  class or race and ethnicity. He goes to all his classes and he is fair to grade. Hard course though. For graduates willing to the the work.

This course of race and ethnicity was mediocre. He does not review Sociology theorist of racism, ideology, religion etc. Very shallow views and he does not really prepare his classes. I will not take another course with him

Took his Race and Ethnicity in  American Politics class.  Definitely not as interesting as I thought it was going to be.  Not a lot of discussion--mostly lecture that is hit and miss.  Same with readings.  Tests are tricky and highly subjective.

Lenihan is a genius in his field. Some of the material will be difficult if you don't have a music theory background; if you do you'll be fine. Some classes Lenihan just talks about music in general rather than music theory; I've learned a lot just from coming and listening. Take a class with Lenihan if you appreciate music - you will enjoy it.
Amazing professor, one of my favorites at washu by far. Really cares about growth and learning. He is a jazz wizard

This is an unbelievable class. It's basically just him talking about his experience in the music industry and it's very light work load; all you have to do is compose a few short melodies. He doesn't really believe in grades, so if you don't get the grade you want he's happy to talk about it and change it.  Totally recommend.

One of my favorite teachers of all time. If you have any questions he's very accessible. His exams and final were take home. He LIVES for music and knows so very much about it. I would have coffee with him just to hear all that he knows. He performs often, too. Take a class of his if you can.
He is just great. I dont need to explain myself beyond that.

Prof. Lenihan is a God among men. He will forgive your 3-week late assignments as long as you show interest in music. I highly recommend him as a theory teacher, conveys concepts clearly and knows EVERYTHING ABOUT MUSIC. Some of his classes will never mention theory at all...classical, jazz, pop music, he knows everything. Highly recommended

An absolute musical genius. If you have the opportunity to take a course with Lenihan, do not pass it up. If you show up and do the work you will get an A. And I promise you, that A will be well-deserved. Just by being in Lenihan's presence you will learn more than you could imagine.

Amazing teacher! Absolute music genius! Very helpful outside of class and hands down the greatest guitarist I have ever had the great fortune of meeting.  If you have any interest in music (jazz, classical or otherwise), take a class or private lessons with him!
Best professor I've ever had.  I learned more in this class than any class I've ever taken.  He integrates the Western canon with the subject at hand to give an enormous view of the world of music.
Inspirational, precise, and...well...cuddly.

Simply, Lenihan is a the greatest musical mind at Wash U.  As a teacher, he can get sidetracked talking about politics or whatnot, but it's worth taking a class with him just to witness this incredible human being in action.  He should start his own religion.
This class was awesome and I learned a lot. It helps you look at music in a different way. Prof. Lenihan is an awesome teacher and everyone should take a class with him.

Kafalenos is certainly a demanding professor, but it's refreshing to have someone who expects you to push yourself. She genuinely cares about her students, and though she has high expectations, she is willing to work with you to help you meet them.

Rather incomprehensible--she has the voice of Miss Fowl from Jimmy Neutron. Kafalenos is clearly very interested in the field of comparative lit, and her excitement shines through in the classroom. She's taught this class for a while, and I wouldn't be surprised if she rigidly formed her analytic opinion of each work examined ages ago. Inflexible.

Difficult class; it is boring because she basically lectures about literary theory the whole time and has a set lesson plan and there's no room for discussion or participation. Her essays are hard and really specific but she just wants you to repeat what she says in class. Nice woman, but often unclear. This class is a requirement for the major.

Honestly, I think that Professor Kafalenos gets an unfair rating here.  Yes she is demanding but it is really refreshing to have a teacher who actually expects us to engage with the material.  She sometimes can be hard to understand but she really knows what she is talking about and does not let unsupported comments stand without explanation.
Most incomprehensible professor I've ever had. It is a mandatory class for the major, but it's miserable. She hates life and wants everyone to be unhappy with her.
painful. necessary for comp lit/comp art majors and doable, but painful. tough grader.

Too old and outdated to teach. Trying to pretend she is talking about important philosophical ideas which easily wins the applause of the bunch of con artists sitting in the class. When will she retire so i can major in Comp Lit again?

She is incredibly smart and really a leader in her field. She may speak kind of funny and look kinda weird, but she knows what shes talking about and she is very nice and helpful. Class was pretty intense though.
She's an old ****
She is nice but very unclear.
No Comments
Considerate, but demanding-- Expect most Wash U students to take big  A s...
The only good thing I can say is that he truly cares about students. Other than that, he is disorganized in his notes and lectures, frequently goes on tangents, and is mostly confusing.

A very nice and funny man, but he is the most disorganized and unclear professor I have ever had. He actually refuses to do examples in class1 WHO DOES THAT?! Take Diffeq with another teacher if you can. I haven't very much in this class.

A nice man with good intentions but relies too heavily on MIT material to teach. Difficult tests at first but got easier throughout semester. Go to class, listen to his goofy stories about formula 1 cars, his wife, and all the weird events he encounters. You will get out what you put into this class, don't freak out because kids failed- theyre lazy

I took Diff Eq "for fun" because I typically like math classes, but I probably won't take any more because of Feldman.  He's a nice guy, but disorganized, unhelpful, and honestly did not seem to know the material very well.  He got confused during his own lectures.  Do NOT take Diff eq from Feldman.

Probably the worst professor I've ever had. He's extremely disorganized. I took calc 3 at a community college and learned MUCH more there, and I'm paying somewhere around 10 times as much for Feldman. Unless you already know Diff EQ, DONT TAKE IT FROM FELDMAN.

I have taken several classes from Prof Feldman.  As stated, he's a nice man.  Think of him as your guide to mathematics; he often presents the view from 10,000 ft and this is a service to his students.  His exams are fair, often easy, but yes, you will need the book.  But you'll also come out of the class a near-expert in the subject matter.

He's very nice and funny. So nice that it'll be almost heartless to hate him, but I have to say his lectures are really bad. Unless you're a genius and prepare to spend double the time on learning yourself, avoid his classes

Hardly anyone shows up for his lectures because they are dull and generally unclear. He is a really nice man, and wants his students to suceed, but I would avoid his classes unless you are willing to teach yourself mostly from the book.

Prof. Feldman is a great guy in term of his enthusiasm as a teacher, but his lecture is extremely unclear and highly disorganized. While he always carefully, and individually, asks his students if they understand the materials he teaches, most students are often embarassed to say no. Most students stop going to class, and instead teach themselves.

Feldman is a really nice guy, but not a good lecturer at all. I actually really enjoyed the class because everyone else stopped coming except me and about 6 other people, so it was more like a discussion-based small group. But I wouldn't advise taking the class from him.
No Comments
worst calc 3 teacher ever.  so pointless.  i might as well have not gone to class.  the second i stopped paying attention to him and reading the book instead, i did better.
Nice, very funny, likable guy. Interesting and easy going class.
Best Prof. at Wash U by far.  Interesting class that is applicable to everyday life.
Nice guy and Great prof. Not too dificult. Makes classes more interesting by joking alot.
funny, sarcastic, interesting. awesome guy, i would/am taking everything he teaches
Great class.  Class time flies by and is very entertaining.
No Comments
No Comments
Great Professor, really interesting class. Very helpful outside of class, wants you to get the most out of it. Great class.
No Comments
Great professor, he really makes the class interesting and fun...makes you want to go to class.
Cunningly witty, mordantly funny remarks, makes the subject applicable and interesting.

Very interesting class.  Possibly the most applicable business class I've ever taken. While he can be sarcastic (he warned us at the beginning of the semester that he might make someone cry haha), he is so loveable and interesting you can't help but like him.

Regan is definitely way strict and demanding, but she is also an awesome teacher if you let her teach you. It can feel like she's just putting you down sometimes, but she's really doing what she knows will make you grow, painfully or not. She helped me figure myself out when I hadn't even known I was confused.

I really wanted to like Regan. I think our personalities clashed a bit, and she made me seriously question whether I am meant to be an artist. (and not in a good way.) She barely EVER praises work, which made me want to do well out of fear, not love. I don't operate well under those conditions. She could however, read me like a book.

Regan is almost singlehandedly responsible for my drawing abilities, which I did not acquire until after my semester in Florence. One day she told me my markmaking was lazy. After that, I was miraculously cured of my inability to draw. Sharp as a tack. And full of great ideas when your project has come to an impasse. And she's so stylish.

Regan likes to say, at the end of 2-D, "see how much you learned even though it was so hard?".  I don't think I learned very much at all.  The projects were either not stimulating at all or had too many restrictions, and the way she guides students through the "creative process" is counteractive to creativity.  Just did not like her teaching style.
N/A

I love Regan!  She is all those things, OCD, incredibly anal about matboard, and if you dont show up to a crit you can forget about your grade, but she inspires SO much creativity, and is a joy to be around for three hours a day twice a week (which is more than I can say for most professors).

I think I can sum up my experience with Regan with one story. A friend of mine broke his nose the night before a project and was due, and spent the night in the hospital. Regan took 20% off his final grade, because it wasn't in on time. This woman has no soul.

Wonderful.  As noted, extremely challenging, well dressed, and possesses a preternatural ability to determine if matte board is cut at exactly 90 degree angles.  While I gave her a low rating for clarity, I mean it in a good way: she gives you freedom.

Awesome lady, wonderful teacher, but incredibly OCD. I'm convinced that if you created the greatest masterpiece the world has ever seen and presented it to her on a piece of matboard without perfect edges, you'd get a B-.
As long as you do things exactly her way, you'll improve and come out with cool suff (P.S. - cut your matte board REALLY PRECISELY).
unbearably attractive, very distracting
Very well dressed.  Also very OCD.  But cool teacher overall
Erik is an incredible teacher who cares for his students as people. He helps you develop your writing and holds high standards for his own teaching. Erik is a wonderful and kind person.
Erik is an amazing professor. I'm so sad that he won't be teaching anymore.

Erik Strobl is amazing. He genuinely cares about each and every one of his students. His feedback on essays is the best feedback I've ever gotten from any teacher ever. I went into this class hating it (as a math major I'm not a fan of using words), but ended up loving it. I wish he taught my math classes.

This was truly one of the most amazing experiences for me at WashU. I started ditching all my classes, but this is the only one that I never missed. Every class is truly incredible, and you're definitely not going to fall asleep (he's gorgeous). He really demands your attention and urges you to participate in discussions. LOVE THIS GUY!
The best class I've taken in college, mostly because of this individual's caring and thoughtful demeanor. He truly cares about his students.
Erik Strobl is amazing.

He is literally one of the most intellectual and articulate people I have ever met. If I could give him a 10/5 for clarity, I would. He is very observant and thorough in his teaching and work, and also relatable and funny.

I had him for writing and he was an all-around great person and is amazing to just listen to. He cares about each and every student and inspired me to become a writer. Take a class with Erik. Also he's really hot, so there's that.
Erik is actually great! He knows how to make writing really enjoyable by breaking it up into parts. One of the best profs here.
Brilliant, kind, understanding, funny. I looked forward to attending every class (even when it conflicted with pregaming for WILD)...he's that amazing of a teacher and man.

There's a reason they don't announce writing 1 instructors until the first day of class. It's because if they did, everyone would be fighting for a spot in Erik Strobl's class. Erik is a remarkably and genuinely funny, caring, fascinating, and all around outstanding professor. I do not like writing but I looked forward to every class.
This man is THE man. He is the most understanding coolest funniest professor.

Daemi is a really caring professor who will go out of his way to help his students. He makes sure you understand whatever question you come to him with, is very accessible outside of class, office hours were good and optional problem session was helpful. His teaching made multiple choice/conceptual questions on exams very easy to handle.

His style of lecturing was writing out many examples, and elaborating on concepts/definitions along the way, on the blackboard, which I personally found helpful for success in this class. He takes questions, stays after class to help, is helpful during OH, and even hosted weekly problem sessions despite the asynchronous model. Good pick for 132
He tries his best and is kind to students, but his lectures could be more helpful. He explains calc 2 like he's speaking to another mathematician

Daemi is one of the most underrated math profs at WashU. I took his calc3 this semester and it was great. Lectures were amazing with helpful examples and he was always patient to take questions. He shows care for his students and genuinely wants you to succeed. I enjoyed our class and the math. Would take again!

Matrix with Professor Daemi was doable. Some tips would be to go to office hours if the Crowdmark is hard and make sure to take diligent notes of the definitions he gives. Also, the practice exams help prepare you for the midterms and finals.

Unclear and hard to follow in the lectures (for me). But, homework is very relevant to tests so if you spend the time each week to skim the textbook, skip through a few khan academy videos, and/or go to academic mentoring so that you can do the homework well, you should do well on tests. Nice guy, just inexperienced and not the best lecturer.

Too much homework. I found him to be unhelpful and a bit rude outside of class. Talked to the board too much. Each lecture was individually good and well-explained, but they didn't always line up with subsequent lectures very well. This made some topics more confusing than they had to be, since it was unclear whether we had seen a topic before.

Teaches by filling boards with a textbook style of progression with definitions, examples, and proofs. Can be a little fast sometimes, but I enjoy this style of teaching through discovery and building up concepts.

Guy clearly knows how to do the math but just has no real ability to teach it. He just stands at the front of the class and does work on the board without much explanation. Better off teaching yourself with the textbook.

Daemi literally just writes and speaks in very low and confusing tones -- it's hard to understand during lectures. Although Daemi is very intelligent, you won't learn much from going to lectures. The homework is ok but hard due to the lack of knowledge students have.

Daemi seems to be talking to the board instead of the students 90% of the time, and he shows no capability as a professor to teach the students. He writes too much, rushes over concepts, and just simply doesn't explain that much. With that being said, he is a very nice person and will explain concepts to you if you ask him with follow up questions.

He was one of three professors during Calc 1, but I only met him during office hours. He helped me a lot when it came to math problems I was struggling on and really took time to get to each of the students' questions! He's really caring and always wished us luck on quizzes or whatever else was going on at the end of office hours. I respect him

I learned a lot in his Intro to NT class. He covered most of the NT in depth, delving into historical figures and how historians use the various books to piece together the past. Jenott's passion for the material and extensive knowledge were clear, and he was always available for questions. I also appreciated his honesty about his potential biases.

Quite literally my favorite class I've ever taken here. Beginning Greek is incredibly complicated, but Professor Jenott was so incredibly supportive. He knows what he is talking about, and explains things very clearly. His feedback is encouraging as well as helpful. He grades very fairly and has an immense amount of patience.

Took him for New Test and he is so cool; I'm gonna take more classes with him. He's super smart and knows his stuff; very interesting material. Sometimes tho he seems tired, and kind of depressed. He also stopped assigning papers in this class, it's only MCQ. Make sure you know exactly how/what to study for the tests!! Otherwise, amazing guy.
A humorous, accessible, caring professor. Never miss his courses if you are interested in early Christian history/biblical studies/Greek and Coptic languages. I really love him.

Dr. Jenott was solid for Scriptures and Cultural Traditions. He tries to make things interesting and grades fairly. I would recommend taking him for another class though because this wasn't really in his area of interest. The classes could've been more discussion based, but it was difficult to jump in depending on your religious background.

Magicians, Healers, and Holy Men was a great class! A LOT of reading, but it was all really interesting. Attendance at lecture wasn't graded, but he doesn't record his lectures or post slides, so be prepared to go to class. TAs grade pretty much everything, so if you get a good one, you can get a good grade. (no tests, just papers) Awesome class!

If you can, take Magicians, Healers, and Holy Men! This class was the best class I have taken at WashU, and Lance was why. I have never been in a large lecture class where the professor actually remembers the names of their students and goes out of their way to talk to them. This class was so fun and just such a refreshingly positive experience.
Dude was vibes. Incredibly passionate about the subject matter. Not an easy class, but still pretty interesting and I would say I learned a lot.
Professor Jenott is a great professor. His lectures are entertaining, and he makes each topic interesting. TA grades almost everything, so as long as you have a nice one, the class is pretty easy.

Lance is amazing. Honestly the best professor I have ever had. He's super chill, accessible, and helpful. He understands that we are people with complicated lives and is super, super accommodating. He really loves his subject and appreciates students taking interest in it as well. Always down for a chat about religion or papers or life. I love him!
Had Prof Jenott for Greek and he was great. Greek is hard but his quizzes and exams were always reasonable and he went through translation at a comfortable pace. Really interesting and personable guy

a fav ! gotta lov lance. he was new this year and th class i took w him was absolutely fascinating. rly good at addressing both theology and history and looking into how they interact; 10/10 would recommend

Genuinely the worst and most unprofessional professor I've ever encountered. She is contradictory about what she wants. Her ego is through the roof without anything to back it up. I can handle blunt, but she is snarky and rude. I have never felt so disrespected. Other professors are good enough professors to be direct -- she is not.

Professor Lewis is extremely unprofessional. Her feedback is not constructive, she attacks your character if she feels you're not living up to her (uncommunicated) expectations. It's true, if you follow directions you should get a good grade. But her directions are incredibly unclear & contradictory, and if you ask questions she gives nonanswers.

Capricious, cantankerous, arbitrary, unresponsive, unclear, petty, pedantic. Barks "louder" whenever a student speaks. Writing standards: maximum redundancy, minimum comprehensibility. Forces students to purchase her own book, "Telling Your Story," an infinitely less helpful distillation of Career Services' advice. Kafkaesque. Sinecure? Retire.

Professor Lewis is tough but fair. She treats her students as any partner would at a law firm. A lot of people complain about not being able to be creative in her class. Legal writing, when you are a beginner, is not about creativity. It's about doing what the assigning attorney expects of you. I learned a great deal from her and respect her a lot.

Isn't a good writer (other Professors who are give opposite advice). Doesn't know law underlying her assignments. You have to just try to write what she wants, but she won't be direct about what that even is. Would indicate she wanted something very different right before assignments were due. Would rather work in CCD but wants LP money I guess.

Class is only "hard" bc the teacher is rude and unhelpful. She is condescending and dismissive.  DO NOT ask another professor or lawyer for clarity. She doesn't want USEFUL writing. Just mimic her samples as best as you can. And prepare to buy her book of 'WISDOM" once it's done. You will learn nothing and pay lots of tuition for it.

The worst professor of my entire academic career.  I don't even know how to begin to explain how she has this job.  Not just the worst professor, but one of the worst human beings I've ever encountered.

The person who wrote the comment below pretty much hit the nail on the head. She is utterly useless, does not reward creative or independent thought, and will make it a point to chastise or insult you on a whim. If you get stuck with her, my best advice is to rewrite her sample memos with as little deviation as possible. Godspeed.

Quite possibly the nastiest professor I've ever had, and likely has one of the lowest IQs for a WUSTL professor. I question her place here--she seems better suited for administrative tasks. She thinks very highly of herself and is very condescending towards students. Rewards those who can regurgitate her poorly-phrased sample sentences verbatim

Professor Lewis is supposed to teach students legal writing, but in practice she only wants you to write what she tells you to write; her "sample" outlines used some ridiculous arguments, but if you deviate from them, your grade will suffer.  She has no interest in actually teaching anyone anything, and she has a really nasty personality.

Prof Lewis is super clear about what she wants in a paper. If you resist and write your own way, then you'll do poorly. If you follow her script, you'll do great. It's that simple. Lewis also cares about her students. She can be terse sometimes, but I thought it forced us to be professional (i.e. first-year associates).

Tolman is a really great lecturer who generally gave hard, but fair tests. Unfortunately, his grading expectations were very hazy towards the end of the semester and the intention behind his grading methodology was hand wavy. This caused many people to be unhappy and/or surprised with their grades in the class.

Prof Tolman is a great teacher. It's no doubt very hard to teach ochem to 300+ students but I think he did a very good job. A lot of studying must be done on one's own though (READ THE TEXTBOOK & do the practice problems). You have to do more than just go to lecture and workshop. studying outside of class is necessary but Tolman is amazing

Prof. Tolman was a great explainer, his lectures were example-heavy and he explained things in depth. He was very accessible in his office hours, etc. He was also very genuinely concerned for his students and wanted everyone to do well!

lectures were "mandatory" but were pretty useless, i learned out of the textbook most of the time / taught myself basically. the class itself was not the hardest it could have been, but my main complaint is his grading. i could've made a better grade in this class if he had not curved everyone's grades down, for no good reason.

I am not sure what I learned this semester. I stopped trying to learn, and I just began memorizing problem sets and text problems. I thought I was getting an A, but I don't know why I ended with a B+. I agree with others that Tolman should get rid of the ipad. Disappointing semester.

OK, start with I took 105/106, but half of the time I had no idea what Prof. Tolman was talking about. My friends who took 111/112 thought this was easy. So, I don't know if it is an issue with me, Prof. Tolman, this class, or my Gen Chem, but this was an awful experience. I did not feel comfortable speaking with Prof. Tolman about this.

Tolman was fine, and he tried to connect with the students. A bit of humor was appreciated. I DO NOT LIKE the IPAD teaching style. This is one of the easier STEM classes you will take at Wash U. Exams are exactly like the practice exam. Realize he came from U Minn, and he likely needs time to adjust to Wash U standards.

Not really sure what these bad ratings are but if you read the book, practice, and attend lectures it is a really fair class especially considering the exams he gives for practice are structured identically to the actual exam.

He did the bare minimum needed for an Organic Chemistry class. I did end up doing better on the exam when I stopped caring about lecture and focused more on the problems. He curves exams down by nearly 20 points and then curves the class down by 10 and expects everyone to be happy. I spent 20 hours a week on this class alone aimlessly.

Tolman is ok as a lecturer. A bit holier than thou. I am not a fan of the IPad lecturing. My main complaint about the class was the lack of transparency for grading criteria and lines for letter grades. I thought I was doing fine on the exams (A work) and then I get a B+. I don't understand why he curved down. This info should be in the syllabus.

He is nice enough and he tries to connect, but it was boring and a waste of a semester. Lectures are done with ipad. Attendance was mandatory, because nobody would come to lecture. At least it was easy - the easiest class I have taken at WU. All exam questions are from assigned problems. His off hrs were during class time. Not cool.
Israeli dilf

Prof Rettig is definitely the nicest professor I have ever had. I have never had a professor who cares so much about the individual welfare of his students. Really just a great human being. The class is really interesting and the few papers you will write are graded pretty easily. Highly Recommend.

Prof. Rettig is one of the kindest and most understanding human beings I have ever met. He does an incredible job emphasizing learning versus stressing over grades. Especially because of the pandemic, Rettig did a phenomenal job teaching in an online format and making sure that students' mental health and wellbeing was okay. Best professor ever.

Rettig is THE BEST! He can make any topic relevant to the world today, knows the most intricate details of every Middle Eastern country, and stays impartial no matter how controversial the topic (eg. Israel and the Palestinians). This class is not much work and I guarantee that getting to Prof. Rettig will make the class worth it!

Hands down the BEST professor I have ever had at WashU! Even if you are not interested in the topic, you should take at least one class with him. He makes seemingly mundane material fascinating and I looked forward to his lectures every week. Lectures are engaging and easy to follow, offers generous extensions, and assignments are reasonable.

His lectures are really easy to follow along and engaging. Elai is extremely approachable and helpful, and he genuinely cares about the success of his students. The workload for the class is manageable and the class content is interesting. One of the most enjoyable classes at WashU.
Best Professor I have had at WashU, very engaging lecturer and open to discussion with students. Would take every class with him if I could!
I wish every class I took at WashU was like the one I took with him. Learned a ton, had to put in work but nothing that felt like grunt work, and actually enjoyed lectures.

Elai is an incredible professor and a great human. It is evident that he wants to make an impact at WashU and leave students feeling hopeful for the future. He cares about the work he does and the student he teaches. He values learning over grades and is flexible and accessible both in providing extra help and deadline extensions. Highly recommend!
elai makes this class unbelievably easy for all students. he also brings in outside people for internship connections. best class ever!

The BEST professor I have ever had!! Elai is so incredibly knowledgable and makes even the most difficult international relations concepts easy to understand. The class is NOT that much work at all. He is one of the kindest and most accessible professors, and is so invested in his students' success. TAKE HIS CLASS!!!!

She is really passive-aggressive, and she has an accent in Spanish which makes it hard to understand her. I think in general Washu should not have non-native-speaking people teaching Spanish classes. She is a tough grader and expects you to participate every single second of class and calls you out even though you might have nothing to say.

DO NOT TAKE A CLASS WITH THIS PROFESSOR! She's the harshest grader I've ever encountered and relies on students to lead discussions rather than actually teaching. It really seems like she DOES NOT care AT ALL about her students and just wants them to suffer. She expects students to read RIDICULOUS amounts. Wish I could have rated her a 0.

Tsuchiya is a nice professor and its clear that she knows everything there is to know about her subject. However, her accent is far from native making her hard to understand at times, and she constantly interrupts you while you speak. Relatively tough essay grader. Expects students to know as much about the literature as she does. Overall, not bad.

She is incredibly smart and knows almost everything there is to know about Spanish literature.  That being said, she doesn't understand that her undergraduate students don't know as much as she does, especially about the history of literary movements in Spain.  Her expectations are too high for a 300 level elective.

Not hard.  Never did the reading and got an A-.  Really boring classes and her accent is far from native.  Be prepared for a lot of stuff about women and gender.  The class should be renamed "el papel de la mujer en la sociedad".  On the upside, she's a nice lady.  Ironically prefers guys to girls even though she's a women's studies prof.
unpleasant, not interesting, nit-picky
No Comments
one of the most irritating prof. i've had so far...says 'mm hmmm' every 2 seconds and interrupts you while you talk.  horrible spanish accent...why can't wash u find native speakers to teach?
Fine - not incredibly exciting or incredibly boring.  I'd recommend her.
No Comments
don't ever take her class ... they should have gotten rid of her long ago, but I think she has TENURE ...

A couple of one-page papers, one take-home midterm, a final paper, and one group project presentation. The lectures were interesting, but I sometimes lost track of what he was saying. Great professor overall who exhibits passion and care. Be prepared to go outside a lot (we analyzed the landscape of east end campus/Brookings as well as forest park)

Professor Frachetti is an amazing teacher. He truly cares about what he teaches, and he does not fail to engage with his students. His inspiration comes from his unique experiences, which you cannot find from anyone else. His class is not difficult at all, but I only suggest taking it with an open mind, and a desire to truly understand the nuances.

Course material was interesting and Prof. Frachetti is a really engaging lecturer. That being said he is almost never around to answer questions or get help from. When you finally get a chance to meet with him he acts like he is too important to answer your questions. It's a shame, the class could be so cool.

Very intellectual man. I love listening to his lectures, and the readings are usually interesting. He will notice if you aren't there even though he doesn't take attendance. The exam is very difficult, but the final is pretty chill. Highly recommend this class if you want a light workload but inspirational class in your schedule.

Really engaging lecturer, but his tests are not on the material presented in class (or in the assigned readings for that matter) and he is terrible to try to get help from. He is unavailable 99% of the time and the 1% of time he will talk to you, he acts like he has better places to be. Wish I would have known, wouldn't have taken this class.
Fun class but exams are relatively hard. Exams are a bit long and should answer faster. Recommend to take it pass/fail.
Great class and great professor. He took a very relevant and interesting topic and made it fun.

Awesome dude- passionate about what he's teaching, which is essential in this class due to its novel subject matter (first time Landscape Studies have been taught here). Has a zest for teaching and is committed to each student's comprehension and success. IS AN ASSET TO THE WASH U COMMUNITY
He is the best professor I ve ever met. He speaks fluent in my language - russin, really smart. I like him......
He is sooo hilarious! And hot. Oh, and a good teacher too.
Very laid-back and funny, tells great stories about his experiences as an anthropologist. Very real.

Professor Guofu's course is very interesting. The content is a bit difficult if you don't have a basic understanding. It is more about application rather than mathematical reasoning, so it is acceptable. There are code examples shared, which is very interesting.

Dear two posters below me - I think your comments say more about you than the professor. PS. Guofu is hilarious, has comprehensive notes, is very helpful outside of class, and is one of the top finance professors (research and teaching) at WashU. He will NOT be fired.
terrible professor  i went to half the classes and got nothing out of them and wondered to myself why i ever went in the first place
The prof. can't speak English! FIRE him please!!!

Tough class covering some tough material.  The guy has an accent but is one of the MOST enthusiastic profs I've ever met.  He loves the subject and tries REALLY hard to make classes entertaining (by making dumb jokes, burning books) and is SOO helpful outside of class. Shows real care for students.

His accent's Chinese, not Korean... but yes, is difficult to understand, but he's very willing to help and answer any questions. His notes are comprehensive, so you learn much more in this class than just what's required by the course curriculum.
Can anybody understand what he says? This is US, not Korea.
Really hard to understand. Really hard material. Not a good combination. He was really nice though, and very enthusiastic.
Really enthusiastic, really into what he does which is awesome! He also has great lecture notes. But the material is hard..and he's somewhat hard to understand
Accent sometimes very difficult to understand, but his lecture notes are excellent.  I took this class a year ago, and still refer back to the notes when I need to know something about options.
The single best professor I've had. Prof. Sewell leaving Wash U is nothing short of tragic. If you're lucky enough to attend whichever school hires him next, I envy you.

Amazing professor, arguably unmatched in his passion for the subject and willingness to help students understand and excel on the material. The kind of teacher that exemplifies the importance of loving what you do, his lectures are engaging and even those with limited interest in his courses should try them out

these comments are kinda overrated. i actually thought he was a little hard to listen to at times and I thought he assigned a lot of reading. not terrible by any means, definitely interesting at times, tests and papers aren't too bad, and he is willing to help outside of class, but i wouldn't say he made the class as interesting as it could've been

Sewell is an amazing professor. He does everything right and cares immensely about the material and the students in each class. He's also the most friendly and understanding prof. I've ever known. I slept through an exam and he let me retake it, no questions asked. Taking his class is a privelege.

Professor Sewell is amazing. Fun, pleasant, engaging, open to different ideas, very knowledgable. I had some questions on the final exam and he met with me for over an hour to talk them over. Take his class even if you don't think you're interested in the material.
Such a nice guy, adorable, and easy to listen to!
Absolutely wonderful. Passionate, engaging, insightful... he's truly one of the best professors at WashU.
Good teacher. Tests are fair, and class is pretty interesting. He is engaging and uses a nice combination of powerpoint, discussion, and film clips.

Very enthusiastic about the class, very knowledgeable. Lectures off of slides so can get a bit dull, but all the slides get put online. Fair grader (though the TA is not), understanding about extensions on papers. Tests are a lot to remember but nothing tricky, just memorizing the material.

Sewell is an AMAZING professor.  If you have an interest in film/tv/media, he is an absolutely phenomenal source of information.  Ive loved both of the classes I've taken with him, and do my best to take at least one with him per semester.  He makes you think about things in a totally new way.  I would recommend his class to anyone.

eh.  This course will be pretty dull unless you have a serious interest in complex/dry media theory, Sewell is kind of goofy and just wants his students to like him.  Likes to talk about current tv shows but the class focuses on much older stuff.  Doesn't take attendance often and is a fair grader.

If you're in MBB pt1 and wondering if its worth it to take pt2... DO IT. Doris is one of the best teachers I have ever had. I never missed class, even when I was sick, just because I wanted to take full advantage of every minute I had as his student. Work load wasn't bad at all, don't miss class because lectures are everything.
Great professor. Would look forward to lectures. Very interesting subject material. Would take again in a heartbeat.

Professor Doris is awesome. Speaks honestly/says what he feels, frequently gives out life lessons, is hilarious. Class involved a lot of random discussion, but it was still interesting. The readings were long and assigned before class, but Doris seemed to mainly want students to understand the topic of the day and be ready to listen and discuss.
Doris is an epic man. He gets it. Took topics of the wilderness with him and I probably learned more in that class than any other at the university.
Most amazing prof ever

There wasn't much work to do for this class, other than reading.  There was one short paper, and two exams (we could choose 3 of 5 questions to write about on exam (he provided 8 possible quesitons before hand) so you could practice for the exams.  Class was kind of boring most of the time, but parts were interesting.  He's pretty relaxed

John Doris is the man.  In a past life, John Doris might've been tarred and feathered.  John Doris could be the antichrist.  Someday, John Doris might be the President.  Regardless of what John Doris was, is, or will be, you should meet this man.  If you want your phil class to challenge your assumptions, and make you THINK, see John Doris.
Excellent teacher!! Learned so much in his classes...wish I could take more.  And  very very sexy btw. Thanks Doris!

This is the professor I had been waiting for in college.  Doesn't care what he wears. Doesn't care what he says. Open to talking about anything. I cried laughing in this class. This is true philosophy; though you may not know what he is saying or what you're supposed to even be learning, I recommend this class just for the experience.
Environmental ethics is a life changing course. Doris is one of the best professors I've ever had. highly recommend
Lots of reading! Grades not too important to him. Just fill out your journal and come to class. Must take class.

This class was set up such that the difficulty ramped up considerably for the third exam. I personally had a difficult time digesting course content and felt like I did well in the course because of my background in Mathematics, not from my improved understanding of Economics. Professor was incredibly kind, just not the right learning style for me.

Agree with below. The professor is nice, but everything else is terrible. I've learned nothing from lectures, and there isn't any resources available for you to study on your own, except for a few unhelpful slides. The questions that show up on quizzes and problem sets are always different than what we are expected to learn in class.

I honestly dont understand how people do well in this class. Lectures start out interesting and you blink and all of a sudden if its just pure "math"? It's a very theoritical class but at the same time it has a lot of mathematical calculations. The textbook doesnt help at all with quizzes or hw's. The entire class is based on 2 hws and 2 quizzes.
Hard to understand but knows his stuff
Entirely composed of lectures where you talk about the world's problems. Interesting topics arise and he provides a unique point of view on a lot of these issues.
I think his lecture was fine. The only problem for me was that his grading standard was not very clear, which annoyed me a lot at the end of the semester.
Said on the second day of class that if you did perfectly on the exams and never attended class, you'd get an A-. Take this class only if you want a GPA boost--otherwise, stay away.

Costas Azariadis is yet another example of why free trade is a great policy. He's probably Greece's best export in the past century. The man's brilliant, but easy to talk to. Those questions people spend entire semesters with in Polisci? Costas can give you an answer in five minutes. Meaning of life? Five minutes more. I'm jealous of his grandkids.

I think the commenters here do not give Azariadis the credit he deserves. Azariadis is a really nice professor and hints at the ways you can get an A in the class. What I heard from a lot of people is that other professor teaching this class give boring lectures and ask very difficult questions in the tests.

Total baller! If you do what he asks (show up to class and review section) you will do well.  If not you won't do as well.  Lectures were for most part very interesting and on current topics.  Good guest lectures.  Tests are easy but graded extremely hard.
total waste of a student's time. absolutely horrible lectures..

I took Literature and Justice (first year writing seminar) with Schmidgen and it was one of my favorite classes I've had at WashU. I improved so much as a writer. Writing is a difficult subject to teach because it is so abstract, but Dr. Schmidgen does a great job breaking large intimidating assignments into manageable chunks.

I took Writing, Lit and Justice. he's a very eloquent speaker that allowed for cool in-class debates. However, I released he is an EXCEPTIONALLY hard grader. I don't think there weremore than one or two A-grade papers the whole semester. It made me focus really hard on all my essays, which is really tough because I took this as a required class...
Lectures are so boring, he typed them out and read from his paper (and his slides are not helpful)

Dr. Schmidgen is the head of the Literature department  yet he is one of the most approachable and helpful professors I have ever had. He is happy to read over papers or talk about your ideas during his office hours, he is very respectful of differing opinions, and he structures his class to be a collective pursuit of truth rather than a lecture.

Professor Schmigden was a great teacher, I really enjoyed his class. He gives some really insightful analysis of the books and is really involved in the class, and very passionate. He is really nice at office hours and involved with students. The topics covered during discussion are very varied and interesting. Take his class!
He's pretty much the man :) I really enjoyed his class; it was fun, easy and laid back. I would highly recommend you take a course with him if you get the opportunity. PS he wears amazing pants.

I'm sure he is a brilliant guy, but he needs to work on the pedagogy a bit.  Really intimidating and most un-helpful in class.  The expectations for his assignments were not communicated well at all.  It was really too bad - I think it could have been a great class.
No Comments
He seems very stern at first, but he is extremely helpful and patient.  He gives lots of attention to your papers, too.  Definitely my favorite professor!

He can be kind of awkward outside of class, but maybe that's just because he's German. Very intelligent, very helpful and clear in class. And he's the only professor I've ever had to put an Ikea futon in his office for his students to sit on. Really good professor.

Very nice, talkative, and welcoming. You will learn a lot and she enjoys teaching content outside of the textbook. The only issue I have is her grading can be very critical of little things, but you will earn a good grade if you try. Overall very good teacher and she wants you to learn. Only 2-3 hours of homework per week. Great professor.
She's the best!!!! I love her energy so much.

Chen Laoshi is a great professor. She cares about her students and genuinely wants them to learn as much Chinese as possible. She changed the pace of our course after she found that we were struggling so that we could focus on learning instead of just getting through lessons. She's a tough grader, but her grading is fair. Great energy in class.
Chen Laoshi is an amazing professor, I would definitely take her class again. I really enjoy her classes, and although the material is sometimes hard, it is always rewarding
Ta shi yi ge hen hao de lao shi. shi fen piao liang! Wo ai Chen lao shi!!

I took heritage Chinese and she was so funny. The class was pretty intense esp because you only meet three times a week instead of the usual 5 for language classes. Preparing for tingxie quizzes and doing the homework definitely took a long time but class itself was a good time
she's amazing!

Chen Laoshi is great.  She is so funny and loves to laugh.  She makes the class a lot of fun.  She is very picky with character strokes, and is probably the harder of Chinese teachers.  But, she makes class fun and is a great teacher - you will learn a ton and enjoy class.  Highly recommend.
She's AMAZING! She's utterly hilarious and is super helpful when you ask for it.  She may be the hardest grader but as long as you pay attention and do your work you'll be fine- mei you wenti!!
Fun and nice, but definitely the hardest of the Chinese Teachers

took him for intro to sociolinguistics. 3 hour lectures on weeknights. horrible. goes off-topic, repeats stories, clearly does zero prep. assigns chapters of reading and never mentions much of it in class. the man is so checked out. heavy final project (12 page research paper) plus 20-minute presentation halfway through the semester. soul-ruining.
