I took Linguistics for Legal Purposes with Professor Baugh and class was always fun, despite being 3 hours on a weekday night around dinner time. He is effortlessly funny and the lectures sometimes go off topic (but we appreciate that). Assignments are easy, readings are manageable, and the professor is very understanding.

A non-traditional professor who prefers to rely less on technology. Most of lectures are just the professor talking about himself, but he makes it quite enjoyable. The final project is a legal report of based on a case of your choosing. There was not much technical information to remember. Participation is valued.

He really tries to get students to talk in class. He is always open to new things (even if they disagree with his own mindset) and is overall really good at lecture. Don't understand the dislike some people seem to have for him. Incredible professor and would love to take another class with him.

Prof Baugh is amazing! His lectures are informative and intriguing. He would love to hear from students and interact with us. His personal anecdotes are kind of legendary. He is not harsh on grading as well. Overall a very knowledgeable and kind professor. Definitely looking forward to taking his course again.

There are tons of readings, but I'm never sure if they are adequate for a program of African-American Studies. Moreover, I don't see the connection between theory and practice, since here we are living at great distance from mainstream society. I guess we need to do research about the other community of speakers who speak a different dialect.

Prof. Baugh is a good instructor and helpful. But he often loses the balance between quality and quantity. In the end it is hard to know which reading is essential and which is just for filling. I cannot recommend him yet.
John Baugh is a wealth of knowledge. His class was intimidating and there was a lot of reading, but he was a shockingly easy grader on the tests and final paper.

Professor Baugh is an all-around great guy. When I first began the class, I was indeed skeptical. But after having completed the course, I've found that he does want us to learn. He does a great job of facilitating discussions and allowing us to think on our own. He's caring and considerate and encourages creativity. I learned a lot.

Dr. Baugh has spent his professional career in linguistics, so he has a tendency to go off on linguistic tangents, but he's widely knowledgeable, impersonally kind, and very respectful and interested in students' opinions. He assigns a lot of reading but responds positively to student feedback, reducing reading assignments as needed. Great overall!

I haven't had a single Spanish professor that I haven't liked and that remained true after taking Professor Cunill's class (302). She does a great job facilitating conversation and shows that she cares about her students. My conversation skills greatly improved thanks to her and this class so I would strongly recommend taking a class of hers.

[Rating for SPAN 302] Such small class size (10 people) that class discussions are very chill. Discussion topics/questions are interesting. Readings/videos are interesting too. She doesn't really correct grammar, which is something I wished she did more often. Nice class overall.

Cunill has been my favorite Spanish dept faculty. She is easy to understand, super flexible, and gives great feedback in order to help you improve. She knows some of what 303 assigns is busy work and doesn't really care if you don't do it. She can be a bit monotone but I love her

Profe Cunill came up with really interesting topics and facilitated a variety of lecture, group discussion, and media content. There was a significant amount of group projects, homework, and quizzes, but it was definitely manageable. She covered a variety of social sciences, from law to psych to public health. Overall, a solid choice!

Prof. Cunill was one of the better Spanish professors I have had. She was easy to understand when speaking. Participation was encouraged and she was considerate enough so that I felt encouraged and free to participate rather than pressured to. The grading system was not 100% clear, but grades were not strenuous anyway.

Prof Cunill is wonderful! She is so sweet and cares a lot about her students. 355 was extremely well organized and her expectations are very clear. If you do the work, you will do well. Could not recommend this class more highly!

This was my second class with Cunill, and she didn't fail to impress. If you participate in class you'll do just fine in this class. The papers seemed to be mostly graded according to grammar (but this was her first year teaching 341 coming from only teaching 308 which is more grammar-y). Also the discussions in class were often quite interesting.

Her lectures are so chill they're almost conversational. Although essays are done independently, she gives a lot of good ideas for theses so as long as you listen and take notes when she discusses the readings, you could practically create a paper out of what you've already been told. Good experience.

She's a great teacher, so easy to understand, and an easy grader. She really just wants you to succeed and be interested in what you're learning about. The class is really interactive and honestly just fun.
Loved the class, inspired me to study in Madrid this summer. Easy but good professor, gives great feedback and incredibly relaxed and helpful about any issues. Absolutely take Prof Cunill if you can.

Not a ground-breaking professor but she explained the material really well and made complex and dense readings easier to understand. Lectures and discussions were always super thought-provoking. My main qualm was that she clearly had favorites in the class and graded accordingly. If you want to be one of her favorites, go to office hours a lot.
Fantastic lecturer, very funny. She *is* heavy on reading though, and you can't get away with skipping it.

Despite using this site quite a bit, this is the first time I've actually gone out of my way to write a review of a professor. In short, Professor Bivar is just gleaming with wit, awareness, and intelligence. She is the type of professor that makes an hour and a half feel like it was nearly 10 min. All in all, one of the best at WashU. Must take!

Lectures were entertaining, but the required reading was unreasonable. She assigns literally hundreds of pages of reading per week and gives pop quizzes on them and expects you to know specific details. Not worth it unless you're able to prioritize it above your other classes.

Bivar gave us a test with a bonus question, then decided later to count the bonus question as an actual graded question on the exam. This brought everyone's grades down on a midterm worth 25% of the final grade. Any reasonable person understands that a student will answer a question labeled "bonus" differently than a normal question.

Prof Bivar is great. She's obviously very smart and her lectures are clear and organized. She's funny and engaging with the class. She expects you to read a lot, so be warned if that's not your strong suit, but overall I learned a lot and the class was a great experience.

I like her multimedia approach to lectures but she rarely shows a genuine understanding of the course material or explains the most profound parts. A stickler by the rules. If you miss more than one class, you will lose half a letter grade. No exceptions given. That is a pretty irritating combination.

The readings she selected are perfect for the class, and the lectures clarified the readings in an engaging, non-biased way. Not particularly easy or hard, but I enjoyed the class because the material is very interesting. She was always available for help outside of class and for help on the final papers. I think she's a great professor.

The books she chose for the class are premium and classics of political economy. Not the best lecturer though, but tries her best to engage students' interest. If you work really hard, it is possible to get an A in her course. But definitely not easy A. Overall, not the most knowledgeable or eloquent prof, but an okay one.
Uncompromising, unforgiving and overall an inept lecturer with deficient knowledge of her field(only seems to know modern French history well) One of my worst educational experiences at Wash U.

One of the best professor's I've ever had. Extremely intelligent individual but knows how to communicate with us lowly students. Lectures are very interesting and engaging. I don't think I would've enjoyed my class if I didn't have him as my professor. Very funny, kind-hearted, helpful, and considerate of everyone's thoughts. A real gem!

Brian Talbot is, by far, the best professor I have had to this point. His lectures are engaging and it is readily apparent that he is an extremely intelligent individual. If you have an opportunity to take a class with him, take advantage.

I had Brian Talbert for biomedical ethics. He was a great prof, very passionate about philosophy. He geared the class such that those with no experience with philosophy could understand the material. The load was quite manageable, though if you dislike group work, partner work, or the chance of being randomly called on, he may not be right for you.
He is amazing at what he does. He does give weekly homework and quizzes, but these are actually pretty helpful. He is funny and he really cares about his students.

Brilliant professor. I'm so glad I got him my first semester, as his class motivated me to continue studying philosophy. He's very thorough in discussing lecture material, and you can tell he thinks hard before answering every question. I improved my writing and critical thinking skills tremendously.

He is my favorite professor and he made me fall in love with philosophy and ethics. I am about to take my third class with him. He goes out of his way to make sure that students are learning and that your questions are answered. He is so helpful, straightforward,  and easy to speak with, he gives great lectures, and he is so kind and funny.

There are few professors who care about their students' success like Talbot does. He does everything he can to help his students during class time, and is extraordinarily helpful in office hours. He'll have a long conversation with you specifically about your views for an essay. Interesting topics discussed in class, would highly recommend it.

I had Brian for an intro level philosophy class, Environmental Ethics. Brian is extremely patient, respectful, and receptive of students' questions in and out of class, absolutely hilarious in a way that doesn't interrupt lectures but keeps them fun and engaging. I didn't sit through a single class without laughing several times. Learned a LOT.

Brian is a passionate and funny lecturer, who is willing to talk to the students. He speaks pretty fast though, and a fast-speaking philosopher is sometimes hard to understand. But overall Brian's class is worth taking

Excellent professor. Very interesting lectures and very knowledgeable about the subject matter. Very organized and easy to talk to. Has really helpful office hours and is willing to talk about just about anything. Take any class with this professor that you can.

Professor Hamilton is one of the most incredible professors I've ever had. He's articulate and his lectures and fascinating. I would highly recommend taking him, as his class pushed me into double majoring in Healthcare Management.

This class was very entertaining. Bart is a great professor that knows how to engage. Some of the guest lecturers were much more interesting than others, but overall this class is fantastic at getting an overview of the healthcare industry. Bart grades the cases much easier than Wichelman. Definitely a good amount of work for the Hackatho project

Loves to joke around during class and enjoys it when students are engaged. Class was quite easy; mostly group projects and a few individual take-home problem sets. The midterm was take-home and super straightforward. Would recommend talking a lot/getting to know him- he seems to show a preference for students who speak up a lot during class.
Bart's the man. Really interesting class with guest speakers. As long as you participate and pick a good group for the cases, it's not hard to do well. Take Bart's classes if you can!
Total frat bro. Very laid back, and if you do the work and show any interest at all you should get an A. Dude is also totally politically incorrect, but that's fine in my book.

Great, fun guy. Enjoyed his class that consists of guest lecturers. 4 case studies (6 total, with 4 counting toward grade) done in groups. Find a good group and you will be set for semester. Good teacher and facillitator
Amazing teacher. Don't be late though or he will make you sing in front of the class.

Bart is the man.  Seriously a very interesting and thought provoking professor.  Does a good job of creating class discussion and is lots of fun.  Very approachable and easy to get him off subject and tell stories.  Do the cases, go to class, and you should be just fine

Bart is an amazing teacher, this was by far my favorite class I have taken at Olin, all you have to do is show interest, and he'll give you a B.  The class consist of a couple of case studies, and group projects.  Take this class if you want to learn while at the same time have some fun!
Bart is a great guy, the class is what you make of it, kids that get into it usually get an A, while the rest get b's....and if your really not with it he will give you a c...nothing lower!

Dr. Walke can be strict, but once you warm up to her it's obvious that she is a great professor with a ton of passion for the subject. Her lectures can be dry but they are full of valuable information. She is incredibly considerate and flexible, and just wants you to get everything you can out of her class. One of the best profs I've had for sure.

Professor Walke is an amazing scholar in her field. Taking this class really helped me to learn a lot about the Holocaust; however the readings are super historical and technical. Get ready to spend hours reading. Harsh grader on papers but do give you detailed feedback. Very friendly outside of class.

I loved this class. Prof Walke takes some time to warm up, but she does care about her students and their overall well-being (grant extensions, communicable etc.) Gives good feedback on papers. Learned a ton and she is very knowledgeable. Pretty lecture heavy and tons of reading. Would take this class again.

Walke comes off super intense but if you do the readings and participate and ask questions she will warm up and slowly get funnier. I think for a freshman she can be scary but as a senior looking back I think she taught me really important lessons about how to take notes in a lecture.
She definitely makes the subject matter accessible and easy.

Professor Walke is a strict instructor who is not the most intimate with her class, but she is an amazing teacher. Once you get to know her, you will feel comfortable with her style and learn a lot. I know she has mixed reception, but I highly recommend her. She is a great scholar with lots of insight and experience on Holocaust history and memory.

Professor Walke is so many things. Kind, witty, strict, insanely smart. I've heard mixed reviews, but by the end of the semester, she became one of my favorite professors, and I loved her class. She can give really boring lectures and makes you read A TON. You'll learn a lot and she really grows on you, but you have to work for your grade.

She is an incredibly harsh grader. Though the material is interesting, she's a pretty dry lecturer. She's very friendly outside of class, but it is not reflected in the course or the grading. Would not recommend.

The homework for this class was an appropriate amount of reading for the college level, but it seemed like a lot compared to high school. The lectures were a little repetitive; some were more interesting than others. The class is structured for student participation. Walke grades pretty harshly, but not too bad. I definitely learned a lot.

I took a freshman FOCUS class from her, so there weren't too expectations. Most days we would just walk in, listen to her tell us a little bit of history, and then talk about the readings we had that week. She leads really good discussions, but there is SO MUCH READING. It probably took about two hours/class. You learn to skim, but still
very interesting class - lots of reading but reading was rarely dry, great prof

I was really interest in this class coming in but it turned out to be a let down. There is SO MUCH READING, and it's all very repetitive, but you still must do it because she gives really specific pop quizzes. Her expectations of the class are ridiculous. Also, she's kind of a dry lecturer. Our TAs also had no knowledge of the subject.
Super cool professor who is very passionate about what he teaches. Grading can be tough and sometimes the material can be much more complex than you're prepared for, but overall pretty good teacher.

chill guy & easy class; can be argumentative. i agree w/ a lot of his views and was still nervous in class discussions. also, note that on the syllabus he reserves the right to mark a grade up or down when the grade does not match his assessment. i didn't attend office hours, so probably do that to avoid being marked down. improved my writing tho

I loved my time with professor O'Bryan; he was a great teacher and cared about his students. His lectures were always interesting and I absolutely loved attending class. He was extremely easy to talk to, and was extremely knowledgeable about writing. In taking this class, I know my writing got better - STRONG RECOMMEND

I started the class liking O'Bryan because he is funny, but throughout the semester things changed. He lost his patience and had a really short fuse during discussions. My class was small and cooperative, so there was no reason to get angry with us. He was also overly assertive with his political views. Not helpful for my Legal Studies minor.

Did not even grade our essays in time for the final or other essays, so feedback was useless. He was also overly critical without real reference to the rubric, which always included way more difficult content than what he would actually describe in class. One of the most difficult and time consuming classes I've taken at WashU

Pretty funny and easy to talk to. But a few things he did ticked me off: He often talked way too much, to the point where he was just dominating the conversation rather than letting us speak. He was also very disorganized. He also made a lot of assumptions about students, so I didn't often feel comfortable sharing my true views in the classroom.
Honestly, the coolest professor at Wash U. Hands down. His lectures are always dynamic and interesting. He's super down-to-earth and really cares about students.
One of my favorite professors at Wash U so far, very fair and eager to help
Pretty good Writing 1
Can be disrespectful at times Doesn't highlight class expectations thoroughly

I don't understand the Sanders hate. He is a very caring professor and as long as you communicate with him, he is understanding. His CW is quite heavy; there are lots of papers, readings, and assignments, but his class can be fun as well. I enjoyed a lot of the assigned material, and as long you stay on top of deadlines, his class is manageable!

He believes that getting 97% on an assignment is a substitute for 100%, making the rest of the grading scale skewed. Assigns a lot more work than other professors in college writing and the writing prompts are vague. However, he did give a lot of good feedback and I noticed a difference in my writing before & after taking this class.
unreasonable amount of homework

Dr. S is an empathetic professor who prioritizes understanding and participation in his classroom. He allows for lively discussions and offers amazing feedback when grading. He is one of the best College Writing professors at WashU.

Dr. Sanders is an amazing professor who clearly loves his job. He is empathetic and understanding towards students and makes a valid effort to ensure all students understand materials and expectations. He allows you to make mistakes without failing you. By far one of the best professors in the department at WashU.

He assigns more homework/ essays than other college writing professors and is a semi-tough grader. However, he cares a lot about his students and what he is teaching. He tries his best to teach his students in the most engaging way possible. Not the easiest class, but I did improve a lot as a writer.
Tons of homework(writing every week), have to write 3000 words in a week, only 3 extensions, really tough grader

Professor shows lack of empathy towards students, and at times seems as though he hates his career. Assigns unreasonable amounts of homework and papers with vague guidelines. Will need to meet with him constantly on what is actually required in order to get a half decent grade.

Sanders gets over-hated given that he teaches a mandatory writing class that everyone has to take. Sanders does give a lot of unnecessary busy work sometimes, but his class is really worthwhile if you want to become a better writer. Out of all the english classes I've ever taken, my writing has improved the most under his instruction.

Does not believe in giving 100% and getting 97% on something was the replacement for a perfect score, making the rest of the grading scale skewed. Confusing grading criteria and made it hard to understand what was expected of you. There are some fun activities and movies, however the writing prompts are often unclear and class was a lot of work.
He is a great teacher!

Prof. Mitchell is very respectful of his students and he really knows his stuff.  Lectures are insightful and crystal clear.  I plan to take another course with him.  A fairly good amount of writing throughout the semester; keep up with the weekly journals or you'll regret it at the end of the semester--believe me.
Heaven & Earth Magic: Harry Smith Anthology--Excellent course, excellent professor.  Learned so much.  He teaches the way all professors should teach.

Outstanding teacher and extremely nice man.  I came to his class knowing little about the subject but learned so much. He makes you feel that you have something worth contributing and he's very good at helping you connect your current knowledge base with the new material.

American Music: From Plymouth Rock to Rock and Roll:  Very interesting course, made us go out and actually experience the music.  Also, I've taken Music of the Beatles with him, again, marvelous.  So nice, too.

Prof. Mitchell is by far my favorite professor at Wash U. I had him for another class, and specifically took this class because I didn't want to graduate without taking another one with him. He's really laid back and makes class fun to go to.

Yeah...it sounds like an easy, blow-off class and probably could be if you wanted it to, but Mitchell was always good at challenging us to actually learn the stuff and discuss things in class.  Best class I've ever taken.
Prof. Mitchell was always available for extra help if needed and was very comfortable with his material. I LOVED his class.
Excellent professor!!!! Extremely knowledgeable, always helpful, treats students like adults and with a lot of respect. I highly recommend Dr. Mitchell!

Dr. Schindler's class on sex and literature was one of the best classes I ever took. He can go off-topic with anecdotes, but he remembers what he did cover and adjusts exams accordingly. The most difficult grading factor are the quizzes, so read the text! If you need this course for a degree requirement, you won't find a better option.

Schindler is vastly overrated. As a WashU professor, he relied far too much on the charisma"authority" of being German and politically "progressive" to charm American students.  Lectures largely anecdotal, outdated, even inaccurate,  +  his pubs record is pretty thin. He's darned lucky. Others are far more deserving of his job + he knows it.

I took German lit with him at U South Fla. No pre-reqs but you'll want experience in literary analysis to do well, Euro history a plus. Course attracts competition so keep up. Little margin for error on grades so study hard. One of the most competent professors at USF, it's worth registering just to sit in the same room as this man.
Awesome professor, completely brilliant, makes any subject interesting.

I had professor Schindler 3 times when I was a student at Washington U.  He was hands down my favorite professor at the university.  He is intelligent, a fantastic teacher, and he cares about his students.  Kudos Dr. Schindler,you deserve it.

I must wholeheartedly disagree with the previous three comments. Having said that, I have only ever taken German classes with him. In my experience, however, he has been fantastic: an absolutely hilarious guy, very easygoing and willing to help if you need it. Personally, I would enthusiastically recommend him for a German class, in any case!!!
Spent two entire classes talking about beer.  This guy is the man, he runs the department and has some funny things to say.  Take anything with him
Had Prof. Schindler for Old Europe and his Soccer Class. One of the best Prof. at Wash U. Great person.

In this class we read 3 novels over the semester, wrote 5 short papers, had a presentation, and an oral midterm. Overall courseload not too heavy and books very interesting. Very fair grader and wants students to succeed. Also funny personality.
Wonderful teacher, inspired me with regards to physics. Went in hating it. Left loving it. Physics 198 is even better.
He's a funny guy, sometimes he isn't the most clear and often makes mistakes. He'll help you if you ask.
Excellent, made me love physics so much I switched from pre-med bio major to pre-grad physics major. Really nice guy, funny, makes class interesting.
Clear explanations. Makes class fun. A must for 197.
Funny guy, enjoyable class, would take again
He's the man, definitely recommend

Professor Wagner is hilarious and loves to crack a joke. His demonstrations and example problems are clear and he goes through them step by step. He stops for questions and keeps the class entertained and awake. He even has a part of class set to disproving movie physics. He's great.
The time for the class is terrible, but he's a good enough professor for it to be worth it.
I had this guy for 197/198 and I really enjoyed his class. The tests are fair if you do the work, and he is always willing to answer questions and help students.

A really caring professor. Very accessible outside of class and committed to helping us with our essays and such. She did a good job of incorporating other things like presentations so it wasn't all just us discussing the readings of the day. Only read about the first half of most readings and was able to do very well on essays and participation.

Nina Davis is the nicest woman on campus. She cares so deeply for her students and really loves talking about Spanish literature and culture. We had readings for every day but it's easy to get by just by skimming. A few papers, a short presentation, and participation grade. Definitely recommend taking her for a relaxed Spanish course.

LOVE HER!!!! She makes 16th century Spanish literature interesting and accessible. The class has a lot of reading, but otherwise just two tests, two papers, and a presentation which are spread out and clearly explained. Class is a good mixture of lecture and discussion, and it has 20 students so you don't have to be constantly participating

Pretty straightforward class.  Most of the grade comes from homework, which is pretty easy.  The class can be boring at times, through no real fault of her own.  My only complaint is that she's American and speaks Spanish with an American accent.  I feel cheated.
Boring class...not really her fault though. She's real nice and very helpful. But the class was really really boring, which is too bad.
Her class is hard, but she's really really helpful - definitely go to her office hours and get to know her.  I really liked the class

She is a good teacher...she knows her stuff, but her class is really hard.  It made me change my mind from being a Spanish major to being a Spanish minor...but, I would still say take her class.  She is organized and tries hard at teaching.

She grades based on how much she likes you. It was the most boring class I've ever taken and probably also the hardest. She turned me from a Spanish major to a Spanish minor to making me contemplate not taking any Spanish next semester. She's horrible.
she is an awesome professor!  she is so smart, and always tried really hard to teach us, even when we weren't responsive.   Her class was rarely boring.

DONT TAKE HIS FYP. One of the most boring and tedious white old man, many micro aggression. all he did is reading slides like a robot. No HW, but tons of things in lecture. The quiz and tests are hard but I am doing OK. But in the end I receive a A- with midterm and quiz over 94%?NO GRADE BOUNDARY in syllabus, NO ONE GRADES posted in Canva.

Most of the students in his class I'm sure would love to give him a 1-star rating. I don't blame them for it - he's incredibly confusing and tries to use a "grades don't matter approach" but then grades REALLY harshly. His only redeeming quality is his genuine passion for plants - its clear he really cares about them. He's also really knowledgeable

Refuses to tell students what will be on tests (which make up most of your grade) or what to study, seems to enjoy withholding information such as the final pop quiz at the end of the course. Much more invested in his love of plants than anything related to students or teaching.

Vierstra is an interesting and fun dude, although he clearly doesn't take kindly to slackers. Lectures are a bit rambly but informational for people who actually like the content. Not much hw. Don't take him if you're looking for an easy science credit tho, tests are brutal and often on things you barely covered in class and aren't anticipating.

This course made me fall in love with plants. He knows what he's talking about but that doesn't make him a good professor. There were many topics that were barely touched on in class but were heavily seen throughout his tests. The class was very disorganized and he is rarely accessible outside class.

Tests are extremely specific on information that was barely covered in lecture or not even on slides. Lecturing is not very organized and he is very knowledgeable on the information but has no time outside of class and is not good at breaking down the information. Unclear what the tests will be on. You get free plants though.

There is no textbook for this class, so all information comes from his lectures. Words we glossed over in class were often tested over. This (as far as I know)is the only class he teaches. He's very knowledgable and passionate, but the class felt disorganized. Definitely someone you would like to know personally, but not your favorite as a teacher.
do not take

Doesn't care about his students personally. Refused to even post midterm grades. Graded every student differently, for the same answers. If you email him to ask questions, he refuses to reply. The class relies solely on lectures, for which he makes his own poorly-designed slides, with no hw or assignments. Tests extremely specific.
Giammar is a great professor that truly cares about his students. EECE 101 is a hard class but Giammar wants to help you succeed if you put in the effort. I would definitely take his classes again.
Dan does a great job of roping you in for ChemE then it gets rough but it's not Dan's fault. Dan's class has been my favorite during my time here.

Love Giammar. Super caring, passionate, and made lectures interesting. A little bit hard to get help with outside of class (office hours were very sparce and for the most part didn't work with my schedule) but was always willing to help. Very inspirational and inspired me to be an engineer!

Dan Giammar is a true gem. He cares about his students and is always happily available for questions/comments/concerns/feedback. Engineering classes are difficult but he has made this class enjoyable as well. Would recommend this class for anyone interested in water systems but also just interested in public health engineering! Very applicable

Really tough grader on extensive problem sets, but is always willing to help out. Lectures were informative and interesting and I feel better prepared to work in water treatment after taking this class.

Giammar was a really great professor this semester in tough class. For the past several years he has taught this course together with Dr. Jay Turner and Giammar is often considered to be the "easier" professor. I found his lectures to be very clear and his tests had a MUCH higher average than those of Dr. Turner.
Giammar is a fantastic lecturer. His tests are difficult, but partial credit helps you make up points. Loves discussing the curriculum in office hours. Overall a great guy and teacher!

Daniel is the most inspirational and caring professor that I have experienced. He is very helpful and cares about his students and whether or not they are learning the material. He is passionate about the work he does and is always enthusiastic.

Its hard for an intro course but it gives you a great feel for what being a chemical engineering major means. if youre in a PST and do the readings, getting an A is possible. Giammar is awesome, really cares about what he is teaching. Lectures can get a little dry but he's nothing if not very organized and methodical.

I took Drawing1 with Dennis last semester. He was pretty chill and the atmosphere was relaxed. The class focuses a lot on figure drawing and he really likes to see improvement (that is mainly what your grade is based upon). I wouldn't say that Dennis is an easy grader, but he's pretty fair. However, I must admit that I don't feel I learned a ton.

I actually had Dennis for both drawing 1 and 2. He is a super chill person and gives really nice feedback both academically and just random stuff. Although the class does get a little boring with the amount of figure drawing we did, the way he approaches figure drawing really helped and definitely made me a lot more comfortable with it.

Dennis is fairly straight forward in his drawing 1 class. A lot of figure drawing, can get fairly repetitive. It's hard to tell whether or not you are doing well in his class as he does not give you any grades. He doesn't assign too much outside work, mostly projects for the weekends that are usually still lifes. Overall, a pretty neutral class.
I loved Dennis! He was always very understanding and helpful. Critiques were always insightful.

Dennis is a very nice person! However the class is a bit boring as we did figures for WHOLE semester!!! But you can always have fun by yourselves like do some creative things with figure drawing you are doing, explore more.

DRAWING: Dennis focuses a ton on figure drawing (every other day with a model). He prefers to give little to no feedback during class, so if you're confident in your drawing abilities it's a relaxing atmosphere that's more about personal exploration, but if you're new to drawing you'll probably struggle to improve. He's empathetic and easy-going.
Great teacher. Very involved in helping students grow in their own strengths. Class is always fun and interesting. Had figure drawing one class per week for nearly the entire semester.
Great! My drawing ability improved significantly with Hyland. However, it helps if you have previous drawing experience. Most inexperienced students will get a get a B-.

The best teacher I have ever had. His class was definitely challenging but I learned so much from him. He is  incredibly supportive and you can tell that he wants his students to do well. He was very understanding when I did not turn in an assignment on time and did not give me a hard time about it at all. This guy is AMAZING.
Great professor. Clear explanation with real-world examples and organized materials. All the cases help you understand the concepts well. Responsive to any questions you have. Recommend!!

Strongly recommended. His finance class is awesome, and he does care about students. He is very smart but patient to answer your "stupid" question. The case study is really hard but it helps you understand the materials.
Gormley is the man! The best teacher I've had at Wash U hands down. I have never seen or heard of a teacher care about his students the way Gormley takes care of them.
The man is a great teacher and will go on to much bigger things. Very smart! Very organized! It was a privilage to learn from him.

Great professor. He is very clear and easy to listen to. His examples and lectures are very helpful (I opened my book maybe once all semester). In general, he is also just a very approachable guy, which helps.
Good teacher.  No need to buy the text book.

Gormley or Godley? This guy is by far the best professor i've had at this school in the past four years.  He makes a boring subject come to life.  I've never seen a professor care about his students as much as Gormley, everyone loves the guy and waits for him to be teaching the class now.
Gormley is awesome! Class is not hard and he explains everything really well. And he's like nerdy-funny, which works real well.

Best professor I've ever had. E-mailed me back numerous times after midnight the night before tests and projects. Very friendly, reasonable. Very good at explaining things. Very thorough. Awesome! Go to class and STUDY THE PRACTICE EXAMS! Its finance, so it won't always be fun, but he makes it as good as it can be. You learn a ton! Great!

I really wanted to give them the benefit of the doubt, but things just kept getting worse. I have taken other courses (required) with content I was hoping/expecting they would be more knowledgeable in. However, they did not improve in their performance as a professor. They could not explain any core EECE concepts. Don't recommend :(

Extremely disorganized lectures. Unable to explain any ideas in class, making everyone confused. The problems on recitation and quizzes never reflect what they were taught during the lecture. Be prepared to teach yourself as you might be more knowledgeable than Dr. Wyckoff. This professor is definitely the worst of all in the EECE department.

I understand she came into WashU right before the semester started, but she still could have been better prepared. I seriously don't know how she got hired. The class was a mess. She didn't even know the material and couldn't read her notes or answer students' questions, which is the number 1 responsibility of a prof. AVOID AT ALL COST!

Worse professor I have ever had in my academic career and that is not an exaggeration. I'm not exactly sure how she got hired by EECE department at Washu, as she does not perform nearly as well as the other educators. She is scattered and all over the place. Her teaching style shows she does not care about the students learning. Self Taught class.

She is very nice and we understand this is her first semester at WashU, but this class was kind of a mess. She copies lecture notes incorrectly from the textbook and is not qualified to answer questions from students. She says attendance to recitation is optional, but they count towards your grade. She could improve by knowing the material better.
Takes off points for everything and anything on quizzes. If you love partial credit DO NOT TAKE.
Shes nice but simply does not know the material.
Better off reading the textbook on your own.

This is her first semester at WashU from teaching at UT and our class is severely underwhelmed. Imagine a professor whose teaching style is copying/reading things incorrectly from the textbook, and when you question a method or equation, getting sassed back or denied an answer. Not very available outside of class.

Surprised that I ended up with an A in the class because of how much I struggled through it.  Going to class is not representative of the content on the exams and honestly just confused me more.  Supplement class with Khan academy for sure.  Super super nice guy though.

Rim is a nice person and an average lecturer. He answers questions thoroughly but sometimes spends too much time doing so, to the point that he leaves out or glosses over critical concepts which end up appearing on the exams. He altered the homework multiple times because there were mistakes or he had forgotten to cover the material.

I do not understand why Prof Rim has been rated so badly. In my opinion, he is a great professor and it is easy to earn a good score in his class. His notes is clear and he tries his best to help students.

Attend lectures, do the homework and you should do well. Lectures follow the textbook so you can get away with skipping lectures, but I wouldn't recommend it because he talks about homework and we got off track from the syllabus schedule. Don't be too stressed if your grade is lower than expected because he curves tests at the end of the year.

Dr. Rim is great! I went to his office hour a couple of times and he is super helpful. After he knows I never went to his class, he did not blame me or complain about it and suggested I read the textbook.

Beginning of the semester was a little rough, lectures could've maybe been planned/organized better. There wasn't too much feedback on work, grading was a little slow. With that said, Rim does care about his students and wants them to be successful. Overall content wasn't too bad, sometimes tests were tricky but partial credit was provided.

Rim is uniquely humorous and caring. Though he usually teaches graduate students, he did adjust his way of teaching throughout the semester, and inspired students to seek knowledge instead of merely achieving good grades.

Restating what someone else said but lectures were definitely super theoretical and often confusing, and he had the habit of going over a lot of proofy stuff and explaining things in an unnecessarily convoluted way. My advice is to learn primarily from the textbook and go to lecture/OH for to review what you read.
His lectures were not helpful AT ALL. A lot of what he explains is super theoretical and is like you are reading a more confusing version of the textbook.

Huo is a mixed bag. His lectures were confusing and straight out of the book. I would strongly recommend reading the textbook before coming to lecture so you won't be as confused. His tests were very difficult (and tedious as far as the algebra went) and the average was about 70, but he curved to an 85, so it wasn't that hard to get an A.

He doesn't teach well. Straight from the textbook. Difficult to follow and understand. Unclear grading criteria. More than once, he switched a policy midway through the semester making things harder for students (changing test format and removing allowed materials).

Very hard exams that focus a lot on conceptual content (so you have to really actually understand the material - not just plug and chug) and lectures derived directly from the book (so class attendance is at maybe a 40% at best). HOWEVER, seems like an extremely nice guy who wants everyone to do well and so far VERY generous with the curve.

Do NOT take matrix with Huo. You're better off waiting to take it w/ Shapiro. Our "practice exams" are Shapiro's old exams but then Huo makes his tests more difficult/conceptual & no notecards. I keep acing the practice exams and failing the real exams, which makes me think I'd be acing matrix with Shapiro instead of being in the C+/B- range w Huo.

Not a great professor. Accent can be hard to understand at time. His lectures are way too abstract/theoretical and delve into proofs more than we need to pass the exams. Does not do enough example problems. I learned infinitely more from WebWork, Crowdmark, and other online tools than I did from him.
The lectures are just so boring. Its hard to pay attention to so you often find yourself just skipping class because you wont get anything out of it

He's perfectly competent but lectures are dense & a bit boring. I felt confident in my math skills going into Exam 1 but did poorly because the test focused a lot on concepts/rules. Engineering students tend to prefer doing actual math/problem-solving questions rather than just memorizing "rules" and ideas. I'd recommend taking with another prof.

While Professor Huo was at times not the best lecturer, he really cares about his students and was always extremely helpful in office hours. He was always ready and willing to clear things up outside of class and was great 1-on-1. The HW was manageable and the tests were written in such a way that I always felt prepared. 10/10 would recommend.

I think he really cared about teaching and took it very seriously, and this showed. I enjoyed the class, and thought he took it at a nice pace. He carefully explained everything and followed the textbook enough so that you could read it over later to get an idea of something you missed. HW was also very fair and manageable.

Wigginton will make you a better writer and he's always willing to help out! Pro tip- finish your drafts early and ask for his feedback. If you apply his suggestions, you should do well. Also, talk in class! Literally no one does and it gets awkward. He likes it better if it's not just him doing all the talking.

I am very bad at writing and I have tons of grammar mistakes in my every essay. In the end, I am so grateful for Professor Wigginton. He has definitely taught me a lot and he is just so nice and available all the time.
Love him!!! great class, really improved my writing
Wiggy is the bomb-dot-com. His feedback and sense of humor made my day. He's a hard grader but if you try, it will pay off. Suavy Bobby for life
Wigginton really teaches you how to write. Tough class but definitely worth it - you will get an A if you submit your drafts to him before the due date and incorporate his comments.

I really learned how to write through this class. He gives great feedback and is always there to help improve students' writing. I could tell that he has a lot of experience with writing and takes writing very seriously. There's a lot to learn.
i can tell that he puts a lot of effots into comments. his classes are often fun!
Incredibly rude to students. Unclear expectations. Very opinionated. Has a huge ego. Very un-engaging classes, nobody really pays attention and he just rants on and on.  Avoid him.
Super nice guy but his lectures could be pretty boring at times. He clearly cared about the students.

Carl was pretty cool overall. Sometimes lectures were hard to follow and the relevance of material wasn't always clear to me, but the class generally wasn't bad. Carl was always really available and willing to help. He wanted students to succeed. The class isn't super easy but if you put in work you will get a good grade.

Carl Sanders is a great teacher and knows every one of his students. His tests were not hard. I studied a lot, but didn't go to every class and still did really well in the class. Once a week quizzes affect your grade so you will have to go to class at least for those.
He is not a tough grader, but you need to be in class to understand the powerpoints.
Great guy, pretty good lecturer, really helpful in office hours.  Helped jump-start an interest in micro. and game theory.  Textbook was useless, just go to class and take good notes.
Great guy and an even better Economics professor. Explained all material clearly and while he seemed kind of nervous in the beginning of the semester, he got much better while teaching this class.
Sanders was amazing! Not only did he take time to meet each student in the class personally, he made econometrics manageable and explained things clearly and precisely. Great prof
no confidence in teaching ability; makes easy concepts seem really difficult; in retrospect not a hard class but it seems that way because he's not a very good teacher

Intro to Pilates with Susan is one of the best classes I've taken. She makes class super fun and is super understanding of all circumstances. Whenever I couldn't make it to class, she proposed alternatives and when I wasn't feeling super energetic, she would provide an easier version of the exercise. She is simply the best!

Prof. Volkan is one of the sweetest, most nurturing professors I've had at WU. She's there for her students outside of class and makes an effort to get to know her students and form long lasting relationships. (I had her 4 years ago and we still talk and visit all the time!) Her classes are taught in a creative and challenging way. Take her class!
Professor Volkam is the best teacher i've had at wash u...you must take this course. she's the sweetest woman alive!!! AHH I LOVE HER!
LOVE LOVE LOVE LOVE LOVE. period.
She is the most engaging, most interested, and most fun professor I have had yet at Wash U.  Take this class, you will learn SO much and have fun in the meanwhile.
I absolutely loved this class, probably my favorite.  She is so sweet and makes class something that you look forward to attending.  You should take this class with her if you get the chance.

My favorite class. Even if you have never taken an acting class before, I highly recommend taking one with her. While it was a 2 hour class, we always wanted it to last much longer. It was tons of fun. You bond with the class and learn a great deal about yourself. Take it if you get the chance.

I loved this class. I have no acting ability whatsoever, but she always finds something nice to say. She's a little out there, but so sweet and so much fun...i'd recommend the class to anyone. There are some assignments but as long as you do them, you will get an A.

It is clear he hates teaching this course and 1Ls in general. He will only teach the dormant commerce clause (which may be useful once in your career if you're lucky). All other constitutional law must be learned whilst in a panic during bar preparation. Someone do this man (and his students) a favor and don't make him teach this class anymore.

Very very difficult. i didn't understand a word that was said this whole class. i could tell that he was very smart and it seemed like a few of the students really were into it. i've heard he is more invested and approachable for his clinic and upper level courses. he seemed like a great teacher but i couldn't figure out what he was talking about.
Idk who the other people are, but nobody I asked thought he was a good teacher.  Heard he is more passionate about his clinic and upper level courses though.

This guy is, I promise you, one of the best professors you will ever have in your life. Class is hard and he has high standards, but law school is graded on a curve so it likely wont mean you will get a bad grade. He is very close to the stereotypical "Paper Chase" professor, but in a good way. He has high standards, but knows his stuff. AWESOME.

BLP. Man. This guy is a machine. Thank god he lets you bring a laptop - class is a roller coaster ride. Buckle in and go. Funny. Seldom varies his outfit. Has argued in front of the SC. Also, as some other commenters mentioned - he WANTS you to disagree with him - just back it up! It's cheesy, but I feel kinda privileged to have sat in his class.
No Comments
Law School was awful, but this guy was good!

Unpop Prof but I liked him. Prof's manner was droning but he was fair and welcomed alternative viewpoints. His joy was for someone to question + even disagree with him. Underneath his gruffness he had a sincere desire to teach a subject he loved. If given the chance, I'd take him again. But due to his volume I would not sit in the front row again.
Kind of monotonous lecturing and not that cohesive but she tried her best to keep everyone engaged.

A lot (!!!) of little assignments and grading could be harsh, but it was all pretty straightforward and lots of drops. I didn't do great on all the assignments but still got an A+. Baitzel's lectures weren't super engaging and I didn't usually go (most people didn't) but she is really nice and caring. Content was usually pretty interesting.

Professor Baitzel is friendly and passionate about archaeology. Each lecture has associated readings and a short quiz, and each week of material has a short discussion assignment. There are 4 reflection papers, 3 "lab" assignments, and a final project. Baitzel is great at posting everything on Canvas and keeping it organized.

I don't know how this professor sets up the grading rubric. This class is not beginner friendly, she would take points off for going over a 300-word limit for a discussion response that asks to list and explain more than 6 factors clearly. This class is definitely not fun. Take the other two intro courses if you can.

Took ANTH 3095 01 last semester and ANTH 190B this spring. Professor Baitzel's classes usually have heavy workload but if you follow the lectures everything will work out just fine. There are several papers and projects but they are not hard. Also she is very very caring and she offered me several accommodations. Nice and sweet prof!
I loved Prof Baitzel for Into to Arch, shes incredibly kind and genuine and really has a passion for her work and research and is caring towards her students.

She's nice and not monotone or boring to listen to, but her lectures were quite disorganized (especially the slideshows themselves), and quizzes, discussion posts, and reflection papers were often confusing to follow. Discussion posts had many requirements but a very tight word limit, which was a bit unreasonable. Quizzes were often convoluted.

Dr. Baitzel is relatively new and not a great teacher yet. However, she cares about her students and is great about receiving feedback! This class was fascinating, but hard. The quizzes are very hard, harshly graded, and the class moves extremely quickly. Only take if you are very interested in osteology or have prior experience.

Professor Abraham is a good professor. He is rather lecture-heavy but his tests and papers are doable. I ended with an A in the class and I never felt as though I was working super hard to get it. If there is one thing to note about him, it is that he PRIORITIZES ATTENDANCE. Don't skip class. If you are taking a religion class, take him.

Professor Abraham just recently transferred to GMU and I took his Religion 100 class this semester. Overall, he's a good professor but his lectures can get boring. There is a quiz due every other class day but you can take them up to 10 times and they're based off of the readings.  He always encourages us to go to his office hours (cause he's new.)
Very interesting lectures and super helpful. Great professor and really understands the material. Keeps everyone engaged and interested
Tough grader, Boring lectures, Always takes the same side.

The best professor I've had at Wash U, I can't recommend him enough. Genuinely interested in student opinions, makes complex material fun and easy to understand. Highly available and helpful outside of class. If you're considering taking a class with him, do it.
Abraham was a pretty funny guy. Class was a little dry at times, but the quizes/tests/papers are all ridiculously easy.

Easily the best professor I've had at Wash U. An all around great guy, he makes discussions fun and engaging. If you're considering taking a class with him, do it. You'll be glad you did. (And ask him about his feelings on Marvel v. DC :))

FANTASTIC professor!  Engaging lectures, interested in student views, even if they contradict his own, and stimulates great class discussion.  Do the readings and come prepared, but it's absolutely worth it for the best class I've had at WashU.  Also really cares about his students and is more than willing to help if you need it.  Great guy!

Great professor---gave us tons tons tons of practice problems so you would have all you need to be prepared for the exam. Also was very patient and helpful in his office hours, even if you had made stupid mistakes like unit conversion errors!   Just a great person in general!

The class was well taught. He gave good notes and assigned relevant homework that helped for the exam. His homeworks were not very easy and it's good to work in groups. His exams were relatively easy if you understand the homework.
Good professor.

Taber was such a great professor! he was very helpful outside of class, and contrary to what others said, i found him very easy to understand. great course... the other ratings are so off!  he was one of the best professors i had at washu
gives great notes!!!  despite all these other bad comments, i really like him!  and i know several other people who think he's a great professor too!
Hard Class.  Hard Professor.  Awful.  Take the other professor for BME 240 if you can!
Horrible teaching style, how can i be interested in something your so obvioulsy not interested in?
Tests were much harder than expected.

Like others said, this class is much more about the environment than about psych. Fitz seems to use the course as a soapbox for his environmental views. However, the material was still mostly interesting and the class was pretty easy as long as you stay on top of the readings/assignments.
Professor Fitz is a wonderful teacher and cares a lot about his students. His grading is straightforward and extremely fair. it is easy to do well in his class and really interesting.

Professor Fitz is rational, fair, encouraging and a very honest person. Agreed that those who don't like him don't understand a lot about the environment and issues going on today. he is one of the most sensible teachers on campus. go into his class with an open mind and you will learn a lot.

I found Dr. Fitz to be extremely interesting and helpful and I feel like the only students who had a problem in his class are those with a bias against environmentalist or liberals in general. His integration of current studies and psychological issues made the class that much more engaging.
Crazy environmentalist. We were actually tested on environmental facts. Rambles and is not very interesting.

The man is out of his mind. It's not a social psychology class, it's a "Don Fitz explains his conspiracy theories for half an hour" class.  If you want to learn about Social Psych, buy the book independently and don't take the class. Of course, Fitz doesn't agree with the book most of the time.

The class requires nothing but regurgitation. You simply need to memorize what is said in class and in the book. Any interpretation should be avoided as it will only lead to you not regurgitating properly. A few questions on each test are very trivial. In short, take this if you don't want to learn anything but simply want a good grade.

Fitz gives 6 exams, 25 m-c questions each, but you get to drop 2 exams, and have the option of skipping the final, which is just questions taken from the 5 previous tests. His notes are cryptic, so go to class or copy a friend, because his tests often nitpick things mentioned in class. Social psych is mostly common sense, so beware boredom

absolutely love diaz as a person. super chill and genuinely seems caring. however, the class is an absolute joke. definitely the easiest class i have taken in my life, to the point where it felt like a waste of time. the class was so superficial that i feel like i didnt really learn anything. beware if you are actually interested in diffeq

Professor Diaz is one of the best professors in WashU math department. He gives clear lectures and cares his students. The practice exams are helpful as they are just mirrors of the real exams, so an A is super easy in his class. I feel this course (differential equations) is really helpful for my career development as it's useful in engineering.
Loved professor Diaz

From what I've heard about other Differential Equations teachers, Diaz is definitely the way to go. You can ace all the exams except the final (the final was very difficult). However, since the final counts for 30% of your grade and is MC with 5pt questions, you NEED to work hard for it and be perfect. Practice exams tell you what you need to know.
Pretty chill. Content is hard, but he will tell you exactly what you need to know. The practice tests will be exactly the same as the real test, but with the numbers changed.

Humberto's DiffEq class is an ideal class. He tells you exactly what you need to know, and makes hard concepts easy. Do the practice exams. Humberto Diaz is a professional mathematics teacher; not only will you learn Differential Equations, you will feel better about your math skills. Very accessible. TAKE HIM FOR DIFF EQ!!!!!

Diaz is a clear lecturer, teaches concepts well and outlines what you need to know. He devotes lectures before midterms to test review. Extremely easy tests; If you knew how to do the practice test, you were set as the real deal is essentially a carbon copy. Crowdmark homeworks were challenging and sometimes unrelated to lecture topics, however.
Gives clear lectures. There could be fewer homework problems, though.

Miles is a God. Best lecturer I hae ever had. He gives an outline out at the beginning of each class that he follows very closely and it makes it super easy to take notes.  I didn't go to every class, but you really should go to all of them.
Awesome prof. A little nerdy, but loves the stuff and makes obscure Chinese history interesting.  Love the layout of his class: no tests.
Very well organized, providing the class with an outline each day with important terms on the back. Knew his subject matter very well. Funny guy that really seems to know how to relate to students.
Lectures were excellent and organized, and I learned a lot more in an intro class than I expected. The TA graded all of our essays and tests, so he couldn't help with some things outside of class.
Very clear and well organized lectures.  Incredibly receptive of students' questions.  A great teacher.
Very clear lectures, well organized. His class is informative, and his assigned readings are very interesting primary and secondary sources.
Very available to students and open to suggestions.  Very clear lectures, though still working on teaching to Wash U students (by his own admission). I'd recommend his classes.
English is not professor Chang's native language, but he did great job to handle with the English teaching. He was a very nice person to help students on any questions.

He is a wonderful and knowledgeable teacher. He always concerns about our grade and encouraged us to study hard and get a better grade. In his teaching, he often used examples from western cultures to explain the Asian art issues.

He is a wonderful and knowledgeable teacher. He is very nice to give us 4 exams and drops the lowest one. He always concerns about our grade and encouraged us to study hard and get a better grade. In his teaching, he often used examples from western cultures to explain the Asian art issues.
very organized class, and wonderful professor.
Each class was very organized and I believe that I have learned a lot about the Asian history, culture and art, this is one of the best professor that I have had at the university.
Professor Qing, is not a great professor!
Their is nothing positive to say! About Professor Qing!

Dr. O'Leary is wonderful in her smaller classes! She makes the class a very welcoming environment and really encourages people to participate as much as possible. She actively ensures that each person's comments are validated and treated as equally important to others, ensuring that no one gets lost under a few loud voices.

Professor O'leary really cares about students. The class was kind of difficult, but I went to see her at office hours which helped so much. She really makes subjects that are hard to wrap your head around interesting and meaningful. Highly recommend.

Prof. O'Leary is great.  In our small class our discussions were like a good book club. You have to participate, but you're not at all scrutinized.  I have been in other small classes and hesitated to talk, but she created a really easy-going, non-judgmental, friendly environment. She let us modify assignments to make them more valuable to us.

Prof. O'Leary cares a lot about her students and how they learn best. It's her first time teaching this, and she's very responsive to criticism, making many changes to please students. When expectations aren't clear, she's happy to clarify and help. I've never seen a teacher be so accommodating and open to students' requests.

Prof. O'Leary shouldn't be allowed to teach students who care about their grade. I have never had a more aggressive professor, one who is spiteful, vindictive, and openly hostile to student requests to have fewer in class discussions and more lectures, since her exams are lecture-based. Can't know what's worth noting in the reading without lecture.

Expects students to be able to read her mind. Runs a 120 person class through small group discussions, but never brings it all together. When asked to lecture more, she gave death by Powerpoint and clearly didn't care or try. Very petty and self-centered.

She cares a lot about making sure we know why the assignments and readings are important to learning the material and there is a lot of flexibility in talking about how it's tied in with our lives, so discussion is more organic.

Prof. Mukai is very nice and accessible if you have any questions, but his lecture is quite boring and basically copying notes. He has a lot of notes and he teaches really fast. I would say this is the hardest class I have taken in college. Even if it says only Math 4111 is required, you should take topology and linear algebra before.

Really surprised to see people give Mukai such great ratings. His lectures are not clear at all and if you're new to differential equations you will need help to understand his textbook. If you don't know Matlab you also have your work cut out for you. Get a tutor and you might be able to work your way through this class
Great teacher! Knows his stuff, and he is a nice guy.
One of the best professors you will ever have! Extremely clear and knowledgable in his field and his text book is amazing!
Keep up with work!

Don't let anyone else teach you Signal and Systems. Mukai is the only one who knows how to do it well. It'll feel like you're not learning anything in this class at first, but by the time the final comes around, everything starts making sense. Daily quizzes so you have to show up to class, but sadly most people leave after this 3 min quiz.
Dr. Mukai is one of the best teachers at Wash U!!!
not helpful

She talks to the board, so it is hard to hear and pay attention...very hard to stay awake and focused in class. The labs are difficult and become increasingly more so as the term progresses. They also contain much more complex coding than Matlab classes usually require, which I suspect is due to her CS background, although she is teaching engineers

After the first week I stopped bringing my laptop because I was getting distracted.  Even without a laptop, I would forget she was talking, she was SO quiet.  She knows her stuff if you go to office hours, but she makes an awesome topic SO boring!

Horrible teacher...does not understand matlab herself and so cannot possibly teach us and does not even try. Pointless to go to class. Labs are hard but working with other people helps. TA's are overworked and never have time to get to you

I used to think it was just students that fell asleep in class, but during this class there were several occasions when she looked about to collapse to the floor for a snooze.  Disinterested, makes frequenct mistakes, and writes in a language closely resembling Arabic, as far as I can judge.  Oh, and one last thing.  This class is boring as hell.
She's really hard, but cool.  And oh soooo hot!
doesn't seem interested in teaching the class so she puts us all to sleep- no point in going to class

I really enjoyed Dr. Bayazit's class; he demonstrated a passion for the material.  His lecturing style emphasized explaining through examples.  I looked forward to his lectures and had fun with the projects.  (It looks like the Machine Learning course has since been renumbered to 517A.)

Bayazit does not understand how to be a professor.  He uses no reference text (unless you count "Google").  He has no organization to lectures and he does not give time for people to comprehend his lectures or the subject matter.  New lab every 2 days without subject being covered amply.  Four Words: Do Work Study Instead.
Lectures completely unclear/unstructured.  Barely knew the subject.  Covered obscure topics instead of core material.  For any course, I suggest waiting for a different professor.
pretty funny guy, and it was worth attending lecture for the first half of the semester... then everything got kinda unstructured

His lectures are useless, and I rarely attended, however the labs and homeworks are actually pretty good, and I always learned the material well after completing them. I would not recomend this class though unless you like to learn things on your own.

I sleep through his class and I don't feel bad at all. I read book but after the lecture it doesn't make sense. It seems like he is teaching himself and does his rough work on the board. It also seems like he just enjoys himself during the lecture and turns around and realises that there are students also here.

Dr. Bayazit is awful. I respect him as a person, but he is a rotten lectuerer. His poor grasp of the English language (he is Turkish) doesn't help, but he does not know what he is doing; he has to be corrected constantly in class. It is definitely worth the trouble to try to arrange some other professor to teach Comp. Architecture.
Amazing professor! Super engaging, assignments are all relatively easy and meaningful. Office hours are always interesting and she seems like she really wants to get to know you!
The professor was great!  extremely organized and cares!  She attempts to learn everyone's name and is truly engaging!
great professor, engaging and very nice!
Really engaging and caring professor

Treitel is very passionate about what she does and is pretty clear in class. She loves to bring art into her lectures and does a pretty good job on making clear what is looking for on test. Overall a good professor!
Keeps the class engaged and interested about the topic at hand.  Extremely approachable.

She is very friendly and her lectures are generally interesting. In her World War I class, she only spent a little time on the actual military aspect of the war and WAY too much time on the home front. A lot of the readings get repeditive and seem unnecessary (nothing you couldn't figure out with common sense). A completely average professor.
Not my professor, but he is my dad, and he's a pretty good dad
Interesting, funny dude. Class isn't that easy--you get out what you put in.
Tough course. Hard tests - tough grader. Great guy, though. Went to HLS.
he was horrible. Its unclear wats on the tests. he doesnt have office hours. The grading is very subjetive. and he plays favorites. The class was 3 hours long and it felt it!
It's 3 Hours Long, But Very Interesting...I really enjoyed it.
No Comments
great course, great adjunct.  adjuncts at wash u are often better than the profs on staff!
Nothing much to say here. Nesse is a very caring professor and gives great feedback. Class isn't too hard, but I learned a lot.
GREAT professor. Very funny, gives interesting lectures, although they definitely drag a little at times. He really cares about his students' success.
Love Erik ! He gives you really good advice and he takes different opinions very well, and he makes classes very enjoyable!

He's a really nice and fun person, and genuinely cares about students. Not an easy class though! He makes you work for the grade - lots of reading, homework, and essays, not to mention quizzes and tests. A bit too much reading and not enough focus on the actual grammar/vocab in my opinion. He doesn't post slides online so go to class.
You can tell Nesse loves what he is doing because he is always very energetic and engaging in class. If you are deciding between professors, choose him.
He was a hilarious and caring teacher that made lectures enjoyable.

He's so sweet and he gets really into what he's teaching. Would definitely take again!! For the essays, make sure you read over them and you're guaranteed a better grade if you go to meet with him before you turn it in.

Professor Bloomquist is a very nice person, and she genuinely does want to help you in class. However, grading was often harsh and inconsistent. Other college writing professors are much more lax, which will get frustrating. Essay prompts were confusing. It could've been just me, but sometimes she talked to us like we were children.

She is nice and very enthusiastic about writing. Despite that, she gives a lot of unnecessary work and grades harshly. I heard that she often caps people at a 90 and slowly raises grades over time, but personally I felt like some of my better essays were severely undergraded. She also can be a bit condescending at times. Not an easy A class.

Dr. Bloomquist is a great professor that really cares about your improvement as a writer and will be open to helping you if you seek help. Sometimes the grading seemed inconsistent, but she was always open to talking about past work.

I quite like Bloomquist, both as a professor and as a person. A couple of caveats:
1. Class is quite homework heavy
2. Grading can be frustratingly inconsistent/marks can seem arbitrary (i personally felt some of my better works were underscored and my poorer ones were overscored)
3. Sometimes exercises/assignments can seem elementary/unnecessary

Bloomquist is probably one of the most enthusiastic and caring professors at WashU. If you want help, she will provide it. She requires you meet with her for 15 minutes, 3 times, and it's really helpful. My only problem with the class is some of the small homework assignments and activities in class seem like a waste of time. Amazing professor.

Dr. Bloomquist is an incredibly nice person who is always willing to help, and puts a lot of time and effort into giving students feedback. However, she was disappointing for a college level writing class. Zoom could have been a barrier, but the way she spoke to us felt like I was back in middle school. I think my writing got worse.
I thought he is really experienced at giving lectures, and he is young so he is gonna improve, quizzes were a little difficult but you are going to do good in the class

He goes too fast for most people in lectures. I don't know if he really knows what he is talking about. hes nice and you can tell he cares but the class is too large for any meaningful relationship with him. easy class though. its just ap psych
His lectures are great! He uses a lot of vivid examples and writes everything super clear on his powerpoints. I really got interested in Psychology because of is class.

Very earnest and tries to make the class feel relatable and fun. His slides were very wordy but I was able to keep up, it helps if you've read the textbook so you know what he's talking about beforehand. I felt like he was in the middle of the three intro psych professors.

He's good at lecturing, albeit a bit fast-paced. He rewords material in a way that is more comprehensible and tries to explain concepts. As his specialty is learning, he (usually) knows what techniques to use during lecture to maximize our learning. Quizzes were quite difficult, but he cares about students. Slides contained salient information.

Colvett is clearly passionate about psychology, but his lectures are EXTREMELY fast-paced and his slides are very word-heavy. Even when typing the information during lecture, it is nearly impossible to get all of the key concepts down. His quizzes are pretty difficult, too. This aside, he is very accessible outside of class and is easy to talk to.

Honestly she is such a nice person. She cares a lot about the material and is funny. I am so bad at writing and she still got me through. I would recommend for anyone. Maybe not to fulfill ur writing intensive credit. You should probably go somewhere easier. She is still super cool though.

Very straightforward class but she makes sure you know your translations after class. She is also really nice to talk to outside of class and has a clear passion for Classics. Just study the translations and the grammar and you should get a high grade. She also gives lots of extra credit on the Cursus Honorum. No surprises

Professor Wilson was a good enough professor. The homework is typical for a 300 level latin class. The classes were also fine. My problem lies with the professor's demeanor. She rarely encourages her students and makes judgemental critiques during translations. I did learn a lot, but I regularly felt beaten down after class.
Prof. Wilson is very nice and encouraging. She brings snacks on exam days. Her grading is not as strict as I expect. The weekly quizzes for Latin 3181 are not bad.

Loved her class. You can tell that she really enjoys the material and tries to make it interesting for students. She listens to students to try to improve the course for them. Quizzes aren't bad if you study, and the homework is only graded as participation.

Prof. Wilson is amazing! She's pretty chill when it comes to admin stuff, but she's clearly serious about the material and loves discussing what she's covering through basically any lens you choose. She brought candy in for Halloween and a Trader Joe's bag full of goodies for the final. Get ready for a lot of small classes and group discussion.
Dr Wessel is an engaging and knowledgeable lecturer. He is deeply knowledgeable and interested in the material and it shows. I could not recommend Dr Wessel more.
Professor Wessel is great. Nice guy, and his lectures are funny and interesting. The class is pretty hard, but overall it's worth.

Wessel is an incredible teacher who is highly knowledgeable in his field. His was one of the best classes I've ever taken at WashU. He's also really accessible and kind; he definitely cares about his students.
Professor Wessel is so nice!!! I regret only finding him out in my senior year. I would definitely take more of his classes if I could.

Hands down the best lecturer I've ever seen. The exams require you to understand conceptual material like the back of your hand, but he designs the tests to fairly represent material covered in lecture. No tricks or gimmicks.   The homework is also pretty fun, actually.
The exams can be rather strange, but I've never seen a better lecturer.

I honestly think the course was fine, and that the only big red flags for Dr. Keadle was that she wasn't as accessible as she should've been for such an information-packed course. You don't have to neglect other classes to study for tests as much as in Orgo, but be prepared to spend time with the material. She's decently nice, but maybe a bit dry.

Quizzes and practicals seem to be graded based on her mood at the time.. Lab is super unorganized; basically just gives you a bunch of paper and you learn by yourself, which is fine, but she doesn't like to go over the answers on the practicals and made us quite uncomfortable about asking any questions.
