Too much material all at once. If youre a pre-med, please dont take this class with the rest of your course load. The averages this year for each practical were below previous years, and she didnt curve it at all. The previous years received curves. She mentions curves, but never follows through with it. My sGPA got tanked worse than orgo.

Frequently dismisses students concerns with mental health/difficulty issues. If you're planning on taking Vert Structure Lab with her be prepared to memorize 500 things for a 40 question test every 2-3 weeks. When the class average for a test was a D she pretty much said "well sucks for you all"

I don't review professors because I don't get close enough with them to feel able to but Dr. K was different. The class is hard by nature, she, however, is very fair and cares that you learn. She expects engagement in the class and if you get to know her she is such a kind-hearted, southern woman wanting the best for her students.

The textbook definition of "tough but fair." Dr. Keadle is an excellent professor and is always willing to go the extra mile for her students.  If you consistently show up to lab and show an actual interest in learning, this will be one of the best courses you take at WashU.
super interesting class minus political correctness!!

Class consists of writing seven 3-5 page memos and taking two exams which are basically memorizing information; it's a pretty easy class.  Sened occasionally gives too much detail, mumbles, or talks exclusively to his TA, but he has so much firsthand experience and knowledge on the subject that if you have any interest, you should take it.

No idea what he's talking about much of the time, but he's very easy and nice to students. Lectures get sidetracked by tangents or absurdly technical details, but he's brilliant and entertaining and willing to help.

Sened has the rare combination of Academic expertise and first hand experience in Israeli politics. The material can be bit dense, but he compensates for it with leniency in grading. Overall, I really enjoyed the course and would highly recommend it.

Gives intricate and hard to understand lectures sometimes, but likes when people come in to ask questions to clarify.  You just have to be pushy about making sure you understand what he's saying.  Very devoted to mentorship of students!
Boring teacher, but will give you a B+ just for showing up, and the final is just the homework reprinted.
Very bad teacher...teach wrong thing...you will learn nothing.
Horrible teacher. I haven't learned anything and sit through class wishing I was somewhere else.
I agree with all the comments above. Avoid him like the plague.
AVOID HIM; SHLD HAVE RETIRED.
I concur with the first rating.  This professor is without a doubt the worst professor I have ever had.

He teaches the exact same material (same final project, and exact same homework assignments) for both of his classes. His lectures are useless, yet attendance is necessary because of all of the random handouts that he gives that may or may not end up being on the final. Mostly everything that he professes is outdated and wrong. I learned nothing.

The CSE523, System Security, is a wonderful course. Prof. Crowley provided a lot of interesting  in-class-studios and homework assignment for this course.  They are well-documented and with a lot of help, if you read the textbook before getting your hand dirty. He encourages you to focus on to work instead of only listen to his lecture.

The class should be an interesting subject but the teaching is horrible. He just repeat what we have read in the textbook. If you only take notes on class you will have no idea about homeworks or labs. Yes, we are supposed to read the textbook, but if the lectures do not help at all, what's the reason of going to class whatsoever?

He assigns lots of reading for general purpose.  The Readings will put you behind.  He frequently rambles in class and thinks he's funny.  His grading disproportionately favors homework assignments more than the class project which you will put most of your time on.  Only reason to come to class is to note errors on his slides which he doesn't fix.
You read the textbook...and then you go to class and see the textbook again on PowerPoint. BORING.

In a class that had a lot of potential, Prof. Crowley himself was more of a disappointment.  His lectures dragged on and were hard to take notes on, as he did not release his slides until after class.  The homeworks and labs ranged from really easy to almost insurmountably hard.  Midterm was fair, though.
Handsome guy, great teacher.

She's kind of wacky? There's a reading every class and a one page response for every reading in addition to the readings from the textbook. Everyone has to present on one of the readings and also do a media update presentation. There's four "quizzes" which are really like 30 question tests, and four essays in addition to the final research essay

This class is basically a joke, it is so easy. However, you do learn a lot about current events like health care and legal statuses and regulations. It isn't a hard class at all, if you need an easy 5th class- you literally just found it.
Easy, Easy, Easy.  Take one of her classes for an easy A.
No Comments
No Comments

Gender Roles - Professor Lindsey is generally nice and helpful, but be prepared to sit in the classroom shaking your head & thinking, "What the #$^??"  Talks about female stereotypes w/technology but still can't seem to work the A/V equipment w/out the male TA's help.  Interesting topics, though.
Prof Elfenbein leads discussions well and is an engaging speaker. It's apparent that she's passionate about the topics she teaches and wants her students to grow as leaders.

Hillary leads a class on a topic that is meant to take people out of their comfort zone. This class forced me (a passive by nature) to come to better learn when to confront and challenge situations in order to achieve more favorable win:win scenarios.

Hillary is a very engaging and informative professor. She does a great job of mixing lecture materials with practice in the classroom. You can tell she cares about her students and is a very genuine individual.

Dr. Elfenbein was an amazing professor. I had her for two classes, and I could not have been more impressed. Fantastic lecturer, great sense of humor and an earnest passion to teach. All she expects is that you be there and be engaged.  Anyone who would ever say a cross-word about her is really commenting on their own character .

This week-long Negotiations course was one of the most intense courses I took during my MBA program; however, it was well worth the effort. Hillary does a great job of breaking down the negotiations process and teaching you how to understand the various factors at play. This should be a required course- many business and professional applications.

This course has provided invaluable training for my current management role. Hillary is able to present best-practices for leadership and leadership development that are evidence-based and firmly grounded in organizational behavior research while also teaching the real-life, practical application of such information.

You dont learn anything in class, so theres no point going to class. Its time for chatting about theatre stuff. You dont learn any technical things. You have to participate in a production and work on a group project, and both of them are highly time consuming and annoying. Have to spend ur weekends stuck in the backstage doing nothing.
He would get very ill-tempered, where he yelled at students and graded unfairly, unnecessarily harsh.

The class is unorganized and the lectures are boring. There is a group project and you have to work a production which can take up to two weekends. It's quite annoying. Would not recommend unless you really need an A. It's not that its hard to get it just a lot of annoying things you have to do. Not worth it. Plus he thinks he's funny and he's not.

One of the nicest teachers I've had. Lets you out of class early lots of days and is excited to see what you're working on. Generally a really understanding guy who wants everyone to do well if the just put forth some effort. DEFINITELY recommend him!

This will probably be my most favorite class in my college career. Intro to Theater Production was wonderful. Awesome professor who loves what he does. Learned a lot. Pretty easygoing and a ton of fun. Never dreaded going to class and he made sure we knew our stuff. The actual work in the theater took lots of time but the experience was memorable.

Had him for Intro to Theat. Production. HIGHLY RECCOMMEND - favorite class of the semester. Less than a lecture than a bunch of friends talking - and noone knew each other when it started. It helps that he loves the subject. He's understanding and easygoing and tries to make sure everyone understands things. A great guy and a great teacher.
a great professor
Homework is so difficult and it is useless.

I think he must be much busier than before. Which means he always seems unprepared for the class. Also, he is reluctant to give credits to the students who struggle. I got A Level in another math class much harder than optimization, so I believe this is not my problem finding this course is really hard to learn. Good luck guys, things are changed.

Professor Li often repeats himself on difficult-to-understand topics which was very helpful. He cares about his students and really wants them to succeed. The final exam schedule was made last minute, which was a bit stressful.

Professor Li was great at explaining things and was usually very organized in class, making sure to cover all of the necessary background information before diving right into the more rigorous optimization content. The material was interesting, the workload was manageable, and there was even opportunities to make up for missed points.
Li really knows his material and is very mathematical. You will struggle but learn to appreciate optimization.

Lectures were fine and workload is pretty manageable. Clearly an extremely intelligent professor and is willing to make time for students or direct them to a TA despite his hectic research responsibilities. Only complaint is that he can be a little too nitpicky and condescending. Highly encourage going to TA office hours if you need help.

Decent enough class. You'll get very familiar with R, and there are a bunch of resources (TAs, Canvas content, decks, etc) to make sure you know what you're doing. Montgomery is a pretty compelling lecturer, but the TAs rock and 100% make the class.

Prof. Montgomery is a solid lecturer and a big help with your final poster. You are graded almost entirely on 4-person group assignments throughout the semester (Lecture Activities, Labs, and Poster Project), but there are a lot of TAs to help. The lectures about R can be really fast and unclear especially if you lack prior coding experience.

data science for politics is manageable if you go to TA office hours. The class is lecture heavy and the final project is a poster project with a group you don't get to choose. I really don't like when groups are assigned for final projects- I ended up doing almost everything for mine. Montgomery is a mansplainer and his lectures are boring.

Overall a good professor. Clear lectures, and the class is easy enough if you keep up with content and go to office hours. Workload is manageable, but class gets harder near the end of the semester. However, he can be condescending and nitpicky, especially when it comes to the group poster projects. Go to TA office hours if you can and you'll be ok

Professor Montgomery is a very intelligent, clear lecturer who keeps his lectures interesting. The class itself was difficult, but he provided a solid intro to social statistics and the coding language R. Textbook use wasn't necessary as he provides summary materials of each concept on his well-managed website.
She knows her matetial and links everything we learn to real life experience.
I love her classes
Love her classes. She cares about us.
I learnt a lot from her.
She knows how to teach. I advise other professors to learn from her.

Horrible professor, she explains nothing never answers questions in class or clarifies how grading works, never returns grades on assignments and the final was on material we were never even taught. Worst professor I've had at WashU

If you have to take Calc II with either Thornton or Bickel, choose Bickel. Her lectures are much more organized, she sets you up to take fantastic notes, and she tries to go through the problems that you will expect to see in every class. Since she was on sabbatical from Bucknell, she might not be at WashU long, but she's definitely a solid choice.
She's probably your best shot at doing well in Calc 2 in terms of teachers. Hard class but pay attention in class and you are halfway there because she really is a good teacher.

Very tough course with multiple choice average on most recent test being 39% before the curve. Great person but just hard material. Better than Blake as far as teaching goes but just hard class conceptually; clearly she's brilliant.
She is a very nice teacher. The class itself is hard, but she tries to explain everything. Overall, she is a great teacher, but the class itself is a pain in the butt.
She will stop and answer your questions if you have any. She is super friendly.

Worst class I've taken in my academic career. You only get graded by the TA, and my TA was awful. The assignment expectations outlined in the syllabus are totally not adhered to when you turn in assignments. Would not recommend or ever take this class again.
Professor Rogowski is as great as they come! My favorite course at Harvard thus far!

Gives fun and engaging lectures with lots of participation, and really tries to get to know students in a fairly big class. Homework/tests allow for a lot of flexibility of responses and are designed to test thinking not memorization. He cares about students learning, being engaged, and enjoying what they're doing, not about numbers & grades.
Super caring about his students, got to know everyone in our 60 person lecture. He says on the first day that he doesn't care about grades. If you learn the material and pay attention, you're good.
One of the best professors I've ever had, if not the best. He's very helpful and very engaging. Really cares about his students. I'm going abroad, and I'm sad he won't be teaching next fall.

Her teaching approach seems to primarily involve reading from PowerPoint slides, which lacks logical explanations. There appears to be an issue with unfair grading in the course, and it's disappointing that feedback is unavailable. Hiring such an ineffective professor for a mandatory class has potentially impacted Olin's ranking negatively.

I would advise against selecting this professor, as she tends to solely focus on presenting her own viewpoints during class without providing precise explanations or responses to her students. It is advisable to keep your expectations low for her class. Her course evaluations tend to be irrational and unfair, resulting in unjust scores.

You can't learn anything in her class, everything has to be done by self-study, the video before the class is just narrating the concept, everything has to be done by yourself, I'm really not sure what the purpose of choosing this class is.

Had a great experience taking MKT 370 this semester. Class consists of a wide variety of deliverables and material, from tests, quizzes, in class presentations, brand challenge, participation, to guest speakers, etc. You really get out what u put in. Kinda tough grader but fair, makes the grading criteria clear. Would definitely take her class!
Ok professor, teaches good concepts but not very engaging. However, if you study, take her practice exams, and review lectures, you should be good in the class.

DON'T TAKE HIS CLASS!!!!

Has behaved inappropriately towards female students historically (see several news articles available online for more information) and is under investigation for SA. Do not attend his class, protest his presence and stand with survivors.
He has been accused of SAing over 7 girls - stay away!!! He was put on leave and now is back.

Prof. Dybvig is a legend in financial academia and practice. His knowledge base is vast and he is up-to-the-minute in his currency on any topic in securities valuation, portfolio theory, and corporate finance.  He is a treasure to WUSTL and an invaluable resource for any student with the foresight to utilize his knowledge.  Seize the opportunity!

An... interesting? guy. I can't say he's a good teacher, though. His lecture style is EXTREMELY dull, but he definitely knows his stuff. Problem was nobody took the class seriously, & so I learned nothing.  If you're looking to learn a lot,  find a different class, or be prepared to go very out of your way to engage him in (or out of) class.

I took Poli Sci 3760 GUE with Bowersox and he is a great and relax professor. All of the lectures were super interesting and he's a really knowledge person. The assignments were low pressure and his grading was pretty easy. Highly recommend taking any class with him!
Love Professor Bowersox and would highly recommend taking his class

Zach is a phenomenal professor, incredibly knowledgeable, and very friendly. I came into the class interested in the topic and feel like I've left with a deeper understanding of the difficulties within the legal, political, and research contexts of human trafficking. If you want a chill prof where you'll learn a lot, definitely take this class.

Prof. Bowersox is easily one of the best professor at WashU. He has such a full understanding of the material which is crucial to how I interacted with the content. He is super accommodating and if you communicate your needs (i.e. for an extension) he will gladly help. This is a professor who you can learn from in every single interaction!

Zach is a really chill professor, has a great sense of humor, and very knowledgable. Can ask good discussion questions and runs great office hours, I could always get help if needed. My one caveat with the course (Human Rights) was how broad it was, if you want a more theory based class or more in depth as opposed to survey, not the right class.

Cntd: Her video also made fun of students using disability services, and she tried to compare herself to another professors stating that its sexist she isn't recognized as much as him. She will just have a lecture, never finish the material she is supposed to cover in class, and then say oops my bad.
This was so easy. Do the 10 half page papers early on in the semester then you can relax. Basically include your notes from the guests and maybe a couple sentences on what interested you.
She's definitely quirky and some of the exam questions are confusing but the class is interesting and she gives you a lot of opportunities to get points. Take lots of notes on everything she says!

I have taken 3 courses with Dr. Quinn-Human Growth and Development, Obesity and Nutrition, and Darwin and Doctors. She is great and really cares about her students. Plus, she takes the time to relate course material to current events (she's always up to date on current research), and takes ample amounts of time to answer any questions.

Quinn had good intentions, but she was quite unclear and hard to follow in class. Lots of information that was overwhelming at times, but super interesting! Get ready for the time of your life: placentas, high fructose corn syrup, germs (you can never have too many), and TB...
Extremely supportive and great all around professor. Class discussions were always extremely informative and intellectually stimulating. One of the best professors I've had!

Amazing professor! Eng is knowledgeable, understanding, encourages creativity, creates great discussion environments and supports students every step of the way, truly caring for their academic and personal growth. His classes are not just intellectually stimulating but also captivating and relevant. You won't regret taking any class with him!

Dr. Eng is one of kindest professors I've had at WashU! Classes are usually short reflections, lectures based on readings, and occasionally short media reviews -- like most humanities classes. He is incredibly supportive of students and their work. Very conscientious grader and understanding if you need to take time off!

Professor Eng is phenomenal. He genuinely cares for every student and is interested in their insights. Participation is a quarter of your grade, but he is understanding if circumstances impact your participation. He provides positive, constructive feedback on papers and allows opportunities for revision. A slay all around. Eternally glad I had him.

Seriously my favorite class that I took the semester that I took this class. Professor Eng is really understanding when it comes to outside circumstances and is flexible on deadlines if you give a good reason. He really encourages a fun discussion space and helps you to understand the gravity of the issues that you discuss.
Prof Godigbe cares about his students and was helpful during office hours. The exams are not extremely difficult if you take the time to study.

I personally disagree with the past couple comments. Tests were very straightforward and fair, got 100s. Dr. Godigbe's lecturing was very clear and helped me to learn a lot, and this was my first business class ever so I was a complete novice. Would highly recommend, Dr. Godigbe legitimately cares about his student's success and is very helpful.

Godigbe does a good job at answering questions but can run through the concepts pretty fast during lecture. Very test heavy (based on lecture) and the grading criteria isn't super clear (but he tries his best to make sure you end up with the highest grade possible). Honestly, he's not a super bad professor but the class itself is terrible.

Bright is online-savvy but fuzzy grading criteria. Lectures are hard to understand unless you do a ton of learning and mastering concepts outside class. Exams really matter -- I had an A before the final exam caused me to earn a C average. Additionally, he schedules multiple students at once for online appointments, which I find disrespectful.

Professor Godigbe was a pretty good professor. The class as a whole was confusing at times because we would be super ahead of the syllabus but tested on content from a while back. The midterms were not too hard and the final was pretty easy. He cares about his students and wants you to succeed.

Rowhea is the reason I declared an Ed. Studies major. She took the time to fully get to know each of us, and why we were taking the course. Didn't assign too many readings (30-50 pgs. weekly) and only assigned work as necessary (with clear grading requirements). I fully recommend taking a course with her before you graduate.

Dr. Elmesky is a very kind and caring professor. Her class is also super interesting no matter what major you may be pursuing. If you have the opportunity, definitely take her class before you graduate!

An amazing professor! There are a lot of readings but it definitely isn't hard to do well and the readings are so interesting that they're worth doing! A lot of people take this class because it's easy but Prof Elmesky still finds a way to get everyone to connect to the material and find the class meaningful, whether they study Education or not
it is impossible to get below an A

If you want an A for minimal work, take this class. If you want to also be inspired and learn a lot, take it with Dr. Elmesky. She is the kindest woman I have ever met and one of the best teachers at WashU!!!
Fun guy, he's had an interesting life, great professor; but other than that, the class is bizarre in a wonderfully good way, tackles a lot of issues.
Nick is awesome! He is so helpful when you need help on writing. Class discussions are wonderful. Take a class with Nick!!

Nick is greatas a person and a teacher.  He is one of the most genuinely caring and enthusiastic people you'll ever meet.  He takes the time to understand you and your degree of comfort with the material, and works with you accordingly.  Take his classes if you want to come out of it with a more nuanced way of thinking.  Can't recommend him more!
he's an awesome guy. take every class you can with Nick!

Nick Miller is a fantastic professor. Had him for Latino Experiences in U.S. but he teaches Writing 1 now. Always engages with students and uses real life examples to support his claims. Truly cares about his students and I'll miss my class with him.
He is really passionate about entrepreneurship and cares a lot about the students.  He gives a lot of office hours time to those who needwant extra help.
Very interesting, involves students, good discussions. Expects undivided attention & professionalism.
Most useful class in school.
Great class
Best class ever!

Cannon is that dude! When legal concepts had the potential to get dry, he would hit you with a hilarious and insightful court case to get you excited about the concept for the lecture. He is hilarious - this was a class I always looked forward to attending. If legal concepts are new it may take a few weeks to adjust, but he is accessible for OH!

Easily the best professor I've ever had. He is incredibly knowledgeable but also just an incredibly cool guy. By no means is this an easy class, but it is very interesting and graded fairly. If you get the chance to take this class you'd be a fool not to!

Incredible class and professor that singlehandedly re-kindled my interest in law. Readings are all well-chosen and interesting, and lectures are engaging and relate to readings. Participation is key to doing well and understanding content. Not too much writing or exams. Definitely take Cannon's class if you have the chance.

Great professor, maybe the best? The class is well-organized and well-taught. He really cares about the students and material. He'll make you work, though, and has no qualms about calling you out if you aren't prepared for lecture. Very VERY bright and accomplished dude, but he still makes time to meet w/ students outside of class.

Best professor at WashU. Way overqualified to be teaching the course but still takes it very seriously and makes sure everyone in the class gets the most out of it. He brings in awesome guest lecturers and just covers great material in general. I have never loved a class more, sad it's over.
She is the worst. Shows blatant favoritism, grades incredibly hard, and is outright rude. If you're thinking of taking (or re-taking) Bio 2960 with her, I'd recommend against it.
She's rude to students, shows high favoritism among certain few, and is horrible at explaining information.  However, her lecture notes are good (probably because they're from another prof).

One of the worst professors. Pays no attention to what students want. Very difficult and frustrating to talk to.  Her lectures are from another professor's powerpoint slides which means she is not fully prepared to teach this course. Strong favorism among the students can be observed.

Very old instructor (not a full-time professor) who significantly lacks the capability of teaching. Very biased. She is a person who loves to get picky, and refuses to admit her own mistakes. Must be avoided.
Summer course lecturer. She is very mean and not good to teaching. I think she is still a lecturer only because of her influential husband. dont take summer bio2960

Stats is hard no doubt, but Dr. Corbett goes the extra mile to help. Keep up with all homework and do the review exercises & you'll be fine. Tests are very hard study A LOT, especially FINAL! Ask questions, email him and he will help always!
Great Professor.... gives a lot of extra credit to help those who are struggling. Very fair, caring professor.
Intro to Statistics, very easy if you do the assigned homework every week. He was extremely nice and helpful. Highly recommended
Brilliant professor!  Goes the extra mile to help students understand concepts.  I highly recommend him.  Tests are a bit hard--had to study a lot.  Recommend this class for non-math majors.
For a math weary student like myself, professor Corbett was lifesaver. He made the material interesting and was always available for questions outside of class.

Frank is a great guy with a genuine passion for what he does. He is downright hilarious at points as well. The class will certainly teach you a lot about advertising and tech. You are only graded on two exams and a term paper, which sounds tough until you realize he provides more than enough questions in the study guides that make exams easy.

Awesome prof and an expert in the field of advertising. Class is interesting and midterm/final are very similar to the study guides he gives beforehand. 10 page paper at the end of the class which is rough, but definitely a pretty easy class.

This class is quite easy. Almost all of the work that you need to do can be done during class with time to spare. Everything is group work though, so that can be annoying. Frank cares more about the idea for a campaign than the actual execution and he loves to give feedback, so if you ask for it, you will be in a great position.

Frank is honestly amazing! His lectures are great and he's passionate about teaching. He's also super caring and understanding. His class was not only easy but I learned a ton of super interesting stuff!

Former advertising man, really knows his stuff. Lectures are great. Material is interesting. Lots of anecdotes about his time in the industry. If you're at all interested in advertising, take one of his classes.

Mullins was a good College Writing professor. She definitely pushes your writing and I learned a lot. She wants you to succeed, so meeting with her and listening to her comments are important for getting good grades. She's kind of quirky, so class was pretty entertaining. Power and Commodity Culture topics were very interesting, good discussions

While she is passionate about folklore and discussion conversations, her cackling laughter at her own jokes continues to haunt me. She poorly communicates the expectations for writing assignments and you can expect averages of a B-. She is nice about meeting one on one but its the only time youll ever learn something worth your time.

I honestly don't even think I can type how I feel about this class into 350 characters. However, I will say that if you do not write in a way in which she is familiar, you can expect to be shook by your grades. Good luck my friends. You're GONNA need it. Also, the questions asked during discussions can be confusing at times.

The professor was very subjective. In personal statements, she said, "your voice is too midwestern." She preferred some styles of writing, and if you did not write like that, you could expect below a C.

Willow Mullins is a great teacher and also the best person I have met here at WashU. It was a pleasure to be able to take her class and I would retake it again if I had the opportunity. She teaches writing 1 and helped make the transition from high school to college much smoother.
Prof Thomadsen has a very particular grading criteria - he can sometimes be a very tough grader, but if you meet with him regularly and put effort into the class you can do well.

Prof Thomadsen is a solid professor. He is usually pretty funny and the grading is very nice. If you put efforts into your assignments you'll be fine. The lectures are somewhat boring, but that could just be because I'm taking it online due to COVID.

Professor Thomadsen rocks. It is clear that he cares and is a really sweet guy and teacher. He just wants you to enjoy class and do well. Tests not too hard, doesn't try to trick you. If you participate, he will love you and be so happy. I took Applied Marketing Research with him. I didn't love topic, but he is great.

Thomadsen is the best Marketing professor at WashU, and you should definitely take his class. He genuinely cares about his students and doesn't try to trick you on exams. He makes class entertaining with his funny stories.

I highly recommend taking Prof. Thomadsen for MKT 370. His lectures are really entertaining and he does a good job of applying the material to real world examples. He's a pretty easy grader, as his goal isn't to make this your hardest class at Olin. He is extremely understanding, and his anecdotes about his personal life are hilarious.

I absolutely adored Prof. Mueller and this class. I learned an incredible amount about domestication, and she did such a great job of explaining concepts (and giving hints as to what would be on the quizzes). Highly recommend this class and professor! (she's also hilarious)

I love love love Professor Mueller. She made culture and environment a much more interesting class and changed the structure of the course to include more projects/essays that help your grade. She really tries to make class fun and engaging and her exams are not too difficult.

Natalie Mueller is absolutely amazing and I wish we could just be best friends. She was so cool, caring, chill, excited about the subject. You could tell she wanted to make the class low stakes and fun for us as long as we put in solid effort and learned something. I loved that. I learned so many interesting things about domestication. Awesome prof
I took Bio 3180 w/ Professor Mueller and learned a lot about domestication of modern species. She is very easy going and the class is chill as long as you pay attention during lectures.
Prof Mueller is an absolute GEM. Love her. Very intelligent, organized, and really cares about each individual student. Would definitely recommend.

Classes were almost all lecture in an un-engaging, awkward atmosphere. Slow paced. Many people called this their "daydream class." Many readings were interesting but very dense. A lot of your grade is based on two papers.

This is an easy class to get a good grade on with minimum effort. However, I recommend doing the readings (or just skim them as some can be pretty long) to be engaged during the lecture. He makes really good points and answers questions creatively. The "essays" all follow the same structure so with practice it will be easy to get good grades.

Dr. Gulledge is very knowledgeable, and the course material was great! Class was centered around readings. No exams - just an essay every week-ish, sometimes graded P/F. Lectures could be slow-paced/hard to pay attention to, at times.
Best professor. I am a huge Gulledge stan.

This professor is super quirky and goofy. Although the course is reading-heavy, you can honestly get away with not reading articles for lectures, although not so much for essays. He also disagrees with most of the readings, so it makes for a more interesting class. His grading isn't too difficult and the end-of-course debate is pretty enjoyable.
Good. Tough but nice. Funny. Interesting choice of books. Different.
GOOD!! Tough but nice
Good!!!
Nice
QP is the hardest class you will ever take.   Barbour is the most educated and well poised professor you'll ever have.
Unique lecturing style - always on the lookout to help students, although he will NOT spoonfeed you answers. Arguably the best professor in the BME department.
great professor take advantage of his office hours
No Comments

I took Dr. Watson for Philosophy of Law and she is my favorite professor I've had at WashU. She genuinely cares to get to know each of her students, and is so understanding/flexible. Her explanations of material are very clear and help explain confusing/difficult content. Also, her humor adds the perfect touch to her classes. 100% recommend.

I'd highly recommend taking a class with Professor Watson! The class is a lot of reading, and grades are based entirely on a few essays throughout the semester. However, it's definitely worth it; Prof. Watson's lectures make the material much easier to understand, and she's very flexible with deadlines. Readings are well-selected and interesting.

Professor Watson is great!

- Attendance isn't mandatory, but it is 10% of your grade and the in-class lectures really help you out.
- Be proactive about reaching out. Professor Watson is pretty lenient with extensions and gives good feedback on outlines; just be sure to chat with her outside of class.

Would definitely recommend a class with her. The readings aren't too heavy but definitely required, as it's pretty obvious who does the readings. A couple papers make up the entire grade but you have plenty of time to write them and gives clear feedback. The grade you get depends on the TA.
Excessive homework and high expectations.
Dr. Gabriela Romero-Ghiretti is the best professor I have ever had. She knows the material very well really cares about each of her students! She will go out of her way to help you!

WOW!! She is great! If you have any doubts, stop it and just take her class. She makes class fun and interesting and is always available to help. Class feels informal and friendly. I actually look forward to going, because of her. Her grading is tough, but so is everyone's at the 308 level.

Gabriela Romero-Ghiretti is an excellent professor.  She is knowledgeable and it is evident that she has a passion for what she teaches.  She has a sense of humor too, which greatly adds to the environment.  Also, she's nice, approachable, and very helpful! You won't be disappointed with her being your professor!
I took his class in Spring 2023 and it was amazing! Prof. Kasten is a super nice guy who will give helpful feedback on essays. His office hours were also really helpful to get extra feedback.
Like any college writing class, there is going to be a lot of writing. Kasten definitely makes it much better. Gives great feedback and I have always found him approachable. Solid.

I never really enjoyed writing until I took citizen scientist with Kasten - he's honestly one of the best professors I've had. He goes out of his way to help you for every essay, and his lectures improved my writing immensely. His class sparked a new interest in science writing for me, and I couldn't be more thankful to have taken his class
Kasten was my college writing professor during fall 2020, and was the only professor I had that actually knew my name. Such a great guy!

Professor Aksoy's class is lecture-based and some classes can feel boring, however many topics are interesting so overall I found the class worthwhile. Her class was not super easy, but also not excessively hard. Aksoy is a bit disorganized but she is certainly an expert on political violence and terrorism, and I learned a lot from her.

Aksoy is a smart professor, but she's a dry lecturer. Not very accessible outside of class and a harsh grader. She means well, but she comes off as disrespectful and uncaring in both her grading and her office hours. Course material and topics on Political Violence are pretty interesting though. Forget asking about extra credit or any sort of help.

I really enjoyed Ethnic Violence with Aksoy; I felt she was a generous grader and a kind professor. She did a pretty good job of teaching in lectures and although I was not completely wow'd by the course, I definitely learned a lot of helpful ways to understand conflict. Lots of reading, midterms not bad, papers super easy, lectures are average.

Really enjoyed Political Violence and learned a lot about current events. Great way to get a broad overview of important conflicts occurring in the world today/in recent history. Midterms were average difficulty for poli sci; they just required very close attention to the readings. One short final paper. All in all not too difficult.

Taught this class more like a seminar, did not teach the law so much as she taught her own opinion. Did not welcome other viewpoints, even if they were not necessarily conflicting. Had very strange, unsubstantiated takes on the law that she taught as gospel. Inflexible with students and assignments were unhelpful. AVOID AT ALL COSTS

She is very well versed in her field and her class is a great place to have debate. The subject matter is interesting and she does a good job about covering many aspects. She was always interesting and approachable.
Good professor. Very organized. Very sharp and is respected in her field.
Relatively easy.  Class went pretty quickly and was somewhat interesting.  I have no interest in family law as a practice area, but it was overall worth taking.

He is an amazing professor.  Every class period has a different theme and purpose, he accepts and encourages all different styles and personal voices, into to playwriting was the best class I've taken here. Truly cares about the students and their individuality.
Wonderful to learn from. Is an amazing writer himself and has fun and insightful ideas about how to improve your work. The best!
Carter really generated a safe and supportive environment for writing and developing plays.  He took the time to get to know each student's style and help him or her to achieve his/her personal best.

Intro to Playwriting is a fabulous class and Carter is a wonderful Prof.  His writing activities are fun and useful, and he is brilliantly creative. The class is a lot of work, but it's all very enjoyable.
Agree with other comments.  He really cares about his students.
Agree with other comments.  Gives you the essay topics before you start the unit, and basically spells out the essay answers throughout the course so you don't have to write the night before.

very sweet man, cares a lot about his subject and his students. very smart and knowledgeable, but sometimes forgets that his students aren't 70 year old theologians too. a LOT of reading, and a lot of abstract ideas, but interesting if you want an open, more secular study of the scriptures

Although his lectures were often abstract and hard to follow, Dr. Poag was an excellent facilitator of class discussion and an extremely kind person.  Fairly easy grader of papers, although his expectations were sometimes difficult to discern.

I took this during the 3 week summer term, which was very intense. She really knows her topic, with 30 years experience. She returned papers within a day, with detailed, helpful feedback. She was reliable and cared about our learning. The format was mostly powerpoint lectures, which meant it was content-driven without creative delivery.

This class had the potential to be interesting; it wasn't. The three hour block was wasted with power point presentations and semi-irrelevant debates. Pedagogically, the instructor does many of the things we as educators are told NOT to do. Irrelevant worksheets abound while actual teaching and engagement with the class are missing.

This class was okay, although there were hugely long powerpoints that were extremely boring.  You'd think a 3 hr class would be more discussion based, but it wasn't, really.  Take home exams were not horrible, though, if you used the book.

Augustin is nice.  But... 3-hr long classes: so boring, in-class time is usually wasted except with maybe speakers.  TOO MANY ASSIGNMENTS but she changes the syllabus every year.  Unsettling mix of low-effort (in-class) and high-effort (assignments).  Required for educ. major.

Waste of time. Demba likes to toot his own horn. Lots of eye rolls from students when Demba lectured, aka read from text book. Took class in 2014, but went to a seminar this winter and learned five times as much as Demba tried to teach.

Isn't engaging, doesn't give straight answers. Teaches but isn't in to teaching. Reads from slides and ends class. Doesn't work problems for clarity. Lots of students stop showing up to class because they'd gain more spending that time reading.

The only reason I ended up with such a good grade: 1) got a lot of tutoring, 2) Demba offered an extra credit project 3) He threw out the in-class final grade because everyone failed. I took nothing away from the class except relief.

He teaches a variety of Econ Classes.  All tests are take home and open book.  sounds easy but they are meticulous.  Very fare instructor who understands if you need to miss a class.  I've had at least 4 of his courses!!
Professor Banjanin inspired me to pursue Russian as a career instrument from the first lesson in 1993. She was serious about her profession and teaching. But a friend of the students.
She was a total ****!
wonderful lady.  Unfortunately this is her last year because the schools closing down the Russian dept.
No Comments
Very smart professor and respectful, ethical human being. He teaches at a very high, intellectual level. He really pushes his students to expand their vocabulary and critical thinking skills.

Although the material was beyond what I understood (or could say I enjoyed) - I liked Dr. Meyer. He has a tendency to talk for long blocks of time, and it can be difficult to follow what his point is; however, he is still a decent facilitator of discussion. Paper topics are odd, but he is a fair grader - make a good argument, and you will be fine.

He clearly both cares and knows quite a bit about the subject matter and tends to pontificate for long periods of time. That being said he is one of the most caring and understanding professors I have had. If you are really interested you'll definitely enjoy his class, if not you might be extremely bored at times but you will learn something.

Professor Meyer is extremely intelligent and well-versed in his subject.  That being said, he enjoys supplying long answers when they are unnecessary and tends to ramble.  Extremely fair grader, he once gave me an A even though he didn't agree with my argument! Overall, I wouldn't avoid his class.

Lyda's a great teacher! She really cares about every students success. She's very enthusiastic and has a deap understanding of the material. To do well, read the chapters, read them again, and participate in class disscussions. Would recommend not asking questions that are answered in the syllabus. I hope to take more classes from her.

Expects professionalism from students, but then has "bad days". Overall she knows what she is talking about and is a good professor, but the experience has been negative. She also schedules the midterm exam for the day after fall break and makes herself available for reviews during the vacation, when many students are not in town.  Thanks.
Very enthusiastic, very knowledgable, easy on the eyes!
Lyda is gorgeous and brilliant and great in class!  It doesn't get any better!!
Fourth year at WashU. He is by far the best lecturer I have had in my WashU career. Engaging and personable with a good sense of humor. Made me excited to go to class
Great guy, understands his students well. Is very young so connects well with current events

Nice guy, does a lot of discussion of current events as they relate to the class material, which was helpful for learning/putting the class in context. He's good at explaining concepts, too. Homework isn't terribly difficult, exams are definitely on the hard side.
Great

She says exactly what are on the slides so if you download her powerpoints you would never have to go to class. The grade consists of 2 papers and 2 "projects" where you either make a slideshow or some fake tweets and call it a day. Oh and the first assignment that was worth 5% of the grade was to draw a map. So it's very easy to do well

Amazing professor. Out of the Wild is a great class you learn a lot and its pretty much just participation and the one final paper. As long as you show up most of the time and talk a little bit you'll be set.

Great professor. Out of the Wild was basically graded on attendance and one giant paper so if you showed up it wasn't that hard. She's very nice, funny and flexible if you want to push deadlines. Great class.

Gives really interesting lectures and really cares that her students are interested in what she's talking about. Very passionate about her field and understanding if you have to miss class. She wants you to enjoy spending time in the class and supports you in other classes as well; she recognizes that her class isn't the only one you're taking.
Prof. Kerschen was really good and really cared about making sure the students understood the material. Not hard for an intro class and helps you expand your knowledge of the language.
Dr Kerschen really cares about her students and is always approachable. However, homework is a bit heavy and a bit more is expected from students absolutely new to the language.

Dr. Kerschen cares a lot about her course and her students. She relies heavily on the textbook by using a flipped classroom approach. She makes classes fun and engaging but sometimes expects too much out of students who are fresh to the language.

She uses a textbook for homework both before and after lecture, but the work she assigns takes about fifteen minutes each to complete. Overall, she's very helpful, super communicative, and incredibly understanding.

Limbeck is a pretty passionate professor and clearly knows a lot about paleontology. However, lectures are often over 100 slides long and fairly unorganized. This class goes over a lot of material and the tests require a LOT of memorization. The only saving grace for this class is that exams are curved heavily and the homework is very light.

If you're taking the dinosaurs because it seems like an easy A, do not take it. Lectures are extremely boring, and there is a ton of material to memorize. She seems nice, but there are definitely other classes that are easier to fulfill credits.

I took her Unearthing the Science of Climate Change class. Great teacher, she really knows what she is talking about and her explanations were easy to understand. Expectations are fair and also very clear. Virtually no homework and a manageable final project. I would really recommend Dr. Limbeck and especially this class for future first-years.

Limbeck is amazing! She is a wonderful addition to the EEPS department staff. Her expectations are crystal clear and she is always available to help/explain. Super clear lectures, very little homework, and very straightforward exams. Challenging and stimulating but not overwhelming! Everyone in EEPS should take a class with her if possible!

Tough grader for the first half of the semester. Then found out nobody was getting an A. The second and third exams were easier and the final grades were fair.

Lectures were easy to follow, and he would repeat/clarify ideas if asked.
Professor Ouyang organized his lectures very nicely.

He certainly has room for improvement as a newly hired AP, and he is quite strict about the completeness of proofs, but this class was really not that bad. He offered a lot of EC to make up for the low midterm avgs and never tested on the hardest concepts. I only got ~70 on the first midterm but really improved over the semester and got an A+.
Worst professor I have had at WashU. He gives absolutely no partial credit and he rarely answers questions on Piazza. His announcements are rude and his grading is horrendous. Avoid him at all costs.

Prof. Moen really rocked this class. Crystal clear demonstration of principles of differential equation enabled me to learn the materials even without looking at the books. He is a mathematician and NOT an engineer. Some engineers who are only interested in getting answers to the math equation may not like him. Nonetheless he did lots of examples.

I really liked Kabe's matrix algebra class. He was quite passionate about the subject; he made it quite obvious. He explained the concepts in terms that were easy to understand. The homework assignments are pretty straightforward, usually containing one or two challenge problems. Tests were exactly like homework sets. Great class.

Kabe understands the material he is teaching but cannot articulate it at all. It's normal for him to spend nearly the whole class on a single problem only to tilt his head to the side and say "Hmm I think I messed something  up" I don't think the material in Math 217 would be difficult if Kabe was able to teach it effectively. Avoid him if you can.

She has come to be one of my favorite professors. I appreciated her disdain for nonsense, as well as her insistence on participation and engagement. Because this was a lower level language course, it was homework heavy, but it never felt like busy work. Take note, she can be savage.

She's a great teacher, and it's easy enough to do well in her class. I struggled understanding her some of the time, but that's because she's a good, native speaker and I'm still a student learning the language. She does a good job working with the students if they seem to be struggling.
Professor Bouamer is passionate about French and strives to make the course entertaining as well as informative. Extremely helpful and sweet. Easier to understand in French than in English!

Don't even think about not reading the case studies!  He can smell it is you don't show up prepared.  If you do your work you will love his classes. Probably one of the best proffers I've had in healthcare management. He's an executive for his day job, so he expects a lot from us be he gives just as much.

He's a healthcare executive so knows his stuff, and expects you to come prepared.  He will work with you if you need it, but don't come with excuses.  Class participation is mandatory, so read the cases!!!  Kinda easy on the eyes.   Oh, there is group work so don't be surprised.

very organized and knowledgeable. Uses Harvard cases for review. no textbook needed. class participation & attendence req'd. one midterm paper and one group final presentation. Would highly recommend!
Has quizzes over his lecture about every week, not very difficult just go over your notes and you should be fine. Great Professor!
Go to class and take notes and you can get an A.
Great ta, accessible, pretty easy grader
One of the worst
Zach is awesome

Dr. Feinstein lectures rather poorly, and tends to gloss over conceptual material. Exams are take-home, so you end up simply replicating methods step by step from lecture and homework, rather than actually ever learning the "why" behind the methods. I don't feel I got much out of taking the course, but it was required for my major.

Dr. Scott is absolutely amazing! MKT 378 is one of my favorite classes I've taken at WashU. The lectures and topics are very relevant, and she presents them in a very easy-to-understand way. The homework assignments are very straightforward and really helpful. Exams aren't hard if you study. I learned a ton from this class and enjoyed it a lot.

Professor Scott was awesome! She answers questions really well and truly does want her students to understand the material and succeed in the class. Lectures were pretty interesting and had a lot of real-world application. There's not a ton of assignments and most of them are fairly straightforward.

Professor Scott is amazing! Lectures are super interactive and fun. Homework is not much, really helpful, and fairly easy. One group project and one final exam, but lots of resources to help you succeed on them. One of my favorite classes and profs at WashU!

Dr. Cameron's class is very interesting. The readings are not necessary but participation is. Lectures and group discussions are usually good but can become boring as the semester progresses. It depends on the semester but be ready for a good amount of classes over Zoom.

I've had two classes with Heather Cameron and can tell that she is invested in her field of specialty. She has helped me understand my career aspirations and was often available to meet with me outside of the classroom to extend my learning. She is a role model and assigns work that is sure to make you think outside of the b-school box.

Professor Cameron's classes are disorganized at best. The "mandatory" readings are not necessary, and most of your grade in the class depends on your attendance, participation, and final project. She is hard to reach outside of class, and she does not post grades. Hard to gauge your grade unless you touch base with her frequently.
Pretty good teacher. There are a lot of readings for homework and his lectures essentially go over the readings, so sometimes they felt redundant. Nice guy.

The professor is super nice and tries very hard, but the class is not very exciting. Lectures are just going over what we read, so I didn't see the point of going to lectures. Readings are heavy in this class, so I would not recommend if you can't read 20+ pages per class.

The class I took was a mix of lecturing and group activities. There is a lot of reading, and doing the reading on time makes the essays and tests a lot easier. Prof. Ishizuka is a really kind teacher who always has time to help, and clearly explains his rubric/requirements for writing assignments. I highly recommend taking his class.

Kareena is one of the best professors I had at WashU. Other reviews say she never gave feedback, but she always gave great feedback to me and my class. Course expectations lined up with university requirements and she was still flexible. Her passion for the subject is obvious and she was always considerate + realistic with students about grades

Professor Gianani was always very constructive and gave applicable criticism while respecting our individual styles. I grew a passion for nonfiction, which I never thought I would like. The workload was challenging, but manageable. The course was very organized and easy to follow. I was encouraged to speak up in discussions, but never pressured to.

Professor did not give us grades till the end of the semester, and I did not get back feedback on ALL my writing. Grading scale is extremely arbitrary. Work load was ridiculous, had to write two whole page letters for every single class and annotate 20 pages. Often forced students to read private writing activities in front of class
Do not take this class. Her feedback was so unhelpful and we didn't get any letter grades until the end of the semester. She often called students out in class and made people feel uncomfortable.

Chris is amazing! Not only the best voice teacher at WashU but probably one of the best in the country. She cares so much for her students and has a vast and flawless knowledge of how the voice works.
Voice of angel, patience of a saint, one of my best memories at Wash U.
Chris is the best voice teacher at WU!
Hard to keep awake. Especially when the professor yawns all the time!

Shelly is SUPER HOT, even though sometimes she can come off as cold as ice. Once you get to know her she is more friendly. She teaches like she is giving a talk at a symposium though, which she needs to change. She knows her neuro though. Too bad she's married to that dork in the department! (J/K, he's a really nice guy...)
Mostly lectured off powerpoint which is sometimes helpful, but makes it hard to stay awake.

Linda is a great instructor. I learned so much information that I still use outside of class. It helps that she is a practicing illustrator and has insight into the world of a graphic designer. It was  definitely a delightful class to take.
No Comments

Linda is quirky & class was always fun - she assigns projects that use a myriad of mediums. Not such an easy grader but that's only because she's forgetful - make friends with her early one and you'll do fine

Presents complicated material as easily as you or I breathe. Very energetic, always recaps previous lessons, and tells you where he's going with the material. Really shows he cares about your success. Homework and tests are VERY hard unless you really know the material inside out. Time spent just playing with equations is always time well spent.
This guy is freakin brilliant. He doesn't even use notes when he lectures, and they're still crystal-clear and helpful. Take ANY class with him
No Comments

Had to jump in on this because I loved Sarah. She was one of the only design professors that I felt understood what I was going for and my aesthetic. I would have dropped out of the program senior year if it weren't for her.
avoid any class taught by this woman.

she's bipolar. you never know if she's going to be nice and inquisitive or a complete authoritarian monster out to humiliate. She does know her material but it can get tedious with typography. she's not consistent. she tells us its a work-structure environment but then doesn't respect the students, gives pointless patronizing lectures over and over

Super nice professor! His materials are definitely harder for a marketing class. Homework and tests are math-heavy rather than concepts. Got a lot of interesting knowledge from this class. The game simulator was fun. If you like math, take this class.
The professor explained the concepts very clearly with many examples. Very useful examples and slides. I like it that the course was not too mathematical as I had expected.

Extremely nice guy. Covers the basics of pricing in a very clear, easy-to-follow way. Did a decent job of making lectures interesting, and grading procedures were pretty generous. Exams were take-home.

She is a very easy marker and decent lecturer given that French 307 is grammar-oriented and not a hard course to teach. However, there is a great distance between her and students. She is enthusiastic without a doubt, but she does not seem to show a genuine interest in students' affairs. I easily see her as a teacher but not as a friend.
BEST PROFESSEUR EVAAAASZ!

Mlle Maucher is the best French professor I have ever had! She genuinely cares for her students. She will challenge you, but is so willing to help you outside of class.  I learned a lot very quickly and enjoyed every minute of it. She does a very good job of making class fun by incorporating different types of media.
Exams based on lectures, easy grader on papers. ALSO, the BEST Twitter!! Go check it out, you won't regret it. Keith is hilarious.

Game theory is a pretty dry subject, and Keith did an okay job at making it interesting. There is limited relation to political science. The class' material is not easy, but there are substantial curves on the three exams. There are weekly problem sets, which are sometimes difficult, but Keith helps a lot at office hours.

He can be a boring lecturer, but he is intelligent and easy to talk to. The material is not hard, but make sure you do the article readings before class. The textbook isn't very necessary. You will do non-graded, small-group work almost daily. The tests and the paper are easy, but don't start researching for the paper a week before it's due.

He doesn't explain concepts very well and is very unorganized, tend to skip over slides and freestyle in class. Even if materials can be dense and long, he struggles to explain the basics. Gradings are very generous. I don't know what I learn from the course and still got an A.

Professor Zhang did have some trouble explaining some topics but I think a lot of it was due to there being so much material to be covered. Although, it is pretty obvious that he is very interested in what he teaches and is very generous in grading/extensions/extra credit.
Struggles to communicate complex ideas and hard to understand. Very unorganized class structure. Frequently misses lecture because of travel to conferences.

I learned more in Nakissa's Muslim societies class than in any other class in my life. His classes are all lecture but he will take tremendous time to answer questions people have during class and is quite accessible outside of class. Take his class!

Professor Nakissa is a must-take WashU professor. His lectures are fantastic, he's passionate about the material he teaches, and his perspective is both fascinating and unique.  Up and coming star, so take a class with him while he's still here!

Beginning of the course was kind of dry, but he's really passionate about the topic, talks a lot. Gets more interesting as the semester goes on, for sure.  Quizzes based completely on reading, would highly suggest keeping up, but he grades incredibly easy.

This guy exudes arrogance. He is a not a nice person, and is absolutely awful at explaining even the simplest of concepts.  Exams were just plain dumb and covered topics we didn't even discuss in class.

The first assignment of the semester (a homework assigned in January) was returned while we were taking the FINAL exam in May. The professor(s) did not care about the class. Exams were difficult. Grading was likely arbitrary. I would recommend reading an imaging textbook over taking this class.
The course was covered in a very interesting way. The textbook for this course wasn't very useful, but the instructor took efforts to make topics clear.

Though the homework takes some time, Jha tries hard to make sure no student is lost and can get good grades by giving lots of extra credits (Most people got A and A+). The most useful and inspiring class I've taken so far!
Definitely one of the easiest for BME electives. <4 hrs per week spent on this course.
Jha is really good at teaching and knows what he's talking about. Exams/HWs/Projects were fairly ok. Easy A :)
Absolute dog. One of the nicest professors I've had at WashU. Hydrology is very hard but she makes it worth.

This is one of those classes that completely changes how you see the world. Homeworks are applications of topics covered in the lectures. Dr. Masteller is really enthusiastic about the topics and makes lectures interesting

The assignments are not indicative of the lectures. I thought that the lectures would help me understand the assignments, but even attending the TA's office hours did not help enough. An extremely tough grader as well and is sometimes not explicit about what is wanted for assignments and exams. I would not take this class unless necessary.
Dr. Hill is an amazing professor. His lectures are very engaging and he has a great sense of humor!

Prof Hill is a great guy! He is very knowledgeable on the subject and you can tell he has a lot of passion for psychology while he is teaching. Be warn, he is a tough grader but it just makes you want to try harder.

I was excited to take Personality Psych but Hill sucked the joy out of it. He doesnt know how to teach and is lucky that Google exists otherwise no one would be crossing a 5 in his exams. His slides seem like they have been made by a toddler who just learnt how to screenshot on their parents' laptop as they are just graphs with 0 explanations

Cheynel is a very caring professor! She is extremely knowledgable about the course content and the class has low workload. Just make sure you understand the content well and prepare for the midterm and final as your grade is dependent on those. The professor and the TAs do many office hours so if you need help, you will definitely receive it!
Overall, Good professor. Her lectures can get a little boring, but she is a fantastic person. The midterm is precisely like the practice exam. Homework is fairly easy since you have unlimited tries

Professor Cheynel is awesome. Her lectures are very engaging and energetic. You can tell she loves her job. The review test is in the same format as the actual exams. She may do the occasional cold calls during simple practice exercises, but if you get it wrong it's not the end of the world. The final exam is reasonably tricky though.

Prof Neiman is an exceptional professor who built an extremely interesting collection of readings & lectures. This has been one of my favorite and most educational classes at WashU. Last 2/3 of the class all reading-based discussion - easy to get by w/o readings but they're worth doing and interesting! Most of your grade is attendance/participation

Neiman is extremely passive-aggressive and lacks empathy for personal struggles and traumatic life events. Stg he thinks his class should be your highest priority and that any opinions countering his are invalid (I lost points on 2 assignments bc he disagreed with my opinions). I highly recommend that you avoid 3105 and Neiman at ALL costs!

The class structure is based on rotating tasks: one week you present on Thursday, next week you present on Tuesday, next week you review a film, and the week after that you get a break. Also a final project at the end (he lets you do creative things with it). Course content was interesting, certainly worth the work. Kind professor that cares.

Outside of class, he is a pretty normal guy, but as a professor, he doesn't really know what's going on. Grading is inconsistent from paper to paper and from section to section. It's very hard to know what he wants until it's actually graded.
It is simply not possible to convince him that his answer key is incorrect. He will defend his incorrect answers indefinitely despite all evidence to the contrary.

The class was intended to be realistic to engineering, but was more realistic to research. He never answered a question directly, and marked me off multiple times for doing something he TOLD me I could do. Basically, completely random grading with no desire to help.

Chel is a fantastic professor who has fundamentally influenced my worldview. She is so caring, and wants to make sure we learn and understand rather than check off boxes for assignments. I highly recommend taking a class with Chel if possible. life changing!
Amazing person and course (GCP)! Her activities, although a bit out there, truly give so much value to the course. One of my favorite classes and best experiences.
N/A

A great and passionate professor! Professor Warren-Grice cares deeply about every student and the well-being of the classroom and offers great support/flexibility in the classroom/course. I would say participation is important but the discussions are not stressful and facilitation is very much student-based so it is very easy to chime in!
I personally really enjoyed class with Professor Warren-Grice, I found her to be approachable and understanding, and foster an inclusive classroom experience.

Worst professor I had in college. The class was easy, but bias in her opinions was ludicrous. Went out of her way to be hostile toward opposing opinions. She was even negative toward certain religions. The class is all busy work, and fully inefficient. She has big-time favorites. If you are not, then you get scolded in front of your classmates.

Paulo was the TA for my History of American Photography art history class. I wrote what was honestly a B- paper and got an A. His comments were clear, there were areas to improve, but I followed the prompt and did the assignment. He is very easy going and approachable too. Almost like the slacker student from high school gone college TA.

Paulo is awesome. He made me look forward to going to Writing One every time we had class. Do the papers according to the prompt, and you'll do well. He is also willing to work with you if you mess up and don't answer the prompt correctly. Get Paulo for Writing One!

Frieda is hands down the nicest, friendliest, and bubbliest professor I've had. She really gets you excited to learn German by being such a sweet, smiley, and enthusiastic person! Very encouraging. The class is so fun yet you learn so much. Even after a semester of not taking German I've retained a lot of it. Definitely recommend!
Frieda is always the best!!! She's so bubbly and I will never forget her smile!!! She's the most amazing teacher I've ever had.

Honestly, the best professor that I've had in college so far. Super genuine and cares about his students' success. Will sometimes skew the topic that we're covering to talk about super interesting that relates to the real world. 1000% recommend taking his class.

Wants students to learn and have fun, and his lectures succeeded in doing this. Explained concepts well and encouraged students to ask questions. He also held office hours where he happily answered questions. However, material covered felt slightly disorganized. I do wish the class was more rigorous, esp. regarding proofs. Felt it to be too easy
